title: Abadejo a la Republicana (Salt Cod with Milk Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod
- onion
- parsley
- nutmeg
- flour
- 2 ounces of lard
- 2 tablespoons of flour
- 1 quartillo of milk

## Directions

1. Let the salt cod soak in water for one hour
2. Chop some onion and parsley, grate nutmeg, and prepare flour
3. Cut the salt cod into very thin pieces and place in a pot of water
4. Bring the pot of water to a boil, then remove from heat, cover carefully, and let the cod rest
5. In a second pot, put 2 ounces of lard and 2 tablespoons of flour
6. Add the chopped onion, parsley, and nutmeg to the second pot
7. Slowly pour in the quartillo of milk while stirring constantly with a wooden spoon, always in the same direction
8. Remove the salt cod from the first pot, dry it thoroughly, and transfer it to the second pot with the sauce
9. Place the pot over heat and let it boil once
10. The dish is ready to serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ajo Blanco (White Garlic Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- blanched almonds
- one clove of garlic
- oil
- vinegar
- water
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Mash blanched almonds with one clove of garlic, a little oil and the corresponding vinegar
2. Crush this mixture very well until it becomes like a somewhat watery ointment
3. Dissolve it well in a tureen or serving dish where the bread has been crumbled with sufficient water and bread
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Albardilla de Carnero Braseada (Braised Saddle of Lamb)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 saddle of lamb (albardilla), deboned
- Salt and pepper
- Fine herbs
- Strips of bacon
- Four to six onions
- A bouquet of fine herbs
- Two ladles of broth
- Equal quantity of white wine

## Directions

1. Bone the saddle of lamb, salt and pepper it strongly, and season with fine herbs on its inner side.
2. Fold it in a bit toward the inside to give it the shape of a cake, and tie it with string so it does not lose its form.
3. Put strips of bacon, four or six onions, and a bouquet of fine herbs in a braising pan.
4. Place the saddle of lamb over this garnish, adding two ladles of broth and an equal quantity of white wine.
5. Let it cook with little heat from below and somewhat more from above, with a dome-shaped lid, for about three hours.
6. After three hours, without removing the meat from the pan, carefully remove the string and then the skin, which will come off easily.
7. Place the saddle of lamb on a platter and sprinkle with dried parsley made into powder, covering it completely as if it were painted green, and serve it with its own juice.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Albardilla de Carnero Panada (Breaded Saddle of Lamb)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 saddle of lamb (albardilla), braised
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Braise a saddle of lamb.
2. After braising, coat it with breadcrumbs and place it in the oven at very strong heat for half an hour, turning it a couple of times.
3. The oven must have capacity to contain the entire saddle of lamb, which when prepared this way is a dish of considerable dimensions and luxury.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Alcachofas á la Barigula (Artichokes à la Barigula)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- large artichokes
- water
- salt
- sausage meat
- bread
- good quality butter
- fine herbs
- flour
- small mushrooms (if available)
- refined oil
- broth

## Directions

1. Clean the artichokes and cook them in boiling water with a little salt, but less cooked than for other dishes. Drain them well of all the water they contain.
2. Make a picadillo with sausage meat and crumbled bread, and mix well with very good quality butter. Sauté this, adding seasoning, fine herbs and a tablespoon of flour.
3. If you have small mushrooms on hand, incorporate them, cut very finely.
4. Fill the artichokes with this picadillo after cutting and removing internally, above the heart, some of the center leaves to form a cavity.
5. Place the artichokes thus prepared in a saucepan whose bottom is lightly bathed with a little refined oil. With the same, brush them on top and cook them for five minutes, covering the saucepan with a lid and fire on top.
6. If you see that they are drying out, moisten them with a few spoonfuls of broth from the pot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Alcachofas á la Italiana (Artichokes à l'Italienne)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- lemon juice
- water
- salt
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Divide the artichokes into four equal parts and remove the heart. Rub them with lemon juice.
2. Cook them with water, salt and more lemon juice. Remove and let drain.
3. Serve them with Italian sauce.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Alcachofas á la Provenzal (Artichokes à la Provençale)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- oil
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare and clean the artichokes and soak them in oil.
2. Place them in a casserole with oil, garlic, salt and pepper.
3. Let them cook with very low heat, and place on top a lid with well-lit embers.
4. Once they are cooked, remove the garlic, place them on a serving dish to sprinkle them afterwards with lemon juice before serving.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Alfajor de Primera Calidad (First-Quality Alfajor)
author: José Castro y Serrano
body:
## Ingredients

- One azumbre of white honey
- Three medios of hazelnuts and one pound of almonds, all toasted and chopped
- One and a half ounces of cinnamon powder
- Two ounces of aniseed, four drachms of clove and four drachms of cilantro, all toasted and ground
- One pound of toasted sesame
- Eight pounds of flour powder, extracted from hard biscuits without salt or yeast, very well baked in the oven
- Half a pound of sugar

## Directions

1. With half a pound of sugar make a syrup
2. Then add the honey, and when it reaches the proper point, add the prepared ingredients, three handfuls of sifted flour and the flour powder
3. Stir it so that everything is well mixed
4. Make the balls while hot
5. Bathe them in syrup
6. Cover them with fine sugar with some cinnamon, and wrap them in paper
7. In each pound of balls there should be from eight to twelve, so that they turn out well
8. Note: The difficulty and secret of the alfajor lies in the proper point of the honey; practice is the teacher here, as in everything
source_title: La mesa moderna

title: Alfajor de Primera Calidad (Spiced Almond and Honey Confection)
author: José Castro y Serrano
body:
## Ingredients

- One azumbre of white honey
- Three half libras of hazelnuts, toasted and chopped
- One libra of almonds, toasted and chopped
- One and a half onza of cinnamon powder
- Two onzas of aniseed, toasted and ground
- Four drachmes of clove, toasted and ground
- Four drachmes of cilantro, toasted and ground
- One libra of sesame, toasted

## Directions

1. Prepare the white honey
2. Toast and chop the hazelnuts and almonds
3. Powder the cinnamon
4. Toast the aniseed and grind it
5. Toast and grind the cloves and cilantro
6. Toast the sesame
source_title: La mesa moderna

title: Almejas a la Coquinera (Clams Fishwife Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- clams
- oil
- garlic
- parsley

## Directions

1. Instead of butter, use oil
2. Omit the scallions and water
3. Leave the clams only with their own juice in the casserole
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Almejas a la Cubana (Clams Cuban Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- large white clams
- butter
- garlic
- parsley
- scallions
- water
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Select the largest and whitest clams, taking care they have no parasites
2. Clean thoroughly in fresh water
3. Cook and open the clams
4. Make a sauté of garlic, parsley and scallions with butter
5. Add the clams and let them brown a little
6. Add water in proportion
7. Season with pepper and salt
8. Let boil for a while
9. Serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Almejas al Natural (Clams in Natural Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- clams
- water
- peppercorns
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the clams and place them in a casserole with the corresponding water
2. Season with peppercorns
3. Let cook over low heat, stirring them a little
4. Sprinkle with lemon juice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Almejas Cocidas (Cooked Clams)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fat white clams
- oil or butter
- parsley, chopped
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Select the fattest and whitest clams
2. Clean them beforehand
3. Cook and open them
4. Set aside their water
5. Add oil or butter and chopped parsley
6. Serve with their own water and lemon juice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Almejas Guisadas (Stewed Clams)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- clams, well cleaned
- butter
- parsley, chopped
- pepper
- salt
- broth, water, or white wine
- egg yolks, beaten
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Place well-cleaned clams in a casserole with butter
2. As they open from the heat, remove their top shell
3. When all are open, check them and remove any parasites
4. Pass the water that has come out of them through a sieve
5. Mix a piece of butter with chopped parsley, pepper and salt, moistening it with broth, water or white wine
6. Let boil for a few minutes
7. At the time of serving, add a beaten egg mixture and lemon juice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Almejas Marineras (Sailor-Style Clams)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram of clams
- best quality oil
- two large onions, trimmed
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- one clove of garlic, chopped
- two spoonfuls of flour
- water
- white wine
- two slices of lemon
- two bay leaves
- a bundle of fine herbs

## Directions

1. Wash the clams well in warm water and keep them in cold water for a good while
2. In a casserole with the best oil, fry two large trimmed onions for one kilogram of clams
3. Do not let them take color and season with a pinch of salt, pepper, clove, nutmeg and chopped garlic
4. Sprinkle with two spoonfuls of flour and let cook until half-toasted
5. Then add water to form a thick paste, and after three boils add white wine, two slices of lemon, two bay leaves and a bundle of fine herbs
6. When it boils vigorously, add the clams, taking care that the casserole is very large
7. Cook the clams this way for three-quarters of an hour over very low heat, with the casserole covered and a pair of hot coals on the lid or two or three pieces of coke in ignition
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Anadillas ó Anadoncillos (Young Ducklings)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Young duckling, cleaned and prepared
- Bacon leaves, one underneath and one on top
- 125 grams of lean veal
- 125 grams of ham, cut into small cubes
- Carrots
- Onions
- Salt and pepper
- Bouquet of fresh herbs

## Directions

1. Cook the duckling in a pot between two bacon leaves, one underneath and one on top, with 125 grams of lean veal and an equal amount of ham cut into small cubes, carrots, onions, good seasoning of salt and pepper, and a bouquet of fresh herbs.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Anadoncillo (Young Duck Braised)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one young duck
- two slices of bacon
- 125 grams of lean veal
- 125 grams of ham, cut into small cubes
- carrots
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- bouquet of fresh herbs
- one bowl of clear broth

## Directions

1. Cook the young duck in a casserole between two slices of bacon, one underneath and one on top
2. Add the lean veal, ham cut into small cubes, carrots, onions, good seasoning of salt and pepper, bouquet of fresh herbs, and a bowl of clear broth
3. Cook for no more than thirty minutes
4. Serve the young duck accompanied as desired
5. Garnish with fresh peas sautéed separately with bacon scraps if desired
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Anca de Corzo Asada (Roasted Roe Deer Haunch)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Roe deer haunch or hind leg
- Lard for larding

## Directions

1. Lard the haunch immediately after hunting
2. Keep it in the air for three or four days according to temperature
3. Roast it in the oven as one would roast a lamb leg, but for less time since the roe deer leg has less meat
4. Serve alone in its natural state, well moistened with its own juices, with a handle attached to the bone
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ancas de Rana al Estilo Filipino (Frog Legs Philippine Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Frog legs
- Pork lard or good Marseille oil
- Cooked potato slices
- Thin strips of red peppers from La Rioja

## Directions

1. Clean the frog legs and soak them, then dry them
2. Fry them in pork lard or good Marseille oil
3. In a serving dish, arrange slices of cooked potatoes
4. Place the fried frog legs on this bed
5. Season by sprinkling, covering everything with thin strips of red peppers from La Rioja
6. Place the dish in the oven at medium heat and serve very hot
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Anchoas a la Parisiense (Anchovies Parisian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Anchovies without spines
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Mustard
- Parsley
- Garlic

## Directions

1. Arrange the anchovies without spines on a plate.
2. Arrange them into decorative figures with hard-boiled egg yolks and whites, cut and combined by color.
3. Make a sauce with two hard-boiled eggs, oil, vinegar, mustard, finely chopped parsley, and finely chopped garlic.
4. Cover the contents of the serving dish with this sauce and serve at the table as a breakfast dish.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Anguila a la Tártara (Eel Tartare Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one eel
- broth
- reduced sauce
- breadcrumbs
- eggs
- tartare sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the eel into pieces and cook in broth sauce
2. When cooked, remove and drain
3. Pass the cooking liquid through a strainer and decrease it by reducing
4. Incorporate several spoonfuls of well-reduced thick sauce
5. Place the sauce in a pan to cool
6. Pass the eel pieces through the cooled sauce and breadcrumbs
7. Then pass through beaten eggs and breadcrumbs again
8. Twenty minutes before serving, place on the grill with low heat over a sheet of paper
9. Arrange on a platter in pyramid form
10. Serve with tartare sauce on the side in a sauceboat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Anguila Frita (Fried Eel)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- flour
- oil
- lemon

## Directions

1. Clean and skin the eel
2. Cut into very small pieces
3. Coat with flour
4. Fry in oil
5. Serve in a pile, alternating with lemon wedges
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Angulas (Baby Eels)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Baby eels
- Salt
- Pepper
- Oil
- Garlic

## Directions

1. Clean the baby eels with a cloth and scald them with boiling water, dry them again and place in a covered serving dish.
2. Sprinkle with salt and a touch of pepper, and keep the dish in a bain-marie so the plate is very hot.
3. At the moment of serving, pour over them, straining, oil fried with garlic, very hot and only in quantity enough to coat the baby eels without leaving excess broth.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Apple Cake (Apple Cake)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 apples, peeled and cleaned of seeds and seed cells
- half a glass of water
- lemon zest
- cinnamon
- half a pound of sugar
- eight eggs, well beaten as for an omelet
- butter for greasing the mold

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and clean them of seeds and seed cells
2. Cook them in a pot with half a glass of water, lemon zest, cinnamon, and half a pound of sugar
3. When the apples are well cooked, pass them through a fine sieve to make a purée
4. Let them cool and then add, stirring gradually, eight eggs well beaten as for an omelet
5. When the mixture is well combined, grease a mold with butter
6. Fill it with the mixture and cook it in a water bath
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Apple Marmalade (Apple Jam)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- lemon juice (from one lemon)
- sugar (one pound per two pounds of apples for preservation, or half a pound if to be eaten immediately)
- cinnamon sticks

## Directions

1. Remove the seeds and seed cells from the apples and soak the pieces in very cold water acidulated with the juice of one lemon, squeezed thoroughly
2. Remove the apple pieces one by one, drain them, and cut them crosswise into very thin slices about the thickness of coins
3. Place them in a saucepan without water over medium heat until they begin to liquefy
4. Stir constantly with a spatula so they do not burn or stick to the bottom of the pan
5. When the apples are almost cooked, incorporate one pound of sugar per two pounds of apples if the marmalade is to be preserved, or only half a pound of sugar if it is to be eaten immediately
6. It is better to eat the marmalade cold
7. Before serving, add a few cinnamon sticks, which should be removed before they have imparted too much flavor to the dish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Arenques Ahumados (Smoked Herring)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked or cured herring
- good oil

## Directions

1. Choose smoked or cured herring, making sure they are not too old as they could be too salty
2. Remove the head and skin completely, dividing them lengthwise into two equal parts
3. Extract the middle spine and all other small bones
4. From each regular-sized herring form two fillets of about ten centimeters long by three wide
5. Cut these fillets into strips of one centimeter wide throughout
6. Place on a pickled plate with good oil and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Arlequín de Judías (Harlequin Beans)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- handful of fine long green beans like those from Gandía
- handful of fresh shelled beans like those from La Granja
- salt
- pepper
- two ounces of beef butter for each pound of the dish
- fine herbs chopped
- clove
- nutmeg grated
- half lemon

## Directions

1. Cook together a handful of each class of beans in boiling water with salt and a pinch of pepper.
2. At fifteen minutes remove them and drain them, and keeping them hot, place them in a vegetable dish where there will be a mixture of two ounces of beef butter—for each pound of the dish—finely chopped herbs, salt, pepper and a pinch of clove and grated nutmeg.
3. Cover the vegetable dish with a pot lid so as not to dirty its own cover, and holding it well with both hands, shake it in the air so that everything mixes very well with the butter that melts from the heat of the beans.
4. Then place the vegetable dish at the mouth of a pot to keep it hot, and place its lid. At the moment of serving squeeze the juice of half a lemon over the dish.
5. Sprinkle chopped parsley generously, because it gives very good appearance and facilitates digestion.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Arroz Blanco (White Rice)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- salt
- rice, well cleaned and washed
- a small amount of pork lard

## Directions

1. First, bring water with a bit of salt to a boil, if possible in an earthenware vessel.
2. As soon as the water bubbles, add about half of well-cleaned and thoroughly washed rice.
3. Leave it on the fire until the moment the liquid begins to run out, and before it dries completely, remove it.
4. Keep it at a certain warmth, but away from the heat.
5. Coat it with a small amount of pork lard, just enough to moisten the top layer, and by the temperature it will spread throughout all the rice.
6. Do not let it toast or form lumps and clots.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Arroz con Leche (Rice with Milk)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Water
- Milk
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make the rice fluffy in boiling water
2. Remove and drain
3. Finish cooking over low heat in milk with sugar and cinnamon
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Arroz con Pollos (Rice with Chickens)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- lard
- garlic cloves
- tomato
- parsley
- artichokes
- peas
- red pepper
- pepper
- clove
- saffron
- rice
- broth

## Directions

1. Clean and cut three or four chickens into pieces
2. Fry them well in a pan or clay pot with lard
3. Add one or two garlic cloves, tomato, parsley, artichokes and peas, stewing everything well
4. Incorporate a little red pepper, pepper and clove and a hint of saffron
5. Add the rice and after two turns cover well with broth and cook over high heat following the previous method
6. This can be made with hens, partridges, etc., but when using tough birds or meats, add the rice only after they are very well cooked
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Arroz Gomar (Gomar's Rice)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One kilogram of the best rice
- One pound of salmon
- One pound of sole
- Half pound of eels
- Meat from two lobsters
- Breasts of six capons
- Twenty wings of quails
- One pound of ham
- Asparagus
- Artichokes
- Mushrooms
- Truffles
- Peas
- Tomatoes
- Peppers
- Cumin
- Pine nuts
- Three dozen crabs
- Small fillets of anchovies
- Oil
- Small onion
- Parsley
- Chervil
- Oregano
- Fennel
- Garlic
- Young garlic
- Saffron

## Directions

1. Prepare all the ingredients as listed
2. Cook together to create the Gomar rice dish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Arroz á la Alicantina (Alicante Rice)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- oil
- dried peppers
- garlic cloves
- tomato
- artichokes or peas
- rice
- saffron
- red pepper
- water
- fresh fish

## Directions

1. Fry three or four small round dried peppers (called flores in Murcia) in oil with speed and minimal frying as they burn easily
2. Set aside the peppers on cloth and in the same oil add two or three garlic cloves, tomato, artichokes or peas depending on season
3. When everything is well stewed, add the rice, stir for a few moments, adding saffron and red pepper
4. Cover with water and when it boils add fresh, clean fish cut into pieces
5. Turn once and cook over good heat
6. Pound the peppers well in a mortar and when done dissolve them in a little water
7. Add this mixture to the rice and let it cook like the previous recipe
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Arroz á la Valenciana (Valencian Rice)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- oil or lard
- peppers
- chickens
- ducks
- pork loin
- sausages
- garlic cloves
- tomato
- parsley
- red pepper
- salt
- saffron
- pepper
- clove
- artichokes
- peas
- green beans
- broth or water
- rice
- eel

## Directions

1. Prepare a pan whose bottom equals the size of the burner where it will be placed, with well-lit charcoal or wood fire
2. Add oil or lard in proportion and when well heated, fry several peppers, then remove them to clean when needed
3. Fry pieces of chickens, ducks, pork loin and sausages until golden
4. Add two or three peeled and chopped garlic cloves, tomato, parsley, red pepper, salt, saffron, a little pepper and clove, stirring everything until well fried
5. Add artichokes, peas or green beans cut into pieces, turn twice to stew
6. Add hot broth or water and let boil until everything is cooked
7. Increase the fire, add necessary broth, and when it boils add sufficient rice until it stands straight when stirred with a spoon
8. Cook over high heat, adding the peppers and some eel pieces
9. When half cooked, reduce the fire and let it cook slowly without stirring or moving it
10. When ready, remove it and after resting briefly, serve it
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Arroz á la Zamorana (Rice Zamora Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of excellent pork lard
- one pound of onions
- half pound of turnips
- parsley
- oregano
- thyme
- half a dozen garlic cloves
- a bit of sweet pepper
- pork leg, ear, and snout
- one pound of good ham
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Melt the four ounces of excellent pork lard in a good earthenware pot or bowl.
2. In it, sauté one pound of onions and half a pound of turnips.
3. Add parsley, oregano, thyme, half a dozen garlic cloves, and a bit of sweet pepper.
4. Fry all of this well, and before the onion takes on color, fry and let it cook for four hours.
5. After incorporating water, add the pork leg, ear, and snout, all well divided into small pieces and properly deboned.
6. Add one pound of good ham and complete the seasoning.
7. When everything boils vigorously, add the rice, which will cook halfway over high heat.
8. Then remove from heat; cover the surface of the pot with very thin slices of fresh bacon.
9. Cover the vessel with an iron lid and place hot coals on top.
10. When the bacon becomes crispy, remove the lid, let the dish rest, and serve directly from the pot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Arroz á la Zamorana (Zamora Rice)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of excellent lard
- one pound of onions
- half pound of turnips
- parsley
- oregano
- thyme
- half dozen garlic cloves
- sweet pepper
- water
- pork paw
- pork ear
- pork snout
- one pound of good ham

## Directions

1. Melt four ounces of excellent lard in a clay pot or bowl
2. Stew in it one pound of onions and half pound of turnips
3. Add parsley, oregano, thyme, half a dozen garlic cloves and a little sweet pepper
4. Fry and cook everything for four hours before the onion takes color
5. After incorporating water, add pork paw, ear and snout, all cut into small pieces and properly deboned
6. Add one pound of good ham and complete the seasoning
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Arroz á lo Cafre (Kaffir-Style Rice)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one watermelon
- pre-fried oil
- small pieces of zucchini
- chicken, fish, or meat
- peppers
- tomatoes
- water
- rice

## Directions

1. Choose a watermelon according to the number of diners.
2. Cut off the top as a lid and carefully hollow it out, removing the flesh in presentable pieces to serve as an appetizer at the beginning of the meal.
3. Continue cleaning the inside of all its flesh until only the rind remains, with the interior well smoothed, resulting in a cavity shaped like a bowl.
4. Inside, place pre-fried oil, small pieces of zucchini, chicken, fish or meat that have been previously seasoned.
5. Season well; add very finely cut peppers and tomatoes.
6. Fill the watermelon with water to two or three fingers below its edges.
7. Add rice in an amount equal to one third of the volume of what is contained in it.
8. Cover with the lid placed upside down, and with a piece of strong canvas, tie and secure the watermelon well so that the lid does not shift.
9. Dig a hole in the ground, bury the watermelon, and cover it with some sand.
10. Then spread quicklime on top to form a mound, and extinguish it with water.
11. After three quarters of an hour, remove the watermelon and serve this perfectly cooked and flavorful rice, made without fire and without a vessel.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Artaletes de Cordero (Lamb Patties)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- legs of roasted lamb
- fat bacon
- beef marrow
- half a dozen egg yolks
- some vegetables
- spearmint
- marjoram
- all spices
- salt
- four hard-boiled egg yolks
- lemon juice
- thin caul fat from mutton
- pork lard

## Directions

1. Cook the legs of roasted lamb
2. When cooked, chop very finely and mix with fat bacon and beef marrow
3. Add half a dozen egg yolks, some vegetables, spearmint and marjoram, seasoning with all spices and plenty of salt
4. Crush four hard-boiled egg yolks with a little lemon juice and add to the mixture
5. Take thin caul fat from mutton and form the artaletes (patties) with it
6. Place them in a well-greased baking dish with pork lard
7. Cook on low heat with heat applied to the lid
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Artichokes in White Sauce (Alcachofas en Salsa Blanca)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes
- White sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the artichokes whole
2. Place them on a serving dish and keep them hot
3. Serve with white sauce in a separate sauce boat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Aspic Muro (Savory Meat Aspic)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Concentrated beef broth made from beef shank, veal knuckles, chicken, and bacon
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Chicken breast cutlets
- Langoustines
- Anchovy fillets
- Shredded salt cod
- Raw oysters
- Parsley
- Shallots
- Gelatin from the broth

## Directions

1. In a bowl, cup, or mold, place slices of hard-boiled egg at the bottom
2. On top, add a layer of cut chicken breast pieces, langoustines, anchovy fillets, shredded salt cod, raw oysters, or any similar items
3. Sprinkle with a fine mixture of chopped parsley and shallots
4. Add another layer of hard-boiled egg slices
5. Add another layer of one of the aforementioned foods
6. Add another layer of parsley and shallots, and continue thus successively until the mold or cup is completely filled and level with the edges
7. Pour the concentrated, well-degreased and clarified broth carefully into the bowl
8. Place the aspic in a very cool, airy location to set
9. To serve, loosen with a spatula the edges of the dish that adhere to the bowl
10. Submerge the bowl, dipping it in and out a couple of times, in boiling water
11. Turn it over onto a plate on which you have first placed a well-ironed napkin
12. When desired, give the broth a blood color by adding a concentrated infusion of finely chopped roasted beet without losing its transparency
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Atún a la Elegante (Tuna in an Elegant Way)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- A wheel of pickled tuna escabeche
- Batter for frying
- Good oil

## Directions

1. From a wheel of pickled tuna escabeche, cut very thin slices on the bias.
2. Soak them in a frying batter and fry them in good oil.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Atún a la Pelotari (Tuna Pelotari Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh tuna slices, two fingers thick
- Refined oil
- Two cloves of garlic, cut into slivers
- Some onions, sliced into rings
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Clove spice
- Salt and pepper
- A good handful of parsley in sprigs
- Beef lard
- White wine from Valdepeñas
- Shallots
- Mushrooms
- Chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare slices of fresh tuna two fingers thick and soak them for four hours in refined oil with two cloves of garlic cut into slivers, some onions sliced into rings, thyme, bay leaf, clove spice, and a good seasoning of salt and pepper, with a good handful of parsley in sprigs.
2. Turn the tuna continuously in the marinade so that it soaks evenly and remove the slices, drain them, and dry both sides of each slice well with a fine white linen cloth.
3. Grease them with beef lard and roast them over a gentle fire on the grill.
4. Separately, prepare a brown-white sauce, well seasoned with salt and pepper, which is thinned with white wine from Valdepeñas, the stronger the better.
5. Chop some shallots and mushrooms and mix them with chopped parsley; incorporate this into the pan and cook until the sauce reduces by half, to moisten with it at the moment of serving the roasted tuna slices, placed on a platter as they come from the grill.
6. Serve this dish very hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Atún Asado al Natural (Tuna Roasted in Its Natural State)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh tuna pieces
- Oil
- Pepper
- Remoulade or tartar sauce

## Directions

1. Brush the pieces of fresh tuna with oil using a kitchen brush and sprinkle with pepper.
2. Roast them over a gentle fire on grills until both sides of each piece crackle.
3. Serve with a remoulade or tartar sauce.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao a la Carmen Sánchez (Cod Carmen Sánchez Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Best Scottish cod
- Fine oil, fried
- Toasted bread crumbs
- Parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Soak the finest Scottish cod available.
2. After six hours, clean of skin and bones and separate into leaves.
3. In thin strips of fish, grease with fine fried oil the entire interior of a terrine or silver serving dish, sprinkling it with toasted bread crumbs, and place the pieces in layers to cover the surface with one row.
4. Cover this lightly with oil and bread crumbs mixed with a little finely chopped parsley.
5. Place another row of cod and more oil and bread crumbs and parsley, and so on successively, until this kind of cake rises two or three inches according to the number of diners.
6. Cover the surface of the pastry well with bread crumbs alone, soaked slightly in oil, and place in the oven at medium heat.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao a la Criolla o Camagüeyano (Creole or Camagüey Style Cod)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Prepared cod
- Six peppercorns
- Parsley
- Six garlic cloves
- Eight toasted hazelnuts
- Bread crumbs
- Vinegar
- Grated nutmeg
- Cold water
- Fried oil
- Garlic
- Lemon juice
- Salt

## Directions

1. After preparing as indicated, dry and cook the cod.
2. When cooked, drain well and place in a casserole.
3. Crush in a mortar six peppercorns, a little parsley, six garlic cloves, eight toasted hazelnuts, bread crumbs, a little vinegar, and grated nutmeg.
4. Dissolve everything with a little cold water and pour into the casserole with the cod.
5. Add fried oil with garlic, lemon juice, and necessary salt.
6. Place on the fire to cook a little and can be served.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao a la Lionesa (Lyonnaise Cod)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece of cod
- onion
- carrot
- 3 or 4 small onions
- butter
- lemon juice
- nutmeg
- pepper

## Directions

1. Soak a piece of cod in plenty of cold water for about thirty hours before using it, changing the water two or three times
2. After this time, clean it well of its scales, wash and drain it, placing it to cook in three times its volume of water with onion and carrot
3. The moment it begins to cook, remove the pot or saucepan, covering it with a lid
4. Shortly after, drain it using a cloth or canvas
5. Remove bones and skin, doing this as little as possible, removing it in flakes which will be placed in a pot
6. Place this over hot ashes or mild coals, covered with its lid and little fire on top, with the only object being to dry it of the humidity it may have
7. Meanwhile, cut 3 or 4 small onions into small cubes, which will be fried in butter until they brown
8. Add the cod inside, sauté everything together a little and season with lemon juice, nutmeg and pepper
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao a la Marinera (Cod Sailor Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked cod
- Cod broth
- Cooked egg yolk
- Garlic
- Pepper
- Raw oil
- Vinegar
- Small cooked onions

## Directions

1. After cooking the cod, place it in a serving dish with a little of its own broth in which a cooked egg yolk, crushed garlic and a little pepper are dissolved.
2. Add raw oil and vinegar and some small onions cooked with the same cod.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao a la Milanesa (Milanese Cod)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- sauce made with broth
- tomato
- cebolletas (small onions)
- 2 tablespoons grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. After desalting, cooking and cleaning as described for the previous recipe, place it in flakes in a pot between two fires, in a roasting pan, with half a quartillo of sauce made with broth, tomato, a little chopped cebolletas and two good tablespoons of grated Parmesan cheese
2. Place the cod in this sauce, stirring well
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao al Estilo de Lima (Cod Lima Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod, prepared and desalted
- Large tomatoes
- Onions, chopped
- Garlic, chopped
- Sweet peppers, chopped
- Parsley
- Oil
- Spices
- Sweet paprika
- Salt

## Directions

1. After preparing and desalting the cod, take some large tomatoes, cut them in half and roast them on grills.
2. Remove the skin and place in a dish to release all their juice.
3. Chop very finely onions, garlic, sweet peppers and a little parsley.
4. Fry these in oil, and after a short time, add the tomatoes and fry everything well.
5. Place the cod in a casserole and pour this mixture over it, adding spices, a little sweet paprika and salt.
6. Boil it for a while so it takes on the sauce, and once cooked, serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao al Estilo de Veracruz (Cod Veracruz Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod, cut into regular pieces
- Water
- Garlic cloves, peeled
- Bread crumbs
- Sweet paprika, ground
- Parsley, chopped
- Oil
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut the cod into regular pieces and place in a casserole with water for about eight hours.
2. When the salt has been released, place in a serving dish, drain the water well, remove the bones, and arrange in a frying pan, well spread out.
3. Add peeled garlic cloves cut in half, bread crumbs, a little sweet ground paprika, and chopped parsley.
4. Place another layer of cod, also spread out; add four ounces of oil, water and salt until it is covered.
5. Cover well and cook over low heat.
6. When no broth remains, it can be served.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao al Gratín (Gratinéed Salt Cod)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- desalted and cooked salt cod without skin or bones
- milk
- ham cut very fine
- onion
- carrot
- bay leaf
- thyme
- parsley
- lard or butter
- three ounces of flour
- nutmeg
- Parmesan cheese, grated
- butter

## Directions

1. Desalted and cooked, clean, and without skin or bones, separate into sheets and incorporate into a sauce prepared as follows: cook milk, stirring it without ceasing with a wooden spatula, until by evaporation it reduces to a little, less than half
2. Meanwhile, sauté in a casserole ham cut very fine, onion, carrot, a bundle of bay leaf, thyme, and parsley, with pork or cow butter; put in about three ounces of flour, and well mixed, season with a little nutmeg
3. Let everything boil for about half an hour, without ceasing to stir, then pass it through a sieve
4. In this sauce, thus prepared, put the cod and give it a few boils
5. Then place it in a serving dish that resists fire; sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese and sprinkle with cow butter; cover with the hood oven or put the dish in the kitchen oven, well heated, until the dish takes color, and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao con Ajo de Arriero (Cod with Arriero Garlic Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked cod
- Oil
- Garlic
- Sweet or hot paprika
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Once cooked and drained, place the cod on plates.
2. Fry oil and garlic, add sweet or hot paprika, and as much vinegar as oil has been used.
3. Add this sauce to the cod when serving.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao con Miel ó Azúcar (Salt Cod with Honey or Sugar)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod
- honey or sugar
- flour
- egg

## Directions

1. Cook the salt cod and drain it; coat it with honey, dip it in flour and fry it
2. Others dip it in egg, then add flour and then sugar on top
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao con Papas al Estilo de Canarias (Cod with Potatoes Canary Islands Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod
- Oil
- Peeled potatoes, cut
- Eight garlic cloves
- Parsley
- Pepper
- Saffron threads
- Bread crumb, soaked
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Clean and desalt the cod; fry it in oil until it browns on both sides, then remove it.
2. In the same oil, fry the peeled and cut potatoes and eight garlic cloves.
3. Once fried, remove them from the pan, then place the cod back with the potatoes and a pitcher of water, letting it cook.
4. When everything is cooked, crush the fried garlic, some parsley, pepper, a few saffron threads and a soaked bread crumb.
5. Dissolve all this in the cod's broth and add it to the stew.
6. Season, bring to a boil once more, and serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao con patatas (Cod with Potatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod
- Flour
- Potatoes
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Pepper
- Bread crumb

## Directions

1. After soaked and wrapped in flour, fry it
2. When golden, add water and peeled potatoes
3. Cook, and when it has consumed most of the water, stew with fried garlic, parsley, pepper and a bread crumb
4. Bring to a boil
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao con Patatas (Cod with Potatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Soaked cod
- Flour
- Water
- Peeled potatoes
- Garlic, fried
- Parsley
- Pepper
- Bread crumb

## Directions

1. After soaking the cod and coating it in flour, fry it.
2. When golden, add water and peeled potatoes and let cook.
3. When most of the water has been consumed, stew with fried garlic, parsley, pepper and a bread crumb, bringing it to a boil.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao con Queso (Cod with Cheese)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod
- Béchamel sauce
- Grated Manchego cheese

## Directions

1. Cook the cod in béchamel sauce and thicken the remaining broth with grated Manchego cheese.
2. Place in a bell-shaped oven until it takes color.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao con tomate (Cod with Tomato)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod
- Onion
- Tomatoes
- Parsley
- Garlic
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Cut into pieces and soak in water to desalt, changing the water from time to time
2. Cook and then place on a board to drain
3. Meanwhile fry onion and tomatoes in abundance
4. Arrange the cod pieces in a suitable pot, putting a layer of tomato and onion on top of the pieces
5. Add a little parsley and some crushed garlic
6. When the pot is full, add water until everything is covered
7. Bring to a boil a couple of times and season with salt if necessary
8. Serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao en Agujas Fritas (Fried Salt Cod Needles)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- desalted and cleaned salt cod
- pepper
- nutmeg
- onions in slices
- parsley sprigs
- oil
- vinegar
- bay leaf
- tarragon
- flour
- beaten egg
- breadcrumbs
- oil for frying
- fried parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. From a good desalted and cleaned salt cod, cut strips or tiras the length of a finger, but without removing the skin
2. When all are cut, place them in adobo in a casserole with pepper, nutmeg, onions in slices, parsley sprigs, oil, vinegar, bay leaf, and tarragon, stirring from time to time
3. At the moment of serving this cod, dry it, pass it through flour, then through beaten egg and breadcrumbs, frying it well in good oil and placing it after draining on the plate with a garnish of fried parsley
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao en Albondiguillas (Cod Meatballs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked cod, minced
- Bread crumb
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Garlic
- Beaten eggs
- Fish stock
- Oil
- Bread crust
- Roasted garlic
- Saffron

## Directions

1. Make a mixture of minced cooked cod, bread crumb, salt, pepper, parsley and garlic all ground together, and beaten eggs in proportion to achieve a regular consistency.
2. Once well mixed, form balls, roll in bread crumb and fry.
3. Cook the meatballs in fish stock and then stew them in fried oil with garlic and a bread crust.
4. Crush the bread crust with roasted garlic, parsley, pepper and a bit of saffron, and mix this with the meatballs.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao en Bolas (Codfish Balls)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover cooked or fried cod
- Two tablespoons of oil
- Two tablespoons of flour
- Half a quarter-liter of broth, milk, or water
- Salt
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Remove skin and bones from the leftover cod and chop it very fine.
2. In a saucepan, heat two tablespoons of oil until it is hot.
3. Add two tablespoons of flour.
4. Stir well, and before the flour takes color, add half a quarter-liter of broth, milk, or water.
5. Season and add chopped parsley.
6. Add the chopped cod.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao en Bolas (Salt Cod Balls)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover cooked or fried salt cod
- two tablespoons of oil
- two tablespoons of flour
- half a quartillo of broth, milk, or water
- salt and seasoning
- parsley
- one egg white

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and bones from the leftover salt cod and chop it very finely.
2. Heat two tablespoons of oil in a saucepan until golden, then add two tablespoons of flour.
3. Stir well, and before the flour takes color, add half a quartillo of broth, milk, or water.
4. Season and add chopped parsley.
5. Add the finely chopped salt cod to the saucepan and remove from heat.
6. Knead the mixture and incorporate a beaten egg white whipped into meringue.
7. Let cool and form into small balls or croquettes.
8. Bread them well and fry in oil at the moment of serving.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao en Cazuela (Salt Cod in Casserole)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod cut into pieces
- breadcrumbs
- garlic
- parsley
- raw oil
- pepper
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the cod into pieces and put it in a casserole or cooking pot, so that it covers the bottom well
2. On top of it put a thick layer of breadcrumbs, garlic and parsley in abundance, then another layer of cod, and so successively until the casserole is full
3. Pour raw oil, garlic, pepper and water on top, which covers everything
4. Cover the casserole and put it on low heat, until it is almost dry, and thus it is served
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao en salsa ligada (Cod in Bound Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod
- Milk
- Water
- Bound sauce
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Soak in equal parts water and milk
2. Scald to remove skin more easily
3. After well prepared, cook in cold water
4. Turn when it begins to boil and remove quickly
5. Drain well and place on serving platter
6. Cover with a bound sauce with a few drops of lemon
7. Serve immediately
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao Encebollado (Salt Cod with Onions)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod soaked at least twenty-four hours
- onion cut into thick slices or rings
- tomato
- garlic cloves
- pepper
- cinnamon
- oil
- cloves
- pepper
- sweet pimiento

## Directions

1. After having soaked in water for at least twenty-four hours, divide into pieces and arrange as follows: place in a casserole a good layer of onion cut into thick slices or rings, tomato, some garlic cloves well divided, and a little pepper and cinnamon
2. On top of this place another layer of cod pieces, on top of that another layer of onion, tomato, and garlic, then another layer of cod, and so continue until the casserole is full
3. Pour oil abundantly over everything, cloves, pepper, and sweet pimiento, and put on low heat, without adding broth until the juice of the onion and tomato has been consumed
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao Guisado (Stewed Salt Cod)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod pieces
- flour
- parsley
- water
- fried onion
- spices
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook some pieces of salt cod, wrap them in flour, fry them and place them in a casserole, and over them parsley and water that covers them, fried onion, spices and lemon juice
2. Cook thus until the greater part of the sauce is consumed
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao Guisado al Estilo de Huelva (Stewed Salt Cod Huelva Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- salt cod pieces, prepared and cooked
- onion
- garlic
- pepper
- saffron

## Directions

1. Prepared and cooked pieces of salt cod, chop onion abundantly, fry it, crush garlic, pepper, and saffron, mix with the onion and add to the serving dish when serving the cod
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao por lo Fino (Cod Prepared Finely)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod soaked for twenty-four hours
- Plenty of boiling water
- Fresh butter from cows
- Steamed potatoes

## Directions

1. Soak the cod for twenty-four hours
2. Clean it of skin and bones and shred it into long strips
3. Submerge the strips in plenty of boiling water and boil for five minutes
4. Cover the pot and leave in the water for half an hour, maintaining it without boiling at high temperature
5. Serve the drained strips between the folds of a napkin on a hot dish
6. Eat with fresh butter from cows, spreading it on the fish on the same plate, accompanied by steamed potatoes as bread
7. Alternatively, serve with oil and salt, or with any sauce served separately, best with a well-bound mayonnaise
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao rebocado (Breaded Cod)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod
- Egg
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. After it has cooked, break into small pieces
2. Dip in egg
3. Then roll in bread crumbs
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao Rebozado (Breaded Cod)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked cod
- Egg
- Bread crumbs
- Oil
- Sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Once cooked, cut the cod into small pieces.
2. Coat them in beaten egg, then roll in bread crumbs.
3. Moisten again with egg and coat a second time in bread crumbs.
4. Fry in oil.
5. If desired, sprinkle sugar on top before serving.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao á la Bechamela (Salt Cod with Béchamel Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- desalted and cooked salt cod without bones and skin
- béchamel sauce

## Directions

1. Once desalted and cooked, and cleaned of its bones and skin, separate into sheets and incorporate into said sauce, and in this manner it will be served
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao á la francesa (Cod French Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod
- Shallots or small onions
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Onion
- Lemon
- Black pepper
- Oil
- Butter
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. After prepared as indicated and cooked, take the platter or pan for serving
2. Place in it shallots or small onions, crushed garlic, parsley, onion, lemon sliced without skin or seeds, peppercorns, two tablespoons of oil and a piece of butter the size of an egg
3. Place the cod on top, cover with a layer of the same mixture and with bread crumbs
4. Place the platter over gentle heat so it simmers little by little
5. Add lemon juice, a little pepper, and brown in a covered oven
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao á la holandesa (Cod Dutch Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod
- Dutch sauce

## Directions

1. Desalt and cook as the previous recipe
2. When prepared, arrange and serve with Dutch sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao á la Provenzal - Brandado (Salt Cod Provençal Style - Brandade)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- about two pounds of well-desalted salt cod from the thickest part
- cold water
- fine oil
- onion
- bay leaf
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take about two pounds of well-desalted salt cod, but making sure it is from the thickest part; cut it into square pieces and put it in a sufficient quantity of cold water so that it cooks on low heat, and when it is about to come to a boil, set it aside and cover it with its lid, leaving it in this condition for about ten minutes
2. After which drain it through a sieve, and then through cloth or canvas so that no moisture remains; carefully remove the bones, leaving the skins
3. Fry in a casserole in very fine oil a chopped onion with a bay leaf, then putting the cod inside and stirring it strongly with the spatula until it becomes a paste
4. During this work put in some tablespoons of very fine oil, letting it fall very slowly, and at the same time some drops of lemon juice
5. By force of work and with the help of the oil and lemon, the cod acquires a whiteness and surprising elasticity, thus transforming itself into a healthy and appetizing food
6. One must be careful not to drop too much oil at once, because it would be very easy for it to break apart, as happens with mayonnaise and garlic-oil or alioli; but this is easily avoided by adding it very little by little
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao á la Railanesa (Salt Cod Railanesa Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- desalted, cooked, and cleaned salt cod
- media cuartillo of sauce made with broth
- tomato
- chopped scallions
- two good tablespoons of grated Parmesan cheese
- melted butter

## Directions

1. After desalting, cooking, and cleaning as described previously, place the cod in sheets in a casserole between two fires, in a roasting pan, with half a quartillo of sauce made with broth, tomato, a little chopped scallions, and two good tablespoons of grated Parmesan cheese
2. Place the cod in this sauce, stirring well so that it binds together
3. Then empty it into a serving dish that can go to the fire and place on top a layer of the same cheese, watering it well with melted butter
4. Finally, cook it in the oven at medium heat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bacalao á la vizcaína (Cod Biscay Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod
- Tomatoes
- Onion
- Oil
- Pork lard
- Toasted bread crust
- Broth
- Choricero peppers
- Flour
- Salt

## Directions

1. After soaked and cut into pieces, give it a boil while roasting a few tomatoes on the grill
2. Remove the skin from the tomatoes and mash well with a wooden spoon on a plate
3. Chop much onion finely and put in oil to sauté
4. When still without taking color, add the tomato and finish sautéing
5. Place the cod in a pot with method and order, pour the tomato, onion and oil in which they were fried on top
6. Leave on low heat until the cod is well cooked
7. Take care to stir the pot frequently so the cod takes the sauce, and always do it on low heat
8. For the sauce, use half oil and half pork lard with sufficient chopped onion, and fry carefully; when the onion is golden, add a toasted bread crust and a little broth
9. Have about six choricero peppers cooked, with veins and dust removed beforehand; their flesh grated with a knife will be added to the fried onion
10. Add a tablespoon of flour and strain everything well through a sieve so that it remains a fairly thick sauce
11. Introduce the cod without large bones into it, and bringing it to a gentle boil, serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Batallón (Lodging House Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of meat cut into small pieces like hazelnuts
- half pound of chopped onions
- one head of garlic
- two or three tablespoons of oil
- paprika
- salt
- four pounds of potatoes cut into slices
- water

## Directions

1. In an earthenware pot in the evening, taking advantage of the day's fire, reheat or recook one pound of meat cut into small pieces, half pound of chopped onions, one head of garlic, and two or three tablespoons of oil, reinforced with a little paprika and corresponding salt
2. Let this cook during the night, burning well
3. One hour before the next day's lunch, pour the contents of the pot into a pot, fill with water, add four pounds of potatoes cut into slices, and serve at the table for twelve people to eat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Becacines en Salmorejo (Woodcock in Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two woodcock
- four lemons, well-fleshy
- zest of one lemon, grated to powder
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- two teaspoons of good French mustard
- half a glass of white wine
- oil

## Directions

1. Roast the two woodcock on the spit at medium doneness after cleaning them very well on the outside, since it is known that inside, like shells and snails, this animal is not emptied.
2. When the woodcock are half-roasted, divide them according to all the good rules of the art of carving, and then cut the wings, legs, stomach, and tail into two pieces, placing the pieces on a platter.
3. On the silver plate (or moirez or alfenide) in which the dissection has been made, crush and squash the livers, intestines, and all the giblets of the birds, squeezing the juice of four well-fleshy lemons and the grated zest of a single lemon over them.
4. Place the cuts of the woodcock on top, seasoning them with salt, pepper, nutmeg, and two teaspoons of good French mustard, adding half a glass of white wine.
5. Place the plate on a spirit lamp (called infierno), and stir constantly with a silver fork so that during this last point of cooking the sauce coats well and the pieces do not stick.
6. Take great care that everything cooks inside without boiling, and when you see that this moment is about to arrive, add a few drops of oil and remove the plate from the flame of the spirit lamp, and begin again until the operation is complete.
7. Eat this salmorejo very hot, with a fork and not with the hand, as with all birds, for the risk of devouring your fingers while sucking the sauce.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Becacín de Repente (Quick Snipe)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen snipe
- fresh butter
- six chopped shallots
- bay leaf
- salt
- pepper
- powdered nutmeg
- one glass of good sherry
- juice of two lemons
- one tablespoon of toasted and grated bread

## Directions

1. In ten minutes sauté half a dozen snipe over high heat in a saucepan with fresh butter, six chopped shallots, a bay leaf, salt, pepper, and a pinch of nutmeg
2. Remove the birds from the saucepan
3. Deglaze the juices with a glass of good sherry
4. Add the juice of two lemons and one tablespoon of toasted and grated bread
5. Stir vigorously with a wooden spatula
6. Return the snipe to the saucepan to give them a couple of boils
7. Serve in a dish very hot with the sauce over the top
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Besugo al Horno (Roasted Sea Bream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sea bream
- Two tablespoons of fried oil
- Water
- Breadcrumbs
- Shallots
- One clove of garlic
- Parsley
- Two tablespoons of fried oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- A pinch of clove spice
- One small glass of white wine
- Orange slices

## Directions

1. In an oval baking dish, place the sea bream well positioned
2. Pour two tablespoons of fried oil and place the sea bream on top, adding water to bathe it at the bottom
3. In a bowl, make a mixture with breadcrumbs, shallots, one clove of garlic and finely chopped parsley; add two tablespoons of fried oil, salt, pepper and a pinch of clove spice, and thin the paste with a small glass of white wine
4. Place the fish in the oven at high heat
5. Leave it for five minutes and remove to coat it well with the paste, which should be somewhat crumbly
6. Make three or four transverse cuts to insert half-slices of orange, and place it back in the oven
7. Serve after twenty minutes
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Besugo al Horno (Sea Bream in the Oven)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- sea bream
- oil
- water
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. In an oval serving dish, in which the sea bream is well placed, pour a couple of spoonfuls of already fried oil, and on top place the sea bream, adding water to bathe it at the base
2. In a bowl make a mixture with bread crumbs
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Besugo Asado (Roasted Sea Bream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- sea bream
- salt
- garlic
- oil
- vinegar
- broth
- lemon or bitter orange juice

## Directions

1. After scaling and cleaning well, dry with a cloth and add a little salt
2. Roast on the grill over low heat
3. When roasted, and at the time of serving, pour fried garlic in oil and a little vinegar over it
4. Also add, if desired, a little broth from the pot with a bit of lemon or bitter orange juice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Besugo Cocido (Boiled Sea Bream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- sea bream
- water
- oil
- garlic
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Put sufficient water in a bream pan, and when it is boiling place the sea bream
2. After one boiling turn it over, and having cooked sufficiently, reduce the broth
3. Fry oil with some garlic and pour it over the fish
4. At the time of serving add vinegar
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Besugo con Cebolla (Sea Bream with Onion)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- sea bream
- onion
- bread
- pepper
- broth

## Directions

1. After cleaning, put whole or cut into slices, to fry in a casserole with chopped onion
2. When the onion is already fried, toast a little bread, crush it with a grain of pepper, dissolve it in broth from the pot and pour it over the sea bream
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Besugo Frito (Fried Sea Bream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- sea bream
- flour

## Directions

1. Remove the bones, clean, and cut into not thick slices
2. Fry this way floured or coated with a paste
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Besugo á la Donostiarra (Sea Bream San Sebastian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sea bream
- Salt
- Raw oil
- Capon feather
- Very hot oil with garlic
- Lemon drops

## Directions

1. Clean the sea bream very carefully, sprinkle it with salt and leave it hanging in a fresh and suitable place
2. One hour before presenting it to the table, place the fish on grills under which live oak charcoal fire burns
3. Take the feather of a capon, wet it well in raw oil and gently coat the sea bream with it, turning it several times until the skin is well toasted
4. When the moment comes to serve it, open the fish and sprinkle it with very hot oil with garlic and a few drops of lemon
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Biftec a la Inglesa (English-Style Beefsteak)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sirloin, cleaned of fat, skin, and sinew
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the sirloin into slices of half an inch thick
2. Flatten them slightly
3. Cook or roast on a cast iron griddle placed over the heat
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Each whole sirloin can yield about twelve regular beefsteaks
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Biftec con Manteca de Anchoas (Beefsteak with Anchovy Butter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Beefsteak
- Anchovy butter
- Potatoes
- Butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the beefsteak as described in the English-style recipe
2. In the center of the platter of beefsteaks, place anchovy butter instead of cow butter prepared in the butler's style, using the anchovy butter formula already given in the sauces section
3. Serve as an essential garnish sautéed potatoes that are browned in cow butter
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Biftec con Manteca de Anchoas (Beefsteak with Anchovy Butter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one beefsteak prepared as the first English-style
- anchovy butter
- potatoes
- beef butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the beefsteak as the first English-style beefsteak, and instead of placing in the center of the platter of beefsteaks the beef butter seasoned à la mayordoma, place the anchovy butter whose formula has already been given in the sauces section.
2. The beefsteak thus seasoned must have as an essential garnish potatoes sautéed and browned in beef butter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Biftec Estofado à la Inglesa (Braised English-Style Beefsteak)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beefsteaks, cut thicker than those prepared for the grill
- beef butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- sufficient quantity of broth to bathe the beefsteaks
- a bouquet of fine herbs
- one onion
- one carrot
- half a turnip
- salt
- pepper
- three tablespoons of rice flour
- a pinch of Cayenne pepper
- four or five shallots

## Directions

1. Brown in beef butter by turning them, some beefsteaks cut thicker than those prepared for the grill.
2. Remove them from the pot, and in the butter that remains add one tablespoon of flour and stir quickly to make a golden roux that is moistened with a sufficient quantity of broth to bathe the beefsteaks, which are incorporated into the sauce immediately.
3. As soon as the sauce begins to cook, add a bouquet of fine herbs, an onion, a carrot, and half a turnip, all of it in very thin slices; season with salt and pepper, and let cook over very low heat with the pot well covered for three hours.
4. Half an hour before serving, add to the sauce three tablespoons of rice flour with a pinch of Cayenne pepper, and four or five shallots placed and sautéed separately in beef butter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Biftec Frito (Fried Beefsteak)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Beefsteaks, thinner and smaller than for grilling
- Salt and pepper
- Flour
- Pork lard
- Blonde sauce enlarged with broth
- Mushrooms
- Cow butter

## Directions

1. Cut the beefsteaks thinner and smaller than for grilling
2. Season them with salt and pepper on each side, flour them, and fry them in pork lard for about two and a half minutes
3. Turn them constantly so they cook evenly, and when removing them from the pan, drain them very well so they release the fat they contain
4. Prepare a blonde sauce enlarged with broth and incorporate mushrooms that have been previously sautéed in cow butter without allowing it to brown
5. Combine everything and let it cook for half an hour, seasoning the dish when serving according to the taste of the diner
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Biftec à l'Anglaise (English-Style Beefsteak)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole sirloin, cleaned of fat, skin, and tendons
- 125 grams of beef butter for six regular-sized beefsteaks
- salt
- pepper
- parsley, finely chopped
- a few drops of lemon juice
- fresh butter, very fresh

## Directions

1. From a whole sirloin cleaned of fat, skin, and small tendons, cut twelve regular beefsteaks, neither very thick nor very thin, cutting the meat against the grain with a long, sharp knife blade.
2. Profile each beefsteak in a round shape by trimming the edges, and then on the cutting board, beat both sides with a heavy, long boxwood mallet.
3. Sprinkle salt on both faces of the beefsteak.
4. Melt 125 grams of beef butter over low heat in a saucepan for six regular-sized beefsteaks, and while the butter is liquid but not very hot, dip the beefsteaks in it on both sides using a fork.
5. Then place all of them together on the grill over coals that should not be too lively nor half-extinguished.
6. When the beefsteaks have been well seized by the fire and have taken on color without being burned, making the cooking give firmness to their meat, then remove them from the fire.
7. Once the beefsteaks are done, place them on a round platter in the form of a crown, and in the center place, for six beefsteaks, 125 grams of very fresh butter, kneaded with salt, pepper, and very finely chopped parsley, seasoned with a few drops of lemon juice.
8. The beefsteaks are served with the maitre d'hôtel sauce as described.
9. The platter in which the dish is served must be very hot beforehand, submerged in boiling water, so that the beef butter melts and remains fluid with the heat of the meat.
10. The beefsteaks may be served English-style with fried potatoes covering the plate literally, or with potatoes cooked in steam and cut into slices, which placed in the center will take on the seasoning of the beefsteak.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Biftec à la Rusa (Russian-Style Beefsteak)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one slice of sirloin four centimeters thick and the full length
- salt
- pepper
- filling of meat or poultry
- kitchen twine
- white paper
- fresh butter

## Directions

1. Cut from a sirloin a slice four centimeters thick and of the full length.
2. Extend it on the cutting board and beat both sides, sprinkling it with salt and pepper.
3. Prepare a filling of meat or poultry, and mold it into a roll or sausage of one inch thick and shorter than the width of the meat blade.
4. Wrap the filling in the meat, rolling it well, and tie it with kitchen twine so that the filling does not escape.
5. Thus prepared, the beefsteak is roasted on the spit after having wrapped it in white paper greased with fresh butter.
6. According to the number and according to the appetite that the diners are presumed to have, prepare one or more beefsteaks, which will be roasted at the same time, skewered together on the roasting spit.
7. They must cook before good fire for at least an hour and a half, and twenty minutes before serving remove the paper and baste continuously with its own juices.
8. Serve these beefsteaks with Spanish sauce apart, or with any other, and if you do not want to spend much, prepare a spicy sauce or take advantage of the juice from the meat of the roast from the previous day.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bizcocho de Saboya (Savoy Sponge Cake)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- five eggs
- two ounces of starch, well sifted
- four ounces of ground white sugar
- fried oil for greasing the mold

## Directions

1. Beat five eggs and mix with two ounces of starch, well sifted, and four ounces of ground white sugar
2. Beat again
3. Grease the mold with fried oil
4. Pour in the batter
5. Cover well and place over gentle heat with heat on the lid
6. Test for doneness with a straw or knitting needle; when it comes out clean, it is cooked
7. It cooks much better in the oven
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bizcochos a la Andalusa (Andalusian Sponge Cakes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two egg whites
- two ounces of pulverized flour
- one and a half ounces of starch in powder

## Directions

1. Beat two egg whites until white as snow
2. Set aside the yolks
3. To the yolks add two ounces of pulverized flour and one and a half ounces of starch in powder
4. Mix everything after beating very well
5. Place in a paper box and put in the oven
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bizcochos Borrachos de Guadalajara (Drunk Cakes from Guadalajara)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve egg yolks
- nine ounces of white sugar
- one pound of superior flour
- twelve egg whites
- one and a half pounds of sugar for clarified syrup
- one quarter liter of Málaga wine
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat in a pot twelve egg yolks with nine ounces of white sugar
2. When well beaten, add one pound of superior flour
3. Add twelve egg whites beaten as for meringue
4. Transfer this mixture to a box made beforehand with a sheet of paper
5. Cut into squares
6. Dip the squares in a clarified sugar syrup made with one and a half pounds of sugar brought to the point of caramel, into which one quarter liter of Málaga wine is mixed
7. Upon removing from the bath, sprinkle with ground cinnamon and let them air dry on esparto mats
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bizcochos Secos (Dry Sponge Cakes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve eggs
- one pound of sugar
- flour
- wafers

## Directions

1. Beat twelve eggs with one pound of sugar
2. When the batter is white and thick, add flour that is well incorporated
3. Place wafers on the bottoms of the cake pans
4. Pour batter into the pans to a thickness of about one inch
5. Cover the pans and apply gentle heat above and below
6. When well set, remove the pans and take out one cake from each
7. Cut these into very thin slices
8. Place them in the oven on pastries to biscotti until they are lightly toasted
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bizcochos sin Harina (Sponge Cakes Without Flour)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of ground sugar
- eight egg yolks
- a little anise
- fried oil or melted butter

## Directions

1. To half a pound of ground sugar, add eight egg yolks and a little anise
2. Beat until very white
3. Form the cakes
4. Cut into cubes and let them air dry
5. When the mass is a bit dry, moisten or sprinkle with fried oil or melted butter
6. Place in the oven until completely cooked
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Boeuf à la Mode (French-Style Beef Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef from the rump
- bacon strips
- carrots
- onions
- cloves of spice
- parsley
- scallions
- bay leaves
- thyme branch
- salt
- pepper
- veal knuckle
- water

## Directions

1. Choose one of the best pieces of rump, lard it with strips of bacon, and put it to simmer slowly for about four hours with carrots, onions, cloves of spice, parsley and scallions, two bay leaves, a branch of thyme, salt, pepper, a veal knuckle and four glasses of water
2. When sufficiently cooked, serve it surrounded by these vegetables
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bogas Asadas (Roasted Bogas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- bogas (fish)
- butter
- rice flour
- fine herbs
- pickles
- olives
- white wine
- lemons

## Directions

1. Roast the fish on grills wrapped individually in paper, with the interior side spread with butter.
2. Serve with lemon halves on the platter for those who wish to squeeze the juice over them.
3. Prepare separately in a sauce boat a white sauce made with rice flour, butter, mixed and well-blended fine herbs, chopped pickles and olives, and sufficient white wine to make the sauce quite thick.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bogas en Papel (Roasted Fish in Paper)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- bogas (fish)
- butter
- flour
- fresh butter
- fine herbs
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Roast the bogas on grills, wrapped in paper spread with butter.
2. Make a white sauce with a handful of flour and fresh butter, adding fine herbs and the juice of a lemon.
3. Serve the bogas dry on a separate plate and the sauce in a sauce boat at the same time.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Bouillabaisse (Provençal Fish Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish of all types
- Bread
- Water
- Olive oil
- Salt and seasonings
- Flour (a small amount)

## Directions

1. In a cauldron with olive oil, fry fish of all types
2. Remove the fried fish and fry bread slices in the same oil
3. Prepare the broth by adding water to the oil in a proportion of ten parts water to one part oil and season
4. In home kitchens, use a frying pan instead of a cauldron and limit the variety of fish to two or three types at most, two white fish and one blue fish
5. Fry the bread and then strain the oil into a cooking pot
6. Add a small amount of flour just before seasoning and before adding the water
7. Clean the fried fish carefully, shred it, and combine with a well-prepared sauce
8. Form the mixture into a pyramid shape to serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Braised Leg of Mutton (Pierna de carnero braseada)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- strips of good bacon
- carrots
- onions
- fine herbs
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- good degreased broth

## Directions

1. Debone the meat and lard it with strips of good bacon, securing it then with kitchen twine so it does not deform
2. Place the meat in a braising pot with many strips of bacon, carrots, onions, fine herbs and an exaggerated seasoning of salt, pepper, clove and nutmeg
3. Pour good degreased broth over it in sufficient quantity to bathe the meat
4. Break the leg bone into three pieces
5. Cook over high heat until the boiling becomes vigorous, and from then on, over low heat, cover the pot with coals on top and leave until properly cooked, testing doneness by introducing a larding needle into the meat from time to time
6. The true way to prepare this dish consists in allowing all the broth to be consumed by the slow cooking, so that when serving the meat, the juice accompanying it in the serving dish is scarcely abundant
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Buñuelos (Spanish Fritters)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- flour
- sugar
- cinnamon
- frying fat (3 pounds)
- paper strips
- confectionery or similar toppings

## Directions

1. Crack one egg into a saucepan and stir with a spoon; when well mixed, add two more eggs, one after the other; mix well, and if the paste is too hard, add half an egg or a whole egg as needed.
2. To test the consistency of the paste, take a quantity the size of an egg on a spoon and let it drip. It should not spread nor remain stuck to the spoon, but should be pulled down by its own weight.
3. When the dough is finished, dust the table with flour, and place a quarter of the dough on it; dust this also with flour so it does not stick to the table or hands, and cut into pieces to form balls of two and a half centimeters, which are lined up on strips of paper four centimeters wide, which will have been lightly greased with frying fat.
4. Put three pounds of frying fat in a frying pan, which is tested by frying a small piece of bread that should barely quiver; place the pan beside the stove; dip the paper strip in the frying fat and remove when the fritters have separated; move with a skimmer and increase the heat of the frying fat by gradually moving the pan to the fire until it is completely over the stove.
5. When the fritters are golden and dry, drain them on a clean kitchen cloth, dust with sugar and arrange in a pyramid on a platter over a folded napkin.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Buñuelos de Manzana (Apple Fritters)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- four large, healthy apples
- brandy or cognac
- 30 grams of powdered sugar
- flour
