- egg whites
- oil for frying

## Directions

1. Select four large healthy apples that should yield two dozen fritters
2. Core the apples using an apple corer about two centimeters wide to remove the core and seeds
3. Peel the apples and cut them crosswise into slices about half a finger thick
4. Place the slices in a dish with brandy or cognac and 30 grams of powdered sugar, mixing carefully to avoid breaking the slices
5. Half an hour before serving, drain the slices on a clean kitchen cloth and dry them well, as this prevents the batter from adhering properly to the apple
6. Prepare a special batter made with flour, brandy or cognac, and water
7. Beat egg whites until they resemble meringue and mix with the batter
8. Take each apple slice with a spoon, wrap it in the batter being careful not to break it, and fry in very hot oil
9. When removed from oil, sprinkle with sugar
10. Whole apples may also be used, but they must be briefly boiled in water before soaking in brandy
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Buñuelos de Pronto (Quick Fritters)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two deciliters of water
- 50 grams of butter
- 15 grams of sugar
- 116 grams of flour
- three or more eggs

## Directions

1. In a two-liter saucepan, put two deciliters of water, 50 grams of butter, and 15 grams of sugar
2. Bring to a boil and remove from heat at the first boil
3. Add 116 grams of flour and mix well with a wooden spoon
4. Return to heat for four minutes, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon to prevent the dough from sticking
5. Remove from heat
6. Break one egg into the pan and stir with the spoon
7. When well mixed, add two more eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition
8. If the dough is too stiff, add half an egg or a whole egg as needed
9. To test the consistency, take a spoonful of dough; it should neither spread nor stick to the spoon, but rather fall by its own weight
10. When the dough is finished, sprinkle flour on the work surface
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cabeza de Jabalí (Wild Boar Head)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Head of a wild boar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg in powder
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Chives, finely chopped
- Tongue
- Brains
- Ears
- Loin of the same wild boar
- Pork lard, melted
- Toasted and sifted breadcrumbs
- Kitchen twine
- Truffle slices (optional)
- Pistachio slices (optional)

## Directions

1. Singe and clean the head of a wild boar with great care, split it lengthwise underneath and remove all the bones without damaging the skin.
2. Divide into very neat strips all the edible parts: tongue, meat, brains; add the ears, cut in the same manner, and if this seems insufficient to fill the deboned head, complete the filling with a little loin from the same wild boar.
3. Place all these pieces in a trough, in overlapping layers, sprinkling each layer with salt, pepper, and powdered nutmeg, finely chopped parsley and chives. Leave the dish in this brine for six days in summer and at least ten days in winter.
4. Fill the head with everything that was in the trough and reassemble it, sewing closed the split from where the bones and edible parts were removed.
5. Secure the head very well with kitchen twine, taking care not to deform it or correcting any slight imperfection, and thus well tied, cook it for ten hours in plenty of water and over low heat in a spacious pot.
6. Once the head is cooked, remove it from the vessel and wrap it in a napkin to squeeze and compress it well with your hands so that all the cooking water drains out.
7. Remove the napkin and twine, place it on a platter, and with a kitchen brush give it a coat of melted pork lard, and sprinkle it with toasted, sifted, and finely ground breadcrumbs so that the grain is uniform.
8. Present the head at the table cold and cut it into vertical slices, beginning with the fattest part and ending with the snout.
9. This preparation will turn out finer if truffle slices and pistachios are added with the viands used to fill the deboned head.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cabeza de Ternera (Veal Head)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal head
- lemons
- white linen cloth
- water
- lemon pieces
- carrots
- parsnip
- onions
- cloves
- bundle of parsley and fine herbs
- salt and pepper in grains
- flour of fine wheat (60 grams, optional)
- cow butter (25-30 grams, optional)

## Directions

1. Scald the veal head and remove the major bones.
2. Rub or scrub the head all over with the two halves of a lemon to blanch it and make it appetizing.
3. Wrap the veal head very tightly in white linen cloth so it does not lose its shape.
4. Place it in boiling water seasoned with the lemon pieces that were used to rub it, plus two or three carrots, one parsnip, two onions with their corresponding cloves, a bundle of parsley and fine herbs, and good seasoning of salt and pepper in grains.
5. Cook for at least four hours over very low heat.
6. For kitchens that do not spare expenses, blanch 60 grams of fine wheat flour in the cooking water and add 25 to 30 grams of cow butter.
7. When cooking is complete, unsew the cloth wrapping and let the head drain.
8. Serve hot with a piquant sauce or cold ravigota.
9. In the same dish where the veal is placed, put four shells filled respectively with finely chopped fine herbs, finely trimmed gherkins, finely minced shallots and scallions, and chopped hard-boiled egg.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cabeza de Ternera a la Tortuga (Calf's Head Turtle Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's head, cooked
- Financiera garnish
- White wine
- Cayenne pepper or sweet Murcia pepper
- Grated nutmeg
- Calf's tongue
- Six hard-boiled eggs
- Cooked crayfish
- Bread cubes (croutons)
- White wine for soaking croutons

## Directions

1. Cook the calf's head until tender.
2. Cut the cooked calf's head into pieces of appropriate size for individual servings.
3. Prepare a financiera garnish separately.
4. Add a couple of glasses of white wine to the sauce, ensuring it is not too thick.
5. Season well with Cayenne pepper or sweet Murcia pepper and grated nutmeg.
6. Cut the calf's tongue into small cubes the size of dice and add to the sauce along with the hard-boiled eggs, also cut into small cubes.
7. Arrange the pieces of calf's head in a pyramid shape on a round, large platter.
8. Pour the financiera sauce over the meat, covering the entire surface.
9. Arrange cooked crayfish over the surface, in number greater than the number of diners, alternating with triangular croutons soaked in white wine and fried, cut to the size of half a calling card.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Café a la Holandesa (Dutch-Style Coffee)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams of freshly roasted coffee
- Half a cuartillo (approximately 250 ml) of boiling water

## Directions

1. Use a tall copper coffeepot, tin-plated inside and out, with a ring at the mouth that holds the cloth filter.
2. Recently roasted coffee should be ground in a hand mill.
3. Place 125 grams of ground coffee in the cloth filter for half a cuartillo of boiling water.
4. Pour the boiling water over the coffee in the filter and cover the coffeepot.
5. Let it strain naturally.
6. Place the coffeepot on the kitchen plate in a location where it cannot boil, as it cools naturally while filtering.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Café a la Árabe (Arabic-Style Coffee)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Coffee, measured according to taste

## Directions

1. In an earthenware pot—it must be a pot and earthenware—heat the necessary water.
2. When it boils vigorously, add the coffee measured according to the taste of the one who will drink it.
3. Cover the mouth of the pot with a napkin well soaked in cold water and formed into a ball.
4. After three or four minutes, uncover the pot and pour in two or three spoonfuls of cold water.
5. With the help of a very fine cloth strainer, transfer the liquid from the pot to a cooking coffeepot.
6. Serve immediately.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Café al Estilo Casero de París (Parisian Home-Style Coffee)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Coffee
- Water

## Directions

1. Use ordinary filters such as those employed in the kitchens of private homes in Paris and all of France.
2. These filters are based on the principle of the Dutch method.
3. Use a tin coffeepot with two bodies.
4. When the coffee is made in such a vessel, transfer it immediately to another vessel of porcelain or glazed earthenware if you wish to drink it in good conditions, as coffee blackens much in tin and takes on a disagreeable acrid flavor.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Café Según lo Hacen en los Cafés (Coffee as Made in Cafés)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 grams of roasted and ground coffee of the day
- Fifteen cups of prepared water
- A few grams of fish glue
- 50 grams of chicory

## Directions

1. Fill two-thirds of a large coffeepot with water and the rest with residue from previously made coffee.
2. Heat until it boils, then remove from the fire and let the liquid settle.
3. When it clears, decant it; this water is what will be used to make the new coffee.
4. Remove and discard the old coffee residue from the coffeepot.
5. Clean the coffeepot and add 200 grams of roasted and ground coffee of the day for each fifteen cups of prepared water.
6. Dissolve a few grams of fish glue in the water and add 50 grams of chicory.
7. Stir well with a spatula and place the vessel over high heat, covered hermetically.
8. As soon as it has boiled for two or three seconds only, remove from the fire and let the coffee clear, stirring well.
9. Decant carefully by pouring in a thin stream, transferring it to an earthenware container that is kept from that moment in a bain-marie, taking care that it does not boil.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Calabacines en Pisto (Zucchini Sauté)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchini
- Onion
- Pepper
- Salt
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Chop zucchini into very small pieces
2. Fry in a pan where onion has already been fried, also chopped fine
3. Let fry until the liquid released is well consumed
4. Add a little pepper and salt
5. Thicken with well-beaten eggs
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Calabacines Rellenos (Stuffed Zucchini)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchini
- Picadillo (minced meat mixture)
- Almonds

## Directions

1. Cut about a finger's length from each end of the zucchini
2. Pierce lengthwise with a reed, extracting the interior part
3. Fill the hollowed interior with picadillo, the same mixture generally used for stuffed onions
4. Cook the zucchini
5. Incorporate a small sauce made with almonds
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Calabacines Rellenos (Stuffed Zucchini)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- zucchini
- picadillo (ground meat mixture)
- almonds

## Directions

1. Cut about one finger's length from each end of the zucchini.
2. Drill through the length with a reed, extracting the interior part.
3. Place a picadillo in the hollowed interior (the same one used generally for stuffed onions).
4. Cook them and incorporate a small sauce made with almonds.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Caldereta (Seafood Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Small fish such as red mullet, sea bass, tinoso, scorpion fish, pica, mácete, gilt-head bream, escamón, and barbudas
- Langostinos (Norway lobsters)
- Amasuelas (clams)
- Lapas (limpets)
- Other shellfish
- Onions
- Parsley
- Black pepper
- Red pepper paste
- Valencia olive oil
- Superior Sherry wine
- Nutmeg
- Guindilla (hot pepper)

## Directions

1. Wash and clean all fish and shellfish with sea water, ensuring the fish is very fresh
2. Cut onion into large slices and chop parsley
3. Half an hour before the scheduled meal time, place onion slices on the bottom of a wide, low pan with a tight-fitting lid
4. Layer the poorest quality fish on top, then cover with onion slices and chopped parsley
5. Sprinkle with black pepper, nutmeg, salt, red pepper paste, and guindilla, and add some of the shellfish
6. Add another layer of better quality fish and cover again with onion, parsley, pepper, guindilla, nutmeg, red pepper paste, and shellfish
7. Continue layering until the pan is full, placing the best quality fish (such as bream and sea bass) on top as a final layer
8. Pour half a bottle of olive oil and half a bottle of Sherry wine over everything
9. Cover the pan hermetically and place a weight on top
10. Prepare a wood fire with large flames and place the pan on stones over the fire
11. Heat until it comes to a boil, then remove from the flame and maintain the boil with hot coals underneath for uniform cooking
12. Cook for fifteen minutes until ready to serve
13. Serve in the same pan, with an experienced person serving to avoid breaking the fish and to ensure good distribution of sauce and shellfish among all diners
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Caldo de Pescado (Fish Broth)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish from the sea, very fresh
- Water
- Salt
- Carrot
- Celery rib
- Chervil
- Parsley
- Onion
- Clove of spice
- Bay leaf
- Butter from cows or olive oil

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of fish per liter of water with salt, a carrot, a rib of celery, chervil, parsley, an onion with its clove of spice, a bay leaf, and a little cow butter or one tablespoon of oil
2. When the fish has cooked well, pass the broth and drain it, but without pressing it
3. Use as a base for all white sauces for fish
4. Can be preserved in a cool place for several days
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Caldo de Vigilia (Lenten Broth)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Beans, dried
- Peas, dried
- Salt
- Parsley sprigs
- Celery
- Carrot
- Onion
- Cloves
- Butter from cows or olive oil

## Directions

1. Cook dried beans and peas in equal parts in water until they break down into a paste, with salt
2. Add a bundle of parsley and celery, a carrot, and an onion with a couple of cloves of spice embedded in it
3. Pass the broth through a sieve
4. Serve immediately as a soup base by adding a good piece of cow butter or two or three tablespoons of oil in which potatoes or another substance that does not impart flavor to the oil have been fried
5. To preserve for many days, decant carefully when cold to separate it from the sediment that forms and deposits in the vessel, without which the broth would turn sour
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Caldo Helado (Chilled Broth)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked meat broth
- Salt
- Egg yolks
- Sugar
- Cinnamon powder

## Directions

1. After the meat is well cooked and seasoned with salt without spices, remove the broth that has no fat, and let it cool
2. For each cup of broth, add two well-beaten and loosened egg yolks and one ounce of sugar
3. Dissolve them with the cooling broth and sugar, stirring everything together
4. If the quantity of broth is small, put it in cups, and for each one a pot of boiling water is needed
5. Place the cup over the mouth of the pot and let it boil until the contents of the cup set like custard
6. Serve with powdered sugar and cinnamon
7. If you have many cups to prepare, use a pot for each one; but if you prefer to make it in a large earthenware dish putting heat above and below, you can, and thus cut it like a tart. It is good and many desire to know how to make it
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Caldo Italiano (Italian Broth)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One piece of bacon
- Veal four times larger than the bacon
- Carrots
- Onions
- A small piece of beef
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, place a piece of bacon and another piece of veal four times larger.
2. Sauté and brown the meat well with the fat from the bacon over very high heat.
3. When this operation is finished, add the meat and bacon to a pot filled with plenty of boiling water.
4. Add carrots, onions, and a small piece of beef.
5. Cook for three hours over gentle heat.
6. If the broth is for the sick, use beef fat instead of bacon.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Caldo Seco Portátil en Pastillas (Portable Dry Broth in Tablets)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two veal feet
- Six pounds of beef or cow meat
- One and a half pounds of veal top
- Five pounds of mutton
- Water
- Salt
- One or two chickens or one or two partridges (optional, for more delicate broth)

## Directions

1. Place all ingredients in an earthenware pot and cook with low heat in sufficient water.
2. Skim carefully and strain the broth, pressing it well.
3. Boil a second time with fresh water and strain again.
4. Let both broths cool and remove the fat.
5. Combine the two broths and strain.
6. Evaporate in a double boiler until it reaches the consistency of paste.
7. Remove from the vessel and spread on marble stone.
8. Cut into small tablets and finish drying in a double boiler or oven until brittle.
9. Enclose in a glass bottle sealed with cork and wax, and store in a dry place.
10. To use: dissolve half an ounce or 15 grams of tablets in water, add a little salt, heat over gentle heat, and serve or use in making soups.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Callos a la Italiana (Italian-Style Tripe)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe, prepared and cleaned
- onions
- parsley
- carrots
- bay leaf
- basil
- cloves
- salt
- paprika
- water

## Directions

1. After being prepared and cleaned, cook with onions, parsley, carrots, bay leaf, basil, cloves, salt, paprika, and a sufficient quantity of water
2. Remove and drain
3. Cut into large pieces about four fingers in size, which should then be breaded
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Callos a la Moda de Caen (Tripe Normandy Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef or cow stomach (panza) with tripe and abomasum
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- bacon cut into strips like paper cigarettes
- carrots, chopped
- onions, chopped
- butter
- bouquet of aromatic herbs
- garlic cloves
- lamb's feet, well blanched (about one quarter of the volume)
- veal, cooked and boned in advance
- celery heart
- bundle of whole leeks
- one bottle of white wine
- one glass of good cognac
- two liters of water
- 300 grams of beef marrow

## Directions

1. Clean the stomach very well, blanch it, and place it in fresh water for one hour
2. Cut into small pieces the size of chocolate ounces and season with salt, pepper, clove, and nutmeg
3. Add bacon cut into strips like paper cigarettes
4. In an earthenware pot, brown the chopped carrots and onions with a little butter, adding a bouquet of aromatic herbs and garlic cloves
5. Place on top about one quarter in volume of well-blanched lamb's feet and some veal that has been cooked and boned beforehand
6. Place the prepared tripe on top with a celery heart and a bundle of whole leeks to maintain moisture during cooking so the dish does not dry out
7. Moisten with one bottle of white wine, one glass of good cognac, two liters of water, and 300 grams of beef marrow
8. Cover with a well-greased sheet of paper and seal with a paste made of water and flour like a crust
9. Bring to a boil over high heat, then continue cooking very slowly for twelve hours with coals around the pot and covered with a domed lid with heat on top
10. At the moment of serving, remove the paste and paper
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Callos Isabelinos (Isabella-Style Tripe)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe, well cleaned
- water
- salt
- pork fat
- roasted garlic head
- ham
- chorizo
- longaniza
- morcilla (blood sausage)
- paprika
- saffron
- parsley
- garlic
- bread soaked
- pepper
- clove
- cilantro
- caraway
- cumin
- cinnamon (optional)
- pine nuts or hazelnuts (optional)

## Directions

1. After cleaning well, place tripe in a pot with water and salt, a piece of pork fat, and a roasted garlic head, proportioned to quantity.
2. Optionally, add ham, chorizo, longaniza, morcilla, or similar items.
3. When everything is cooked, remove the pork fat, mash it, and dissolve it with a little paprika, saffron, parsley, garlic, and soaked bread, along with small amounts of spices: pepper, clove, cilantro, caraway, and cumin.
4. Add cinnamon, pine nuts, or hazelnuts if desired, ensuring all flavors are balanced and no single taste predominates.
5. Let cook a while and serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Callos á la Italiana (Italian-Style Tripe)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe, cleaned and prepared
- onions
- parsley
- carrots
- bay leaf
- basil
- cloves
- salt
- paprika
- water
- melted butter
- scallions
- garlic, finely chopped
- pepper
- hot sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the cleaned tripe with onions, parsley, carrots, bay leaf, basil, cloves, salt, paprika, and sufficient water.
2. Remove and drain the tripe, then cut into thick pieces about four fingers in size.
3. Coat the pieces in melted butter mixed with parsley, scallions, finely chopped garlic, salt, and pepper.
4. Serve with a very spicy sauce.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Callos á la Madrileña (Madrid-Style Tripe)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Five pounds of tripe for ten persons
- Garlic
- Carrots
- Onions with cloves
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Bay leaf
- Thyme
- Rosemary
- Four veal hands
- A pair of chorizos
- One morcilla (blood sausage)
- Three quarters of a pound of lard
- Six onions
- Twelve cloves of garlic
- Three quarters of a pound of lean, well-cured ham, cut into dice
- Chili pepper
- Fresh or preserved pimiento strips
- Good clean broth
- White wine

## Directions

1. Three days before eating, purchase the tripe from the best tripe shop and wash, clean, and scrape the tripe thoroughly.
2. Place the tripe in a basin and for twenty-four to thirty hours keep it under running water from a fountain spout so that the water in the basin remains fresh.
3. The day before eating, cut the tripe into squares smaller than chocolate ounces and boil in plenty of water in a large pot with garlic, carrots, onions with cloves embedded in them, salt, pepper, nutmeg, bay leaf, thyme, and rosemary.
4. Add four veal hands, a pair of chorizos, and one morcilla, and ensure the cooking, without being vigorous, is animated and even for eight consecutive hours, as any interruption will ruin the dish.
5. When the chorizo and morcilla are cooked, remove and reserve on a plate, then remove the veal hands, bone them carefully, and shred the meat.
6. Once the tripe is cooked, remove from the pot, draining well, and let cool in a basin or large dish for an entire night.
7. The next day, two and a half to three hours before eating, wash the tripe in hot water to eliminate the gelatinous and slimy coating covering it.
8. In a large saucepan over high heat, in three quarters of a pound of lard, sauté six onions and twelve cloves of garlic with three quarters of a pound of lean, well-cured ham cut into dice.
9. Season and add a few points of chili pepper, then add the chorizo and morcilla in small pieces.
10. When the onion takes color, sauté the tripe and veal hands, being careful that nothing sticks to the bottom of the pan, then quickly add good clean broth that covers the dish well.
11. As soon as everything begins to boil, place strips of fresh or preserved pimientos on top and put the tripe in the oven at medium heat for two hours, or if there is no oven, over very low heat on the stove for slow cooking.
12. Serve very hot and drink plenty of white wine.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Canapé de Anchoas (Anchovy Canapé)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sandwich bread slice
- Oil
- Parmesan or Manchego cheese, grated
- Twenty to thirty anchovies
- Milk

## Directions

1. Cut a slice of sandwich bread.
2. Fry it in oil and place it in a baking pan on a bed of grated Parmesan or Manchego cheese.
3. Place twenty to thirty anchovies soaked in milk solidly on the bread.
4. Pour fine oil over it.
5. Sprinkle with the same grated cheese.
6. Bake in the oven at medium heat for fifteen minutes.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cangrejos (Crabs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- good butter from cows
- refined oil
- one large onion, about the size of a lemon
- parsley
- tarragon
- thyme
- bay leaf
- clove
- nutmeg
- salt
- pepper
- two cloves of garlic
- flour
- half a quartillo of water
- crabs, well washed and freshly castrated
- half a quartillo of white wine

## Directions

1. Heat the butter cut with a spoonful of refined oil, add the onion, finely chopped, parsley also chopped, tarragon, thyme, bay leaf, clove, nutmeg, salt, pepper and two cloves of garlic
2. When the onion takes color, add a heaping spoonful of flour, and when it has toasted well, incorporate half a quartillo of water at the same time as the crabs, well washed and freshly castrated
3. Over very high heat, with the pan covered, sauté the contents for three minutes and let cook over low heat for ten minutes, after having added half a quartillo of white wine
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cangrejos a la Bordelesa (Crabs Bordeaux Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- four dozen crabs
- two ounces of very good butter from cows
- one tablespoon of refined oil
- one large onion the size of a lemon
- parsley
- tarragon
- thyme
- bay leaf
- clove
- nutmeg
- salt
- pepper
- two cloves of garlic
- one heaping tablespoon of flour
- half a quarter-liter of water

## Directions

1. Fry in two ounces of very good butter from cows, mixed with one tablespoon of refined oil, one large onion the size of a lemon, chopped very finely, parsley also chopped, tarragon, thyme, bay leaf, clove, nutmeg, salt, pepper and two cloves of garlic.
2. When the onion takes on color add one heaping tablespoon of flour, and when it has toasted well incorporate half a quarter-liter of water at the same time as the crabs, well washed and freshly cleaned.
3. Over very vivid fire, and with the saucepan covered, sauté the contents for three minutes and let cook over gentle fire for ten.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cangrejos al Natural (River Crabs Cooked Naturally)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- river crabs
- salt
- pepper
- thyme
- bay leaf
- parsley
- onion
- water

## Directions

1. In a saucepan add salt, pepper, thyme, bay leaf, parsley, onion cut into slices and the necessary amount of water so that the crabs are well bathed.
2. When the water boils in bubbles, over vivid and sustained fire, add the crabs after being well washed and cleaned—an operation carried out by pulling the swimming fin found in the middle of the tail, and which upon coming out pulls out with it a black and very bitter intestine.—This operation is done at the moment of going to cook the crabs, so that they die in their stew.
3. After four or five minutes of the water beginning to boil, if the crabs are small, and after six or seven if they are large, remove the saucepan from the fire, and so that they do not dry out, leave them a quarter of an hour in their water and serve them cold, or as a garnish.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Caponada (Italian Capon Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- capon breast
- unsweetened crackers
- hard-boiled eggs
- pitted olives
- olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Make a salad with capon breasts, unsweetened crackers not too large, slices of hard-boiled eggs, and pitted olives
2. Season everything beforehand with olive oil, salt, and pepper
3. At the moment of serving, add water and vinegar and mix together
4. The dish should be soaked in its dressing, not brothy
5. In summer, use ice-cold water
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Caponada (Italian Capon Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Breasts of fattened capon
- Unsweetened crackers, not very large
- Hard-boiled egg slices
- Pitted olives
- Olive oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Water
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Make a salad with the capon breasts, crackers, hard-boiled egg slices, and pitted olives
2. Season everything beforehand with olive oil, salt, and pepper
3. Incorporate water and vinegar at the moment of serving
4. The dish should be soaked in its dressing, not soupy
5. In summer, use ice water, and it becomes the Italian version of gazpacho
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Capón en Sal (Capon in Salt)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- vegetables for broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook a well-prepared, clean capon in a pot with the same vegetables as for a good stew, but not as much water
2. When cooked, remove from the pot and place on a serving platter near the heat
3. Decrease the small amount of broth that should remain
4. Reduce it considerably
5. Serve the capon in this broth, sprinkling it with coarse salt or what is called cooking salt
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Capón á la Siciliana (Capon Sicilian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One well-fattened capon
- Melted beef butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Truffles
- Champagne wine
- Beef butter for basting
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare a good fattened capon and rinse its interior with melted beef butter, seasoning well with salt and pepper
2. Fill a pot halfway with truffles and Champagne wine and bring to a boil for about ten minutes over high heat
3. Remove the pot from the fire and place the capon inside to marinate in the aroma for three hours
4. Remove the capon and briefly brown it in beef butter, then let it cool
5. Insert a piece of beef butter inside the capon and squeeze the juice of a lemon over it
6. Braise over low heat for one hour with the butter from the browning
7. Ten minutes before serving, pour the Champagne and truffles over the capon
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Caracoles a la Borgoñona (Snails Burgundy Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- snails
- water
- salt
- ash
- beef butter
- parsley
- garlic head
- cloves
- thyme
- laurel
- flour
- egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Wash and prepare the snails as previously described
2. Place them in a pot of boiling water with a little salt and a bundle of ash tied well
3. After a quarter of an hour remove them and their shells come off easily
4. Wash in cold water and cook again
5. Drain and place in a saucepan with beef butter, parsley, one head of garlic, two cloves, thyme, laurel, and one tablespoon of flour
6. Mix everything and thicken, without cooking, with egg yolks
7. When serving, add a little lemon juice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Caracoles a la Extremeña (Snails Extremaduran Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- snails
- onion
- oil
- salt
- water
- vegetables
- bread
- spices
- fennel
- thyme
- garlic
- flour
- sour juice

## Directions

1. Wash the snails in four to six waters
2. Place them in a wide vessel with water and heat over fire until they emerge from their shells and die with the body completely outside
3. Remove and wash them very well with salt and more waters
4. Cook them to remove the verdín
5. Fry onion with oil and mix with the snails, sautéing them well and adding salt and water
6. Cook for three to four hours
7. Mince vegetables and pound them with a little bread, seasoning with spices, fennel, and thyme
8. Dilute the mixture with the broth from the snails; if the broth is clear, add fried flour
9. Add garlic and sour juice at the time of serving
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Caracoles con Cebolla (Snails with Onion)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- snails
- water
- salt
- thyme
- laurel
- parsley
- oregano
- onions
- oil

## Directions

1. Prepare and wash the snails as previously described
2. Place them in cold water and heat progressively until it reaches boiling, skimming well
3. Add salt, thyme, laurel, parsley, and oregano
4. When cooked, drain them well
5. Fry onions in oil and add them to the pan so they sauté together, turning frequently
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Caramelo (Homemade Caramel)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound powdered sugar
- 1 liter cold water

## Directions

1. Place half a pound of powdered sugar in a pastry pan.
2. Melt it by stirring with a wooden spoon.
3. When the sugar is well melted, let it boil over the fire for one hour at a very slow boil with the spoon inside so you can stir from time to time.
4. When the melted sugar has acquired a very dark brown color, add one liter of cold water.
5. Let the sugar, which should have solidified when the cold water was added, dissolve well over a slow fire, and let it boil for twenty minutes at the edge of the fire.
6. Let it cool, put it in a bottle, which should be carefully corked for use when needed.
7. The caramel should be the color of dark mahogany, which is obtained by making it over a slow fire.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Carbonadas (Braised Lamb Chops)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb breast, bones removed
- Three or four slices of ham
- Bacon slices
- Two onions
- Clove
- Sprig of thyme
- Bay leaf
- Carrots, sliced into wheels
- Salt and pepper
- Two ladles of clean broth from the pot

## Directions

1. Remove the large bones attached to the cartilage from a lamb breast and divide it into eight equal pieces, oval at one end and pointed at the other.
2. In a casserole, place three or four slices of ham, and on top the lamb pieces, covered with bacon slices.
3. Add two onions, clove, a sprig of thyme, a bay leaf, and carrots cut into wheels, seasoning with salt and pepper and moistening with two ladles of clean broth.
4. Cook over low heat, below and above, for three hours.
5. Remove the lamb pieces, degrease the juices, reduce them, and pour over the meat on a serving platter.
6. Serve the lamb pieces with a minced mushroom garnish, which is its obligatory garnish.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Carbonadas (Lamb Chops with Ham and Bacon)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb breast
- Three or four slices of ham
- Bacon slices
- Onions
- Clove spice
- Herbs

## Directions

1. Remove the large bones attached to the cartilage of a lamb breast and divide it into eight equal pieces, oval at one end and pointed at the other.
2. Place three or four slices of ham in a pot and on top place the lamb pieces, covered with slices of bacon.
3. Add two onions, clove spice, and herbs.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Carbonadas Italianas (Italian-style Braised Lamb Chops)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat slices, cut in square form
- Ground meat filling
- String

## Directions

1. Cut meat slices into square form and wrap a ground meat filling around them as if they were wafers.
2. Proceed in the same manner as explained for simple carbonadas, but tie each portion well with string so the filling does not escape.
3. Remove the string when serving.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cardo con Tuétano (Cardoon with Marrow)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cardoon stalks
- Salt
- Pork lard or oil
- Broth from the pot, clean and defatted
- Roux-based sauce (rubia-blanca)
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Kill the corners of the stalk and cut into pieces fifteen centimeters long.
2. Blanch the pieces in boiling water with a pinch of salt until the outer layer comes off easily.
3. Add cold water to the hot water to be able to put your hands in and manipulate the cardoon pieces, removing the skin and putting them in very cold water as they are peeled.
4. Remove and let drain very well to put them in a saucepan with a very loose roux-based sauce.
5. Wet with broth from the pot, clean and defatted, in quantity sufficient to cover all the cardoon, and cook over very low heat until all the sauce is consumed.
6. Place the cardoons in a serving dish, and pour over at the moment of serving a white sauce well bound with two or three egg yolks, dissolved in half a cup of water.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Carlota Rusa (Russian Charlotte)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- apple marmalade (prepared as in the previous recipe)
- slices of crumb from French bread two or three days old, half a finger thick
- butter, melted and lukewarm
- apricot marmalade, quince paste, currant jelly, or any other sweet with fluidity

## Directions

1. Prepare an apple marmalade as in the previous recipe, but keep it on the heat longer so that it loses as much water as possible
2. Cut slices of bread crumb into triangular pieces rounded at the base, so that when joined together the point of all of them is the center of a circle
3. Dip the bread slices in lukewarm butter and place them on the bottom of an enameled porcelain pan, covering it completely
4. Line the interior walls of the pan with slices of the same bread, cut into rectangles two fingers wide and as long as the height of the pan, also greased with lukewarm butter
5. Pour the prepared apple marmalade into this mold, and with a spatula make a hollow in the center
6. Without removing the spatula, fill it with apricot marmalade, quince paste that is somewhat loose, currant jelly, or any other sweet that has fluidity and can adapt well there
7. Even out the surface of the dish when the pan is full, and cover it with a garnish of bread equal to that on the bottom
8. Bury the pan up to the rim in hot ashes, and cover the pan with a dome lid well loaded with heat
9. Let the charlotte cook until the bread on top has browned enough, then turn the dish over onto a platter so that the slices of bread from below, which will also be browned, are on top
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Carlota Ruso-Madrileña (Russian-Madrid Charlotte)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- cat's tongue biscuits (lenguas de gato)
- apple marmalade
- guava jelly or well-set coffee cream

## Directions

1. Line the inside of a mold evenly with cat's tongue biscuits
2. The biscuits must fit well together so that there is no space between them
3. Fill the mold with apple marmalade and even out the surface
4. Close the surface with the same biscuits in a double row
5. Turn out onto a platter
6. Fill the hollow that results in the dish with guava jelly or well-set coffee cream
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Carne de Membrillo (Quince Paste)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- very ripe and yellow quinces
- water
- sugar in proportion of five pounds sugar to six pounds fruit

## Directions

1. Choose very ripe and yellow quinces and prepare them to heat the syrup afterwards, which should be in the proportion of six pounds of fruit to five pounds of sugar; peel the quinces and put them with sufficient quantity of water in a small cauldron, which should be on the fire so that the quinces do not turn black, and add them in pieces as they are peeled, in hot water, taking care that the boiling does not stop from when it begins, so that it does not take on the color of wine and is well yellow and pleasant to the sight
2. When the water does not cover them well, always add more, well heated and boiled, in no way cold; as soon as the pieces are well cooked and soft, but still whole, remove them with a skimmer and spread them on a clean cloth, and when cold, pound them well in a marble mortar or pass them through a fairly open strainer
3. Make a thick syrup of half a cuartilla of water per pound of sugar, and well clarified, pour into it—cold or hot, it makes no difference—the prepared quince paste, put it on the fire and stir until it takes the consistency of preserve; this is also poured while hot into glasses, jars or small tin boxes, and after four days have passed; if the open boxes are placed in an oven for half an hour, a thin film forms which preserves the sweet for a long time
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Carne de Vaca Cocida en Albondiguillas (Beef Meatballs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- potatoes
- butter or fat
- eggs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mince very fine the piece of beef with potatoes cooked in the ashes
2. Add a little butter or fat from cattle, some whole eggs, salt, pepper, etc.
3. Mix it all well together, form balls that can be browned in butter
4. Serve with a piquant sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Carne de Vaca Emperejilada (Beef with Parsley)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted fat or pork lard
- parsley
- mushrooms
- breadcrumbs
- good broth
- boiled beef
- pork lard
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Place at the bottom of a metal or fire-resistant plate a little roasted fat or pork lard
2. Make a bed of very finely chopped parsley and mushrooms
3. Cover this bed with breadcrumbs
4. Place the slices of boiled meat on top, arranging them side by side, continuing until all meat is used
5. Moisten with good broth and dampen from time to time
6. Simmer gently for three quarters of an hour
7. Serve with potatoes browned in pork lard, arranged around this stew
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Carnero Verde (Green Mutton)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton from any part of the animal, cut into small pieces
- bacon, cut into pieces
- oil
- onion, finely chopped
- meat broth
- parsley, chopped fine like snuff
- mint
- young garlic shoots
- lettuce hearts
- pine nuts, soaked and chopped
- spices
- lemon or orange juice

## Directions

1. Sauté the mutton pieces over low heat in an earthenware pot with the bacon pieces, oil and finely chopped onion
2. After two hours, stirring constantly so the dish does not stick, add meat broth
3. When everything is very well cooked, add the chopped parsley, mint, young garlic shoots, lettuce hearts, chopped pine nuts, spices, and lemon or orange juice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cauliflower Salad (Coliflor en Ensalada)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Let the cauliflower cool after being prepared and cooked as previously described
2. Season with oil, vinegar, salt and pepper
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cauliflower à la Parmesana (Coliflor a la Parmesana)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- White sauce
- Parmesan or Manchego cheese, grated
- Breadcrumbs
- Beef lard, fried

## Directions

1. Prepare the cauliflower as in previous recipes
2. Cover it with white sauce in which grated Parmesan or Manchego cheese has been added
3. Cover with a layer of breadcrumbs and grated cheese
4. Brown the edges in fried beef lard
5. Add another layer of breadcrumbs and cheese
6. A quarter hour before serving, place in a serving dish in a convection oven to form the crust or gratin and take on color
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cebollas Asadas (Roasted Onions)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- oil
- pepper
- sugar
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut both ends off the onions and remove the outer thick leaves.
2. Place them in a casserole with oil, pepper, sugar, and ground cinnamon.
3. Roast over gentle heat, covering with an iron lid and with coals on top until they are well browned.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cebollas Rellenas (Stuffed Onions)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- lean meat, chopped
- fat bacon, chopped
- parsley
- garlic
- breadcrumbs
- fried onion
- salt
- spices
- oil
- toasted flour
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Make a mixture of chopped lean meat and fat bacon, parsley, garlic, breadcrumbs, fried onion, salt, and spices.
2. Remove the inner layers of the onions with a knife.
3. Fill the onions with the mixture and place them in a casserole with the filling facing upward.
4. Cook them.
5. Make a sauce with fried oil, toasted flour, and a few drops of vinegar.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cercetas con Nabos (Teal with Turnips)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- teal
- strips of pork belly
- turnips
- butter
- salt and seasoning

## Directions

1. Mix the teal with strips of pork belly and roast on a spit
2. Cut the turnips into small cubes like dice
3. Sauté them in butter with appropriate seasoning
4. At the moment of serving the birds, cover them with this garnish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Champagne Cup (Champagne Cocktail)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large bottle Roéderer Champagne
- 1 small siphon of seltzer water
- 1 small glass of Sherry
- 1 larger glass of Curaçao
- 1 equal glass of Martel Cognac
- 1 whole fine lemon zest
- 2 large spoonfuls of ground sugar
- 1 quarter pound of strawberries, lightly crushed
- 1 pound of ice

## Directions

1. Mix everything well one hour before serving
2. Incorporate the ice at the last moment
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Pour into white Bohemian crystal pitchers to serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chanfaina (Organ Meat Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- liver
- lungs
- cooked blood
- all from mutton
- oil
- onions
- garlic
- spices
- chopped parsley
- paprika
- water
- flour
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cut pieces of liver, lungs and cooked blood from mutton
2. Sauté in oil, adding onions, garlic, spices, chopped parsley and paprika
3. After everything is well sautéed, add water and bring to a boil
4. Fry flour until well browned, dissolve it with water, and add to the stew to thicken the broth
5. If desired, an egg mixture can be added
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chaufroix de Alondras, Cogujadas o Malvises a la Bella Vista (Chaufroid of Larks, Crested Larks, or Warblers Bella Vista Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Larks, crested larks, or warblers
- white wine
- breasts of other birds of the same species
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- paper boxes
- white wine for moistening
- meat jelly from consummate broth
- truffle shavings

## Directions

1. Clean the birds and rinse their interior thoroughly with white wine.
2. Fill them with a minced filling made only from breasts of other birds of the same species, converted into paste and kneaded with butter, well seasoned with salt and pepper.
3. Roast them in the oven prepared in this way, over gentle heat without allowing them to take color.
4. Once roasted, let them cool.
5. Place each bird in loosely fitting paper boxes with the breast facing upward.
6. With a brush and good white wine, moisten the bird well and fill the box.
7. Cover the bird with meat jelly from consummate broth, sprinkled and scattered with truffle shavings.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chocha a la Vizcaina (Woodcock Biscay Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock with its entrails
- Pork butter
- Bacon slice
- Small onions
- Broth
- Sherry
- Small turnips
- One very finely chopped onion
- Flour

## Directions

1. After removing the entrails from the woodcock (which will be kept for the sauce) and arranging it so the beak comes out between the right breast, place it in a small pot with pork butter, a thin slice of bacon, two or three small onions, two cups of broth, a measure of Sherry, and four small turnips, being careful to move the pot from time to time and adding broth if it reduces.
2. When the woodcock has cooked enough, fry separately one very finely chopped onion, and when it is half done, add the chopped entrails of the woodcock to the same pan so they release all their juice, but being careful to first remove the crop or pouch, which gives a bad taste. Add a little flour and all the sauce together.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chocha Asada (Roasted Woodcock)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One woodcock
- Bacon leaves, thin
- Twine or string
- Woodcock giblets
- 60 grams of beef lard
- A pair of truffles
- Croutons
- Watercress

## Directions

1. Pluck and singe the woodcock.
2. Clean and truss it, passing the beak through the body.
3. Remove the giblets by opening along the back and extracting the intestines.
4. Mince the giblets and mix with 60 grams of beef lard and trimmed truffles.
5. Stuff the woodcock body with this mixture.
6. Cover the woodcock with thin bacon leaves, securing them with twine according to the illustration.
7. Roast over gentle, even, and prolonged heat so that the abundant meat of the bird cooks through well.
8. Remove the bacon and twine at the moment of serving.
9. Serve with fried croutons soaked in the juices and plenty of watercress on the platter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chochas Rellenas (Stuffed Woodcocks)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcocks, plucked and cleaned
- pork belly
- parsley
- garlic
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Open the woodcocks underneath the tail sufficiently to extract the intestines and offal
2. Chop all the extracted parts with a piece of pork belly about half the amount of the woodcock offal, parsley, garlic, salt, and pepper
3. Fill the woodcocks with this mixture
4. Cover them with thin slices of pork belly
5. Roast over low heat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chocolate (Hot Chocolate)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One and a half ounces of chocolate
- Water or milk

## Directions

1. Break the chocolate into small pieces
2. Dissolve it in boiled water or milk in a saucepan
3. Heat gradually while stirring constantly with a wooden spatula
4. Let it cook over low heat for a quarter hour so that the mixture acquires consistency
5. Serve very hot
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chocolate con Ámbar (Chocolate with Amber)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Chocolate
- Amber at the proportion of 60 to 72 grams per pound of chocolate

## Directions

1. Prepare chocolate with amber in the specified proportion
2. Serve a quarter portion (cuartilla) of the mixture to provide restorative properties
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Choucroute (Fermented Cabbage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilo of choucroute
- 1 pound of smoked bacon, finely sliced
- 1 sobreasada or chorizo weighing half a pound
- 125 grams of pork lard
- half a cuartillo of white wine
- 2 ladles of good clean broth
- salt and pepper
- pinch of grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Desalt the choucroute by soaking in water for three hours. Drain well and press it with two casserole lids.
2. Place the choucroute in a casserole over gentle heat with 1 pound of smoked bacon cut into very fine slices, 1 sobreasada or chorizo weighing half a pound, and 125 grams of pork lard.
3. Moisten with half a cuartillo of white wine and 2 ladles of good clean broth. Season carefully with salt and pepper and add a pinch of grated nutmeg.
4. Cook the cabbage with the bacon and chorizo for five consecutive hours over very low heat.
5. When serving, cut the sausage into slices and arrange them on the platter with symmetry. If the broth is abundant, reduce it over a lively fire while arranging the dish, but without adding anything else.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuleta de Vaca a la Buena Dicha (Beef Chop Good Fortune Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef chop, prepared as in previous recipes and properly seasoned
- 125 grams of beef fat

## Directions

1. Sauté the prepared and seasoned chop in 125 grams of beef fat
2. Turn occasionally so it browns on both sides
3. Then place the pan over low heat or on a stovetop in a location where it does not receive direct heat action
4. Cover the pan
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuleta de Vaca a la Madrileña (Beef Chop Madrid Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef chop, prepared and larded
- rice cooked plain
- cooking broth and juices from the chop

## Directions

1. Prepare and lard the chop as described for previous preparations
2. Cook it in the same manner as the Milanese style chop
3. Instead of macaroni, place plain cooked rice on the serving platter
4. Place the meat on top of the rice and pour its juice over everything
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuleta de Vaca a la Milanesa (Beef Chop Milanese Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef chop, prepared and larded
- one quarter liter of broth
- one quarter liter of white wine or Sherry
- three hours cooking time over low heat
- macaroni
- broth from the stew pot
- grated Parmesan cheese, or equal parts Gruyère and Manchego cheese

## Directions

1. Cook one beef chop, prepared and larded but slightly more seasoned, in one quarter liter of broth mixed with one quarter liter of white wine or Sherry
2. After three hours of cooking over low heat, remove the pan from the heat
3. Pass the juice through a sieve and reduce by half
4. Separately, cook macaroni in broth from the stew pot and sprinkle generously with grated Parmesan cheese, or if not available, equal parts Gruyère and Manchego cheese
5. Place the macaroni on a serving platter and place the chop on top
6. Pour the reduced cooking juice over everything
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuleta de Vaca a lo Hortera (Beef Chop Hortera Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef chop, prepared and larded
- one liter of broth
- 125 grams of fresh bacon
- a bouquet of parsley and scallions
- six carrots
- one bay leaf
- two onions with cloves
- one head of red cabbage, cut into slices

## Directions

1. Prepare and lard the chop as previously described
2. Cook over low heat for one hour in one liter of broth with 125 grams of fresh bacon
3. Add a bouquet of parsley and scallions, six carrots, one bay leaf, two onions with cloves, and one head of red cabbage cut into slices
4. Let cook two hours more
5. Remove the meat, take off the twine, and place on a large serving platter
6. Arrange the cabbage and carrots around it
7. If the broth is thin, reduce it over high heat before pouring it over the dish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuleta de Vaca Tarascada (Braised Beef Chop, Tarascada Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one well-trimmed and full beef chop
- lard for larding
- salt and pepper
- twine
- bacon slices
- ham slices
- two cups of good broth without fat
- six carrots
- a bouquet of fine herbs and scallions or leeks
- one bay leaf
- two cloves, minced into one onion of suitable size

## Directions

1. Choose a well-trimmed beef chop and have the butcher prepare it, reserving the bone and trimmings for broth
2. Lard it as if making braised beef and season the surfaces
3. Tie with twine so it does not lose its shape during cooking
4. In a braising pan, place bacon slices and ham slices
5. Place the chop on top, covered with thin bacon slices
6. Moisten with two cups of good broth without fat
7. Add six carrots, a bouquet of fine herbs and scallions or leeks, one bay leaf, and two cloves minced into one onion
8. Place the pan over high heat until it comes to a boil, then cover and continue cooking over low heat for three hours or more, until the meat is very tender
9. Remove the chop from heat, remove the twine, and keep it warm in some of the cooking broth
10. Separately, pass the remaining juice through a sieve and reduce over low heat until it reaches a thick consistency
11. Pour the sauce over the chop, which has been placed on a serving platter
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuleta de Vaca á la Buena Dicha (Beef Chop Good Fortune Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams beef butter
- 1 beef chop, prepared and seasoned

## Directions

1. Brown the beef chop in the butter, turning occasionally so it colors on both sides.
2. Place the pan over low heat or on a cooktop away from direct flame.
3. Cover with a lid and place fire on top, renewing it every half hour.
4. After two hours of cooking, remove the meat from the pan and remove the string.
5. Place on a serving dish and pour the degreased cooking juice over the top.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuleta de Vaca á la Real (Beef Chop Royal Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 beef chops in one piece
- 500 grams veal shoulder
- 1 veal hand, deboned and cut into four pieces
- 1 liter concentrated broth
- 12 carrots
- small onions
- beef butter

## Directions

1. Have the butcher remove the bones from the end chops, keeping only the bone from the middle chop with its attached piece of meat.
2. Prepare, lard, season and tie the meat as described in previous formulas.
3. Place in a braising pan with the veal shoulder, veal hand cut into four pieces, and concentrated broth.
4. After two hours of cooking, add the carrots cut lengthwise into equal pieces.
5. Continue cooking for two to three more hours until the meat is very well done.
6. Remove from the pan and remove the string.
7. Place on a serving dish, arranging a row of small onions browned and colored in beef butter around it, and another row of carrots cooked at the same time.
8. Pass the strained, degreased and reduced juice through a sieve and pour over the meat and vegetables at serving time.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Carnero a la Practicón (Lamb Chops Practicón Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chops
- pork lard
- red peppers
- meat broth
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Brown and sauté the lamb chops for two minutes in a pan with pork lard, adding them one by one
2. Insert each browned chop into a whole red pepper that has been previously cooked in meat broth and trimmed at the stem
3. Drain each pepper well and with its chop inside, well seasoned, roast over strong heat on a grill or in the oven
4. Serve the stuffed peppers on a platter with tomato sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Carnero al Estragón (Lamb Chops with Tarragon)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb chops
- Tarragon, chopped fine like snuff
- Salt and pepper
- Potato purée

## Directions

1. Prepare the chops as in the previous formula.
2. When turning them on the grill, sprinkle with tarragon chopped fine like snuff at the same time as salt and pepper.
3. Arrange the chops on their sides in a crown on a round platter with potato purée in the center.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Carnero al Minuto (Lamb Chops Minute)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb chops
- Salt and pepper
- Potatoes, fried or golden in oil

## Directions

1. Prepare the chops by removing the skin and anything that cannot be eaten, except the main bone.
2. Beat them on both sides with a mallet or wooden spatula and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
3. Quickly sear over high heat on the grill, turning only once so the juices are not lost.
4. Serve with fried or golden potatoes, boiled in steam.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Carnero Panadas y Esparrilladas (Breaded and Grilled Lamb Chops)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb chops
- Melted cow butter
- Bread crumbs
- Salt and pepper
- Potatoes
- Tomato sauce, thick

## Directions

1. Prepare the chops as described.
2. Oil them or coat both sides with melted cow butter.
3. Cover with bread crumbs and roast on the grill over not-too-strong heat, turning two or three times so they cook through.
4. Season with salt and pepper on one of the turns.
5. Serve in a crown with potatoes and a thick tomato sauce, well strained but without binding.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Carnero Salteadas (Sautéed Lamb Chops)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb chops
- Cow butter
- Vinegar
- Meat juices
- Potatoes
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare and beat the lamb chops.
2. Sauté them in cow butter, turning repeatedly, completing this in five or six minutes.
3. With the same butter used for sautéing the meat, make a light brown sauce; add a thread of vinegar and a couple of spoonfuls of meat juice if available.
4. Pour this sauce over the chops, which will be waiting on a platter, with some garnish in the center or around.
5. Serve with potatoes, also sautéed separately in cow butter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera al Natural (Grilled Veal Chops)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops
- melted butter or refined oil
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. After they are slowly grilled on the grill, taking care of the fire so they do not burn, and after having soaked them thoroughly, better than merely spread, in melted butter or refined oil.
2. When the chops are on the grill and with each turn given to them, season with salt and pepper without excess and serve from the grill to the table.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera al Natural (Veal Chops Plain)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops
- beef lard, melted, or refined oil
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal chops by cleaning them of skin and greasy parts
2. Flatten them by beating them firmly on both sides with a wooden spatula kept for this purpose
3. Slowly roast them on the grill, taking care with the fire so they do not burn
4. After soaking them well, better than merely coating them, in melted beef lard or refined oil
5. When the chops are on the grill and with each turn given to them, season with salt and pepper without excess
6. Serve them from the grill to the table
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera en Salsa Picante (Veal Chops in Spicy Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops
- spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal chop as described above, well breaded or natural.
2. Place underneath the spicy sauce, allowing one deciliter of sauce for each chop.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera Mechadas (Larded Veal Chops)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops with more meat
- thin strips of ham
- butter
- broth
- fried bread cubes

## Directions

1. Cut leaving more meat, and after larding them finely with very thin strips of ham using larding needles, pass them through butter to brown them a little.
2. Let them drain of the butter, and when cold arrange the ends and place in a pan with a little broth.
3. Give them a couple of boilings and dress with fried bread cubes, which is how they are served.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera Panadas (Breaded Veal Chops)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- grated bread
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs, finely chopped
- veal chops
- melted butter or lard or refined oil

## Directions

1. Grate the bread and mix well with salt, pepper, and finely chopped fine herbs.
2. Prepare the chops and soak them in melted butter, lard, or oil as if they were to be prepared plain, so that the grated bread adheres perfectly to the meat.
3. Grill on the grill and serve alone or with the garnish that best suits.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera Salteadas (Sautéed Veal Chops)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- six well-prepared veal chops
- 125 grams butter or lard
- salt and pepper
- bread croutons
- meat juice
- broth

## Directions

1. Sauté six well-prepared chops in a pan, without breading them, in 125 grams of butter or lard.
2. Let them take color over high heat, turning them continually, and finish cooking with only the fat and the proper seasoning.
3. Serve these chops over bread croutons fried in lard, and when placing them in the dish as a base for the chops, pour over them the meat juice, extended with a little broth.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera à la Amorosa (Veal Chops in the Loving Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops
- lard
- good oil
- bacon scrapings
- ham bits
- sweet wine (Malaga, Marsala, or Alicante)
- broth or meat juice
- salt and pepper
- bread croutons

## Directions

1. Beat the veal chops slightly and flour them, one by one, evenly.
2. In a pan, for twelve chops, melt one ounce of lard with three tablespoons of very good oil, some bacon scrapings and ham bits.
3. Sauté the chops over high heat, and when they have taken on a good color and are well browned, moisten them with two glasses of Malaga, Marsala, or Alicante wine, or any sweet wine.
4. Let it cook over low heat until the wine is consumed, and then season and add broth or a little juice to bathe the meat.
5. At the moment of serving, remove the chops, which are placed on a plate over croutons of equal shape, and pour over them the sauce that is in the pan, straining and sifting it through fine linen.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera à la Milanesa (Veal Chops Milanese)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- six good veal chops
- melted butter
- grated bread
- salt
- pepper
- six eggs
- Parmesan cheese
- 250 grams fresh butter

## Directions

1. Prepare six good veal chops and soak them in melted butter so that they take the grated bread well mixed with salt and pepper.
2. Beat six eggs, and in the beaten egg dip the already breaded chops, and immediately bread them again with grated bread mixed with Parmesan cheese.
3. It is necessary to insist on this second breading so that the chops are covered with the greatest amount of grating.
4. Melt 250 grams of very fresh butter and fry the chops in it, which should take on a very good color.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera à la Papillote (Veal Chops en Papillote)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops
- filling for chops
- white paper, greased and cut

## Directions

1. Prepare the chops as for the previous formula, or better yet, spread both sides with a chop filling that has already been explained how to make.
2. Then wrap them in white greased and cut paper, as shown in the drawing, and grill them on the grill.
3. They can also be roasted on a gentle fire in a braising pan or on a tray in the oven.
4. Serve these chops with their paper wrapping, which can be well browned but not burned.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera à la Vallisoletana (Veal Chops Valladolid Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two chops together, each with two ribs
- meat for meatballs filling

## Directions

1. Cut two chops together, each comprising two ribs, and removing the bone from one of them, flatten them well and perfect their shape.
2. Open the meat across, beating the two parts with the handle of the knife, but without separating from the bone.
3. Fill the hollow in the middle with a finely chopped meat filling from meatballs, and join the two parts of the meat of the chops together again, and roast on the grill until they are well done.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera á la Antigua Española (Veal Chops Old Spanish Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops
- butter or oil

## Directions

1. Allow the piece of meat from which the chops are to be cut to age, then remove the large bone from the end, the skin, the tendons, and the fat if desired.
2. Flatten with a mallet or iron or wooden plank, and round them with a knife, cleaning the bone or rib of the muscular meat, leaving only the end bare for easy gripping.
3. Roast on the grills a little, then stew in a pan with butter or oil, as preferred and convenient, or leave them on the grills so they reach their proper point of cooking and can be presented at the table.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera á la Mayordoma (Veal Chops Steward Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops
- mayordoma sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare as breaded chops and roast on the grill.
2. Add the mayordoma sauce always underneath the chops, calculating one tablespoon of sauce for each one.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas de Ternera á la Vallisoletana (Veal Chops Vallisoletana Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two veal chops together, each comprising two ribs
- meat from small meatballs for stuffing
- bone from one of the chops

## Directions

1. Cut two chops together, each comprising two ribs, and remove the bone from one of them; flatten them well and perfect their shape.
2. Open the meat across, pounding both parts with the back of a knife, but without separating it from the bone.
3. Fill the hollow in the middle with a finely chopped mixture of meatball meat, and join the two parts of the chop meat together again.
4. Roast on the grill until well done.
5. Cover the bottom of a serving dish with the same chopped mixture.
6. Place the chops on top, sauce them with their own juice, and keep them in a hot oven for four minutes, then serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas Verdes de Cerdo (Green Pork Chops)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- lemon juice
- parsley, chopped
- two tarragon leaves, finely chopped
- half a dozen shallots
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- nutmeg
- pork chops
- toasted and grated bread

## Directions

1. In a bowl or tureen, macerate in lemon juice for six hours a good amount of chopped parsley, two tarragon leaves finely chopped, and half a dozen shallots, with salt, pepper, cloves and nutmeg.
2. After that time, grill the chops on a grill grate over high heat.
3. Little by little incorporate the fat that the chops release into the bowl and mix it with the marinade, which is thickened and bound with toasted and grated bread.
4. At the moment of serving, place the chops on a very hot platter and coat them on both sides so that the meat is not visible with this paste garnish, using a spatula for this.
5. Despite the precaution of heating the platter, the time spent in the operation and the garnish that is cold will cool the chops; to serve them hot after being prepared, put them in the oven with the door open or cover them with a cloche cover with fire on top.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chuletas à la Luis Felipe (Chops Louis Philippe Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops
- kitchen twine

## Directions

1. Beat and cut the veal chops well and season them raw, tie three together, well stacked with kitchen twine, as if they were a single thick chop, and thus grill them on the grill, very well done.
2. Serve them plain, without the twine, of course, but in the form and position they had on the grill, and they are eaten as the curious reader will see.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Chupe (Lamb and Potato Stew-Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One liter of water
- Two pounds of lamb leg, cut into very small pieces, cleaned of fat, tendons and skin
- Oregano
- One large onion the size of an egg, finely chopped
- Half a tablespoon of paprika
- One heaping tablespoon of lard
- Potatoes, cut into pieces the size of coins

## Directions

1. Put a liter of water to boil in a pot and at the first boil, add two pounds of lamb leg cut into very small pieces, cleaned of fat, tendons and skin.
2. Cook the meat over slow and steady heat for three hours, without needing to skim the broth.
3. Season and add a little oregano, one large onion the size of an egg finely chopped, half a tablespoon of paprika, and one heaping tablespoon of lard.
4. Continue cooking the broth for a quarter hour.
5. Thirty minutes before serving the dish, which with these quantities is calculated for six people, add potatoes cut into pieces the size of coins.
6. This stew-soup must be very brothy, so care should be taken during cooking, and if the broth reduces or is consumed, add water before seasoning.
7. Serve with a spoon.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Civet de Liebre (Hare Civet)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams butter or lard
- one good tablespoon flour
- 125 grams bacon in shavings
- hare, quartered
- one and a half quarts good red wine
- bouquet of fine herbs
- two dozen mushrooms (champignons), trimmed
- two bay leaves
- salt and pepper
- two dozen small onions the size of walnuts
- lard for reheating the onions
- triangular croutons

## Directions

1. Make a dark roux or brown sauce with 125 grams butter or lard and one tablespoon flour.
2. In this roux, sauté and brown 125 grams bacon shavings.
3. Add the quartered hare pieces to the pan and brown them over high heat to firm the meat.
4. Pour one and a half quarts of good red wine into the pan, ensuring all hare pieces are well covered.
5. Add a bouquet of fine herbs, two dozen trimmed mushrooms, two bay leaves, salt and pepper.
6. Cook over high heat so the sauce reduces violently by at least two-thirds.
7. When the hare is cooked, add two dozen small onions previously sautéed separately in lard.
8. From that point, let the civet rest by the fire without cooking, keeping it very hot for serving.
9. Minutes before serving, add triangular croutons to the dish, well soaked in the sauce, to garnish the platter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cocido (Ordinary Beef Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 750 grams of meat
- 125 grams of bone
- 4 liters of water
- 30 grams of salt
- 150 grams of carrots
- 150 grams of onion
- 200 grams of leeks
- 10 grams of celery
- 1 clove
- 150 grams of turnips

## Directions

1. Fill the stove well with charcoal and light it properly from the start
2. The pot can cook for three hours without needing to touch the stove once the fire is well lit
3. Whenever it is necessary to increase the fire, avoid very rapid boiling; vegetables that cook at a boil are not suitable for the stew, which needs to simmer over low heat
4. Be careful when placing the lid on the pot to leave an opening of two fingers
5. Pierce the clove into an onion and add to the pot along with all other ingredients
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cocido de mi Tierra (Island Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- six liters of water
- two kilos of beef from the leg or breast
- one ring of blood sausage
- three chorizos
- five ounces of pork
- five ounces of chickpeas from Castile or Lanzarote
- three doses of salt
- pumpkin, over one kilo more or less
- chayote
- cabbage
- green beans
- corn
- tender bubangos
- yams
- pears
- five pounds of potatoes
- three pounds of sweet potatoes
- saffron
- garlic
- cloves

## Directions

1. After the sun has traveled a short space, light the black stove with hazel or heather coals
2. Pour six liters of water into the cooking pot
3. Add the beef from the leg or breast, blood sausage ring, chorizos, and pork
4. Add the chickpeas and salt
5. Cook until these parts are cooked, then set them aside
6. As the pot boils vigorously with steam rising inside, add the pumpkin, chayote, cabbage, green beans, corn, and tender bubangos, yams, and pears
7. When the pepper and all these ingredients are seasoned, remove the vegetables
8. Replace them later with potatoes and sweet potatoes
9. Grind saffron, garlic, and cloves together in the mortar with an iron pestle
10. Pour one spoonful of broth into the mortar and dilute the spices
11. Throw the spices directly into the pot
12. As the sun declines, return the vegetables to the fire
13. Unite all the components and cook for a brief time, receiving full heat
14. Place the resulting dish on a wide platter, white as an almond flower
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cocido Extraordinario (Extraordinary Beef Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram 500 grams of meat
- 250 grams of bone
- 8 liters of water
- 60 grams of salt
- 300 grams of carrots
- 300 grams of onions
- 400 grams of leeks
- 25 grams of celery
- 2 cloves
- 300 grams of turnips

## Directions

1. Fill the stove well with charcoal and light it properly from the start
2. The pot can cook for three hours without needing to touch the stove once the fire is well lit
3. Whenever it is necessary to increase the fire, avoid very rapid boiling; vegetables that cook at a boil are not suitable for the stew, which needs to simmer over low heat
4. Be careful when placing the lid on the pot to leave an opening of two fingers
5. Pierce the cloves into onions and add to the pot along with all other ingredients
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Codornices a la Chita Callando (Quail the Quiet Way)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Quail
- Pork lard
- Bay leaf
- Red bell peppers
- Broth
- Sherry wine

## Directions

1. Clean the quail and tie their legs and wings.
2. Season and grease them with pork lard inside, adding a bay leaf.
3. Sauté and brown them in pork lard.
4. Place each quail inside a fat, clean red bell pepper.
5. Give the prepared peppers a turn in butter and, continuously basting them with a little broth and Sherry wine, roast them in the oven on high heat for about a quarter hour.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Codornices a la Villapaterna (Quail Villapaterna Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Quail
- Truffle about the size of a hazelnut
- Butter from Isigny cows with the volume of a plum
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bread crumb
- Fresh bacon leaf
- Water

## Directions

1. Pluck, eviscerate and clean the bird very well.
2. Tie and bridle its legs and small wings and introduce into the body a truffle about the size of a hazelnut, wrapped in good butter from Isigny cows and about the size of a plum.
3. Season inside with salt, pepper to the diner's taste, and close the opening with a bread crumb.
4. Cover the quail completely with a very thin fresh bacon leaf and place the bird, thus prepared, in a baking dish with a hermetically sealed lid.
5. Cook for one hour in a water bath and serve on a fried bread crouton as a lunch dish.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Codornices a la Vitoriana (Quail Vitoria Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Quail
- Butter
- Broth or water
- Flour
- Butter from fresh cows
- White wine
- Water

## Directions

1. Clean the quail well and remove the legs, head and wings.
2. Pierce the thighs with small sticks or reeds from one side to the other.
