3. Place them in a cooking pot with butter and add broth or water.
4. Remove and split them lengthwise down the middle.
5. Remove the small bones and fry again in butter with a little hot broth or water.
6. Make the pastry dough with one and a half pounds of flour, one pound of fresh cow butter and one glass of white wine at the start, the rest water.
7. Work the dough well so it is not hard. After working it well, place the butter in the middle of the dough, extend it a little and then fold the dough over it, doing this three times.
8. Cut one round piece for the base and then one long strip; moisten it slightly with a feather in water and place it around the edge.
9. Cut another round piece for the lid and place it in a well-heated oven with heat above and below, moistening it from time to time with butter on top.
10. As it begins to cook, cut off the lid and remove the middle leaves and let it cook more. When it reaches the right point, remove it.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Codornices Asadas (Roasted Quail)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- quail
- butter from cows
- salt
- bread crumbs
- pepper
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. After cleaning, turn the quail on the roasting spit.
2. Have a pot on the fire with butter from cows, in which you will add salt, some bread crumbs, a pinch of pepper, and one egg yolk for every two quail.
3. With some feathers, brush the quail repeatedly with this mixture until they are well browned.
4. Alternatively, roast them slowly and wrapped in grape leaves, and serve with whatever sauce is most pleasing to the diner.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Codornices con Guisantes (Quail with Peas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- quail
- a slice of veal
- a slice of ham
- carrots
- onions
- aromatic herbs
- bacon strips
- oiled paper
- cooked peas with bacon

## Directions

1. Cook the quail in a pot with a slice of veal and another of ham, carrots, onions, and a bunch of aromatic herbs.
2. Cover immediately with strips of bacon and oiled paper.
3. Place fire above and below.
4. After removing the broth, add cooked peas with bacon.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Codornices Guisadas (Stewed Quail)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- quail
- fresh bacon
- water or broth
- onion
- parsley
- garlic
- spices

## Directions

1. After plucking and cleaning well, melt fresh bacon that is not rancid and fry the quail whole.
2. When browned, remove them and place in a pot with water or broth from the boiled meat.
3. In the same grease, fry onion, parsley, chopped garlic, and spices.
4. Add everything to the pot and cover it with brown paper.
5. Cook at low fire.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Codornices á la Jardinera (Quail Gardener's Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eight quail for four persons
- butter from cows
- water
- four to six types of legumes
- large croutons
- broth or concentrated meat juice

## Directions

1. Prepare, truss, and bridle the quail.
2. Brown them over strong fire in butter from cows.
3. Moisten with a quarter of a measure of water and let them cook at low fire for half an hour.
4. Separately, cook four to six different types of legumes.
5. In a baking dish, arrange the quail on large croutons, forming a star with heads grouped in the center.
6. Fill the spaces between them with cooked legumes, combining types and colors.
7. When the dish is well decorated, pour broth from the pot or concentrated meat juice.
8. Bake in the oven at gentle fire for a quarter of an hour.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Codornices á la Vitoriana (Quail Vitoria Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- quail
- small sticks or canes
- butter
- broth or water

## Directions

1. Clean the quail well and remove the legs, head, and wings.
2. With small sticks or canes, pierce through the thighs from side to side.
3. Place them in a cooking pot with butter and add broth or water.
4. Remove and split them lengthwise down the middle.
5. Remove the gizzards and fry them again.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Col de Pega (Game-Stuffed Cabbage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Lombard or white headed cabbage
- 6 small birds
- oil
- roasted chestnuts
- beef marrow
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Proceed in the same manner as for the previous recipe, but instead of ground meat filling, fill the hollow of the heart with six small birds, well greased inside and outside with oil, and whole roasted chestnuts.
2. For this dish, the sauce from cooking should be very reduced, thick and greasy, which is achieved by using beef marrow instead of cow butter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Col Rellena (Stuffed Cabbage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Lombard or white headed cabbage
- ground sausage meat
- roasted chestnuts, chopped
- fine herbs bouquet
- salt and pepper
- kitchen twine
- bacon slice
- carrots
- onions
- whole cloves
- broth from the pot
- 115 grams fresh cow butter
- 1 tablespoon potato starch

## Directions

1. Scald a Lombard or white headed cabbage in boiling water. Open the leaves slightly without deforming the vegetable and carefully remove the heart.
2. Prepare a filling of ground sausage meat with an equal quantity of roasted and chopped chestnuts, a bouquet of fine herbs, and good seasoning of salt and pepper. Place this filling in the hollow left by the heart.
3. Tie the cabbage with kitchen twine and cover the filling with a slice of bacon.
4. Place the cabbage in a braising pot or casserole in which there is a good wide slice of bacon. Around it place carrots and onions studded with whole cloves.
5. Cover completely with broth from the pot and cook over gentle heat. Place the cabbage on a serving platter.
6. Strain the sauce and reduce it by half, adding 115 grams of fresh cow butter and 1 tablespoon of potato starch.
7. Once the sauce is bound, pour it over the cabbage.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Coles de Bruselas (Brussels Sprouts)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- salt
- cow butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the Brussels sprouts. Submerge them in plenty of boiling water at a rolling boil, with an excess of salt.
2. After five minutes, remove them and pass them through two or three cold waters.
3. Drain and dry them over a lively fire in a casserole with cow butter, salt and pepper.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Coliflor en Salsa Blanca (Cauliflower in White Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cauliflower
- salt
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Boil salted water in a large pot, and when it boils vigorously, add the cauliflower well washed in several waters and divided into florets that form the head.
2. Cook for a quarter hour. Remove and let drain. To prevent it from cooling, place it in a casserole without covering it, near the fire.
3. Prepare the white sauce and bind it in a small casserole. Take the cooked cauliflower pieces one by one and arrange them in an enameled earthenware soup tureen with the florets facing down and tightly packed.
4. Take a silver or white metal platter of equal size but not as deep, which is to be used for serving the dish, place it as a cover over the tureen and turn it over so that the cauliflower appears whole on the serving platter.
5. Pour the sauce over the top.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Como se Asan los Pajaritos (How to Roast Small Birds)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- young birds such as onceyos, swifts, thrushes, turtle doves and other tender small birds
- thin roasting spit or iron or cane skewers accommodating six birds each
- slices of fat bacon
- grated bread
- salt
- lemon wheels
- sugar
- lemon juice
- cinnamon
- pepper
- broth

## Directions

1. Thread the birds on a thin roasting spit, pierced through, or place them on iron or cane skewers, six birds to each skewer.
2. Tie the skewers to the spit.
3. Place a slice of fat bacon between each bird.
4. Put to roast.
5. When roasted, sprinkle grated bread and salt over them so they are well covered.
6. Serve with lemon wheels.
7. Prepare a sweet-and-sour sauce with a little sugar, lemon juice, a little cinnamon, a pinch of pepper, salt, and a little broth.
8. This sauce is very good for all roasted meats, for roasted or fried chicken livers, for veal and bird cutlets, and particularly for the sick, as most of them enjoy this sauce because everything sweet-and-sour is of good taste.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Compota de Camuesas (Camuesa Apple Compote)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- camuesa apples
- 8 deciliters syrup at 16 degrees
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Cut camuesas in half, removing the cores and seeds.
2. Peel them, taking care that they remain of equal size, and place 8 deciliters of syrup at 16 degrees in a saucepan with a capacity of 2 liters, adding the juice of half a lemon to cook all this slowly.
3. When the camuesas are cooked, drain them in a strainer, and then strain the syrup, which is reduced to 32 degrees.
4. Once the camuesas are completely cold, arrange them in the compote dish, one in the middle and the rest around it, and sprinkle with the syrup.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Compota de Castañas á la Vainilla (Chestnut Compote with Vanilla)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- four dozen beautiful chestnuts
- water
- sugar syrup at 16 degrees
- one-quarter of a vanilla pod

## Directions

1. Remove the shells from four dozen beautiful chestnuts and place them in fresh water
2. Place them on the fire in a pot with capacity of two liters with one and a half liters of water, and blanch them; that is, remove the inner skin or peel, and place them in a pan, avoiding that some rest on top of others
3. Cover with syrup at 16 degrees, and then make the mixture tremble over the fire, just barely, for just under half an hour
4. When the chestnuts are cooked, drain them in a sieve
5. Strain the syrup through a chinois strainer and reduce it to 30 degrees
6. Arrange the chestnuts in the compote dish and water them with the syrup
7. To the chestnut compote, made as just described, add one-quarter of a vanilla pod
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Compota de Ciruelas Pasas (Prune Compote)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams dried plums
- water
- 5 grams cinnamon stick

## Directions

1. Weigh 500 grams of dried plums; wash them and place them in a saucepan with a capacity of 1 liter, which is then filled with water.
2. Cook over low heat and add 5 grams of cinnamon stick.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Compota de Ciruelas Pasas (Stewed Prune Compote)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams of prunes
- water to fill a one-liter pot
- 5 grams of cinnamon stick
- 60 grams of sugar

## Directions

1. Weigh 500 grams of prunes; wash them and place them in a pot with capacity of one liter, which is then filled with water
2. Cook over low heat and add 5 grams of cinnamon stick
3. When the prunes are cooked, that is, when they are soft and spongy but without opening, drain them in a sieve; strain the broth through a chinois strainer and remove the cinnamon; then add 60 grams of sugar and reduce the broth to 32 degrees
4. Place the prunes in the compote dish
5. Pour the reduced broth over them when it is completely cold
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Compota de Peras de Invierno (Winter Pear Compote)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 5, 7, or 9 beautiful winter pears of equal size
- 2 liters syrup at 16 degrees
- 1 teaspoon vegetable carmine
- 1/4 vanilla stick

## Directions

1. Select 5, 7, or 9 beautiful winter pears of equal size, as much as possible, and peel them, taking care that they remain very smooth and avoiding letting the knife leave marks.
2. Place them in a saucepan with a capacity of 2 liters and cover them with syrup at 16 degrees, to which add 1 teaspoon of vegetable carmine and 1/4 of a vanilla stick.
3. Boil very gently until they are completely cooked, which is tested by seeing if the pears yield to finger pressure, and let the pears cool in the syrup.
4. Drain them and reduce the syrup over high heat until it reaches 32 degrees.
5. Place one pear in the middle of the compote dish and the other 4, 6, or 8 around it, and pour the syrup, which should be cold, over them.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Compota de Pichones (Squab Compote)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams of bacon scraps
- butter
- four squabs
- mushrooms
- bouquet of fresh herbs
- salt
- pepper
- two ladles of broth
- blond-white sauce
- cooking juice from the squabs

## Directions

1. Sauté the bacon scraps in butter, then quickly brown and sear the four squabs in the same pan, turning frequently to color evenly
2. Add sliced mushrooms, a bouquet of fresh herbs, salt, pepper, and two ladles of broth
3. Cook over low heat until the squabs are well done
4. Remove the squabs from the pan and place on a preheated serving dish
5. Arrange the mushrooms and bacon scraps around them
6. Prepare a blond-white sauce and thin it with the cooking juices from the squabs
7. Pour the sauce over the birds and serve immediately
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejo a la Marengo (Rabbit Marengo-Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- garlic

## Directions

1. Clean the rabbit and cut into pieces.
2. Place the pieces in a pot with olive oil, salt, pepper, and garlic.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejo a la Marinera (Rabbit Sailor-Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- 125 grams beef or pork lard
- 125 grams bacon, cut into small squares
- 1 heaping tablespoon flour
- 2 ladles of broth
- 2 ladles of red wine
- 1 good river eel
- bouquet of fine herbs
- 2 dozen small onions
- pork lard for the onions
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare the rabbit as in a gibelota, but using red wine instead of white wine.
2. When seasoning, add a good river eel cut into pieces the same size as the rabbit pieces.
3. Cook everything together and serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejo a las Finas Hierbas (Rabbit with Fine Herbs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- beef lard
- mushrooms
- parsley
- bay leaf
- 1 tablespoon flour
- wine and broth in equal parts
- salt
- pepper
- rabbit liver

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit meat into pieces and brown in beef lard with mushrooms, parsley, and chopped bay leaf.
2. Add a tablespoon of flour, wine and broth in equal measure.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Cook the liver separately and when serving, add it to the sauce and mix together.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejo Asado (Roast Rabbit)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole rabbit
- butter from cows or pigs
- bacon strips
- oil or fat for basting
- salt

## Directions

1. First thing to do is sauté the whole rabbit in cow or pork butter and let it cool
2. Then lard it easily with thin strips of bacon, the same way as done with hare
3. If the rabbit is very tender, it will be good to wrap it in a sheet of greased paper so it cooks without drying out
4. Remove the paper halfway through cooking so it browns, moistening it frequently with its own juices
5. Serve the roast rabbit alone on a platter, and apart in a sauce boat, its own juice, reinforced with a spicy sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejo en Cajetines (Rabbit in Paper Cases)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- young rabbit, cut into pieces
- butter
- fine herbs
- liver of the rabbit
- egg yolks
- bacon slices
- butter for spreading
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the young rabbit into pieces and sauté them in butter.
2. Place them in paper cases with a filling composed of fine herbs, the liver of the same rabbit, and two egg yolks to give it consistency.
3. Cover the intervals of the case with this filling and cover everything with slices of bacon and paper spread with butter.
4. Place in the oven in this manner, and before serving remove the fat and add an Italian sauce.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejo Escabechado (Pickled Rabbit)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- bacon and raw ham for larding
- olive oil
- thyme
- bay leaf
- parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Remove the bones and lard the rabbit with bacon and raw ham.
2. Close and tie with twine.
3. Put in a pot with olive oil, thyme, and bay leaf, and brown until it no longer releases liquid.
4. Remove, drain, and cut into pieces that fit into jars with wide mouths.
5. When no more pieces fit in a jar, fill with olive oil and cover with moistened parchment.
6. This way it can be preserved for a long time.
7. To serve, slice the pieces into very thin rounds and arrange in a serving dish decorated with chopped parsley and oil.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejo Esparrillado (Grilled Rabbit)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- lard
- fine herbs or anchovy butter

## Directions

1. Clean and open the rabbit lengthwise.
2. Flatten it with a mallet.
3. Place on the grill, wrapped in a sheet of paper spread with lard.
4. When cooked through, remove the paper to serve.
5. Mix with fine herbs or anchovy butter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejo Frito (Fried Rabbit)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- white wine
- lemon juice
- thyme
- bay leaf
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- picante sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit into pieces and place in a marinade of white wine.
2. Add to the marinade the juice of a lemon, thyme, bay leaf, garlic cut into small pieces, salt, and pepper.
3. After two hours, drain and coat in flour mixed with water.
4. Fry the rabbit.
5. Serve with a picante sauce.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejo Guisado a la Inglesa (Rabbit Stewed English-Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- bread crumbs moistened in milk
- parsley
- sage
- pepper
- chopped bacon
- beef marrow, salted
- bacon leaves
- white wine
- onion or lentil sauce or puree

## Directions

1. Clean the rabbit well.
2. Stuff it with bread crumbs moistened in milk, parsley, sage, pepper, chopped bacon, and salted beef marrow.
3. Sew and close the rabbit.
4. Cook in a fairly large pot on a bed of bacon leaves and white wine.
5. To serve, add an onion or lentil sauce or puree.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejo Guisado á la Campesina (Rabbit Stewed Country-Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit, skinned and cleaned
- raw oil
- parsley
- garlic
- pepper
- hot water
- bay leaves
- cloves
- cinnamon
- capers or lime slices (optional)

## Directions

1. Skin and clean the rabbit without wetting it
2. Cut into pieces and place in a pot or casserole with raw oil, parsley, garlic and pepper to sauté over low heat
3. Add hot water and when half-cooked, add bay leaves, cloves and a pinch of cinnamon
4. It will be very good with some capers or lime slices
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejo Salteado según Gouffé (Rabbit Sautéed, Gouffé Method)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- 30 grams butter
- 3 tablespoons oil
- 1 pinch nutmeg
- 2 pinches mixed spices
- 2 pinches salt
- 2 pinches pepper
- 25 grams flour
- 2 deciliters white wine
- 1 deciliter broth
- 20 grams small onions, chopped
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the rabbit and cut it into pieces.
2. Place in a flat sauté pan: 30 grams butter, 3 tablespoons oil, 1 pinch nutmeg, 2 pinches of mixed spices, 2 pinches salt, and 2 pinches pepper.
3. When the butter is melted, place the rabbit pieces in the sauté pan over high heat for twenty minutes.
4. After this time, remove the rabbit and place on a serving dish.
5. In the same pan add 25 grams of flour, stirring circularly over the fire for one minute.
6. Add two deciliters of white wine and one deciliter of broth.
7. Bring to a boil for five minutes, then strain the sauce through a chinois strainer.
8. Clean the sauté pan and return the sauce and rabbit to it.
9. Add 20 grams of chopped small onions, well washed, and 1 tablespoon of chopped parsley.
10. Bring to a boil and serve at the first boil in the sauce.
11. Do not cook further as boiling hardens them after the first boil.
12. If necessary, the rabbit can be sautéed in an ordinary pan over the flame, proceeding the same way but taking care to constantly sauté the rabbit so it cooks evenly.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejo á la Marengo (Rabbit Marengo)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit, cleaned and cut into pieces
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- bay leaves
- nutmeg powder
- mushrooms
- parsley
- butter
- flour
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. After cleaning, cut the rabbit into pieces and place them in a pot with oil, salt, pepper, garlic, some bay leaves, and powdered nutmeg.
2. Cook with heat below and above for a quarter of an hour.
3. Remove half of the oil and add mushrooms and chopped parsley, removing the bay leaves and garlic.
4. Thicken the sauce with fried butter and flour, add the juice of a lemon, and serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Conejos Guisados Fríos (Cold Braised Rabbits)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted rabbits
- salt
- pepper
- salad greens
- lemon slices
- oil
- vinegar
- candied fruit strips
- pomegranate seeds
- confits
- lettuce hearts
- olives (pitted)

## Directions

1. Roast the rabbits
2. Mince all the meat from the loins and legs, leaving only the heads
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Make a salad of all herbs
5. Compose the dish of your minced rabbit and salad, making a ring on the plate, placing small piles of meat alternating with salad
6. In the middle place some well-prepared salad with all the things usually added, with lemon slices, and season with oil and vinegar
7. Decorate the outside with some strips of candied fruit, pomegranate seeds, confits and all the things that are usually put on salads
8. In the middle place some whole lettuce hearts standing upright, and over the minced rabbit or salad place pitted olives
9. This dish is good for afternoon meals, because it should be eaten cold
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Congrio (Conger Eel)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kilogram piece of conger eel
- 200 grams onion cut into thin slices or strips of half a centimeter
- 20 grams parsley sprigs
- 2 bay leaves
- 2 deciliters vinegar
- 1 garlic clove
- 30 grams salt
- 20 grams peppercorns
- water

## Directions

1. Purchase a piece of conger eel of one kilogram; if the piece contains intestines, be careful to remove them
2. Wash and tie it as you would meat for a stew, then blanch for a quarter hour in boiling water
3. Remove from water, drain and place in a pot with a capacity of four liters, which will be filled with water in such a way that it well covers the fish
4. Add the onion cut into thin slices or strips of half a centimeter, parsley sprigs, 2 bay leaves, 2 deciliters of vinegar, 1 garlic clove, 30 grams of salt, 20 grams of peppercorns
5. Cook very gently for half an hour, drain and serve on a platter over a napkin, garnishing it with parsley
6. Serve separately in a sauce boat either white sauce or hollandaise sauce, or better yet melted butter
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cooked Artichokes (Alcachofas Cocidas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut off the tips and trim the leaves of the artichokes
2. Boil in plenty of water with plenty of salt, as with all green vegetables
3. When the liquid boils vigorously, cook until pulling a leaf from an artichoke causes it to separate immediately
4. Place in a colander on a plate
5. When they can be squeezed slightly with the fingers without burning, drain them well so they release the water
6. If they are to be eaten only cooked, place them in the oven with the door open or on the edge of the stove to dry slightly and not cool
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cordero Aprovechado (Leftover Lamb)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams beef butter
- pieces of roasted lamb
- 1 liter water or broth from the pot
- lamb head, well cooked
- lamb heart
- lamb liver
- lamb offal
- lamb feet and hands
- salt
- pepper
- bundle of fine herbs
- two onions
- one carrot

## Directions

1. Brown the 125 grams of beef butter to a deep color
2. Add pieces of roasted lamb, water or broth from the pot, the well-cooked lamb head, heart, liver, offal, feet and hands
3. Season with salt, pepper, a bundle of fine herbs, two onions and a carrot cut into strips
4. Cook for two hours
5. Serve with the head in the center, surrounded by all the offal
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cordero Asado (Roast Lamb)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Whole lamb or half lamb or quarter lamb
- Thin sheets of bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- White oiled paper

## Directions

1. Cut off the head and debone the neck up to the shoulder blades.
2. Keep the two halves of the front quarters open and spread apart by means of strong small reeds cut to measure and used as levers.
3. Break in half the bones of the two hind legs in order to cross the legs, mounting one on top of the other.
4. Secure the lamb to the roasting spit so that it cannot move or deform.
5. Cover or line it entirely with thin sheets of bacon and wrap it afterwards in white oiled paper, which is tied.
6. Carry it to a very strong fire and roast it for two hours.
7. Remove the paper and remove also the bacon sheets that have not melted or been consumed.
8. Rekindle the fire to give color to the meat on all sides.
9. Season with salt and pepper, sprinkling well.
10. Serve when the skin is very crispy.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Costillar de Cerdo (Pork Ribs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- ribs of roasted pork
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. When many ribs of roasted pork are cooked in one piece and some are left over, cut them when cold
2. Soak them in oil
3. Dust with salt and pepper
4. Bread them with breadcrumbs
5. Turn them on the grill over very gentle heat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Crema al Natural (Plain Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh cream
- Sugar molded, one quarter of the weight of the cream

## Directions

1. Mix fresh cream with the fourth part of its weight of molded sugar
2. Serve as is without further preparation in a porcelain compote dish, or better and more elegantly, in a shallow bowl that fits well in a silver cup or other metal vessel
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Crema Borracha (Drunken Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter litre of good white wine
- sugar
- lemon zest
- cinnamon
- seven or eight eggs, using the yolks

## Directions

1. Pour one quarter litre of good white wine, sugar, lemon zest and cinnamon into a saucepan, and bring to a boil.
2. Break seven or eight eggs to use the yolks, which are beaten with a spatula until well combined, also binding them with the wine, which is poured in little by little.
3. Then pass through a sieve and pour into the cups so that it curdles in a water bath.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Crema de Café (Coffee Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Coffee, infused for three or four hours
- One liter of good cream
- Two egg whites
- 60 grams of molded pilón sugar
- 15 grams of pulverized gum arabic

## Directions

1. Place coffee in infusion for three or four hours in one liter of good cream after having roasted it without allowing it to become black
2. Pass the cream through a strainer to separate the coffee beans
3. Add two egg whites, 60 grams of molded pilón sugar, and 15 grams of pulverized gum arabic
4. Beat everything a great deal to the point of meringue
5. Remove the foam as it forms and continue until the liquid part has concentrated
6. Serve like the whipped cream in a round nickel dish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Crema de Café (Coffee Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter litre of water
- two ounces of coffee
- equal quantity of milk
- sugar
- a little flour powder
- five egg yolks

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, bring one quarter litre of water to a boil; when it boils vigorously, add two ounces of coffee and stir well with a spoon, putting it back over the fire until it increases in volume four or five times over the vessel.
2. Let it rest to settle clearly, without straining and mixing it with an equal quantity of milk and sugar, and cook everything again so that it reduces a little.
3. In a pan, dissolve very well with a little flour powder five egg yolks, which are stretched with the milk and coffee, until the mixture is perfect.
4. Then pass this through a sieve.
5. In a deep serving dish or in caramelized molds on the inside, set the cream to curdle in a water bath.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Crema de Chantilly (Chantilly Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- four quartillos of good fresh cream
- one spoonful of gum tragacanth powder
- powdered sugar
- two small glasses of maraschino, rum, or another good liqueur

## Directions

1. Pour four quartillos of good fresh cream into a porcelain bowl with one spoonful of gum tragacanth powder.
2. Place the bowl over crushed ice and beat the cream well with a wire whisk.
3. As thick, compact foam forms, remove it with a skimmer and place it in a sieve to drain, continuing this same operation until no more foam remains.
4. After it has drained well, place it in another clean bowl.
5. Add the powdered sugar as needed.
6. Before serving at the table, add two small glasses of maraschino, rum, or any other good liqueur.
7. This cream is placed on a serving dish, over a fine pastry tart or in a timbal formed with sponge cakes.
8. It can also be flavored with vanilla by grinding it with piloncillo sugar; with chocolate by dissolving one ounce in half a glass of boiling water; with orange or lemon by scraping the zests.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Crema de Chocolate (Chocolate Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of chocolate
- two ounces of ground sugar
- one quarter litre of milk
- a little cream
- five egg yolks

## Directions

1. Pound two ounces of chocolate into powder and place in a saucepan with two ounces of ground sugar, one quarter litre of milk and a little cream.
2. Let it cook until reduced by a third, and when it is already cold or almost cold, incorporate, as in the previous cream, the beaten mixture of five egg yolks, and finish the operation in the same way.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Crema de Plombieres (Plombières Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eight egg yolks
- one tablespoon of rice flour cream
- three glasses of very hot milk
- necessary sugar
- one grain of salt
- two glasses of Kümmel, or vanilla, lemon, etc. for flavoring

## Directions

1. Mix well in a saucepan eight egg yolks and one tablespoon of rice flour cream, three glasses of very hot milk, and place over the fire, stirring well with a wooden spoon.
2. As soon as it begins to thicken, remove from the fire without ceasing to stir, and heat again until it cooks for a few minutes.
3. Add the necessary sugar and one grain of salt, and when it is cold, pour it into another saucepan and place over ice, stirring from time to time with the spoon.
4. As soon as it takes consistency with the cold, add two glasses of Kümmel, or flavor beforehand with vanilla, lemon, etc.
5. Place in a serving dish, or in small cups, or over sponge cakes.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Crema Instantánea (Instant Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Six egg yolks
- One quarter pound of sugar
- Three glasses of rum
- Double the amount of egg whites beaten to meringue point

## Directions

1. Beat the six egg yolks with the sugar and rum
2. Mix with the egg whites beaten to meringue point
3. Thicken slightly over heat
4. Remove from heat and beat again
5. Pour into a punch bowl to serve cold in cups with fine or coarse crackers
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Crema Provenzal (Provençal Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- White sauce base
- garlic
- small fragments of mushrooms
- fine recooked oil

## Directions

1. Rub the interior of a sauce boat with garlic
2. In the sauce, place very small fragments of mushrooms instead of capers and fine recooked oil instead of lard
3. This will yield the famous Provençal cream, very tasty for eating boiled cod after a good soaking
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Crema Sambaglione (Italian Foam)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve fresh egg yolks
- four glasses of Marsala or Sherry wine
- six ounces of sugar
- one handful of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take twelve fresh egg yolks and four glasses of Marsala or Sherry wine, six ounces of sugar and one handful of cinnamon; place everything in a saucepan over a lively fire, stir quickly with a chocolate whisk until the foam has filled the saucepan; serve immediately in cream cups.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Crema Zurrada (Whipped Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One liter of cream
- 125 grams of impalpable powdered sugar
- Five grams of gum of tragacanth, also in powder
- Thirty grams of orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Put all these ingredients in an earthenware bowl
2. Beat everything strongly and for a long time with a small bundle of wicker rods until the mixture becomes entirely the same foam
3. Let it rest afterward for five or six minutes without touching it
4. Place in a pyramid on a special shallow dish, somewhat fluted and brilliant, so that the delicacy reflects on the edges like in a mirror
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Crestas y ríñones de gallo (Rooster Crests and Kidneys)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rooster crests
- rooster kidneys (double the number of crests)
- salt
- pepper
- herb bouquet
- flour
- white-blonde sauce
- broth
- gravy from roasted bird
- 60 grams beef butter
- flour
- fine herbs
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Soak the rooster crests and kidneys in water and wash in several cold waters
2. Cook them with salt, pepper and an herb bouquet in water blanched with a spoonful of flour
3. Prepare a white-blonde sauce separately, lengthen it with broth, adding a ladle of gravy from roasted bird
4. Incorporate the crests and kidneys and let them cook in the sauce very slowly for about 15 minutes
5. At the moment of serving, melt 60 grams of beef butter kneaded with a little flour and finely chopped herbs into the sauce
6. Add all the juice of one lemon
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Criadillas de Cordero Empanadas (Breaded Lamb Testicles)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb testicles
- Egg, beaten
- Salt and pepper
- Breadcrumbs
- Pork lard for frying
- Lemon quarters
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Blanch the testicles with their membranes in boiling water until they whiten
2. Immediately submerge them in very cold water
3. Remove and cut into pieces like orange segments
4. Wrap them in beaten egg seasoned with salt and pepper
5. Coat with breadcrumbs, then dip again in egg and coat once more with breadcrumbs
6. Fry in pork lard
7. Serve in a pyramid on a well-pressed napkin on a platter with croutons and lemon quarters for each guest to squeeze juice as they eat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Criadillas Fritas (Fried Testicles)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb testicles
- egg
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs
- pork lard
- lemon
- croutons

## Directions

1. Clean the testicles well and remove filaments and bloody threads
2. Cut in half, blanch in boiling water until white, then immediately submerge in very cold water
3. Remove and cut into pieces like orange sections
4. Dip in beaten egg seasoned with salt and pepper, coat with breadcrumbs, dip again in egg
5. Fry in pork lard
6. Serve in a pyramid on a well-pressed napkin on a platter with croutons and lemon quarters for guests to squeeze juice over them as they eat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Croquetas (Croquettes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover meat from white meats such as veal and poultry
- béchamel sauce made with broth, water, or milk
- toasted breadcrumbs
- beaten egg
- pork fat for frying

## Directions

1. Mince the meat very finely but without making it pasty.
2. Mix with béchamel sauce made with broth, water, or milk so that the mixture is thick and well bound.
3. Empty the contents of the pan onto a cutting board and let cool in a cool place for two or three hours, cooling and drying.
4. Form the mixture into a square cake about an inch and a half high on the cutting board.
5. With a large knife, score it equally into a grid pattern.
6. Each small square is the measure of one croquette.
7. In a bowl almost full of toasted breadcrumb grating, take a portion of cake with a spoon and toss it until you can pick it up with your hand and mold it into a cylinder.
8. Soak the croquette in beaten egg and in another bowl coat it with new breadcrumb grating.
9. When all croquettes are made and lined up on the cutting board, fry them in hot pork fat at the moment of serving.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cuajar de Cerdo (Pork Chitterlings)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- pork chitterlings
- pork butter

## Directions

1. Wash and rewash the chitterlings
2. Let them sit overnight in brine and in the open air
3. Drain and cut into strips like macaroni
4. Sauté and fry in good pork butter until well browned and crispy
5. Serve with a side dish of chard sautéed in oil
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Cuarto de Corzo a la Inglesa (Roe Deer Quarter English Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One quarter of roe deer cut to the beginning of the ribs
- Ground salt
- Six eggs
- Water
- Flour, two to three pounds depending on the volume of meat
- Ten to fifteen grams of salt
- Cow or pig lard for greasing
- Bread sauce or currant jelly for serving

## Directions

1. Cut a quarter of roe deer to the beginning of the ribs, taking half of the middle section
2. Beat to tenderize
3. Dust with ground salt all over and leave for a couple of days
4. Prepare a firm dough by beating six eggs with a little water
5. Add flour, varying in quantity from two to three pounds according to the volume of the meat
6. Add ten to fifteen grams of salt
7. Knead the dough very well and let it rest for one hour, wrapped in a slightly damp napkin
8. Extend the dough with a rolling pin on the work table and form a sheet the thickness of a coin
9. Completely wrap the quarter of roe deer in this dough, sealing the edges of the dough by moistening them slightly
10. Wrap it very well in a sheet of thick paper greased with cow lard and roast on a spit with gentle fire
11. After three hours of cooking, remove the paper so the dough crust takes color
12. Serve on a platter in the dry, with the dish resting on a well-pressed napkin
13. Serve separately in a sauce boat the famous bread sauce or currant jelly
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Dulce de Crema de Boniato (Sweet Potato Cream Confection)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of sweet potato
- one pound of untoasted almonds
- six egg yolks
- one pound of superior sugar
- half a cuartillo of water
- zest of one lemon

## Directions

1. Place half a cuartillo of water, the sugar, and the sweet potato at half-cooked; add the almonds, let the syrup cool, dissolve the six egg yolks and combine everything, stirring it without ceasing until it becomes a thick cream
2. Before putting the water, sugar, and sweet potato on the fire, add with the sweet potato, or with the sugar, or with the water (it makes no difference), the zest of one lemon
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Dulce de Crema de Boniato (Sweet Potato Cream Dessert)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of sweet potato
- One pound of almonds, untoasted
- Six egg yolks
- One pound of superior sugar
- Half a quarter-liter of water
- Zest of one lemon

## Directions

1. Put half a quarter-liter of water, the sugar, and the sweet potato on to cook until half-done
2. Add the almonds and let the syrup cool
3. Dissolve the six egg yolks and combine everything, stirring constantly until it becomes a thick cream
4. Before putting the water, sugar, and sweet potato on the fire, add the zest of one lemon with the sweet potato, sugar, or water (it makes no difference)
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Eggs of Any Desired Size (Huevos del Tamaño que se Quiera)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Multiple eggs, quantity depending on desired size
- A clean bladder

## Directions

1. Break as many eggs as desired, more or less depending on the size desired for the final egg.
2. Separate the yolks from the whites when breaking them.
3. Take a very clean bladder whose capacity can hold all the yolks and fill it with them after they have been well beaten.
4. Tie the bladder very well to prevent air from entering and suspend it in boiling water until the yolks have taken on a suitable consistency.
5. Untie the string of the bladder and remove a giant yolk.
6. Then take a larger bladder, place the beaten egg whites in it, place the yolk in the middle, and tie it again.
7. Expose everything to the heat of boiling water until the enormous white has hardened enough that the final bladder can be broken and release the enormously large egg.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Eggs with Ham and Parsley (Huevos con Jamón)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve eggs
- Very fine slices of raw ham
- Pork lard
- Salt
- Pepper
- Dried parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites separately and keep the yolks, two by two, in small bowls.
2. In a round baking dish or pan at least twenty centimeters in diameter, line the bottom completely with very fine slices of raw ham.
3. Add pork lard on top and place the pan on strong heat over a griddle, preferably not over a small stove.
4. When the fat begins to sizzle, pour all the beaten egg whites over it, and as soon as they set, add the yolks one by one.
5. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and finely chopped dried parsley.
6. Serve as an appetizer course.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Eggs à la Florentine (Huevos a la Florentina)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- A small amount of pork lard
- Water
- Salt and seasoning
- Plenty of finely cut parsley
- Eggs
- Tender croutons

## Directions

1. In a dish, platter, or pan that can go to the fire, melt a small amount of pork lard.
2. Before it turns russet, add water, seasoning, and plenty of finely cut parsley.
3. Off the heat, carefully add the eggs one by one.
4. Over low heat, poach them.
5. Serve immediately with very tender croutons.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Eggs à la Russe (Huevos a la Rusa)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Pork lard
- Milk
- Flour
- Butter from cow's milk
- Eggs and breadcrumbs for breading
- Oil for frying

## Directions

1. Fry the eggs in pork lard that is not boiling and let them cool on a board.
2. Separately, make béchamel (milk, flour, and butter from cow's milk).
3. Once prepared, place it with a spoon over the eggs, waiting for this paste to cool.
4. Then coat everything with eggs and breadcrumbs, frying it in very hot oil.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Eggs à la Saint-Roch (Huevos a la San Boque)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two eggs per person
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter from cow's milk, about the size of a walnut

## Directions

1. In small specially-made bowls or dishes of metal or porcelain, like those used for eggs en cocotte, place two eggs in each.
2. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, parsley, and breadcrumbs to cover the surface.
3. Add a piece of butter from cow's milk about the size of a walnut.
4. Cover the bowls and cook the eggs with heat on top to the desired degree of doneness.
5. Serve and eat the eggs in the same bowls.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Emparedados de Queso (Cheese Sandwiches)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- fresh Burgos cheese
- dry sherry wine
- eggs
- milk
- sugar
- lemon zest
- fine clarified oil

## Directions

1. Prepare sandwiches by spreading the bread with a thin layer of good fresh Burgos cheese
2. Lightly moisten with dry sherry
3. Coat in egg beaten with milk, sugar, and grated lemon zest
4. Fry until golden in fine clarified oil
5. Dust while hot with powdered sugar
6. Serve these sandwiches cold
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ensalada Conferencia (Conference Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- one kilogram potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- clove
- nutmeg
- parsley, finely chopped
- tarragon, finely chopped
- onion, finely chopped
- one tablespoon good vinegar
- six tablespoons oil
- six lettuce heads for the leaves

## Directions

1. Hard boil six eggs and steam one kilogram of potatoes
2. Two hours before eating, mash the eggs in a large salad bowl with salt, pepper, mustard, clove, nutmeg with parsley, tarragon and onion in sufficient quantity, mixing with one tablespoon of good vinegar
3. Add six tablespoons of oil, and in this thick sauce add the potatoes and mix, stirring for a long time
4. Cover everything with the leaves of six lettuce heads, well washed and dried
5. At the moment of eating, add vinegar that should be strong and in quantity half the amount of oil
6. Shake and mix well, stir vigorously and eat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ensalada de Apio (Celery Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- mustard

## Directions

1. Choose the most tender, wash and remove the strings; cut like the other salads, and dress with oil, vinegar, salt, pepper and mustard
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ensalada de Apio (Celery Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- tender celery
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- mustard

## Directions

1. Choose the most tender celery
2. Wash and remove the strings
3. Cut as with other salads
4. Dress with oil, vinegar, salt, pepper and mustard
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ensalada de Escarola (Endive Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- endive
- tomato
- oil
- cumin
- salt
- vinegar
- water
- garlic

## Directions

1. Chop the tender and white part, mash tomato on a plate with oil, cumin, salt, vinegar and water or garlic if desired; mix everything with the endive, and it can also be dressed only with oil and vinegar and a hint of garlic
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ensalada de Escarola (Endive Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- tender and white part of endive
- tomato
- oil
- cumin
- salt
- vinegar
- water or garlic to taste

## Directions

1. Chop the tender and white part of the endive
2. Mash tomato in a plate with oil, cumin, salt, vinegar and water or garlic if desired
3. Mix everything with the endive
4. Alternatively, dress the endive with only oil, vinegar and a hint of garlic
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ensalada de Lechuga (Lettuce Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- small onion
- mint
- oil
- salt
- vinegar
- hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Well washed and the white part chopped more or less finely to each one's taste, chop small onion and green mint; mix everything and dress with oil, salt, vinegar, garnishing with hard boiled eggs in slices
2. Whole hearts can also be presented at the table, and each person can chop them on their plate and dress them to their taste
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ensalada de Pimientos (Pepper Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- green tender peppers
- tomato
- oil
- salt
- vinegar
- garlic
- cumin
- mint

## Directions

1. Choose the most tender peppers; peel and cut into thin round slices
2. Mash well-peeled tomato with the seeds removed, add oil, vinegar, salt, cumin and a pinch of dried mint or chopped green mint if available, mix everything well, add the necessary water, and they can be served
3. This formula is from La Rioja
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ensalada de Pollo (Chicken Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- beef
- salt
- pepper
- oil
- vinegar
- hard boiled eggs
- lettuce

## Directions

1. Shred a chicken to convert all its meat into threads and scraps
2. With a quarter part of cooked beef make a minced mixture and prepare a thick sauce with it, seasoned extensively, oil and vinegar
3. Incorporate the chicken scraps gradually, stirring little by little each time they are added, while drizzling oil over them
4. When everything is finished, stir more and complete by adding more vinegar
5. Serve in the same salad bowl, garnishing with hard boiled eggs and lettuce leaves and hearts
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ensalada Francillon (Francillon Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- refined oil
- aromatic vinegar from Orleans
- two glasses of Bordeaux white wine, Iquem brand
- parsley
- chervil
- aromatic herbs
- sea dates (dátiles de mar)
- celery stalk
- truffles
- Champagne wine

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes in broth and slice them
2. While still warm, season with salt and pepper, refined oil and aromatic vinegar from Orleans
3. Add two glasses of Bordeaux white wine, Iquem brand, and finely chopped parsley, chervil and aromatic herbs
4. Cook sea dates in broth with a celery stalk, cleaned well of filaments
5. Mix everything together, first removing the shellfish from their shells, combining with the seasoned potatoes, stirring slowly with two spatulas
6. The proportion of sea dates to potatoes should be one third less, so that the shellfish flavor is barely perceptible
7. Once the salad is well mixed and tossed, cover the domed surface with slices of truffles cooked in Champagne wine
8. Make this salad two hours before serving so it is completely cold
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ensalada Marina (Seafood Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- endive, lettuce or other greens, well washed and dried
- three tablespoons fine oil
- one tablespoon strong vinegar
- one dozen salted anchovies in brine from glass jars

## Directions

1. One hour before eating, toss the greens with three tablespoons of fine oil
2. At the moment of serving, incorporate one tablespoon of strong vinegar and one dozen salted anchovies in brine
3. Mix everything well and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ensalada María Tubau (María Tubau Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- eggs
- parsley
- small onion
- garlic clove
- mustard
- vinegar
- fried oil
- salt
- lettuce
- celery or watercress
- asparagus tips
- artichoke hearts
- mushrooms
- truffles
- beetroot

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes unpeeled and cook eggs in the same pot
2. In a bowl, mash six hard boiled eggs, incorporating finely chopped parsley, a little small onion, a garlic clove, seasoning to taste, a touch of mustard and all the vinegar the salad dressing will need
3. Once cooked and peeled, slice the potatoes and place them at the bottom of a salad bowl
4. Pour fried oil that has been prepared in advance and cooled, sprinkling with salt, making sure the potatoes are well soaked in the liquid
5. On top, place well-cleaned, well-washed and well-dried lettuce leaves, mixed with celery or watercress, and cover everything with desired garnish
6. The salad may include a variety of vegetables to enhance its appearance: asparagus tips, artichoke hearts, mushrooms, truffles, beetroot, and whatever else is desired
7. At the time of serving, pour the dressing as explained and mix, toss or work the salad for a quarter hour
8. María Tubau salad should have no sauce residue in the dish in which it is prepared; this is its true point
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ensalada Repelada (Repelada Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- watercress
- pimpinella
- fennel

## Directions

1. Make it with different healthy herbs, such as watercress, pimpinella, fennel, etc.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Entrecote Ángel Maro (Angel Maro Entrecote)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One substantial entrecote steak, meat aged four days in winter or two days in summer
- Pork lard

## Directions

1. Have a very hot fire ready.
2. Strike the entrecote on both sides with a boxwood mallet on a wooden cutting board, then clean it and remove all attached fats, skin, nerves, and tendons.
3. Trim the meat so that only uniform, solid meat remains, with no trace of anything else—this is the secret to success.
4. Put enough pork lard in a pan so that when melted, the entrecote can be submerged two or three times without the fat stewing or startling it, but only to completely impregnate it with lard.
5. Remove the entrecote from the pan with an iron fork, holding it suspended in the air so the fat drips onto a plate.
6. Empty most of the fat from the pan, leaving it only lightly greased, and place it on very hot fire until the small amount of remaining lard crackles.
7. Using the same fork, place the entrecote in the pan, shaking the pan slightly.
8. After two or three minutes, small air bubbles will appear on the surface of the meat and burst. Then sprinkle the entrecote with ground salt—nothing but salt—and turn it over.
9. The same bubbling effect appears on the second surface, and complete the seasoning with another dusting of salt.
10. Remove the pan far from the fire and cover it hermetically with a fitted lid and weight on top.
11. After five minutes, serve it alone on a very hot plate.
12. Before cutting the entrecote, you will see it bathed in abundant juice according to this method, and the meat will be as tender as sponge cake.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Entrecote á la Bordelesa (Ribeye Steak Bordeaux Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 entrecote (ribeye steak)
- refined oil
- parsley, chopped
- shallots, chopped
- salt and pepper
- beef butter

## Directions

1. Heat the grill or pan lightly greased, with the meat rubbed with fat on all sides and very high heat.
2. Cook the entrecote on the grill or in the pan, rubbing the meat with refined oil.
3. On the second turn of the entrecote, sprinkle over it a spoonful of chopped parsley and shallots, dusted with salt and pepper.
4. Carefully transfer the meat with a spatula to a serving dish with about a walnut of beef butter.
5. Serve as the juices from the meat mingle with the melted butter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Entrecote á la Parisiense (Ribeye Steak Parisian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 entrecote (ribey steak), 15 centimeters long and 6-7 centimeters wide
- melted beef butter
- parsley, finely chopped
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Flatten the meat by striking it on its edge (thickness) and refine and shape the edges with a knife.
2. Soak the raw meat in melted beef butter.
3. Over high heat, sear and cook directly on the cooktop, turning to cook the other side when the first side is done.
4. Place on a serving dish with a touch of beef butter kneaded with finely chopped parsley, salt and a touch of pepper.
5. Before serving, mark the meat with three or four lines using a heated poker from the stove to simulate the toasted marks of grill grates.
6. Serve with fried potatoes or other elegant potato preparations.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Epigrama de Cordero (Lamb Epigram)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a lamb
- Thin strips of bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- A trace of beef butter
- A ladleful of broth
- Beef butter, melted and lukewarm
- Breadcrumb coating well salted and peppered

## Directions

1. Separate the shoulder blade and debone it completely.
2. Lard it with thin strips of bacon and season with salt, pepper and fine herbs.
3. Tie and secure it well in a rounded form that bulges on one side.
4. Braise it in a pot with a trace of beef butter and a ladleful of broth.
5. Continue quartering what remains of the half lamb: the ribcage, the breast, the flank, the hind quarter, etc., and cook all of it together in the juice and seasoning of the shoulder blade, which will already be braised.
6. When cooking is finished, strain the sauce, degrease it and reduce it if it is clear.
7. Allow the ribcage and shoulder blade to cool, but compress them beforehand between two pot lids so they do not deform as they cool.
8. Once cold, pass and repass them through melted and lukewarm beef butter and coat them with finely grated breadcrumb well salted and peppered, giving them color on the grill over a strong fire.
9. Separately, sauté the small chops as described for mutton, and roast on a spit the leg and all the pieces already prepared.
10. Place the shoulder blade, removing the string, in the center of a serving dish, and arrange all the other pieces around it, making the difference of each piece of meat and its special seasoning evident to the eye.
11. Pour over the dish thus arranged the sauce from the cooking, which can be increased with meat juice, incorporating in all cases lemon juice.
12. Serve this dish very hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Escabeche de Besugo (Pickled Sea Bream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- sea bream
- salt
- oil
- water
- vinegar
- lemon slices
- bay leaves

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the fish well, add a little finely ground salt and leave for one day hanging in the shade in a ventilated place
2. Then cut into pieces and fry in oil until golden
3. This same oil, with one quarter part water and three parts vinegar, some slices of lemon and bay leaves, is poured into the same pot in which the pieces of sea bream have been arranged and covered very well
4. After ten or twelve days it can be served
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Escabeche de Congrio (Pickled Conger Eel)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- usable pieces of conger eel
- good vinegar
- complete seasoning of all herbs
- onions
- garlic
- oil

## Directions

1. Place the usable pieces of conger eel in a saucepan and cover well with good vinegar.
2. Season with a complete seasoning of all herbs, onions, garlic, and other seasonings.
3. Cook this for ten minutes.
4. When the dish is cold, place it with its broth in a narrow-necked pot or vessel.
5. Cover the surface of the liquid with oil.
6. Prepared this way, the conger eel will last many months.
7. Serve the pickled conger eel as an appetizer on a platter with a garnish of chopped parsley.
8. Eat it with very hot fried oil presented in a sauce boat.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Escalopes de Salmón (Salmon Escalopes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon
- Cow butter, in sufficient quantity
- Salt and pepper
- Tomato sauce, or genovese sauce or Italian sauce
- Breadcrumb croutons

## Directions

1. Cut the salmon into thin slices, and from the large ones make two or more to make them all equal.
2. Place in a pan with cow butter in sufficient quantity and season, but do not overdo it.
3. Sauté over high heat until completely cooked and place in a crown on a round platter.
4. Pour a sauce in the center, preferably tomato sauce.
5. If this is not to the guests' liking, or if there is no fresh tomato, genovese or Italian sauce are suitable to substitute for it.
6. When placing the salmon escalopes in a crown, it is a good effect to intersperse them with breadcrumb croutons of the same size.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Escalopes de Solomillo con Trufas (Sirloin Scallops with Truffles)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin
- beef butter
- truffles
- broth
- Sherry wine
- Spanish sauce or roasting juice
- mushrooms as alternative to truffles
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the sirloin, whether raw or cooked, into thin small pieces, then trim them like five-peseta coins
2. Sauté the raw or cooked meat in beef butter
3. Serve with sautéed and cooked truffles in broth with Sherry wine, pouring over all a Spanish sauce or roasting juice
4. As an alternative to truffles, mushrooms prepared the same way can be used, and in many cases the scallops are served only with very thick tomato sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Escorzonera con Salsa Blanca (Black Salsify with White Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kilos of salsify
- 2 liters of water
- 2 tablespoons of vinegar
- 100 grams of finely chopped beef kidney fat
- 2 tablespoons of salt
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Place the salsify in a pot with 2 liters of water and 2 tablespoons of vinegar
2. Cut the heads off the salsify and scrape them completely to remove the black skin, placing them in the water as they are scraped
3. Put 100 grams of finely chopped beef kidney fat in a 3-liter pot and melt it without browning
4. Add 2 liters of water, salt, and 2 tablespoons of vinegar
5. Stir over high heat until the first boil
6. Put the salsify in the pot and cover, leaving a 4-centimeter opening
7. Cook very gently for half an hour at the side of the stove
8. Drain and cut into 7-centimeter pieces
9. Place in a 2-liter pot, cover with white sauce, and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Escorzoneras Fritas (Fried Salsify)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify
- flour
- water
- oil
- lemon juice
- pork lard
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Clean the salsify by scraping and washing well in boiling water, cutting the stems in half
2. Cook at a rolling boil for 15 minutes in boiling water
3. One hour before frying, dissolve 1 tablespoon of flour in 2 tablespoons of water
4. Season and add another tablespoon of oil and a few drops of lemon juice
5. Let the batter rest
6. Coat the salsify in the batter
7. Fry in pork lard over very high heat and serve immediately
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Esperinques Fritos (Fried Smelts)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- smelts
- milk
- flour
- oil for frying

## Directions

1. Thread the smelts spaced out by half dozens on needles or lancets as shown
2. Dip the threaded smelts in milk, coat with flour
3. Fry them in very hot oil very quickly
4. Serve still threaded, with each string being generally one diner's portion
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Espinacas con Azúcar (Spinach with Sugar)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach paste made with beef butter
- Sugar lumps
- Milk, two tablespoons
- One egg yolk

## Directions

1. Incorporate sugar lumps to the paste when it has been made with beef butter, and stir much while the sweet incorporates into the dish.
2. The egg binding, instead of making it with water, is made with milk.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Espárragos Cocidos (Cooked Asparagus)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus bundles
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Gather asparagus into a bundle without untying it, and cut all the stems together at once so that all the ends are equal, leaving them at a length three times that of the edible part.
2. Untie the bundle, scrape the non-edible extremities, wash very well, and arrange them neatly in a package on the removable bottom of an asparagus cooker box, securing them with a few turns of twine.
3. Place the asparagus cooker box full of water over high heat so that the asparagus submerges well.
4. Add a little salt, and when the water boils vigorously, place the asparagus inside and cook for twelve minutes.
5. Remove the asparagus, cut the twine from the removable bottom, and place them on a silver or metal tray whose flat part or bottom is covered with one or two well-pressed napkins, leaving the edges of the tray uncovered.
6. Arrange the asparagus on the tray with care and skill in two bundles, with the tips of the two bundles touching each other, so that the diner may easily serve themselves by taking them with their own left hand and with the aid of a small spatula in the right hand.
7. Serve with a sauce of choice, accompanied by a double-spouted gravy boat of the same metal, with its saucer in one piece.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Estofado de Cordero (Catalan Lamb Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from the legs of roasted lamb
- bacon pieces
- butifarra
- kid goat
- one head of garlic
- white wine
- brandy
- bay leaf
- oregano
- tarragon
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a chopped mixture with the meat from the legs of roasted lamb
2. Form a paste or dough and manipulate and spread it well
3. Expose it to brisk charcoal fire on grills and once it takes good color, divide into pieces
4. Place the pieces in a pot, seasoning with the appropriate salt
5. Add some pieces of bacon and butifarra, kid goat, one whole head of garlic, some white wine and brandy in proportion, bay leaf, oregano and tarragon
6. Cook everything in a well-covered pot with brown paper and a bowl of water on top as a seal to refresh the stew if necessary
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Estofado de Vaca a la Gentilhombre (Gentleman's Style Beef Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- A solid piece of beef, preferably from the top round
- Thick strips of fresh bacon for larding
- A veal hand (foreleg), deboned
- A bouquet of fine herbs
- One large onion
- Two or three cloves
- One quarter of a liter of good broth per kilogram of meat
- White wine from Rueda or Yepes, one quarter liter per kilogram of meat
- Half a dozen carrots, trimmed
- Two tablespoons of strong vinegar
- A thin, wide slice of bacon as wide as the palm of the hand

## Directions

1. Two hours before cooking begins, soak the piece of beef in white wine from Rueda or Yepes in the proportion of one quarter liter per kilogram of meat, turning the meat frequently so that the soaking is uniform
2. Season and spice the meat raw, after larding it with fairly thick strips of fresh bacon
3. Place the prepared meat in a braising pot with the deboned veal hand, a bouquet of fine herbs, and one large onion in which two or three cloves are studded
4. Pour over this a bowl of good broth and let it cook very slowly for one hour
5. After one hour, turn the meat and add the white wine that was used for soaking, along with half a dozen trimmed carrots, and continue cooking very slowly until the meat is well done
6. When one hour remains to finish cooking, add two tablespoons of strong vinegar
7. Although the meat bathes in the broth, place over it, like a cap, in the same liquid, a thin and wide slice of bacon as wide as the palm of the hand
8. The broth must bathe the meat for at least four-fifths of the cooking time
9. When serving hot, degrease the broth very well, which will then take on a gelatinous consistency
10. Place the piece of meat on a round platter, and the veal hand and vegetables, cut into equal-sized pieces, are arranged around it, pouring the broth over in small quantity, only enough to moisten and soak the dish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Estofado de Vaca Ordinario (Ordinary Beef Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- onion
- clove
- garlic head
- bay leaf
- oregano
- wine
- parsley
- oil

## Directions

1. Put in a pot the meat, cut into regular pieces, with two or three onions and a clove of spice, a head of garlic unpeeled, a bay leaf and oregano, a glass of wine, parsley and a jicara of oil per pound of meat
2. Brown everything over low heat and stir often
3. Place brown paper in the mouth of the pot and a cup of water on top
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Estofado de Vaca à la Gentilhombre (Braised Beef Gentleman Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- A solid piece of beef, preferably from the top muscle
- Fresh bacon strips, quite thick
- Salt and spices

## Directions

1. The piece of beef for this preparation should be solid, if possible from the top muscle.
2. Lard it with strips of fresh bacon that are quite thick.
3. Season and spice it raw.
4. This dish, which is none other than the best Beef à la Mode in the Parisian style, is corrected and titled by me, as one of the best preparations for beef after roasting, and very suitable for large households with good management.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Extracto de Caldo de Vaca en Pastillas (Portable Beef Broth Extract in Tablets)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two veal feet
- Six pounds of beef or cow meat
- One and a half pounds of veal top
- Five pounds of mutton
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place all ingredients in an earthenware pot and cook with low heat in sufficient water.
2. Skim carefully and strain the broth, pressing it well.
3. Boil a second time with fresh water, strain again, and let both broths cool to remove the fat.
4. Combine the two broths.
5. Strain and evaporate in a double boiler until it has the consistency of paste.
6. Remove from the vessel and spread on marble stone.
7. Cut into small tablets and finish drying in a double boiler or oven until brittle.
8. Store in a glass bottle sealed with cork, sealed with wax, and kept in a dry place.
9. To use: dissolve half an ounce or 15 grams of these tablets in water, add a little salt, heat over gentle heat, and serve or use in making soups.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Farinato (Salamanca Pork and Bread Sausage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram of pork fat
- 600 grams of chopped onion
- one kilogram of bread, crumbled and moistened 24 hours before
- 250 grams of raw oil
- 125 grams of paprika
- salt
- a pinch of anise seeds

## Directions

1. Place one kilogram of pork fat in a cauldron over the fire, stirring constantly so it does not stick
2. When half melted, add 600 grams of chopped onion and continue stirring with a wooden spoon
3. After a quarter hour, add one kilogram of bread that has been crumbled and moistened 24 hours before, continuing to stir
4. Pour 250 grams of raw oil and 125 grams of paprika over the mixture, add the corresponding salt and a pinch of anise seeds
5. Keep over the fire, stirring constantly until the ingredients form a harmonious whole, or a lightly compact mass
6. While hot, stuff into casings
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Farinatos (Spanish Cured Meat Sausage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 600 grams chopped onion
- 1 kilogram bread, crumbled and moistened 24 hours prior
- 250 grams raw oil
- 125 grams paprika
- salt to taste
- a small handful of anise seeds
- beef tripe, one quarter in length

## Directions

1. Keep stirring so ingredients do not stick together
2. When half melted, add 600 grams chopped onion and continue stirring with a wooden spoon
3. After a quarter hour, add 1 kilogram crumbled bread that has been moistened in a basin 24 hours prior, and continue stirring
4. Pour 250 grams raw oil and 125 grams paprika over the mass, add salt to taste and a small handful of anise seeds
5. Keep on the fire, stirring constantly until the ingredients form a harmonious whole, that is, a slightly compact mass
6. While hot, stuff the mixture into beef tripe pieces, each one quarter in length
7. Hang the farinatos to air dry in the kitchen near the hearth
8. After fifteen days, take down the farinatos and flatten them on a board
9. Hang them again, and they are ready to be eaten; the longer they hang, the richer they become
10. To serve in Castile at mid-morning snack time, fry them in small pieces with pork lard
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Filetillos de Carnero (Lamb Fillets)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Thin fillets cut from the lamb loin
- Bread crumbs
- Fresh vegetables in equal form and size
- Cow butter

## Directions

1. Cut thin fillets from the high loin of the lamb.
2. Bread them and grill them on the grill.
3. Serve with a garnish of all the fresh vegetables on hand, prepared in equal form and size, and previously reheated and cooked in cow butter to produce the true jardinière garnish.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Filetillos de Lomo de Cerdo Asados (Roasted Pork Loin Fillets)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork loin
- lard for larding
- strong vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut thin, large fillets from the pork loin, about the size of the palm of the hand
2. Lard or mechanically prepare them as you would fricandeau of veal
3. Soak them in strong vinegar for 24 hours
4. Drain them and thread them one beside the other, with their faces in contact, onto a spit
5. Secure the spit properly on the roasting rod so they roast well, presenting them to the fire on their edge
6. Baste them with their own juices as you turn them, and salt and pepper during the operation
7. Unthread the fillets and serve generally with potato purée or in their own juices, with an abundant finely chopped mixture of pickled gherkins
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Flan (Custard Flan)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams of flour
- 1 whole egg
- 6 egg yolks
- 200 grams of powdered sugar
- 1 tablespoon of orange blossom water
- zest of 1 lemon
- 1 quarter liter of milk
- puff pastry dough

## Directions

1. Dissolve 60 grams of flour with 1 whole egg in a saucepan
2. Add afterwards 6 egg yolks, 200 grams of powdered sugar, 1 tablespoon of orange blossom water, the zest of 1 lemon, and 1 quarter liter of milk
3. Line the inside of a smooth, not tall mold, though it may be very wide, with a good layer of puff pastry dough
4. Pour in the flan mixture
5. Bake in a bell oven with heat from above and below
6. When cold, remove from the mold to serve, dusting it with sugar
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Foie-gras de Familia (Family-Style Foie Gras)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one hand of veal
- one pound pork liver
- one quarter pound bacon
- one pound onions
- four garlic cloves
- one veal marrow bone, well filled with marrow
- one liter water
- half liter white wine
- all spices: parsley, laurel, mint, thyme and rosemary
- leftover roasted veal
- one quarter pound lard
- two tablespoons flour
- broth from cooking

## Directions

1. Cook for six consecutive hours over low heat in one liter of water and half liter of white wine: one hand of veal, one pound of pork liver, one quarter pound of bacon, one pound of onions, four garlic cloves and one veal marrow bone, well filled with marrow.
2. Season with all spices: parsley, laurel, mint, thyme and rosemary.
3. After six hours, having incorporated the leftover roasted veal one hour before, shred everything, and aided by its own broth, pass through a sieve to form a purée.
4. In a saucepan, melt one quarter pound of lard, and in it brown two tablespoons of flour.
5. Thin and bind with the broth from cooking.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Fricando (Braised Veal)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of veal shoulder, about three pounds
- thin strips of bacon
- broth
- sorrel

## Directions

1. Debone and lard a piece of veal shoulder of three pounds with thin strips of bacon.
2. Place the meat in a braising pan with two deciliters of broth.
3. Put on the heat and let it boil until the broth is reduced, preventing the meat from sticking to the bottom of the pan.
4. When the juice of the meat takes on a yellowish tint and becomes consistent and gummy, add another six deciliters of broth.
5. Let it cook very gently for one hour and a quarter with very slow boiling, the pan half covered with its lid.
6. After this time of slow cooking, remove the lid and replace with a flat iron lid with a shovelful of coals on top.
7. Baste the veal every four minutes until a yellowish and shiny tint appears; this operation serves to concentrate.
8. Arrange the meat in the serving dish.
9. Strain the juice through a chinois strainer; decrease it and pour it over the piece of veal.
10. Place the sorrel puree in the serving dish first; the piece of veal on the sorrel and baste with the juice.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Fricasé de Pollos (Chicken Fricassee)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two chickens
- 125 to 250 grams of beef or pork butter, depending on the importance of the dish and its garnish
- Salt
- Pepper
- One tablespoon of flour
- Plenty of mushroom scrapings
- Rooster crests and crayfish
- Two ladles of clean broth
- Small onions, double or triple the number of pieces of poultry

## Directions

1. Quarter two chickens and place the pieces in fresh water mixed with white wine or two tablespoons of vinegar
2. Drain and rinse, and place in a pot over low heat with 125 or 250 grams of beef or pork butter
3. Season with salt, pepper, one tablespoon of flour, plenty of mushroom scrapings, rooster crests and crayfish
4. The chicken pieces should not take color in the fat, but rather firm up their meat
5. Moisten with two ladles of clean broth and cook over high heat for up to one hour
6. Half an hour before serving the dish, add very small onions to the pot in double or triple the number of pieces of poultry
7. Bind the sauce after removing the chicken and placing it in a pyramid on a platter
8. Pour the sauce over it without letting the mushrooms and onions fall, then arrange them with a spoon to garnish the fricassee with art and symmetry
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Fried Artichokes (Alcachofas Fritas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients
