- Artichokes
- Egg or batter for coating
- Oil or pork lard

## Directions

1. Cook the artichokes and let them cool
2. If they are the size of a lemon, cut them into four pieces lengthwise
3. Coat them with egg or wrap them in a frying batter
4. Fry them over high heat in oil or pork lard
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Fried Cauliflower (Coliflor Frita)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Parsley
- Batter or egg for coating

## Directions

1. Prepare the cauliflower first in an adobo with salt, vinegar and parsley
2. Cook it as is customary
3. Let it drain to remove excess moisture
4. Wrap it in a batter or coat it with egg
5. Fry it in oil, dividing it into florets
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Fruta en Sartén (Fried Fruit Pastry)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of flour
- 6 eggs, yolks and whites
- a little ground salt
- 2 tablespoons of brandy
- a little orange blossom water or zest of 1 lemon
- water and milk in equal proportions

## Directions

1. In a pot or saucepan, place 2 pounds of flour, 6 eggs (yolks and whites), a little ground salt, and 2 tablespoons of brandy, as well as a little orange blossom water or zest of 1 lemon
2. Dissolve all this with a proportionate quantity of water and an equal quantity of milk to obtain a kind of porridge, more thin than thick
3. Leave in this state for 2 or 3 hours
4. When ready to fry, place the pan on the fire with a little lard or a couple of tablespoons of oil
5. When hot, pour a spoonful of batter that spreads in the pan to form a very thin layer
6. When fried on one side, turn it over by striking the pan handle
7. Remove, dust with powdered sugar, and continue the operation until all the batter is prepared
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gachas Manchegas (Manchego Porridge)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- pork jowl (papada de cerdo)
- oil
- paprika (pimentón)
- clove (very little)
- caraway (alcaravea)
- pepper (pimienta)
- flour made from bitter vetch (harina de almortas)
- water

## Directions

1. In a little hot oil, add pork jowl cut into small pieces.
2. Remove these pieces when fried, and in the resulting fat add paprika, clove (very little), caraway and pepper.
3. Gradually add a certain quantity of bitter vetch flour to brown in the fat.
4. Add fairly hot water little by little until the flour is dissolved and cook, constantly stirring with a wooden paddle until the porridge no longer tastes raw.
5. When the porridge is ready, place the pieces of pork jowl on top.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Galantina de Perdiz (Partridge Galantine)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover partridge meat
- Gelatin
- Truffles
- Egg white
- Crushed ice
- Glazed game broth
- Rice
- Ravigote sauce

## Directions

1. Make balls with the leftover partridge.
2. Prepare molds for galantine and coat the inside with gelatin.
3. Decorate with truffles and egg white, then plunge into crushed ice.
4. Pour gelatin into the molds to a height of about one inch.
5. Coat the partridge balls with glazed game broth.
6. When the gelatin begins to set, place the balls on it, one in each mold.
7. Fill the molds with gelatin, cover with a lid, and place ice on top.
8. When the galantines are firm, remove from the mold and place on a rice base covered with ravigote sauce.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gallina al natural (Chicken Boiled Plain)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook the chicken in water with a pinch of salt in its most elementary seasoning
2. The broth resulting from the cooking will be the best broth for the first food of the sick
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gallina con arroz (Chicken with Rice)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams of bacon
- pork lard
- chicken
- rice in quantity proportioned to the volume of the chicken
- clean broth
- salt
- two or three spoonfuls of roasting juice

## Directions

1. Brown 125 grams of bacon in pork lard in a pot
2. Remove the bacon from the pot and place the chicken, turning it so it browns well and tempering the fire so the fat does not become black
3. In a separate pot, bring rice to boil in clean broth in quantity proportioned to the volume of the chicken
4. When the rice is half cooked, remove the chicken from the pot for a moment and add the rice, stirring well so it mixes and soaks in the juices
5. Place the chicken back in the pot with the bacon well browned and cut into pieces
6. Moisten with broth and finish cooking on very low heat until the chicken and bacon are done
7. At the moment of serving, add two or three spoonfuls of roasting juice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gallina de Guinea en su propio jugo (Guinea Fowl in Its Own Juice)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 guinea fowl, cleaned and without entrails
- melted lard
- lemon juice
- salt
- garlic, crushed
- parsley, chopped
- pepper
- 2 or 3 bay leaves
- half a bowl of raw oil
- water
- 8 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Place the cleaned guinea fowl on a grill, anointing it frequently with melted lard, lemon juice, salt and crushed garlic
2. When half roasted, place it in a casserole with lard, chopped parsley, pepper, salt, 2 or 3 bay leaves, the remaining lard from roasting, half a bowl of raw oil and enough water to cover it
3. Let it boil until tender
4. Remove the guinea fowl and strain the broth into the casserole
5. Dissolve 8 egg yolks in the broth, beating them well so the broth thickens
6. Let it boil a little and pour over the guinea fowl, which should be in a serving dish, and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gallina deshuesada (Boned Chicken)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken, killed and plucked
- one pound of pork loin, minced very fine
- three pounds of veal from the leg, minced very fine
- salt
- pepper
- ham slices
- onions
- carrots
- garlic
- thyme
- parsley

## Directions

1. When the chicken is hot after being killed and plucked, open it with a pocketknife from the back of the neck to the tail, cutting carefully until the bones are reached
2. Cut carefully the meat and skin adhered to the bones and remove them so that all the inside comes out and only the meat and skin remain
3. Mince one pound of pork loin and three pounds of veal from the leg very fine, seasoned with salt and pepper
4. Fill the chicken with this mixture, adding some ham slices and filling all the hollow spaces of the chicken so that it acquires the same form as when it had bones
5. Sew the open part with needle and thread and wrap it in a new well-sewn cloth
6. Cook in water with onions, carrots, garlic, thyme and parsley until done
7. Remove when it is seen that it is cooked
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gallina dorada (Golden Chicken)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken, well cleaned
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- spices
- bacon slices
- egg yolks, beaten
- sugar
- cinnamon
- celery
- watercress
- endive

## Directions

1. Boil the cleaned chicken a little in a pot or broth with salt, pepper, onion and spices
2. Wrap it in bacon slices and place on a spit
3. Roast over low heat
4. Remove the bacon slices and continue roasting the bird until it takes color
5. Anoint it with beaten egg yolks
6. To serve, you can add a little sugar and cinnamon and present it on a bed of celery, watercress or endive
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gallina á la morisca (Moorish-Style Chicken)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 hens or 4 young chickens
- onion
- bacon in dice
- broth
- spices (excluding cloves)
- vinegar
- fresh butter
- flour
- vegetables, chopped (optional)

## Directions

1. Roast the hens or chickens
2. Cut them into quarters
3. Fry a little onion with a little diced bacon; stew it very well
4. Add broth and season with all spices except cloves, and cook slowly
5. Add a little vinegar so they are well soured
6. If you have some fresh butter, add it inside and you can fry a little flour in this butter as described previously, because this dish should not carry eggs
7. If you wish to add a little chopped vegetable, you can
8. This dish should come out a little yellow
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gallinas guisadas (Stewed Hens)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken, cleaned and prepared
- pork lard
- chopped onion
- parsley
- broth
- wine or vinegar
- fresh lard
- spices
- chopped vegetables, particularly sorrel

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned and prepared chicken into quarters
2. Brown the quarters in pork lard with chopped onion and parsley
3. When browned, add the necessary broth to cover everything and a little wine or vinegar and fresh lard
4. Season with spices and if desired, add chopped vegetables, particularly sorrel, which make a good stew
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gazpacho (Cold Spanish Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- salt
- one raw bell pepper
- two tomatoes of good color and size
- crumb of half a loaf of bread, soaked in water
- one small cup of raw oil
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Place salt, raw bell pepper, and two tomatoes of good color and size in a mortar, crushing everything perfectly
2. Add the crumb of half a loaf of bread that has been prepared soaked in water
3. Mash this mixture together, adding one small cup of raw oil in the same manner as is done with mayonnaise, so that it binds well after working it for at least a quarter hour
4. Add vinegar and water to taste
5. Pass through a strainer
6. Place small pieces of bread in the resulting thick broth
7. Serve in summer during hot hours
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gazpacho (Spanish Cold Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- salt
- one raw bell pepper
- two tomatoes of good color and size
- the crumb of half a loaf of bread
- water for soaking bread
- one small cup of raw olive oil
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. In a mortar, place salt, a raw bell pepper, and two tomatoes of good color and size, crushing everything perfectly.
2. Add the crumb of half a loaf of bread that has been prepared soaked in water, and mash this mixture together.
3. Mix in a small cup of raw olive oil in the same manner as is done with mayonnaise, so it binds well after working it for at least a quarter hour.
4. Add vinegar and water to taste.
5. Pass through a strainer, and to that thick broth that results, add small pieces of bread.
6. Eat in summer, during hours of heat.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gazpacho de Extremadura (Extremaduran Cold Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- oil
- salt
- one or two cloves of garlic
- bread crumbs soaked
- water
- bell pepper (optional)
- onion
- tomato
- green bell pepper
- cucumber

## Directions

1. In a mortar place a little oil, salt and one or two cloves of garlic; mash well
2. Add a little crumb of soaked bread and mash again with the oil, garlic and salt until they form like an ointment
3. Add a few drops of water and stir with the pestle so it begins to dissolve, gradually adding more water while stirring until the mortar is full
4. Empty into a serving dish and finish adding the desired amount of water
5. Crumble bread by hand into pieces, not with a knife, leaving it to soften in this broth until ready to eat
6. About a quarter hour before eating, add a little diced onion for flavor
7. Add vinegar only at the moment of serving, as if added earlier the bread absorbs it all and becomes sour
8. Diced tomato, green bell pepper and cucumber are very good additions; tomato and bell pepper are almost always added
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gazpacho de Granada (Granada-style Cold Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- water
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt
- cumin
- garlic
- onions (optional)
- cucumbers (optional)

## Directions

1. Crumble the bread
2. Add sufficient water, oil, vinegar, pepper, salt, crushed cumin and garlic
3. If desired, add onions and cucumbers sliced into rounds
4. Mix together
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gibelota de Conejo (Rabbit Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- 125 grams beef or pork lard
- 125 grams bacon, cut into small squares
- 1 heaping tablespoon flour
- 2 ladles of broth
- 2 ladles of white wine
- mushrooms
- bouquet of fine herbs
- 2 dozen small onions
- pork lard for the onions

## Directions

1. Prepare the rabbit and quarter it as shown on page 443.
2. Brown it well in 125 grams of beef or pork lard and equal quantity of bacon cut into small squares.
3. When the bacon is very golden, remove it from the pot, and in the same fat, brown the rabbit pieces until the meat becomes firm and toasted.
4. Set aside the browned rabbit on a plate.
5. In the same pot with the same fat, toast a heaping tablespoon of flour, and moisten with two ladles of broth and the same amount of white wine.
6. Immediately incorporate into the pot the bacon pieces and rabbit pieces that were set aside.
7. Add trimmed mushrooms and cook over high heat.
8. When the gibelota lacks only a quarter of the cooking time, season with salt, pepper, a bouquet of fine herbs, and two dozen small onions that have been previously browned in pork lard.
9. Manage the cooking so that the sauce is thick and reduced when the rabbit is cooked through.
10. Serve very hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gin-Cok-Tail (Gin Cocktail)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 wine glasses Dutch gin
- 0.5 wine glass curaçao
- 0.5 wine glass bitter bitters, preferably Angostura
- scrapings of nutmeg
- zest of 0.5 lemon
- crushed ice, equal volume to the spirits combined
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. In a punch bowl, combine the gin, curaçao, and bitters
2. Add the nutmeg scrapings and lemon zest
3. Add crushed ice in equal volume to the combined spirits
4. Beat the egg well and mix both white and yolk with everything, stirring for a considerable time little by little
5. Sweeten according to the consumer's taste
6. Serve as an aperitif
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Glaseado (Brown Glaze Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two good chickens
- three pounds of calf kidney fat
- four carrots
- two large onions with cloves inserted
- one ladle of concentrated broth
- one bouquet of fine herbs
- consumed broth
- white roux made with flour
- one good handful of mushrooms
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Place the two chickens, calf kidney fat, carrots, onions with cloves, concentrated broth, and bouquet of fine herbs in a pot.
2. The important point in preparing the glaze is that the meats do not stick to the pot and do not take on color.
3. When the juice begins to thicken, moisten with a little consumed broth and add gradually, little by little, until it well bathes the meats.
4. Prick the meat at once with a knife so that it releases its juice, and let it cook over low heat.
5. Prepare separately a white roux with flour, in which cook a good handful of well-cleaned mushrooms, cut up and seasoned beforehand in the cold with salt and lemon juice.
6. Mix the white roux with the broth in which the meats are cooking.
7. Continue cooking everything for two hours.
8. Skim and degrease and strain through a metal mesh strainer.
9. If the preparation is done well, the glaze will result white.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gran Jugo (Grand Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- a good layer of butter from cows
- half a pound of bacon
- one pound of ham
- two pounds of beef
- concentrated broth

## Directions

1. On the bottom of a saucepan of considerable capacity, place a good layer of butter from cows, and on top half a pound of bacon and one pound of ham, trimmed into very thin slices, with two pounds of beef, cut into slices half an inch thick.
2. Place the saucepan over high heat, let the meats stick a little but without burning, and wet with a small ladle of concentrated broth.
3. Prick the slices of beef so they retain none of their juice.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guarnición de Cebollas (Onion Garnish)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices of bacon
- thin slices of Trevélez ham
- twelve onions the size of apples
- a heaping spoonful of pork lard
- roasted meat juice from the previous evening
- beef broth
- one egg yolk
- a thread of vinegar

## Directions

1. Fill the bottom of a casserole with thin slices of bacon, and on top, as a second layer, place very thin slices of Trevélez ham.
2. Trim twelve onions the size of apples into slices, and use them to cover the ham and bacon.
3. When the onions have cooked almost over brisk heat, remove and set aside the ham and bacon.
4. Add a heaping spoonful of pork lard, stir the onion in the casserole with a spatula, lower the heat and take care that it does not brown.
5. Season well and add roasted meat juice from the previous evening to a small pot of broth, then bind with a dissolved egg yolk and a thread of vinegar.
6. This onion garnish serves for all kinds of dishes, and can be presented as a platter at family tables with the ham and bacon, though these should be saved for other uses.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guarnición de Patatas (Potato Garnish)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- salt
- beef butter or pork fat
- shallots
- parsley
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel and wash the potatoes and cook them in salted water until well drained
2. In a saucepan with a wide base, place the potatoes in a single layer with a good amount of beef or pork fat to soak them in
3. Complete the cooking on the stove, stirring continually with a spatula until the potatoes are cooked and well saturated with fat
4. Add finely chopped shallots and parsley, a touch of salt and pepper
5. Serve in a dish around the cold meat from the stew
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guarnición de Patatas Ruientes (Garnish of Roasted Potatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled, washed, and well-drained potatoes
- beef or pork lard
- fresh beef lard or strained pork lard
- a few spoonfuls of juice or Spanish sauce
- or veal, mutton, or roasted loin broth from the previous day's meal

## Directions

1. Place the peeled, washed, and well-drained potatoes in a casserole with plenty of beef or pork lard to saturate them.
2. The casserole should have a wide base so that all the potatoes can be placed in a single layer on the bottom without overlapping.
3. Place the casserole over a brisk fire, also adding heat to the lid, and take care to turn the potatoes one by one so they do not burn and brown evenly.
4. Once done, remove them from the fat in which they have been sautéed, and place them in another ordinary casserole with a little fresh beef lard or strained pork lard, and add a few spoonfuls of juice or Spanish sauce.
5. When these substances are not available, they can be substituted with veal, mutton, or roasted loin broth, set aside from the previous day's meal for this purpose.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guarnición de Patatas Rusientes (Rustic Potato Garnish)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- beef butter or pork fat

## Directions

1. Peel, wash and well drain the potatoes
2. Place them in a saucepan with a wide base with plenty of beef or pork fat to soak them in, arranging them in a single layer on the bottom
3. Place the saucepan over high heat and also place heat under the lid
4. Turn the potatoes carefully to cook them evenly
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guarnición Financiera Blanca (White Financière Garnish)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of beef butter the size of an egg
- one heaping spoonful of flour
- water or clear broth
- the same ingredients as the dark financière (poultry quenelles, mushrooms, truffles, artichoke hearts, veal sweetbreads, tongue à l'écarlate, olives, wine or sherry, salt and pepper)
- two egg yolks
- milk

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, place a piece of beef butter the size of an egg and one heaping spoonful of flour
2. Mix well over high heat and then moisten with water or clear broth
3. Incorporate the same ingredients as in the dark financière garnish and cook over low heat for half an hour
4. Remove from heat and bind with two egg yolks mixed with milk
5. Pour into a serving dish to garnish the dish to be served
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guarnición Financiera Obscura (Dark Financière Garnish)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of broth
- a handful of rooster crests and kidneys and other rooster offal, cooked and prepared beforehand
- another handful of poultry quenelles
- an equal quantity of mushrooms, chopped and blanched in boiling water
- truffles, cut into pieces
- a couple of artichoke hearts, also cut into pieces
- veal sweetbreads
- two slices of tongue à l'écarlate, finely chopped
- twenty or thirty pitted olives
- one glass of white wine or sherry
- salt
- pepper
- three or four spoonfuls of meat juice
- starch or flour for binding

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, place one liter of broth with a handful of rooster crests and kidneys and other rooster offal, all cooked and prepared beforehand
2. Add another handful of poultry quenelles, an equal quantity of mushrooms that have been chopped and blanched in boiling water, truffles cut into pieces, a couple of artichoke hearts also cut into pieces, veal sweetbreads, and two slices of finely chopped tongue à l'écarlate
3. Add twenty or thirty pitted olives, one glass of white wine or sherry, salt and a touch of pepper
4. Cook everything for twenty minutes over very low heat
5. Add three or four spoonfuls of meat juice and bind with starch or flour
6. Pour this garnish into a serving dish and place the prepared dishes on top to serve à la financière
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guarnición Flamenca (Flemish Garnish)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- turnips
- cabbage
- lettuce hearts
- beef or pork lard
- onions
- Spanish sauce or meat juice from previous meals

## Directions

1. Cook carrots and turnips in salted water in proportion to the size of the dish to be garnished.
2. Before cooking, cut the vegetables into pieces of equal shape and size.
3. Separately, cook cabbage and lettuce hearts, and sauté them in beef or pork lard with several onions, taking care that they do not burn.
4. Place the carrot pieces, turnips, lettuce stalks, and cut cabbage symmetrically in a circle around the dish, and the onions around the vegetables in the form of a cord, pouring Spanish sauce or meat juice from previous meals over everything on the platter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Gubios Fritos (Fried Gudgeon)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- gudgeon fish
- flour
- oil

## Directions

1. Scale the gudgeons and gut them
2. Clean and dry with a fine cloth without washing
3. Place in a napkin and sprinkle flour over them
4. Gather the four corners of the napkin as if making a bundle and shake the fish to coat thoroughly with flour
5. Let rest in the napkin for half an hour until the fish's moisture has made the flour adhere well
6. Flour again with fresh flour
7. In a large pan holding four pounds of oil, fry about thirty gudgeons the size and thickness of a finger over high heat to perfection
8. Do not fry more quantity at once in the same oil, nor fry thirty gudgeons in less fat, as this will spoil the operation
9. When well fried, the gudgeons are eaten in their entirety including head, bones, and flesh
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisadillo de Pavo (Turkey Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover turkey
- pork fat
- onion
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- clean broth
- turnips
- dried parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover turkey into pieces the size of walnuts and sauté and brown them in pork fat with a chopped onion.
2. When the onion has taken color, dust with flour, season with salt, pepper, and a bay leaf, and moisten with clean broth.
3. Cook slowly over low heat with turnips cut into thin slices.
4. At the moment of serving, dust the stew with dried parsley powder.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado a la Cazadora (Hunter's-Style Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover roasted hare or rabbit meat
- Flour
- Eggs
- Spicy sauce or tomato sauce
- Ground meat
- Streaky bacon
- Cow's butter
- Milk

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover roasted hare or rabbit into small pieces and coat them with flour and egg.
2. Fry the coated pieces.
3. Dress them in a thick spicy or tomato sauce.
4. Mix the ground meat with streaky bacon and make a paste with flour, cow's butter, and milk, creating a type of béchamel.
5. Cook this hash over low heat for half an hour until it thickens with the milk.
6. Let it cool, and hours later make croquettes, either coat them in flour or wrap them in breadcrumbs to fry.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado Aristocrático o Chipolata (Aristocratic Stew or Chipolata)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams salted bacon
- two dozen white sausages
- quenelles or meatballs
- mushrooms
- truffles or earthnuts
- white wine or dry sherry
- Spanish sauce or good pot broth
- sweetbreads of veal or lamb
- rooster combs

## Directions

1. Brown the bacon and white sausages in a pot
2. When the bacon is also browned, add quenelles, meatballs, mushrooms, and truffles or earthnuts, cut into equal volume to the sausages
3. Wet everything with white wine or dry sherry
4. Add Spanish sauce or good pot broth, and once the sauce has reduced by half, give a couple of boils with the seasoned meat already incorporated
5. Serve the stew
6. Alternatively, this stew can be made by cooking at the same time as the sausages, veal or lamb sweetbreads and rooster combs
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado Aristocrático ó Chipolata (Aristocratic Stew or Chipolata)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams of salted bacon
- about twenty white sausages
- quenelles or small meatballs
- mushrooms
- truffles or truffles of the earth
- veal or lamb sweetbreads, in equal volume portions to the sausages
- white wine

## Directions

1. Give color in a saucepan with 125 grams of salted bacon to about twenty white sausages.
2. When the bacon has also colored, incorporate quenelles or small meatballs, mushrooms, and even truffles or truffles of the earth, cut in quantity equal in volume of each substance to that of the sausages.
3. Moisten everything so it is covered with white wine.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado Blanco de Ternera (White Veal Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal scraps
- two tablespoons flour
- butter or lard
- one ladle of clear broth
- one ladle of cream or two of milk
- mushrooms (champignons)
- small onions
- salt and pepper
- three egg yolks
- water

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, brown two tablespoons of flour in butter or lard, and moisten with one ladle of clear broth and another of cream, or in its absence, two of milk.
2. In this sauce, seasoned slightly, place the pieces of veal to warm, but without allowing them to cook.
3. Separately, cook mushrooms and very small onions in clear broth; season with salt and pepper and incorporate into the meat.
4. When serving this white stew, bind it with three egg yolks dissolved in water, so that the stew becomes creamy and slightly yellowish.
5. Make sure the sauce is not too thin, resulting in a dish fit for a good table, and more than leftovers, a variant of any of the white veal stews.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado Blanco de Ternera (White Veal Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of veal breast
- butter from cows or pork
- flour
- broth or water
- about two dozen large onions the size of walnuts
- about two dozen small mushrooms or champignons
- about two dozen pitted olives
- salt and pepper
- dried parsley powder
- egg yolk
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the veal breast into pieces the size of chocolate ounces.
2. Pass the veal pieces in cow or pork butter without letting them brown. Remove them with a skimmer and set aside on a plate.
3. In the same butter, without letting it take on color either, brown two tablespoons of flour.
4. Immediately moisten with broth or water, and when the sauce is thickened and somewhat thin, incorporate the veal pieces that were set aside all at once, along with about two dozen large onions the size of walnuts, an equal number of small mushrooms or champignons, and the same quantity of pitted olives.
5. Cook everything over low heat until the sauce reduces and becomes like a paste.
6. Half an hour before serving, season with salt and pepper, and sprinkle with a good tablespoon of dried parsley powder.
7. Away from the heat, with the dish already in a covered serving bowl, bind the sauce with one egg yolk dissolved in a couple of tablespoons of water.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado de Aldea (Village Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- butter or oil
- parsley
- onion
- garlic
- water
- spice

## Directions

1. Reheat the meat in a pan or cooking vessel with butter or oil, parsley, onion and chopped garlic
2. When the meat is half cooked, transfer to a pot or cooking vessel
3. Add a little water and let cook, seasoning with a grain of spice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado de Anguilas (Eel Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eels
- garlic
- parsley
- pepper
- saffron
- clove
- cinnamon
- salt
- water
- pine nuts or hazelnuts
- bread crumb
- egg

## Directions

1. Choose river eels that are best, preferring those with dark back and white belly
2. Cut eels into pieces, wash well and put in a cooking vessel
3. Add garlic, chopped parsley, pepper, saffron, clove and cinnamon
4. Cook with water and salt
5. As they cook, make a sauce of pine nuts or hazelnuts with a piece of moistened bread and garlic
6. If the hazelnuts are toasted, the bread should also be toasted
7. Add the sauce and let give a couple of boils, moving the vessel so it does not scorch and adding salt in proportion
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado de Carne Cocida Antes (Stew of Previously Cooked Meat)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped onions
- butter
- a little broth
- meat cut into pieces
- a little vinegar or mustard

## Directions

1. Reheat chopped onions in rounds with butter, adding a little broth
2. Cook the meat cut into pieces in this
3. Once it has cooked a little, add a little vinegar or mustard
4. Serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado de Carnero (Lamb Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Pieces of lamb breast, the size of chocolate squares
- Onions, cut into rounds, in equal quantity to the meat
- Two or three carrots
- Several turnips
- A sprig of thyme
- A bay leaf
- Two or three ladles of clean broth from the pot
- Potatoes, cut into informal but equal pieces the size of pigeon eggs
- Lard, very hot

## Directions

1. Cut pieces of lamb breast the size of chocolate squares.
2. Put onions cut into rounds in equal quantity to the meat in the bottom of the cooking pot, and place the meat on top with two or three carrots, several turnips, a sprig of thyme, and a bay leaf.
3. Add two or three ladles of clean broth from the pot and cook everything over very low heat, but very low indeed, until the sauce is almost completely consumed.
4. Then add an equal quantity of broth or water as before, and let it cook again for two hours over heat even lower, if possible, than the first cooking.
5. One hour before serving, incorporate potatoes into the stew, cut although uneven and the size of pigeon eggs, but before putting them in the pot it is advisable to sauté and brown them separately in very hot pork lard.
6. It is not necessary to bone the pieces of meat, nor to degrease the sauce, nor to reduce it.
7. The only variation can be to put more or fewer potatoes, or none at all, and substitute them with fine turnips, because there is nothing that goes better with stewed lamb than tender turnips.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado de Carnero (Mutton Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from leg
- salt
- butter
- bacon
- garlic
- parsley
- toasted garlic
- pine nuts or almonds

## Directions

1. Take meat from leg, cut into small pieces and put in a pot with salt, butter, bacon, garlic and chopped parsley
2. Before cooking make a sauce with toasted garlic, parsley, pine nuts or almonds
3. Let cook for a while
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado de Carnero Rápido (Quick Lamb Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Very small and thin pieces of lamb breast
- Salt
- Pepper
- A pinch of flour
- Broth
- Fine cuts of potatoes

## Directions

1. Over high heat in a skillet, brown very small and thin pieces of lamb breast.
2. Brown them well and even toast them a little.
3. Season with salt and pepper, dust with a pinch of flour, and wet with a little broth.
4. Add fine cuts of potatoes.
5. In one hour the stew is ready.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado de Carnero á la Castellana (Castilian-Style Mutton Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton shoulder
- oil
- turnips
- butter
- flour
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- onions
- cloves
- bay leaf

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton shoulder into pieces and place in a pot with oil
2. Give it several turns over good heat until it takes on a good golden color, then remove and drain
3. Cut turnips into strips, pass them through the previous sauce and let them take color; remove and drain them as well
4. Make a roux with butter and flour, pass the mutton through it, thinning it with broth
5. Add salt, pepper, parsley, onions, cloves, bay leaf, and add the turnips
6. When the meat is almost cooked, remove the fat and finish cooking, letting everything boil slowly
7. If the sauce is too thin, let it thicken over the fire
8. Place in a serving dish, garnishing with the turnips, and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado de Carnero á la Murciana (Murcia-Style Lamb Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb breast, cut into pieces
- A little lard
- Flour
- Three ladles of hot water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bay leaf
- A pinch of cumin
- One tablespoon of sweet paprika
- Onions
- Potatoes, equal size or cut equal
- Three green peppers, fried in good oil beforehand

## Directions

1. Brown the lamb pieces in very little lard and dust with flour until well browned, without burning.
2. Immediately wet with three ladles of hot water and season with salt, pepper, bay leaf, a pinch of cumin, and one tablespoon of sweet paprika.
3. Add onions and potatoes of equal size or cut equally.
4. Cook everything over very low heat for three hours.
5. One hour before serving, incorporate the strips of the three green peppers that have been fried beforehand in good oil and drained before adding them to the pot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado de Prínceps (Stew of Princes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams lean ham
- cooked carrot from pot broth
- six mushrooms called champignons
- two truffles
- 125 grams beef butter
- one tablespoon flour
- one cup concentrated broth
- one glass Champagne wine
- a few tablespoons of Spanish sauce
- juice of one lemon
- a good piece of separately roasted meat

## Directions

1. Reheat and partially cook the ham in its own fat in a pot
2. Remove from pot and cut into small pieces
3. At the same time, cut a carrot cooked in pot broth, six champignon mushrooms and two truffas into equal sized pieces
4. Put everything in the pot with beef butter and dust the stew with one tablespoon of flour
5. Wet with one cup of concentrated broth, one glass of Champagne wine and a few tablespoons of Spanish sauce
6. When everything has cooked well and very little sauce remains, season this stew with the juice of one lemon
7. Serve with a good piece of separately roasted meat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado de Vaca (Beef Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of beef leg
- salt
- bay leaves
- bacon, part fat and part lean
- garlic
- pepper
- oil
- sufficient broth
- spices
- parsley
- tomato

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of beef leg into small pieces and add to the pot with salt and bay leaves
2. Chop bacon that is part fat and part lean, fry it and also add to the pot
3. Mash garlic with pepper, dissolving it in a little water, and add to the stew
4. Add oil to reheat it at low heat
5. When appropriate add sufficient broth and season with salt, spices, parsley and tomato
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado de Vaca, Carnero o Ternera (Stewed Beef, Mutton, or Veal)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- butter or oil for frying
- bread
- one ounce of fried bacon per pound of meat, finely chopped
- one tomato weighing about two ounces
- meat
- flour
- pepper
- salt
- water
- parsley
- garlic

## Directions

1. Heat butter or oil to fry, add a little bread
2. When well fried, remove it, keeping only the oil or butter
3. Coat the meat with flour, a little pepper, and corresponding salt
4. Let it brown (rehogue) until golden
5. Add necessary water and let it cook until tender
6. Make a sauce with parsley, garlic, and everything that was removed when adding the meat
7. After one more boil, it can be served
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado Francés (French Ragout) or Navarin
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one kilogram of mutton with bone, cut into large pieces like eggs
- one quarter pound of butter
- one pound of chopped onions
- flour
- water in volume four times greater than the pot contents
- salt
- pepper
- carrots
- small onions
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Heat the butter and brown the cut mutton pieces until they achieve a blonde color in a wide pot
2. Add one pound of chopped onions
3. When they have taken color, dust lightly with flour and add water in a volume four times greater than the pot contents
4. Let boil over low heat for three hours
5. At half cooking time, season and incorporate carrots, small onions, and potatoes, which must always cook over low heat
6. The onion will disappear and the sauce will reduce to just enough liquid so that all the meats and vegetables are well impregnated, resulting in a thick consistency
7. Serve when the meat and vegetables are well cooked
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado Militar (Military Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- meat cut into small pieces
- oil or butter
- chopped onion
- a little sweet pepper
- garlic
- pepper
- tomatoes, peeled and chopped
- salt
- hot water or pot broth
- bay leaf
- potatoes cut into thick pieces

## Directions

1. Chop the meat into small pieces and put to reheat in a pot, pan or cooking vessel with oil or butter, chopped onion, a little sweet pepper, garlic, pepper, tomatoes that have been peeled and chopped, and corresponding salt
2. Cook at low heat with its own broth, stirring from time to time
3. Add hot water or pot broth, adding at the same time a little bay leaf and some potatoes cut into thick pieces
4. Let it cook for the necessary time
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado Muy Casero (Very Homestyle Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- lean meat from beef leg
- chopped bacon
- onion
- garlic cloves
- spices
- white wine
- parsley
- salt
- paper

## Directions

1. Take the needed lean meat from beef leg and put to cook in the morning in the meat pot
2. When cooked, remove and let cool, cut into pieces
3. Fry chopped bacon in small pieces, and in its fat fry the beef
4. After frying, put in the pot where it is to be stewed
5. The bacon that is removed from the pan is put back in and fried again with chopped onion
6. Put everything in the pot with all spices, some mashed garlic cloves, white wine and parsley
7. Cook everything at low heat and cover the pot with double paper, putting a pot of water on top
8. Half an hour before serving season with salt
9. If the beef is good it has better taste than veal
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado Ordinario (Ordinary Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound meat
- half onion
- parsley
- two garlic cloves
- pepper
- half teaspoon paprika
- one tomato or tomato preserve
- two bay leaves
- one cup oil
- salt
- potatoes
- turnips
- carrots
- artichokes
- peas
- squash
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. For each pound of meat put in a pot half onion chopped, a little parsley, two mashed garlic cloves, a dust of pepper, half teaspoon of paprika, one tomato or tomato preserve, two bay leaves, one cup of oil and corresponding salt
2. Put everything together on low heat, stirring often because it can stick easily
3. When browned add enough water to cook
4. When half cooked potatoes, turnips, carrots can be added, and in their time artichokes, peas, squash and mushrooms
5. When well cooked the dish can be served
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisado Árabe de Carnero (Arab-Style Lamb Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Fat slices of veal breast
- Oil
- Garlic cloves
- Parsley
- Mint
- Salt
- Pine nuts
- Onion, chopped
- Water
- A handful of rice
- Beets, cut into rough pieces

## Directions

1. Fry and brown in oil fat slices of veal breast and also brown some garlic cloves.
2. Incorporate parsley, mint, much salt, pine nuts, and some chopped onion.
3. Cook this in a pot over low heat with plenty of water.
4. Half an hour before eating, add a handful of rice and beets cut into rough pieces to fill the vessel.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisantes a la Inglesa (English-Style Peas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas
- Salted water
- Fresh butter from cows
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Submerge the peas in boiling salted water
2. Remove and drain them to place on a serving platter
3. Make a hollow in the center and place a piece of fresh butter from cows, kneaded with chopped parsley
4. Cover and wrap the butter with the spatula, as the heat of the peas will melt it
5. Serve immediately
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisantes a la Varsoviana (Varsovian-Style Peas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas
- Fresh butter from cows
- Small tender onions
- Lettuce hearts
- Sugar
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Wrap raw peas in fine fresh butter from cows
2. Place on gentle heat with small tender onions and two or three lettuce hearts
3. Seal the pot hermetically, placing a greased white paper before the lid
4. Let cook for half an hour
5. Lightly dust with ground sugar
6. Do not bind with egg yolk
7. Do not salt or season until the last moment, and only sparingly
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisantes al Natural (Peas Prepared Naturally)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One liter of shelled peas
- 30 grams of beef butter
- Two or three onions
- A pair of lettuce hearts
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 125 grams of beef butter
- One tablespoon of flour
- Two egg yolks
- Half a glass of water

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan one liter of shelled peas with 30 grams of beef butter, two or three onions, a pair of lettuce hearts, and good seasoning of salt and pepper.
2. Cook this over gentle heat and sauté in the saucepan frequently, or better yet, stir with one of the boxwood spatulas that should be available in a kitchen, so that the peas do not stick, before they begin to release the vegetable water they contain.
3. When cooking is finished, remove from the saucepan the parsley bundle and the lettuce, leaving only the onions.
4. Add 125 grams of beef butter kneaded with one tablespoon of flour; stir vigorously everything for two minutes.
5. Remove the saucepan from heat and pour over the peas, at the moment of serving, two egg yolks dissolved in half a glass of water.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Guisantes con Azúcar (Peas with Sugar)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas prepared as in the natural recipe
- Two or three lumps of sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the peas exactly as in the natural formula.
2. Add two or three lumps of sugar at the moment of serving.
3. The sugar dissolves and sweetens the entire dish equally, without need of stirring.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Habas Caseras (Home-Style Broad Beans)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh green broad beans
- Salted water
- Pork lard
- Good chorizo
- Bacon
- Sweet pepper

## Directions

1. Shell fresh green broad beans until you have one liter
2. Remove the little black skin or nail at one end
3. Wash and cook in boiling salted water with rolling boil
4. When cooked, which is proven by seeing if they crush with finger pressure, place with part of their water in a pot with pork lard and appropriate seasoning
5. Separately, fry slices of good chorizo and strips of bacon
6. Incorporate into the beans with a little sweet pepper
7. Remove from heat at the first boil
8. Serve this succulent dish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Habas Verdes (Green Broad Beans)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Small fresh broad beans
- Oil
- Garlic
- Toasted hazelnuts
- Toasted bread
- Water
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Remove only the ends of the beans
2. Cook first and then drain
3. Place in a pan and add oil with fried garlic
4. Make a sauce with toasted hazelnuts, a little toasted bread soaked in water and vinegar, pepper, and eggs proportionate to the sauce
5. Dilute the sauce with seasoned water
6. Pour over the beans
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Habas Verdes (Green Fava Beans)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- small fresh fava beans
- oil
- garlic, fried
- toasted hazelnuts
- toasted bread
- water
- vinegar
- pepper
- eggs

## Directions

1. Remove only the ends of the fava beans.
2. Cook the beans first, then drain them.
3. Place them in an earthenware dish and add oil with fried garlic.
4. Make a sauce with toasted hazelnuts, a little toasted bread soaked in water and vinegar, pepper, and eggs proportional to the sauce.
5. Dilute the sauce with seasoned water and pour it over the beans.
6. Let them boil over gentle heat.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Helado de Crema (Cream Ice Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks, ten
- Milk, two cuartillos
- Refined sugar, half a pound
- Chocolate, half a pound (optional)

## Directions

1. Mix the egg yolks, milk, and sugar perfectly and put over low heat, stirring without stopping until it reaches a regular thickness
2. Remove at once, and after a short time freeze it
3. If adding chocolate, cook it with common water first and then mix it in
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Helado de Crema ó Nata de Leche (Cream or Milk Foam Ice Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one and a half quartillo
- Cream, half a quartillo
- Lemon peel
- Sugar, twelve ounces

## Directions

1. Put everything in a pan to cook until it begins to thicken, stirring constantly with a spoon
2. Remove from heat, pass through a cloth, let cool
3. Put in the freezer to freeze
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Helado de Crema ó Nata de Leche (Frozen Cream or Milk Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One and a half quarter-liters of milk
- Half a quarter-liter of cream
- Lemon rinds
- Twelve ounces of sugar

## Directions

1. Put everything in a saucepan to cook until it begins to thicken, stirring always with a spoon
2. Remove then from the heat, pass through a cloth, let it cool and then pour it into the sorbetera to freeze it
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Helado de Flor de Naranja (Orange Blossom Ice Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Orange blossoms, eight ounces
- Sugar, twelve ounces
- Water, two azumbres

## Directions

1. Dissolve the sugar with the water
2. Pour it boiling over the orange blossoms placed in a vessel, cover well and leave in infusion for six hours
3. Pass through a sieve and freeze
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Helado de Fresa (Strawberry Ice Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Strawberries, two and a half pounds
- Red currants, half a pound
- Water, one quartillo
- Sugar, one pound

## Directions

1. Crush the strawberries and currants in a sieve so the pulp passes through and the seed grains remain on top
2. Pour water over the dregs, press and stir well, and combine the liquid that passes with the previous liquid
3. Dissolve the sugar over heat in a little water, combine everything, mix well, and once it is cold, freeze it
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Helado de Vainilla (Vanilla Ice Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk, one and a half quartillo
- Cream, half a quartillo
- Vanilla, half an ounce
- Sugar, twelve ounces

## Directions

1. Split the vanilla and cut into small pieces
2. Pound with a little sugar in a stone mortar until it pulverizes
3. Put in a pan with the milk, cream, and sugar, and cook until it thickens a little
4. Pass through a cloth, and once it is cold, freeze it
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevo Frito (Fried Egg)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- egg
- oil or lard

## Directions

1. Break the egg, putting the white in one cup and the yolk in another
2. Lightly agitate the white without beating it
3. Place the pan over high heat
4. Heat the liquid (oil or lard) until it produces a thin vapor with a brownish color, turning into the characteristic blue smoke of fats when boiling begins
5. At this moment, remove the pan from the heat
6. Pour the white in a thin stream from a hand's breadth of height, as if decanting
7. Immediately and without losing time, with great speed, throw the yolk into the middle of the white
8. Cover the pan and after three minutes remove the lid and take out the fried egg, placing it on the plate in which it is to be eaten
9. Do not add salt; the consumer should add it to their taste
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos a la Buena María (Eggs the Good Mary Way)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Water
- Parsley
- Olive oil

## Directions

1. Make them the same as eggs on a plate, but instead of fat use water, finely chopped parsley, and one tablespoon of good oil for every half dozen eggs.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos a la Tripa (Eggs with Onions and Milk)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Butter
- Onion
- Flour
- Milk
- Salt

## Directions

1. Brown the butter and in it fry for every two eggs a well-sliced onion about the size of an apple.
2. Dissolve a little flour in a cup of milk and add it along with the hard-boiled eggs cut into rounds.
3. Season and let it cook very slowly for about a quarter hour.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos al Jugo (Eggs in Gravy)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Meat juice

## Directions

1. Poach them in the meat juice.
2. Serve them in a deep dish over a large crouton.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos al Plato (Eggs on a Plate)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Oil or butter
- Butter or oil for basting

## Directions

1. In a metal plate or pan, heat a little fat—oil or pork butter, but properly butter from cows.
2. Carefully add the eggs, letting them set as desired over moderate heat.
3. Serve in the utensil in which they are made.
4. To give them shine on the surface and make the yolks appear clear, brush them with melted butter or oil with a spoon, cover with a lid and some coals on top for a couple of minutes.
5. Eggs on a plate accept all manner of garnishes—croutons, blanched ham, peas, asparagus tips, mushrooms, truffles, anchovy strips, sausages, etc.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos al Raspado (Scraped Bread Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- breadcrumbs
- beef butter
- 1 anchovy
- parsley
- onion
- garlic
- 3 egg yolks
- salt and pepper
- fried bread crusts

## Directions

1. In a cooking pot, combine scraped breadcrumbs with beef butter, finely chopped anchovy, parsley, onion, and garlic.
2. Mix everything together with 3 egg yolks over low heat.
3. Break the remaining eggs on top.
4. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs and season with salt and pepper.
5. Surround with fried bread crusts.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos Amerengados (Meringue Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs (separated into whites and yolks)
- salt
- thirty grams of powdered sugar
- one tablespoon of orange blossom water
- one liter of cream (or two liters of milk as substitute)
- lemon peel
- one hundred grams of sugar
- two tablespoons of orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Separate the whites from the yolks of six eggs.
2. Beat the whites to stiff peaks and add salt with 30 grams of powdered sugar, one tablespoon of orange blossom water, and continue beating so that the mixture combines well.
3. Apart, over low heat, reduce by a quarter one liter of cream, in which the peel of one lemon with one hundred grams of sugar and two tablespoons of orange blossom water will have been added.
4. If there is no cream, substitute with two liters of milk, which will be reduced by half, cooking it very slowly.
5. When the milk is boiling, the beaten whites are added successively in portions with a small ladle, so that they have a spherical form.
6. Turn the balls in the milk so they cook evenly, and as they coagulate they are removed and placed to drain on a sieve or hair sieve.
7. When all the whites are cooked, remove the milk from the fire and incorporate four yolks, stirring the milk strongly and quickly, so that it gains consistency without curdling.
8. Place the eggs in a pyramid on a platter and pour the cream or milk over them, prepared as explained above.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos con Tomate á la Andaluza (Andalusian Eggs with Tomato)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- good Andalusian oil
- tomatoes, well peeled
- 2 eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Fry the peeled tomatoes well in good Andalusian oil.
2. Season and reduce the sauce by half.
3. In the same oil, fry 2 eggs Spanish-style, soft inside with crispy edges.
4. Serve on a plate with the tomato sauce on the side.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos de Sorpresa (Surprise Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 raw egg
- small pieces of truffles
- salt
- a small piece of beef butter
- eggshell
- lime and egg white paste

## Directions

1. Drill a hole in the raw egg with a diameter of a coin.
2. Introduce small pieces of truffles and a little salt with a small piece of beef butter.
3. Stir everything inside with a stick, breaking the yolk.
4. Seal the hole with a patch made from another eggshell and a mixture of lime and egg white.
5. Let it dry.
6. Boil the egg in water and serve in an egg cup with the patched end facing down.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos en Batalla (Battle Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs for 6 people
- very fine slices of raw ham
- pork butter
- salt
- pepper
- dried parsley

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites separately and keep the yolks, two by two, in small bowls.
2. In a round baking dish at least 20 centimeters in diameter, place very fine slices of raw ham on the bottom, covering it completely.
3. Pour pork butter over it and place the dish over strong heat on a griddle rather than a burner.
4. When the fat begins to sizzle, pour all the beaten egg whites over it.
5. As soon as the whites set, begin adding the yolks.
6. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and dried parsley.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos en Cocotte (Eggs en Cocotte)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Butter or lard

## Directions

1. In small earthenware or porcelain ramekins with handles, place about a walnut of butter or half a tablespoon of oil in each.
2. When the fat is hot, add the egg and complete its cooking in the oven at moderate heat.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos en Manteca Negra (Eggs in Black Butter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Fresh unsalted butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Place a good piece of fresh unsalted butter in a pan and let it take on a deep caramel color.
2. Add the eggs with great care and season with salt and a pinch of pepper.
3. When the white is well set and before the yolk sets, slide them gently together from the pan to a dish.
4. Pour the butter over them and sprinkle the surface with a little parsley.
5. In the same pan, heat a spoonful of vinegar, which is also added to finish the operation.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos Escalfados (Poached Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Water

## Directions

1. In a sauté pan with a small amount of water, add the eggs so they set.
2. Remove them with a skimmer without breaking them.
3. They constitute an appetizing dish with white rice, spinach paste or sorrel paste, or tomato sauce.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos Fritos (Fried Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Pork lard or oil (ten times the volume of an egg for every half dozen eggs)

## Directions

1. Fry the eggs in pork lard or oil; the fat must be prepared so that the oil is free of bad smell and acrid flavor, and the lard is entirely anhydrous, without water
2. Make the fried eggs one at a time, and serve as they are made, immediately from the pan to the plate
3. In a large pan, add lard or oil in a quantity ten times greater than the volume of an egg, calculated for frying half a dozen
4. Break the egg, put the white in one cup and the yolk in another, agitate the white lightly without beating it
5. Put the pan over high heat
6. When the liquid is hot, it will smoke with a thin vapor that takes on a brownish color, turning into the characteristic blue smoke of all fats when boiling begins
7. At that moment, remove the pan from the fire, pour the white in a stream from about a hand's breadth of height, as if decanting, and immediately, without losing time and with great speed, throw the yolk in the middle
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos Fritos en Buñuelo (Fried Eggs as Fritters)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Lard or oil

## Directions

1. When the lard or oil is ready for frying, add the egg that has been previously emptied into a cup.
2. The amount of fat, as in all frying, must be considerable.
3. With a dry skimmer, gradually fold up the white until you can flip the egg, which will look like a fritter if the operation is carried out quickly and over high heat.
4. Serve the eggs thus fried with puffed potatoes and shoestring potatoes, serving everything together in elegant disorder on a napkin, on a silver or Ruolz plate.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos High-Life (High-Life Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- brick-shaped sandwich bread
- eggs
- lard or olive oil

## Directions

1. Cut slices from brick-shaped sandwich bread two centimeters thick, in the direction of the width
2. Using a glass whose mouth diameter is slightly smaller than the square of the slice, press it into the bread as if using a cutter, to about half the depth of the piece
3. Empty the marked part of the breadcrumb and it will remain like a hollow, which should be refined and profiled carefully, moistening the bread with water
4. In a large frying pan capable of holding at least four slices, put a sufficient quantity of lard or olive oil according to the taste of the diners
5. Over high heat, when the liquid in the pan is ready to fry, quickly throw the bread slices with the hollowed part facing up
6. In each hollow, drop an egg without loss of time, as soon as the crumb has taken on a toasted color
7. Remove and serve on a well-pressed napkin placed over a round dish of English metal or Ruolz silver
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos Hilados (Threaded Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of sugar
- one liter of water
- two dozen egg yolks

## Directions

1. Clarify two pounds of sugar in one liter of water, taking care that the point is weak, as soon as it begins to thread.
2. Sift two dozen yolks through a clear sieve with the aid of a spatula, and add them gradually to the clarified sugar, stirring very quickly during cooking, which will last two minutes.
3. This mass or paste is poured into a funnel of cylindrical form, whose bottom is like that of a metal strainer, with holes the size the egg threads should have, and these are collected in very cold water, aiding their passage through the funnel with a wooden pestle or handle that weighs and presses the mass.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos Pasados por Agua (Soft-Boiled Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- water

## Directions

1. Place the eggs to be soft-boiled in cold water, covering them well
2. Over high heat, bring the water to a boil
3. At the first bubble of boiling, remove the vessel from the fire
4. Serve the eggs to the table in a bowl or covered dish, filled with the water in which they cooked, cooled slightly with cold water to stop all cooking while preserving the heat
5. The eggs will be uniformly soft-boiled with slightly set yolks and milky, loose, and uniform whites
6. Use only very fresh eggs for this preparation
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos Revueltos (Scrambled Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 2 ounces of beef butter
- 1 tablespoon of milk or water
- a small piece of beef butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Heat 2 ounces of beef butter in a pan.
2. Separate the egg whites from the yolks.
3. Beat the yolks separately in a bowl, seasoning them and adding 1 tablespoon of milk or water.
4. Beat the whites in a meringue in another bowl.
5. Combine the yolks and whites together, mixing very well, and add a small piece of beef butter.
6. When the butter in the pan is boiling, pour in the mixture.
7. Hold the pan by the handle with your left hand and beat the eggs with a wooden spatula in your right hand during cooking.
8. Cook until the desired consistency is reached, either firm or soft according to taste.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos Tiernos (Soft-Boiled Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Water

## Directions

1. When the water is boiling vigorously, put the eggs into the pan all at once and with caution, so as not to break them.
2. Let them cook for four minutes.
3. Remove them and place them in cold water to peel them.
4. Serve them in the sauce of your choice.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos Tiernos (Soft-Cooked Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- water

## Directions

1. When the water boils vigorously, put the eggs in the pot at once and with caution
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos á la Aurora (Aurora Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 150 grams of beef butter
- breadcrumbs
- parsley, finely chopped
- chives, finely chopped
- salt and pepper
- tomato sauce or béchamel
- pieces of fried ham

## Directions

1. Hard boil the eggs and cut them in half lengthwise or crosswise.
2. Remove the yolks and mash them together with 150 grams of beef butter, breadcrumbs, finely chopped parsley and chives, salt and pepper.
3. Fill the hollows of the egg white halves with this mixture, forming a rounded mound.
4. Place the filled eggs symmetrically in a baking dish with a little beef butter.
5. Bake in the oven at low heat for 15 minutes.
6. Serve separately with tomato sauce or béchamel sprinkled with pieces of fried ham.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Huevos á la Turca (Turkish Eggs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 to 10 eggs for 4 servings
- truffles
- beef butter
- 2 tablespoons of cooked, cold cow's milk
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- croutons fried in pork butter

## Directions

1. Brush the truffles clean and cut them into small pieces.
2. Sauté the truffles in a pan with beef butter over high heat.
3. Beat the egg whites until foamy in one bowl.
4. In 2 tablespoons of cooked, cold milk, beat the yolks, incorporating parsley, salt, pepper, clove, and nutmeg.
5. Combine the yolks with the whites and continue beating, then add half an ounce of beef butter in a piece.
6. Pour the mixture into the pan with the truffles.
7. Stir and turn the eggs with a wooden spatula until the mixture is uniform and gelatinous in consistency.
8. This operation takes only a few seconds.
9. Present with croutons fried in pork butter, cut into squares, rounds, or triangles.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Hígado de Cerdo a la Cazadora (Pork Liver Hunter's Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- refined oil
- one pork liver
- bacon, minced
- parsley, minced
- shallots, minced
- salt
- pepper
- bacon, in thin leaves
- drippings juice from roasting
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Place in a serving dish with refined oil a pork liver, which is split lengthwise down the middle without the two pieces separating, so that it looks like an open lamb kidney.
2. Then make incisions lengthwise and crosswise, as if it were gridded like a bar of chocolate, and the grooves or marks of the cuts are filled with a hash of bacon, parsley and shallots minced.
3. After two hours, moisten the entire surface of the liver with the oil that is in the dish, and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
4. Then close the liver, which was open like a book, lard it with thin leaves of bacon and roast it in the oven or on the plate, in a roasting pan or baking sheet.
5. When the liver is well cooked, place it on the serving platter, skim the fat from the roasting juice and strain it to pour over the dish, strengthened with lemon juice.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Hígado de Cerdo Salteado (Sautéed Pork Liver)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- pork liver
- beef butter
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs
- shallots, finely chopped
- half a tablespoon of flour
- one ladle of broth
- a thin stream of vinegar
- one tablespoon of rum or cognac

## Directions

1. Trim the pork liver into small equal pieces and sauté in a pan with beef butter, turning frequently so that the cooking is uniform.
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, and remove the liver pieces from the pan when they are slightly overcooked.
3. In the fat from the sauté, add finely chopped fine herbs and shallots and half a tablespoon of flour, with one ladle of broth and a thin stream of vinegar.
4. Once the sauce is bound, return the liver pieces to the pan so that they are soaked and cooked in the sauce, just for two minutes, adding one tablespoon of rum or cognac to aromatize the dish at the moment of serving.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Hígado de Raya (Skate Liver)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- bread slices
- raw oil
- butter of cow's milk
- 125 grams of butter
- skate liver
- parsley
- small onions
- shallots
- capers
- anchovy fillets
- soft bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut slices of bread that are twice as long as they are wide and soak them in raw oil
2. Fry the bread slices in cow's milk butter to make canapés
3. Melt 125 grams of cow's milk butter in a pot and stew and cook the skate livers in it with plenty of chopped parsley, small onions, shallots, and capers, all chopped very finely
4. When the liver is cooked, remove it from the pot and set aside
5. Place the fried bread slices on an oval metal platter
6. Spread each piece of bread first with a layer of the finely chopped vegetables cooked in their liquid
7. Place a piece of skate liver on top of each bread slice with a couple of anchovy fillets, then finish with a second spreading of the fine herbs mixture
8. Once all the canapés are garnished equally, sprinkle them with very finely crumbled soft bread
9. Cover the platter with a bell cover loaded with a small amount of coals just to brown the surface of the dish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Hígado de Ternera a la Casera (Veal Liver Home Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- 125 grams of beef butter
- bacon strips
- salt and pepper
- one onion
- two carrots
- one quarter bottle of white wine

## Directions

1. In a pot with 125 grams of beef butter, sauté the veal liver which has been larded with thick strips of bacon.
2. Turn to brown on all sides and season moderately with salt and pepper.
3. Add one onion, two carrots, and one quarter bottle of white wine.
4. Cook for about three hours with the pot well covered, turning the meat frequently to prevent sticking.
5. Serve hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Hígado de Ternera a la Italiana (Veal Liver Italian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- olive oil
- finely chopped fine herbs
- mushrooms, finely chopped
- salt and pepper
- white wine

## Directions

1. Cut the veal liver into pieces.
2. In a pot, add several spoonfuls of oil, finely chopped fine herbs with some finely chopped mushrooms, and place a layer of liver pieces on top.
3. Moisten with more oil and sprinkle with fine herbs and mushrooms as before, but season strongly with salt and pepper this time.
4. Add another layer of liver and another layer of seasoning, continuing in this manner until all liver is used, ending with a layer of mushrooms and fine herbs.
5. Cook all of this at low heat with one quarter bottle of white wine and with the pot well covered.
6. Serve hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Hígado de Ternera a la Marinera (Veal Liver Mariner Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- quarter kilogram of beef or pork butter
- flour
- finely chopped fine herbs
- red wine
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. In a quarter kilogram of beef or pork butter, sauté and brown the veal liver cut into thin, equal pieces like domino tiles.
2. Turn with a wooden spatula and dust with flour and two good spoonfuls of finely chopped fine herbs.
3. Moisten with a glass of red wine.
4. When the sauce has come to a boil two or three times, remove the liver pieces and place on a preheated serving platter.
5. Add to the remaining sauce a good handful of mushrooms that have been trimmed and cooked beforehand.
6. After this has simmered for three or four minutes, pour the entire sauce over the liver on the platter.
7. Serve hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Hígado de Ternera Asado (Roasted Veal Liver)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- bacon strips
- salt and pepper
- caul fat or well-greased white paper
- meat juice
- shallots
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Lard the veal liver generously with thick strips of bacon.
2. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Wrap well in caul fat or well-greased white paper.
4. Roast on a spit at low heat, as the liver must be well-cooked.
5. Remove the wrapping and place on a serving platter.
6. Pour over it meat juice mixed with a minced shallot mixture and the juice of one lemon.
7. Serve hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Hígado de Ternera Frito á la Italiana (Fried Veal Liver Italian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- four eggs
- two spoonfuls of olive oil
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- olive oil for frying
- thick tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the veal liver into thin slices in the shape of ladyfinger biscuits
2. Make a batter with four eggs, two spoonfuls of olive oil, salt and pepper
3. Coat the liver slices one by one in the batter
4. Dust with flour
5. Fry in olive oil
6. Arrange on a serving platter in a crown shape
7. Fill the hollow center with a thick tomato sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Hígado de Ternera á la Italiana (Veal Liver Italian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- olive oil
- fine herbs
- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- white wine, one quartillo
- meat juice

## Directions

1. Cut the veal liver into pieces
2. Pour a few spoonfuls of olive oil into a saucepan
3. Add chopped fine herbs and finely chopped mushrooms
4. Place a layer of liver pieces on top
5. Moisten with more olive oil and sprinkle with fine herbs and mushrooms as before, seasoning heavily with salt and pepper
6. Continue alternating layers of liver and seasoning until all liver is used, finishing with a layer of mushrooms and fine herbs
7. Cook everything slowly over low heat with one quartillo of white wine in a tightly covered saucepan
8. When the liver is cooked, remove it from the saucepan and place the pieces on a serving platter
9. Pour the cooking liquid over it, reinforced with meat juice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Hígado de Ternera á la Papillote (Veal Liver en Papillote)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- bacon strips
- refined olive oil
- fine herbs
- onion slices
- salt
- pepper
- greased paper

## Directions

1. Lard a good veal liver generously with strips of bacon
2. With a sharp knife, cut the liver into slices one finger thick
3. Soak the slices for two hours in refined olive oil beaten with fine herbs, onion slices, salt and pepper
4. Wrap each liver slice individually in sheets of greased paper
5. Place on a grill over gentle heat for at least half an hour, turning the papillotes from time to time
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón a la Austríaca (Austrian-Style Ham)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices of raw ham
- bread slices without crust, passed through butter
- fine herbs
- mushrooms
- truffles or earth truffles
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. In a casserole or pot, place thin slices of raw ham and bread slices (without crust) that have been passed through butter, along with fine herbs, mushrooms, and truffles or earth truffles, arranged alternately with the ham
2. Sprinkle everything with breadcrumbs
3. Bake in the oven for two or three hours to serve hot, or better yet, place it over hot coals with fire on top in a suitable pot, very common in Austrian kitchens
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón a la Maillot (Ham Maillot Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- plenty of water
- bottle of Jerez wine
- rice
- carrots
- lettuce
- onions
- green beans
- paper for decoration

## Directions

1. Perfect and desalt the ham for two days
2. Wrap it in white linen and place it in a brasier with plenty of water
3. Let it boil with very gentle heat until completely cooked
4. When the ham is cooked, drain and trim it
5. Place it in another brasier with a bottle of Jerez wine
6. Let it boil very gently for half an hour with the brasier well covered
7. Remove the ham and glaze it
8. Form a base of rice in the bottom of the serving platter, lower than the thickness of the ham, about four centimeters; place the ham on the base and arrange small bunches of carrots, lettuce, onions, and green beans around it
9. Decorate the ham bone with a bow of curled paper
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón Alimón (Arimón's Ham)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- oil, well burned
- onion scraps
- modest piece of ham cut into small pieces
- one tablespoon of flour
- 350 grams of chickpeas
- one cabbage
- water
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. In a casserole, pour some well-burned oil, some onion scraps and a modest piece of ham cut into small pieces
2. Put everything on the fire to fry, adding one tablespoon of flour right away
3. Before or after, as you wish, have boiled and cooked 350 grams of chickpeas and one cabbage
4. Unite the cooked chickpeas and cabbage with their water to the ingredients in the casserole
5. Add pepper and salt
6. Cook for half an hour over low heat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón Cocido al Natural (Boiled Ham, Natural Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Carrots
- Onions
- Cloves
- Nutmeg
- Peppercorns
- Thyme
- Bay leaves
- Water
- Toasted breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare the ham by removing all the exposed surface meat and trimming the edges of fat which are always yellowish.
2. Saw off the tip of the bone with a bit of the knuckle, which can be used whole or divided in the pot.
3. Desalt the ham for one, two or three days depending on how salty it is, in an iron pot or ham kettle filled with water.
4. Remove and wrap in a strong white linen cloth, tying and pressing with the four corners.
5. Place to cook in a braising pot in plenty of water so the meat is bathed.
6. Add carrots, onions, cloves, nutmeg, peppercorns, thyme and many bay leaves.
7. Cook all this for five hours over low heat.
8. Check if done by inserting a larding needle: if it enters without resistance, the ham is cooked.
9. Remove the ham from the pot and untie the cloth knots to debone it with great care.
10. Tie the cloth again but pressing tightly, and place in a deep dish until completely cooled.
11. Remove the cloth completely and lift off the ham rind, which comes off easily.
12. Sprinkle the surface that was covered by the rind with toasted breadcrumbs passed through a sieve.
13. Decorate the handle with a paper bow.
14. Serve on a well-pressed napkin in an oval dish when ready to eat.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón con Espinacas (Ham with Spinach)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach paste
- fried ham slices
- croutons
- fat or butter from frying the ham

## Directions

1. Prepare spinach paste as described in the vegetables section
