2. Arrange it in a pyramid or dome shape on a round platter
3. On its surface, place with art and symmetry fried ham slices alternating with croutons sautéed in the fat or butter in which the ham was fried
4. The ham slices and croutons should have, if possible, the same form and size, generally triangular for the ham
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón con Tomate (Ham with Tomato)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fried ham slices
- thick tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Place the fried ham slices in a covered dish or vegetable server
2. Pour thick tomato sauce over the top
3. Leave the dish near the heat so the sauce penetrates well into the ham
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón de Pescado (Fish Ham - Historic Dish)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- carp fillets
- tench fillets
- eel fillets
- salmon fillets
- carp roe
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- beef butter
- fine herbs
- carp skin
- fine new linen cloth
- water
- white wine

## Directions

1. With fillets of carp, tench, eel and salmon, make a hash that is mixed with carp roe, salt, pepper, clove, nutmeg, beef butter and fine herbs
2. Knead everything and give it the shape of ham, covering the surface with carp skin
3. Wrap it in fine, new linen cloth and sew it very tightly
4. Cook this newly made ham for one hour in water and white wine in equal parts, well seasoned and spiced in the liquid
5. When the dish is cold, unsew the wrapping and prepare it on a platter decorated with watercress and purslane
6. Cut it into slices like ham, and neither the appearance nor the taste of this Bourbon dish is disagreeable
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón de Pescado (Fish Ham)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- carp fillets
- tench fillets
- eel fillets
- salmon fillets
- carp roe
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- beef butter
- fine herbs
- carp skin

## Directions

1. Make a hash with fillets of carp, tench, eel and salmon, mixed with carp roe, salt, pepper, clove, nutmeg, beef butter and fine herbs
2. Knead everything and shape it like a ham, covering the surface with carp skin
3. Wrap it in fine, new linen cloth and sew it very tightly
4. Cook this newly made ham for one hour in equal parts water and white wine, well seasoned and spiced
5. When the dish is cold, unsew the wrapping and serve it on a platter decorated with watercress and purslane
6. Cut it into slices like regular ham
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón Dulce (Sweet Ham)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- small lean ham slices
- beef butter
- half teaspoon of flour
- broth
- drops of pure wine brandy
- powdered sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the small lean ham slices and fry them in beef butter
2. Remove them from the pan and set them aside on a plate
3. In the same butter, add half a teaspoon of flour and moisten with broth and a few drops of pure wine brandy
4. Dust with powdered sugar according to the diners' preference for sweetness
5. Incorporate the ham and cook for ten minutes away from the heat source, with the pan covered and its lid topped with hot coals
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón Empapelado (Paper-Wrapped Ham)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fine, thin ham slices
- oil
- pepper
- parchment paper

## Directions

1. Place fine, thin ham slices on a sheet of parchment paper, oiled and sprinkled with a little pepper
2. Wrap in the paper making a large papillote
3. Roast in the oven at medium heat or on a grill, turning a couple of times
4. Serve in the same paper on a platter
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón en Costrón (Ham Loaf)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- ham scraps, finely chopped
- fine herbs, finely chopped in equal amount to ham scraps
- ham fat fried with any lean leftover ham in equal amount to the chopped mixture
- thin slices and rounds of bread crumb in sufficient quantity

## Directions

1. Finely chop ham scraps, adding an equal amount of finely chopped fine herbs, and mix everything together
2. Add an equal amount of fried ham fat mixed with any leftover lean ham from a previous meal
3. Cut thin slices and rounds of bread crumb in sufficient quantity
4. In a round casserole, layer the bread slices one on top of another, forming layers, and between them place a bed of bread covered with the chopped mixture, and another layer of ham slices, alternating until the casserole is full, forming a mass in the shape of a loaf, ending with a crust
5. Turn it over onto its reverse side and place it in a camp oven, where it will be kept until it toasts heavily
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón en Dulce (Glazed Ham)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- water
- three bottles of superior white wine (preferably Sherry)
- thyme
- rosemary
- spearmint
- nutmeg
- tarragon leaves
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the ham in water over low heat for twelve hours in a quantity such that the water is consumed at the end of that time.
2. Debone the ham, trim and arrange it in the shape of a loaf or cake, and wrap and sew it very tightly in a piece of linen cloth, well washed and rewashed.
3. Cook for twenty-four hours over low heat and until the liquid is consumed, in three bottles of superior white wine—Sherry preferably—taking care that the pot or pan is deep so that the wine bathes the ham well.
4. Add a little thyme, rosemary, spearmint, nutmeg and a few leaves of tarragon.
5. Cover the pot well so that all the steam from the boiling is consumed inside.
6. After twenty-four hours, remove the ham and place it, without removing the wrapping, in a dish or mold of proportionate size.
7. Place much weight on top with small boards to press it well.
8. When it is completely cold, overturn the mold and place and unwrap the ham on a platter, arranging it for presentation according to the aesthetic preferences of the preparer.
9. If you want it to make a good appearance, dust its surface with sugar, which is burned or browned with a hot iron.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón en Gelatina (Ham in Aspic)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one small ham
- thyme
- basil
- veal slices
- generous white wine
- pot broth
- two lemons, peeled and cut into thin rounds
- fine herbs bouquet
- four onions
- two heads of garlic
- six or eight cloves
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil

## Directions

1. Soak a small ham in plenty of water for at least twenty-four hours to desalt it
2. Cook it in water with a good handful of thyme and basil
3. Remove the ham and place it in a pot prepared with veal slices
4. Add generous white wine, pot broth, two peeled lemons cut into thin rounds, a bouquet of fine herbs, four onions, two heads of garlic, six or eight cloves, thyme, bay leaf, and basil
5. Let it cook well
6. Allow it to cool and serve with the aspic that has formed
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón Encebollado (Ham with Onions)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fine, thin ham slices
- pork butter
- two bowls of cold water
- vinegar
- twelve medium-sized onions
- one tablespoon of flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Fry and brown fine, thin ham slices in pork butter
2. Successively soak them in two bowls of cold water acidulated with vinegar
3. In the frying fat, soften twelve medium onions cut into dice
4. When the onion is done, add one tablespoon of flour and make a light brown sauce
5. Moisten and bind with the vinegared water
6. Incorporate the ham slices and cook everything over low heat in a covered pot for half an hour
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón Frito (Fried Ham)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham slices
- Flour
- Pork lard
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Scald the ham slices in a skillet with boiling water and give them three boils.
2. Remove the ham and immerse it successively in two cold waters.
3. Make it drain and dry with a napkin.
4. Dust the slices lightly with flour, just enough to coat them.
5. Fry them in three minutes in plenty of hot pork lard.
6. Serve with a pinch of pepper.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón Olózaga (Olózaga Ham)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- ham slices with some fat
- pork lard
- powdered sugar
- half a tablespoon of water

## Directions

1. Cut slices of ham with some fat and keep in water for three or four minutes
2. Make a small box from half a sheet of paper and coat its bottom well with pork lard
3. Place the ham there, adding powdered sugar and half a tablespoon of water
4. Place on the fire over the grill, turning the slices
5. After three minutes the dish is ready
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón Tapado (Covered Ham)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of beef butter
- six slices of ham, large as the palm of the hand and thin as the edge of two duros together
- ham broth
- one glass of Jerez wine
- pinch of pepper
- six eggs

## Directions

1. Melt one ounce of beef butter in a dish that can go to the fire or in a skillet for six ham slices
2. When the butter is sizzling, sauté the ham slices one by one until they pass and blanch, taking care that they do not toast
3. Remove the dish from the fire and place all the ham slices in a single layer, lining the bottom of the plate
4. Put everything back on the fire and moisten with a ladle of broth and a glass of Jerez wine with a pinch of pepper
5. Drop six eggs one by one on top so they set and cover the ham, which is only discovered when serving at the table
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jamón á la Austriaca (Ham, Austrian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Thin slices of raw ham
- Bread slices without crust, passed through lard
- Fine herbs
- Mushrooms
- Truffles or earth nuts (criadillas de tierra)
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. In a dish or casserole, place thin slices of raw ham and bread slices passed through lard.
2. Layer alternately with fine herbs, mushrooms, and truffles or earth nuts.
3. Sprinkle everything with breadcrumbs.
4. Cook in the oven for two or three hours to serve hot, or better yet, place on hot coals with fire on top in a casserole appropriate for this purpose, common in Austrian kitchens.
5. Serve hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Judías Secas a la Tío Lucas (Dried Beans Uncle Lucas Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried beans
- Water
- Onion, chopped in volume equal to the beans
- One tablespoon of fried and re-fried oil per quarter pound of beans
- One ounce of well-trimmed bacon per quarter pound of beans
- Salt
- One head of garlic, cleaned but not separated into cloves
- Bay leaf
- A pinch of paprika
- One tablespoon of strong vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash the beans very well and soak them in slightly salted water for two or three hours.
2. Cook them as instructed, according to their drying time, in plenty of boiling or cold water until the skin begins to wrinkle.
3. Remove and drain them.
4. In a pot, add the beans with chopped onion in volume equal to the beans, one tablespoon of fried and re-fried oil and one ounce of well-trimmed bacon per quarter pound of beans.
5. Season well and add a head of garlic cleaned but without separating the cloves, a bit of bay leaf and a pinch of paprika.
6. Let everything cook over excessively slow heat, and when the bean disintegrates between your fingertips, the onion has disappeared and the broth or juice presents a viscous appearance, moisten with one tablespoon of strong vinegar, give a final boil and off the heat, before serving, with the vessel very well covered, let it rest.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Judías Secas á la Tío Lucas (Dry Beans Uncle Lucas Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- dry beans
- salt
- water
- onion, chopped in volume equal to that of the beans
- one tablespoon of fried and re-fried oil
- one ounce of well-trimmed bacon for each quarter pound of beans

## Directions

1. Wash the beans that are to be prepared very well and soak them in slightly salted water for two or three hours.
2. Then cook them as stated, according to their drying time, in plenty of water, either boiling or cold, and until their skin begins to wrinkle.
3. Remove them then and drain them.
4. Place them in a pot with chopped onion equal in volume to that of the beans, one tablespoon of fried and re-fried oil and one ounce of well-trimmed bacon for each quarter pound of beans.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Judías á la Carmen (Carmen's Green Beans)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- green beans, finest variety
- salt
- fresh water
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the green beans of the finest variety, cut them lengthwise and place them to cook for ten minutes with plenty of salt in boiling water at a rolling boil.
2. Remove them in a colander and place them in a bowl with fresh water, from where they pass to another bowl, also with water, and there they lose the excess salt.
3. Cold and drained, at the moment of eating them season with oil, vinegar and a pinch of pepper, and it results in a luxury salad if one knows how to garnish it.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Jugo de Carne (Meat Juice)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams of beef rump
- 250 grams of veal rump
- One veal foot or hand
- One large carrot
- One large onion
- Parsley, chervil, tarragon, bay leaf, and thyme in proper proportion
- Cloves
- Nutmeg
- Salt
- Pepper (optional)
- Water

## Directions

1. Cut the beef rump and veal rump into pieces the size of plums.
2. Place them in a saucepan with a large carrot and a large onion, cut into thin slices.
3. Make a small bundle with parsley, chervil, tarragon, bay leaf, and thyme, tied with white thread, and add this seasoning.
4. Season with clove, nutmeg, salt, and a pinch of pepper if desired.
5. Note that the seasoning should err on the side of being bland, as any strong spice would be inconvenient for sauces in which the juice will be used.
6. Cover the saucepan and sweat over high heat, moving the utensil frequently.
7. When the pieces of meat and the pieces of onion and carrot begin to brown, and when the juice they release sticks to the saucepan, add cold water in sufficient quantity so that everything is completely covered, without excess water.
8. Without stopping stirring with a spatula, scrape up the juice that is stuck to the saucepan.
9. Add the veal foot or hand, cut into three or four pieces.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Langosta a la Americana (Lobster American Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster
- butter from Isigny
- four large onions the size of eggs
- dry white wine
- Spanish sauce
- tomato puree
- Cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the lobster in the natural way, that is, in cold water in which it bathes well, remove it at the first boil and let it cool to completely remove the shell.
2. Cut the tail into large pieces and place them in a deep serving dish that can go to the fire forming a ring.
3. With the meat from the legs and everything found in the interior of the head make a very fine chopped mixture, and with it fill the hollow of the ring.
4. Sauté well in a saucepan with butter from Isigny four large onions the size of eggs, chop them very finely, and after three minutes, the sautéed mixture, moisten with dry white wine and let cook half an hour.
5. Then add Spanish sauce and tomato puree in equal parts, and a hint of Cayenne pepper.
6. Reduce the sauce by two-thirds over vivid fire, and strain through fine sieve, pouring it into the serving dish over the lobster.
7. Ten minutes before serving this dish, prepared as has been said, cook in the oven, over gentle fire for a quarter of an hour.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Langosta al Chocolate (Lobster with Chocolate)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lobster
- Fine clarified oil
- Onions, finely chopped
- Parsley
- Chervil
- Spices
- ½ ounce powdered chocolate
- Pot liquor broth
- 1 glass of Jerez wine

## Directions

1. In fine clarified oil, sauté or braise finely chopped onions, parsley, chervil and a dash of spices
2. Bind with half an ounce of powdered chocolate and lengthen the sauce with a little pot liquor broth, sprinkled with a glass of Jerez wine
3. Blanch and divide the lobster beforehand into small pieces, preserving each one its part of shell
4. Finish cooking over low heat, incorporating the lobster into the already prepared sauce, with fire on top of the pot lid
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Langosta en Ensalada (Lobster Salad)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cooked lobster
- Hard-boiled egg yolks
- Capers
- Small pickled cucumbers
- Anchovy fillets
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Pepper powder
- English or French mustard, optional

## Directions

1. Cook the lobster
2. Break it apart and extract the meat
3. Make a salad composed of hard-boiled egg yolks, capers, small pickled cucumbers cut into thin rounds and anchovy fillets
4. Season everything with oil, vinegar and pepper powder, and if desired, English or French mustard
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Langosta en Salsa de Langosta (Lobster in Lobster Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cooked lobster
- Fine herbs, finely chopped
- 2 shallots, finely chopped
- 2 tablespoons refined oil
- 1 small glass of aniseed brandy
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Open the cooked crustacean and extract from the body the substance that fills it
2. If the lobster is female, add the thousands of roe that are attached in clusters below the first articulation of the tail
3. Crush and beat and mix all of this with a wooden spatula
4. Add the finely chopped fine herbs and shallots, two tablespoons of refined oil and one small glass of aniseed brandy
5. Once the mixture is well made, clarify or lengthen it with lemon juice in sufficient quantity to give the sauce the consistency desired
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Langosta á la Mayonesa (Lobster with Mayonnaise)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cooked lobster
- Mayonnaise sauce
- Hard-boiled egg yolks
- Lettuce hearts
- Various garnishes

## Directions

1. Cook the lobster
2. Serve the lobster whole and open, split in half lengthwise on a napkin
3. Serve the mayonnaise sauce separately in a sauce boat
4. Garnish the platter with hard-boiled egg yolks, lettuce hearts and other decorative items
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lapa Soup (Limpet Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- many limpets
- seawater
- a little oil
- a large quantity of garlic
- flour
- a little paprika
- crackers

## Directions

1. Cook many limpets in seawater
2. Remove the shellfish from their shells
3. Sauté them separately in a little oil with a large quantity of garlic
4. Sprinkle with flour and a little paprika
5. Add the water in which they cooked, passed through a strainer
6. Crumble crackers to serve the dish as a soup
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lapas (Limpets Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- many limpets
- sea water
- oil
- large quantity of garlic
- flour
- pimentón
- galletas (ship's biscuits)

## Directions

1. Cook many limpets in sea water
2. Remove the limpets from their shells and sauté them separately in a little oil with a large quantity of garlic
3. Sprinkle with flour and a little pimentón, then add the water in which they cooked, passed through a strainer, and crumble in galletas to serve the dish as a soup
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lechecillas de Carpa (Fried Carp Soft Roe)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp soft roe
- Water
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Oil for frying
- Frying batter
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Soak the carp soft roe in fresh water, renewing it many times over an hour.
2. In a pot over high heat, put a quarter of a liter of water with salt and a little vinegar.
3. When it begins to boil, add the soft roe to give two or three boils.
4. Remove and let drain and cool.
5. When cold, wrap in frying batter and fry in very hot oil until golden.
6. Serve on a platter over a napkin, as with all fried foods, with a garnish of fried parsley.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Leg of Mutton as Venison (Pierna de Carnero en Corza)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- white wine
- strong vinegar
- salt
- peppercorns
- thyme
- rosemary
- tarragon
- juniper berries
- mint stems
- bacon strips

## Directions

1. Make a marinade with good white wine and very strong vinegar, plenty of salt, peppercorns, thyme, rosemary, tarragon, some juniper berries, and one or two mint stems.
2. Prepare the leg of mutton as a venison quarter would be prepared, that is, skinned and larded with thin strips of bacon.
3. Soak the leg, well bathed in the marinade, for three days.
4. Remove the meat from the marinade and roast it carefully on a spit.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Leg of Mutton English Style (Pierna de Carnero á la Inglesa)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- twine
- flour
- white linen cloth
- turnips
- salted boiling water
- cow butter
- capers
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take a good leg and without skinning it, break the shank to fold it over the meat and secure it with many turns of twine.
2. Dust the leg completely with flour.
3. Wrap it in white linen cloth as if covering it, leaving no part uncovered.
4. Place in a pot and braise with twenty turnips cut into slices and enough hot salted water to cover the meat.
5. Cook slowly for two hours.
6. Remove the turnips and the leg with its juice.
7. Finish cooking the turnips separately in water and pass them through a fine strainer.
8. When the turnip purée has good consistency, add a couple of spoonfuls of thick cream, form into a pyramid, and season with salt, pepper, and grated nutmeg.
9. Remove the wrapping from the leg.
10. Melt cow butter and mix with capers.
11. Serve the meat with its garnish of turnip purée, over which pour the juice.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Leg of Mutton Hortensia Style (Pierna de carnero à la Hortensia)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- fresh bacon
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- fine herbs, finely chopped
- half liter of Sherry wine
- small onions
- sausages in strings

## Directions

1. Lard the meat with fresh bacon and prepare it as for cooking in a pot
2. Soak it in oil for twelve hours with plenty of salt, pepper and finely chopped fine herbs
3. Place the leg in a pot with half a liter of Sherry wine
4. Add a garnish of small onions and sausages in strings
5. Cook slowly for five hours with the pot well covered
6. Serve as a lunch dish, which can be completed with mushrooms Bordelaise style and lettuce salad seasoned with small onions
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Leg of Mutton in Water (Pierna de carnero en agua)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- long strips of bacon
- carrots, split
- three or four onions
- bouquet of fine herbs
- salt
- pepper
- leg of mutton, deboned or well tenderized

## Directions

1. Place long strips of bacon, split carrots, three or four onions, a bouquet of fine herbs with salt and pepper at the bottom of a braising pot
2. Place the deboned leg of mutton on top, or if not deboned, well tenderized with a mallet and with the protruding bone cut but not completely removed from the piece of meat to which it is attached, so as to be able to reconstruct the handle for good presentation at the moment of serving
3. Fill the pot with water and skim as soon as it begins to boil
4. Let it boil until the liquid reduces to three quarters
5. Remove the leg of mutton from the vessel, strain or pass the broth through a sieve, and return both meat and liquid to the pot
6. Cook from this moment with great slowness until it reaches its point
7. If the broth is clear or thin, reduce it separately
8. At the moment of serving, add to its broth a little Spanish sauce or the juices from roasted meat with a glass of white wine
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Leg of Mutton Peasant Style (Pierna de carnero à la campesina)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- beef butter
- salt
- pepper
- two or three onions
- two or three carrots
- one cup of degreased broth
- flour or potato starch

## Directions

1. Debone the leg, tie it and secure it well with twine
2. Sauté it in beef butter until it is well browned on all sides
3. Add salt, pepper, two or three onions and as many carrots and one cup of degreased broth
4. Cook slowly over low heat for five hours in a cooking pot
5. When the leg is almost cooked, strain or pass the sauce and thicken it with half a spoon of flour or potato starch, but only after it has been well degreased
6. Finish cooking without changing the intensity of the heat
7. Serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Leg of Mutton Provençal Style (Pierna de carnero à la provenzal)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton, good size
- twelve garlic cloves
- anchovy fillets, well washed, double the amount of garlic cloves

## Directions

1. Embed symmetrically in the meaty part of the leg of mutton twelve garlic cloves and twice as many fillets of well-washed anchovies, as if they were larding strips of bacon
2. Roast the meat in the same manner and with the care recommended for roasted leg of mutton
3. While the meat is roasting, peel one liter of garlic cloves
4. Blanch the garlic by scalding it in boiling water, submerging it repeatedly in different waters, then let it cool in fresh water
5. Cook the garlic in a bowl of broth
6. When the leg of mutton is roasted to its point, well degrease the juice it has released
7. Serve the meat with the garnish of garlic
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Leg of Mutton Scottish Style (Pierna de Carnero á la Escocesa)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- water
- pepper
- salt
- thyme
- bay leaf
- spices
- parsley
- chervil
- onions
- carrots
- white sauce
- pickled cucumbers

## Directions

1. In a large pot where the leg can fit comfortably, boil a large quantity of water with pepper, salt, thyme, bay leaf, all manner of spices, parsley, chervil, onions and carrots.
2. When the water boils vigorously, place the leg in the pot, cover tightly and continue cooking vigorously without stopping for an instant.
3. Cook for as many quarter hours as the leg weighs in pounds. For example, if it weighs four pounds, cook for one hour.
4. Remove immediately from the pot and serve with white sauce and trimmed pickled cucumbers.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Leg of Mutton Stewed Manchegan Style (Pierna de Carnero Estofada á la Manchega)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- aged bacon
- spices
- red wine
- vinegar
- onions
- turnips
- carrots
- aromatic herbs
- garlic
- pepper

## Directions

1. Bone the leg of mutton well and lard it with aged bacon, seasoning with some excess of spices.
2. Place in a pot where the meat fits comfortably.
3. Cook in a quarter liter of red wine and two spoonfuls of vinegar, with six onions the size of a hen's egg, turnips, carrots, aromatic herbs, a couple of garlic cloves and a hint of pepper.
4. Cook this very slowly for four hours with the pot well covered.
5. Serve with a little of its degreased broth.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Leg of Mutton with Dried Beans (Pierna de Carnero con Judías Secas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- dried beans
- water

## Directions

1. Prepare the leg of mutton as previously described.
2. Cook the dried beans beforehand in water.
3. Half an hour before serving, incorporate the well-drained beans into the meat and its juices.
4. Vaporize the water they contain by reheating slowly for about fifteen minutes, during which time they will absorb the meat juices.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Leg of Mutton with Other Legumes (Pierna de Carnero con Otras Legumbres)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- legumes

## Directions

1. Prepare the leg as previously described.
2. Cook the legumes beforehand in water and have them ready.
3. Half an hour before serving, incorporate the well-drained legumes into the meat and its juices after vaporizing the water they contain.
4. Allow them to stew slowly, cooking gently for about fifteen minutes, during which time they will absorb the meat juices.
5. Instead of beans, by the same procedure, the leg can be served with cauliflower, pea purée, sorrel paste or spinach paste, lentils, etc.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Leg of Mutton with Testicles (Pierna de Carnero con Criadillas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- mutton testicles
- thick bacon
- white wine

## Directions

1. After removing the bone from the leg of mutton and taking out half a pound of meat from the center, mince it with testicles to replace the removed part.
2. Lard with thick bacon and tie.
3. Lard again with testicles cut into thin strips.
4. Keep this way for twenty-four hours.
5. After that time, cook over low fire, surrounded by strips of bacon and moistened with white wine.
6. Strain the sauce, reduce it and serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lengua de Vaca a la Escarlata (Beef Tongue Scarlet Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One beef tongue
- Pepper
- Saltpeter
- Salt
- Cloves
- Thyme
- Laurel
- Casing (as for sausage making)

## Directions

1. Remove the hard, rough part of the tongue by holding it over live coals until the skin loosens.
2. After separating the skin, rub the tongue with pepper and saltpeter.
3. In a terrine, on a layer of salt, place the tongue and cover it completely with more salt mixed with cloves, thyme, and laurel.
4. After 24 hours, add more salt, and do so every day so the tongue is continuously bathed in this brine.
5. After fifteen days, remove the tongue from its marinade, drain it very well, and introduce it into a casing as if it were sausage.
6. Tie the two ends of the casing very well so it fits snugly, and hang it in the smoke for several days in a fireplace where wood is burning, but high enough so it does not become charred.
7. When ready to cook the tongue, soak it first for five hours.
8. Then place it in a pot full of water with thyme, laurel, and cloves. Cook for six hours over very low heat.
9. Let it cool in its own broth, and remove it clean of adhering fat.
10. Serve cold as a cold dish, either sliced on plates as you would serve sausage, or alternating slices with ham in aspic or turkey galantine, as a cold appetizer at a luncheon or light meal.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lengua de Vaca al Gratín (Beef Tongue Gratinéed)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef tongue
- white wine
- melted beef butter
- toasted bread crumbs
- beef butter, melted
- parsley
- shallots
- cumin
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place the cooked tongue in very well-cut slices in a baking dish or round serving dish that can go in the oven.
2. Moisten with white wine and pour a little melted beef butter over it.
3. Cover the entire dish with toasted bread crumbs kneaded with melted beef butter, parsley, shallots, and a little cumin, all very finely chopped.
4. Cook in the oven over strong heat for twenty minutes.
5. If there is no oven in the kitchen, place the pan over low heat so the dish does not brown underneath, and cover the pan or dish with a camp oven lid, well loaded with hot coals on top, for half an hour of cooking.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lengua de Vaca Asada (Roasted Beef Tongue)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- lard or bacon for larding
- salt
- pepper
- sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue for at least four hours as previously described.
2. Remove the skin and lard it like a piece of sirloin.
3. Place it on the roasting spit until it is well done.
4. Serve this dish with a sauce seasoned with salt and pepper, mixed with the juice from the tongue, or else over a purée of potatoes or dried peas.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lengua de Vaca con Pepinillos (Beef Tongue with Gherkins)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- water
- bacon strips
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- parsley
- onions
- veal strips
- beef strips
- carrots
- fine herbs
- broth
- gherkins

## Directions

1. Clean the tongue well and boil in salted water for about half an hour
2. Pass to cold water and remove the skin when cold
3. Season bacon strips with salt, pepper, spices, minced parsley and onions
4. Lard the tongue with the seasoned bacon strips
5. Place in a casserole with well-seasoned fine herbs, bacon strips, veal strips, beef strips, carrots, onions and spices
6. Moisten with puchero broth
7. Cook the tongue on very low heat for four hours
8. When serving, remove the outer skin
9. Have strained broth with gherkin pieces to pour over as sauce
10. Place on serving platter and pour the gherkin sauce over all
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lengua de Vaca en Pepitoria (Beef Tongue in Pepitoria Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef tongue
- beef broth, one cup
- white wine, one quarter liter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley, finely chopped
- two egg yolks
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked tongue into strips after peeling it.
2. Simmer very gently in a saucepan with one cup of broth and one quarter liter of white wine.
3. Season moderately with salt and pepper.
4. When the broth has reduced by half, arrange the slices symmetrically on a serving dish.
5. Away from heat, incorporate into the sauce finely chopped parsley and two egg yolks diluted in a little broth with a dash of vinegar.
6. Pour the thickened sauce over the tongue at the moment of serving.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lengua de Vaca en Salsa Picante (Beef Tongue in Spicy Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- carrots
- onions
- celery
- spicy sauce or sauce of choice

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue as you would cook the meat for cocido, with carrots, onions, and a stalk of celery.
2. Cook for six hours, which is preferable to five hours.
3. Remove the skin from the tongue after taking it out of the pot.
4. Make an incision lengthwise to open it flat on the serving dish.
5. Pour a spicy sauce or sauce of your choice over it.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lengua de Vaca en Salsa Picante (Beef Tongue in Spicy Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One beef tongue
- Carrots
- Onions
- One celery stalk

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue as you would meat for a stew, with carrots, onions, and a celery stalk.
2. Six hours of cooking is better than five.
3. Remove the skin from the tongue after taking it from the pot, make a lengthwise incision to open it flat on the plate, and pour spicy sauce or whichever sauce you wish over it—this is an appreciative dish and goes with everything.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lengua de Vaca Mechada y en Salsa (Larded Beef Tongue in Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- cold water
- bacon strips
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- dark espeso sauce
- blond sauce
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Soak the tongue for twenty-four hours in cold water, changing it several times
2. Blanch in boiling water and scrape to remove the skin
3. Lard with strips of bacon seasoned with pepper, salt and minced parsley
4. Cook slowly for four to five hours
5. Remove from heat and open the tongue lengthwise without separating it
6. Place on serving platter
7. Remove fat from the cooking liquid
8. Coat with a thick dark sauce
9. Can also be served with a blond sauce or tomato sauce instead
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lengua de Vaca à la Papillote (Beef Tongue in Paper)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue, cooked and cooled
- filling for cutlets
- fresh bacon, thinly sliced
- white greased paper

## Directions

1. Cut the cooled cooked tongue into slices half an inch thick, cutting them diagonally so they have more surface area.
2. Prepare a filling like that made for cutlets.
3. Coat both sides of each piece of tongue with the filling.
4. Place thin slices of fresh bacon on top of the filling.
5. Wrap the prepared pieces of tongue in white greased paper and roast them on the grill.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lengua de Vaca á la Marinera (Beef Tongue Seafarer Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- broth
- red or white wine
- small onions
- lard
- flour
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue
2. Remove the skin and cut into wheels
3. Strain the broth in which it cooked and remove the fat
4. Place in a casserole with two glasses of red or white wine
5. Add small onions fried in lard with a bit of flour
6. Reduce the sauce to the right consistency
7. Place the tongue on pieces of fried bread
8. Pour the sauce over the tongue
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lenguado al Gratín (Sole Gratinée)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sole fish for four persons
- Thirty grams of butter
- Two deciliters of white wine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Italian sauce
- Grated and toasted breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Choose fish for four persons; remove the gills, intestines and black skin; scale the white skin, wash and dry.
2. Make an incision on the skinless side of half a centimeter deep on each side of the spine, without touching it, tilting the knife from left to right.
3. Clean, then place in an oval dish thirty grams of butter, two deciliters of white wine, two pinches of salt, and two pinches of pepper.
4. Place the sole also and let it cook for five minutes, covering it with two spoonfuls of Italian sauce and sprinkling with a layer of grated and toasted breadcrumbs of half a centimeter.
5. Cover the gratin dish with the baking dish cover, with moderate fire on top, as there will also be on the stove, ensuring that cooking is not interrupted.
6. Serve at quarter of an hour.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lenguado Fiambre (Cold Sole)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- sole
- flour
- oil for frying
- mushrooms
- parsley
- scallions
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- oil
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the sole as if for frying, and make an opening along the spine where the dark skin is
2. Wrap it in flour and fry it
3. After letting it drain, carefully remove the spine
4. Prepare a filling composed of fried mushrooms, finely chopped with parsley, scallions, salt, pepper and nutmeg, seasoned with oil and lemon juice
5. After kneading the filling, reheat it well and introduce it in place of the spine to form a kind of savory sausage with the fish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lenguados a la Colbert (Sole Colbert)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- sole
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- oil for frying
- 100 grams of mayordoma sauce
- parsley
- lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare a sole and make an incision from the gills to four centimeters from the tail to remove the spine
2. Break the spine from top to bottom with the back of the knife so it can be removed easily
3. Coat the sole with egg and breadcrumbs, and fry it
4. Once fried, remove the spine and place 100 grams of mayordoma sauce in the opening
5. Serve with parsley and a lemon cut into quarters
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lenguados a la Colbert (Sole Colbert)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sole fish
- Egg
- Grated bread
- Oil for frying
- One hundred grams of majordomo sauce
- Parsley
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare a sole and make an incision from the gills to four centimeters from the tail to remove the spine.
2. Break the spine with the back of the knife, from top to bottom, so it can be removed easily.
3. Coat the sole with egg and grated bread, and fry.
4. When fried, remove the spine and place one hundred grams of majordomo sauce in the opening.
5. Serve with parsley and a lemon cut in quarters.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lenguados al Estilo de Normandía (Sole in the Style of Normandy)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sole fish
- Butter
- Parsley
- Thyme
- Chopped onion
- White wine
- Broth or water
- Salt
- Pepper powder
- Grated nutmeg
- Twelve oysters
- Twelve clams
- Truffle pieces or earthnut truffles
- Veal strips
- Ham or bacon strips
- Flour
- Small onion
- Carrot slices
- Bay leaves
- Mushrooms
- Lemon juice
- Bread slices

## Directions

1. After cleaning the fish, remove the skin if very large and place on butter in a fish kettle or baking dish where it fits comfortably.
2. Add parsley, thyme, chopped onion, a glass of good white wine and another of broth or water, salt, pepper powder, grated nutmeg, twelve oysters and twelve clams which have been briefly boiled to remove them from their shells and make them white, and some truffle pieces or earthnut truffles, putting it all on the fire with a little butter.
3. For the oysters and clams sauce: in another pan or small saucepan, fry lightly small strips of veal and ham or bacon without browning them.
4. Add half a spoonful of flour, stir everything very well and add a little broth, a small onion, carrot slices, pepper, nutmeg, bay leaves, and some salt.
5. When the meat is cooked, add this strained broth and sauce over the sole.
6. Place on the fire to cook, covering it with fire also on the baking dish cover.
7. After the fish is cooked, place it on the plate and add some mushrooms cooked with lemon juice, small slices of bread moistened with milk and fried in butter to serve it very hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lenguados al Natural (Sole Prepared Naturally)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sole fish
- Sea water or very salted water
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cook the sole in sea water or very salted water.
2. Remove from the fish kettle and drain.
3. Serve on a napkin on a platter with melted butter in a sauce boat.
4. Note: The sole should be added to the water only when it is boiling vigorously.
5. Ten minutes without interruption are sufficient for the operation.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lenguados al Plato (Sole on a Plate)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned sole fillets
- fine herbs
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- fine spices

## Directions

1. Cut sole fillets along the back from already emptied and cleaned fish
2. Cover with fine herbs chopped and reheated in butter
3. Place the dark side down on a plate in which fresh butter has been spread
4. Pour melted butter over them as well
5. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs, salt and fine spices
6. Place over high heat for two minutes to set and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lenguados Asados (Roasted Sole)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sole fish
- Butter
- Fine herbs
- Chopped garlic
- Salt
- Pepper
- Breadcrumbs
- Mustard
- White wine
- Mushrooms
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. In a baking dish or fish kettle, place a sauce of butter and fine herbs.
2. Add chopped garlic, salt and pepper.
3. Place the sole on top, covered with a light layer of grated bread crust and melted mustard.
4. Add a little white wine and some mushrooms.
5. Place in the oven at low heat.
6. When done, remove and add lemon juice.
7. Alternatively, it can be placed over coals on the baking dish cover.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lenguados Fritos (Fried Sole)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sole fish
- Milk
- Flour
- Abundant oil for frying
- Parsley for frying
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Make an incision on the darker side of the fish down the middle, from head to tail.
2. Soak in milk for half an hour.
3. Drain well and flour heavily.
4. Submerge in abundant hot oil for frying.
5. Fry for only a few minutes until done.
6. Serve adorned with fried parsley, which is eaten with the fish, well sprinkled by the diner with lemon juice.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lentejas Estofadas (Braised Lentils)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- dried lentils
- good piece of streaky bacon
- onions
- carrots
- salt and seasoning

## Directions

1. Season the lentils with a good piece of streaky bacon, onions and carrots, with good seasoning.
2. Cook slowly so the lentil becomes tender and delicate to the palate.
3. Avoid rapid cooking, which will make the dish hard and inedible.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Liebre Asada (Roasted Hare)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- bacon strips, finely sliced
- butter or lard
- flour
- meat broth
- white wine
- hare liver
- shallots
- fine herbs
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Lard the meatiest parts of the hare with very fine strips of bacon.
2. Before larding, briefly reheat the prepared hare in butter or lard in a pan just enough to firm the meat slightly and give it tenderness.
3. Allow to cool, then lard easily, following the model that also works for rabbit.
4. Roast the hare for one hour.
5. For the sauce: brown flour in butter, moisten with a ladle of broth and a glass of white wine.
6. Add the hare liver, finely minced, with two or three shallots and fine herbs, all finely chopped, with appropriate seasoning.
7. Reduce the sauce by half so it becomes thick and tasty.
8. Incorporate the juice from the roasted hare.
9. Serve the hare without sauce on the platter, with the sauce presented separately in a sauceboat.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Liebre Asada (Roasted Hare)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One hare
- Fine strips of bacon lard
- Cow or pig lard for sautéing

## Directions

1. A hare can be roasted without larding, but it tastes better when the meatier parts are larded with very fine strips of bacon
2. Since hare meat does not have enough consistency to lard raw, once the hare is ready and prepared to go into the roaster, sauté it in cow or pig lard in a pan and only briefly, just enough for the meat to firm up slightly and acquire tenderness
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Liebre Guisada (Stewed Hare)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- white wine
- butter or bacon
- spices
- two cloves garlic, crushed
- salt
- onion, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Cut the hare into pieces and soak in white wine.
2. Fry with butter or bacon.
3. Transfer to a pot with spices, two crushed cloves of garlic, and salt.
4. In the remaining fat, fry finely chopped onion and add to the pot with the wine in which the hare soaked.
5. Cook for at least two hours.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lo Romesco (Catalan Fish Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- minimal quantity of oil possible
- garlic heads
- one dried chili pepper, emptied inside
- one or more other dried chili peppers, finely chopped
- red wine from Priorat or similar fortified wine
- fish of all kinds, blue and white, large and small
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. In an iron cauldron, fry the smallest possible quantity of oil until it is boiling hot.
2. Add the garlic heads and one dried chili pepper emptied inside, which can be removed or left at the consumer's preference.
3. Remove from heat and add the finely chopped dried chili peppers, so that the red vegetable neither burns nor darkens from overcooking.
4. Sprinkle the prepared mixture with red wine from Priorat or similar fortified wine.
5. Clean the fish of all kinds, blue and white, large and small, and cut it into equal pieces.
6. Incorporate the fish into the mixture and add water so that it barely covers the fish during cooking.
7. Cook over high heat for twenty minutes.
8. Serve in clay bowls.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lomo de Cerdo en Cotelettes à la Remuñana (Pork Loin Cutlets à la Remuñana)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork loin
- lard
- salt
- nutmeg powder
- four glasses of good sherry

## Directions

1. Cut the pork loin into slices one centimeter thick, cut on the bias
2. Coat each slice lightly with lard and season with salt and a dusting of nutmeg
3. Place them in a frying pan with four glasses of good sherry and cover well
4. After ten minutes they will give off a very rich aroma
5. In less than another ten minutes the pan will be as clean as a plate
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lomo de Ternera á la Campesina (Veal Loin Peasant Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- a good veal loin cut lengthwise
- thin strips of bacon
- lemon slices
- raw onion slices
- oil
- salt and pepper
- butcher's twine
- white greased paper
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Lard the fattiest part of a good veal loin cut lengthwise with thin strips of bacon.
2. Place in a long serving dish, and place on top about six lemon slices and as many raw onion slices.
3. Pour in enough oil so the meat becomes well soaked and the oil overflows around the veal in the dish.
4. Season with salt and pepper in moderation.
5. After a quarter hour, turn the meat over, seasoning again.
6. After twenty minutes, wrap the veal loin, tying it well with butcher's twine, in white greased paper, and cook in a pan with the rest of the marinade.
7. When the meat is almost cooked, remove the paper and finish cooking over very low heat.
8. Serve with a little of its own juice and potatoes arranged around it, cooked beforehand and browned in the frying.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lomo de Ternera á la Flamenca (Veal Loin Flemish Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal loin
- cabbage
- carrots
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the same as the previous recipe, but cook the veal with fire above it in a camping oven.
2. When serving, garnish with cabbage and carrots cooked beforehand and reheated in butter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lomo de Vaca (Beef Loin)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef loin
- Suet
- Oil
- Salt
- Laurel leaves
- Onion
- Butter
- Paper

## Directions

1. Choose a loin of the best quality and place it in a suet melter with suet in branches; when the suet is about to boil, introduce it with a cord into the pot, leaving it until it is half cooked
2. Allow it to drain and place it in a cool location so that the suet, condensed by the cold, forms a covering and in some way a layer or crust around the loin
3. When you want to roast it, place the spit in front of a very lively fire, taking care to detach the parts of suet that separate in layers; then all the suet will flow, being careful not to baste the loin with it
4. Serve it in slices when cooked, and it will yield such an abundance of juice that it is a true inundation
5. Alternatively, roast the loin by coating it with oil after removing the bone; sprinkle it with fine salt and place some laurel leaves and strips of onion on top; keep it in this condition for one, two or three days depending on the season; pierce it with the spit through the thickest part of the loin, wrap it in paper well soaked in butter, and place it to roast over strong fire, serving a sauce separately
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Lomo á la Española (Spanish-Style Pork Loin)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean pork meat
- lard
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- ground pimiento

## Directions

1. Cut the lean pork meat into solid pieces about the size of chocolate ounces
2. In a deep frying pan with very little pork lard, brown and sear the pieces over a lively fire
3. Season with salt, abundant pepper, and four cloves of garlic, finely minced
4. Continue sautéing the meat so it cooks evenly
5. Dust with ground pimiento, and to prevent it from blackening, moisten the meat first with a small ladleful of broth and a few drops of lemon or vinegar
6. Continue sautéing for about half an hour
7. Then cover the pan and set it aside near the fire so the meat steams and puffs up
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Macarones Verdes (Pappardelle) (Green Pappardelle)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bunch spinach
- salt
- water
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 pound flour
- 2 ounces beef fat
- broth
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Cook a bunch of spinach with water and salt, and once fully cooked, pound it.
2. Mix this paste with a dough composed of four eggs, half a pound of flour, and two ounces of beef fat.
3. Mix everything well with a rolling pin, extending it on a board.
4. Cut into strips similar to noodles, leaving them to dry in the sun. This appears to be indispensable, as oven heat is not suitable.
5. Once well dried, cook them in meat broth.
6. Serve sprinkled with Parmesan cheese.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Macaroni al Gratín (Macaroni Gratin)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- salt
- water
- butter
- 30 grams grated Parmesan cheese
- 1/2 tablespoon toasted breadcrumbs
- 15 grams butter

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni as in previous recipes.
2. Line a small gratin dish with a thin layer of butter.
3. Fill it with the macaroni, forming a mound approximately six centimeters high.
4. Sprinkle with 30 grams of grated Parmesan cheese and half a tablespoon of toasted breadcrumbs.
5. Melt 15 grams of butter and pour it over the macaroni.
6. Place over low heat and cover with an iron lid with quite hot coals on top.
7. If the burner flame is lit properly, the macaroni should be cooked in ten minutes.
8. For fast days, macaroni gratin can also be made by substituting the broth with milk.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Macaroni al Jugo (Macaroni with Meat Gravy)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds macaroni
- salt
- water
- grated Parmesan cheese
- 2 pounds sirloin
- 2 ounces beef fat or pork fat
- 1 large onion the size of a fist
- parsley
- 2 ounces bacon
- spices
- 1 glass water
- 3 pounds tomatoes

## Directions

1. Cook the macaroni in plenty of water over high heat, seasoning with salt.
2. After a few minutes of cooking, and taking care that they do not fall apart and remain somewhat firm, remove and drain.
3. Arrange the macaroni in a deep dish in layers interspersed with finely grated Parmesan cheese.
4. Pour the meat gravy over everything at the moment of serving.
5. To prepare the gravy for two pounds of macaroni: brown two pounds of sirloin in a saucepan with two ounces of beef fat or pork fat.
6. Add one large onion the size of a fist, parsley, two ounces of bacon, and spices, chopping everything very fine.
7. When the onion is consumed without darkening, moisten with one glass of water and increase the fire, then add three pounds of well-cleaned and crumbled tomatoes.
8. As the tomato quickly reduces, add the water calculated for the desired quantity of gravy and leave everything to cook over low heat for two hours.
9. Remove the meat, which can be eaten cold at another meal, and pour the broth or gravy over the macaroni.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Macaroni de la Pulla (Macaroni from Puglia)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds macaroni, thin as a pencil
- salt
- 2 pounds tomatoes
- clarified oil
- raw tomato halves

## Directions

1. Purchase macaroni as thin as a pencil.
2. Half-cook two pounds of macaroni by dropping them into a large quantity of boiling water, seasoning with salt.
3. Remove from the pot or saucepan and place in a colander to drain the water.
4. In a very clarified oil and in a skillet, fry two pounds of tomatoes; season and pass through a fine sieve to obtain a thick broth.
5. Place the macaroni in a deep, round serving dish.
6. Pour the tomato sauce over and cover the entire surface with halves of raw tomatoes, upon which drop some raw oil.
7. Place the dish in the oven under high heat for fifteen minutes and serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Macaroni à la Napolitana (Neapolitan Macaroni)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- salt
- water
- beef stock from the pot
- butter
- Parmesan cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Prepare a good stew, making sure it does not burn and that the sauce takes on a rather dark color
2. When necessary to add stock, use stock from the pot
3. Put the macaroni to blanch or cook with salt and water until they reach the degree of cooking most suitable, since some prefer them very soft and others not as much
4. After cooking, drain them in a sieve so they drain well
5. Then transfer them to a saucepan that has beef butter, which should be kept near the fire so it does not cool and the macaroni will be well coated
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Macaroni á la Napolitana (Neapolitan Macaroni)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- salt
- water
- beef stock
- beef fat or lard
- tomato sauce from a good stew
- grated Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Prepare a good stew, ensuring it does not burn and the sauce takes a sufficiently dark color. When necessary to add broth, use that from the pot.
2. Blanch or cook the macaroni with salt and water until they reach the desired doneness, as some prefer them very soft and others less so.
3. Drain the macaroni in a sieve until they release all their water.
4. Pass them to a saucepan containing beef fat, which should be kept near the fire so it does not cool and the macaroni become well coated.
5. Pass the sauce or gravy from the stew through a sieve.
6. Arrange the macaroni in a serving dish in layers, alternating with grated Parmesan cheese and sauce until all is used.
7. Serve hot. If good beef fat is not available, pork fat may be used.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Magdalena Picón (Picón's Madeleine Cake)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of butter for melting
- butter for greasing the mold
- a quarter pound of ground sugar
- a quarter pound of flour
- lemon zest equal to about one teaspoon of coffee
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Melt two ounces of butter in a pan
2. Grease a mold or cake pan with melted butter on the inside and let it cool
3. In the pan, add a quarter pound of ground sugar and another quarter pound of flour, with lemon zest equal to about one teaspoon of coffee
4. Beat and mix all this with the melted butter until forming a homogeneous and crumbly mixture, like sand
5. Add two eggs, whites and yolks
6. Stir and beat with a spatula to bind the batter, like thick pap
7. Fill the greased mold and bake this in twenty minutes at a strong oven heat
8. Let cool and remove from the mold
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Manjar Blanco (Almond Blanc Mange)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sweet almonds
- twenty bitter almond kernels, all peeled
- cold water
- three glasses of simple jelly, somewhat warmed

## Directions

1. Prepare by putting one pound of sweet almonds and twenty bitter almond kernels in cold water infusion for twenty-four hours, all of them peeled.
2. Pound them immediately, sprinkling during this operation with some spoonfuls of very cold water; and when no fragment is distinguishable from them and the paste has enough homogeneity, add four or five glasses of water, strain this mass first through a cloth, pressing it with force, and then through a napkin.
3. Mix three glasses of simple jelly, somewhat more than lukewarm, and empty the resulting liquid into the mold.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Manos de Cerdo Trufadas (Truffle-Stuffed Pig's Feet)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's hands, already cooked à la Sainte-Menehould
- fine forcemeat for birds
- truffle trimmings, cooked separately in good white wine or clean broth
- caul fat from pork or veal
- melted beef lard
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Debone the pig's hands that have already been cooked à la Sainte-Menehould
2. Prepare a fine forcemeat as for stuffing birds and mix it with a good quantity of truffle trimmings that have been cooked separately in good white wine or clean broth
3. Stuff the deboned hands with this mixture and wrap them in caul fat from pork or veal, which is the transparent membrane that envelops the fat
4. Without delay, soak the truffle-stuffed hands in melted beef lard
5. Coat them generously with breadcrumbs and brown them on the grill over moderate heat, taking time to color them well
6. Serve the truffle-stuffed pig's feet alone, accompanied by a few sips of Champagne
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Manos de Cerdo à la Santa Menehould (Pig's Feet à la Sainte-Menehould)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's hands, singed, scalded and thoroughly washed
- bacon slices
- pieces of lean veal
- carrots
- onions
- clove buds
- bouquet of fine herbs
- one and a half quartillo of good red wine
- one and a half quartillo of clean broth
- melted beef lard
- breadcrumbs
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Split the pig's hands lengthwise along the inner curved side
2. Tie them around with kitchen twine, wrapping many times so they do not lose their shape during cooking
3. In a braising pan, place bacon slices, pieces of lean veal, carrots, onions with clove buds stuck in them, a bouquet of fine herbs, one and a half quartillo of good red wine and one and a half quartillo of clean broth
4. The amount of liquid should be proportioned to the quantity of hands being prepared, as cooking must last twenty-four hours and proceed very slowly
5. Allow the hands to cool completely after cooking
6. Cut the strings that hold them
7. Soak them well in melted beef lard and bread them with breadcrumbs seasoned with salt and pepper
8. Grill them over high heat to serve very hot and dry, omitting the sauce in which they cooked
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Manos de Ternera (Veal Hands)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal hand
- paste for frying made three hours in advance
- fried eggs
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the veal hand in the pot with the stew.
2. Debone and set aside.
3. The next day at lunch, cut into small pieces the size of walnuts.
4. Coat the pieces in the frying paste.
5. Fry them.
6. Serve accompanying fried eggs, or alone, with tomato sauce.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Manos de Ternera al Natural (Calf's Hands Prepared Naturally)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's hands, deboned and cut into medium pieces
- Broth
- Fine herbs, finely chopped
- Shallots, finely chopped
- Oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the deboned calf's hands, cut into medium pieces, in clean broth for three hours with the same seasoning and same ingredients as for calf's head prepared naturally.
2. When cooked, serve on a platter in slices or thin pieces.
3. Separately, provide a dry garnish of finely chopped fine herbs and shallots.
4. Each diner seasons their portion on their plate with oil and vinegar.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Manos de Ternera en Escabeche y Fritas (Calf's Hands Marinated and Fried)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Good vinegar
- Water
- Salt and pepper
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Shallots, finely chopped
- Calf's hands, deboned, cut into pieces, and cooked
- Frying batter
- Pork lard for frying
- Parsley sprigs for garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare a marinade with good vinegar diluted half with salted and peppered water, finely chopped parsley, and finely chopped shallots.
2. Soak the deboned, cut, and naturally cooked calf's hands in this marinade for three hours while cold.
3. Remove the hands from the marinade and coat them in frying batter.
4. Fry them in pork lard until they are a good color, crispy, and very hot.
5. Serve adorned with parsley sprigs on a well-folded napkin placed on the platter, arranged in a pyramid or dome shape.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Manos de Ternera en Salsa (Veal Hands in Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal hands
- onion
- parsley
- roasted hazelnuts, finely ground
- nutmeg
- pepper
- two bay leaves
- white wine from Rueda

## Directions

1. Soak the hands in very hot water for a considerable time.
2. Clean and peel carefully to remove all skin residue.
3. Cook at low heat for five hours.
4. Transfer to another pot with the prepared sauce.
5. The sauce should contain: onion, parsley, finely ground roasted hazelnuts, a little nutmeg, a pinch of pepper, and two bay leaves.
6. Cook the hands in this sauce for another hour at low heat.
7. A few minutes before removing from heat, sprinkle with several spoonfuls of white wine from Rueda.
8. Serve hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Manos de Ternera Guisadas (Calf's Hands in Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's hands, cooked
- Broth from cooking
- Flour
- Fine herbs
- Two egg yolks
- Tarragon vinegar
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the calf's hands as described previously.
2. Remove the hands from the broth and reduce the broth by adding a pinch of flour and fine herbs with appropriate seasoning.
3. Return the calf's hands to the sauce for a few minutes.
4. At the moment of serving, bind the sauce with two egg yolks dissolved in water and a small stream of tarragon vinegar.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Manos de Ternera Rellenas (Stuffed Calf's Hands)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's hands, deboned carefully
- Quenelles filling, well seasoned
- Spanish sauce
- Sherry wine

## Directions

1. Debone the calf's hands carefully and with precision, taking care to split them only along the length of the inner side without damaging them.
2. Cook the hands naturally until done, then drain and let them cool.
3. Fill the hollow of each hand generously with a well-seasoned quenelles filling.
4. Tie with kitchen twine so that the filling does not escape.
5. Recook for half an hour in Spanish sauce with a glass of Sherry wine.
6. When serving, remove the twine as the hands are placed on the platter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Manos de Ternera Rellenas (Stuffed Veal Hands)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal hands
- quenelles
- salt and pepper to taste
- twine
- Spanish sauce
- one glass of Jerez wine

## Directions

1. Carefully debone the veal hands, splitting them only along the inside edge without damaging them.
2. Cook the hands in their natural state, then drain and let cool.
3. Fill the hollow with a well-seasoned quenelle stuffing.
4. Tie with twine to prevent the filling from escaping.
5. Cook for another half hour in Spanish sauce with one glass of Jerez wine.
6. Remove the twine as you place each hand on the serving platter.
7. Pour the cooking sauce over top and serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Manos de Ternera Rellenas y Fritas (Stuffed and Fried Veal Hands)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal hands
- quenelles
- salt and pepper to taste
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- oil for frying
- spicy sauce or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the veal hands with quenelle filling as in the previous recipe.
2. Do not tie with twine.
3. Coat the hands in beaten egg, then breadcrumbs, then egg again.
4. Fry well until golden.
5. Serve with spicy sauce or tomato sauce poured over top.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mantequilla de Ajo (Garlic Butter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One dozen cloves of garlic, well peeled
- 60 grams butter

## Directions

1. Pound the garlic cloves well
2. Strain them and add to the butter
3. Continue pounding until the mixture is complete and uniform
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mantequilla de Anchoas (Anchovy Butter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams anchovy fillets, well washed and cleaned
- 60 grams beef lard

## Directions

1. Pound the anchovy fillets thoroughly and well in a mortar, without adding any liquid or seasoning
2. Continue pounding while incorporating the beef lard into the mortar
3. Continue pounding until the butter is well worked and uniform
4. Present at the table as a spread, or reserve for seasoning and garnishing other dishes
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mantequilla de Anchoas (Anchovy Butter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams of anchovy fillets, well washed and cleaned
- 60 grams of beef butter

## Directions

1. Pound the anchovy fillets well in a mortar without adding any liquid or seasoning
2. Incorporate 60 grams of beef butter into the mortar
3. Continue pounding until this butter mixture is well manipulated and uniform
4. Present at the table as a spread or reserve for seasoning and garnishing other dishes
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mantequilla de Cangrejos (Crab Butter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two dozen crabs
- One pound beef lard

## Directions

1. Dry the crabs in the oven
2. Pound them in a mortar until they are converted to powder
3. Knead the powder with the beef lard and place everything over low heat so that the lard liquefies
4. Pass and squeeze by compression through a metal sieve placed over a vessel filled with cold water
5. As the crab butter passes through the strainer and falls into the water, it will solidify
6. Remove the solidified butter from the water and shape it, while cleaning and drying it with a white cloth, repeating as many times as necessary to remove all moisture
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Masa Cristallante (Crisp Dough)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour
- Cow butter, three-quarters pound per pound of flour
- Water
- Two egg whites, beaten until snowy
- Powdered sugar, one quarter-pound
- A pinch of gum arabic, sifted

## Directions

1. Place the flour on the table and make a hole in the center
2. Put the butter in small pieces into the hole at a ratio of three-quarters pound per pound of flour
3. Add some water and two egg whites beaten until they reach the consistency of snow with powdered sugar and a pinch of sifted gum arabic
4. Mix everything together
5. Knead for half an hour
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Masa en Hojas (Layered Dough)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of cow butter per pound of flour
- Water, cold

## Directions

1. Mix the dough well, adding cold water
2. Flatten with a rolling pin until it reaches the thickness of a coin
3. Brush the surface well with cow butter
4. Fold in half
5. Pass the rolling pin over it again and brush again with butter and flour
6. Repeat this process as many times as desired to create layers, at least three times
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Masa para Bollos (Dough for Rolls)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour
- Beer yeast, one ounce per pound of flour amasada, or one ounce per four pounds of dough
- Water, lukewarm
- Cow butter, one good quarter-pound per pound of flour
- Three eggs per pound of flour
- Fine salt, two grams per pound of flour

## Directions

1. Begin by making a leaven: place a mound of flour on the table, about one quarter of the total flour to be used
2. Make a hole in the middle and add beer yeast
3. Pour lukewarm water over it and knead well
4. Place the dough in a trough, make two cuts in the shape of a cross, cover with a cloth and let it ferment
5. When the leaven has acquired the required quality, knead separately the rest of the flour with cow butter, eggs and fine salt
6. Join the leaven to the dough and fold the dough many times, sprinkling it with flour
7. Let it rest at least eight hours before making the rolls
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Matelotes (Fish Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of lamprey
- pieces of eel
- pieces of tench
- pieces of carp
- butter
- onion, finely chopped
- small onions the size of walnuts
- flour
- red wine
- salt and seasoning

## Directions

1. Sauté the pieces of lamprey, eel, tench, and carp in butter with the finely chopped onion and small onions.
2. Season well and sprinkle with flour.
3. Add red wine and let it cook for half an hour over high heat.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Menudo á lo Gitano (Gypsy-Style Tripe Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe or offal
- hot water
- vinegar or lemon juice
- salt
- veal hand or bone
- ham, cut into pieces
- Extremaduran chorizo
- Castilian chickpeas
- lemon juice
- garlic heads
- paprika
- bread soaked in saffron
- saffron
- cumin
- cilantro

## Directions

1. Wash the tripe or offal in hot water, then wash again with good vinegar or lemon juice.
2. Cut into small pieces and place in a pot with water, salt, veal hand or bone, ham pieces, Extremaduran chorizo, Castilian chickpeas, juice of half a lemon, and whole garlic heads with paprika.
3. In a mortar, mix bread soaked in saffron, three garlic cloves, cumin, and cilantro with some of the offal broth.
4. Add this mixture to the stew and let it thicken slightly.
5. Serve always very hot.
6. If using tripe, turn inside out before cooking.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza a la Inglesa (Hake in the English Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One large piece of hake
- Salt
- Lemon juice
- Melted cow butter
- Egg yolks
- Breadcrumbs
- Cow butter
- Cooked potatoes
- Fried bread slices
- Butter sauce

## Directions

1. Take a large piece of hake and after cleaning and removing the central bone, split in two from top to bottom
2. Clean both pieces well of small bones and skin, leaving only the two rounded loins
3. Cut into thin slices across, about half a finger thick, and flatten them carefully
4. Season with salt and lemon juice, place on a strainer to drain for a time. Dry with a cloth
5. Pass through melted cow butter mixed with egg yolks, then coat with breadcrumbs, pressing with the knife blade
6. Place the well-prepared pieces in a pot or pan with melted cow butter. With all remaining meat, make a forcemeat to fill a smooth mold, which will be cooked in a water bath one hour before
7. At serving time, reheat the pieces, being careful they brown on both sides. Empty the mold onto the platter, placing on top cooked potatoes mashed into the shape of purée and around the pieces alternated with slices of fried bread, and separately a butter sauce if desired
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza a lo Imprevisto (Hake Prepared Unexpectedly)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Whole hake
- Water
- Two ounces of the best butter, from Isigny or Montpellier
- Shallots
- Parsley
- Chervil
- One clove of garlic
- One spoonful of flour
- Water
- Small pickles cut like capers
- One egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cook the whole hake in water without any seasoning, in a short broth; leave in the fish kettle away from the fire to serve in a platter, after the fish is drained, with its sauce on top
2. For the sauce: reheat two ounces of the best butter in it, sweating shallots, parsley, chervil, and one clove of garlic, all very finely chopped
3. Add one spoonful of flour toasted to medium color and some water to dilute it
4. Cut small pickles like capers and, as the French say, blanch them, then bind the sauce with an egg yolk dissolved in cold water
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza Asada (Roasted Hake)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- hake pieces from the middle body downward, including the tail
- beef butter
- white wine, about half a quarter-liter for a quarter-pound of butter and three pounds of hake
- breadcrumbs
- parsley, chopped
- salt

## Directions

1. Take the piece or pieces of hake desired from the middle body downward, including the tail.
2. Soak the pieces and remove the scales, then dry them with a napkin.
3. Place them on wire grills to roast over low heat until they are golden on all sides.
4. Melt beef butter in a pan and remove the foam it releases.
5. Add about half a quarter-liter of white wine for the quantity of a quarter-pound of butter and three pounds of hake with breadcrumbs and chopped parsley.
6. Let the sauce boil only once, pouring it over the pieces and serving them promptly.
7. Proportion the quantity of white wine proportionally according to the amount of butter and hake used.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza Cocida al Natural (Hake Boiled Plain)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Hake
- Water
- Salt in grain

## Directions

1. Clean the hake and place it whole in a fish kettle filled with cold water
2. Put on the fire with a pinch of salt in grain, not to salt but to precipitate the gelatinous part of the fish
3. The moment the water begins to boil, remove the kettle from the fire and keep the hake there until serving or preparing it as prescribed
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza con Guisantes (Hake with Peas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- hake
- peas

## Directions

1. Prepare hake stewed as in other recipes
2. Sauté the peas separately beforehand
3. Incorporate the peas into the sauce at the moment of serving
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza en Salsa Vinagreta Caliente (Hake in Hot Vinaigrette Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Hake
- Cold water
- Onion
- Garlic
- Parsley
- All spices
- Good tarragon vinegar
- Six hard-boiled egg yolks
- Hard-boiled egg whites
- Fried hot oil

## Directions

1. Cook the hake whole in cold water over high heat. At the first boil, remove the pot from the fire as the fish is cooked
2. Work together for a quarter hour a mixture of chopped onion, garlic, parsley, and all spices with good tarragon vinegar
3. Add six hard-boiled egg yolks and mash and mix with the previous mixture
4. Add the hard-boiled egg whites cut into small pieces
5. At the moment of serving, place the hake in a deep platter. Pour over it the hot fried oil in quantity to complete the prepared seasoning, which is poured over all
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza Escabechada (Pickled Hake)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- hake
- oil
- laurel leaves
- salt
- lemon slices
- garlic
- water
- vinegar

## Directions
