1. Clean the fish well and cut into regular slices
2. Fry in oil
3. Once cold, place in a flat iron fish platter
4. Layer with laurel leaves, salt, lemon slices, and mashed garlic
5. Cover with equal parts water and vinegar
6. Can be served the next day or kept for several days in the same platter
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza Frita a Estilo de Cádiz (Hake Fried in the Style of Cadiz)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Hake
- Flour
- Abundant oil

## Directions

1. Cut the hake into pieces the size of walnuts, as if pinching small pieces
2. Flour the hake beforehand
3. Fry in plenty of oil
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza Frita a lo Vulgar (Hake Fried in the Common Way)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Hake pieces from the middle or loin
- Flour
- Beaten eggs
- Pork lard or oil

## Directions

1. After washing and scaling the hake pieces, remove the bones and skin while raw
2. Cut into thin slices and flatten them with a spatula
3. Coat in flour, then in beaten eggs
4. Fry immediately in pork lard or oil until dry
5. Serve. Some season at the table with a little vinegar or juice of sour orange or lemon
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza Frita á lo Vulgar (Fried Hake Common-Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- hake pieces from the middle or loin
- oil for frying

## Directions

1. After washing and scaling the hake pieces from the middle or loin, remove the bones and skin while raw.
2. Cut into thin slices and flatten them with a spatula.
3. Fry the slices.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza Guisada (Stewed Hake)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Oil
- Onions
- Shallots
- Garlic
- Parsley
- Flour
- Water

## Directions

1. In a pot, heat oil and blanch well-trimmed onions, shallots, garlic, and parsley
2. Before the onion takes color, dust with flour and season
3. Add water in sufficient amount to cover the hake in a single piece and place it in the pot
4. Bring to a boil over high heat, then let cook slowly for half an hour
5. Strain the excess broth, which is reserved for soup the next day; the remainder with all the seasoning constitutes the sauce for the stewed hake
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza Guisada como en Vigo (Hake Stew as Made in Vigo)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- oil
- parsley
- garlic
- salt
- breadcrumbs
- water
- hake
- lemon or orange juice

## Directions

1. Heat fried oil in a pot with parsley and minced garlic
2. Add salt and breadcrumbs, sauté briefly
3. Add water and the hake to cook
4. Once done, serve with a few drops of lemon or orange juice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza Pronta (Quick Hake)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- hake
- good oil
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- dried parsley
- cooking water from the fish
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook hake in good salted water
2. Place in a shallow metal plate or bowl
3. Using a wooden spatula and fork, remove all bones and skin, leaving the flesh clean and intact
4. Heat good oil (two tablespoons per pound of hake)
5. Away from the fire, add two minced garlic cloves to the hot oil in the pan
6. Sprinkle the hake with ground salt, a pinch of pepper, and one teaspoon of dried parsley powder
7. Pour the hot oil over the hake and cover the plate
8. When bringing to the table, add two tablespoons of the water in which the fish cooked and a touch of vinegar to the sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza Rebozada (Battered Hake)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- hake
- salt
- lemon juice
- flour
- egg

## Directions

1. Clean the fish well and cut into slices
2. Sprinkle with salt and a few drops of lemon
3. Coat with flour
4. Fry in plenty of hot oil
5. Dip in well-beaten egg
6. Fry again
7. Remove and drain well
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Merluza Rebozada (Breaded Hake)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Hake
- Salt
- Lemon juice
- Flour

## Directions

1. Clean well and cut into slices, dust with salt and a few drops of lemon, coat with flour
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mermelada de Albaricoques (Apricot Marmalade)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds of apricots, weighed without the pit
- two pounds of pilón sugar, broken into very small pieces

## Directions

1. Quarter four pounds of apricots, weighed without the pit. Cut the quarters into thin slices and place them in a basin or tub with two pounds of pilón sugar broken into very small pieces
2. Place the basin in a cool place, and from time to time move the mixture with a spatula. Leave the marmalade to macerate for eight hours. Then transfer it to a large pot over low heat and cook until it acquires good consistency, stirring constantly so that the marmalade does not stick
3. When it is well cooked, and if you want it to be finely made, pass it through a fine sieve very little by little, with great patience and while it is still boiling, so that the skins and fibers of the fruit remain in the sieve, which in ordinary marmalades form part of the dish, so that nothing is lost
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mermelada de Manzanas (Apple Marmalade)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fine, firm, and slightly tart apples
- lemon juice
- water

## Directions

1. Choose fine, firm, and slightly tart apples.
2. Peel and cut them into quarters, remove the seeds and their compartments, and soak the pieces in very cold water acidulated with all the juice of one lemon, which is pressed perfectly.
3. Remove the apple pieces one by one, drain them and cut them across into very thin slices the thickness of coins, which greatly facilitates cooking.
4. Put them in a saucepan without water over medium fire, until they begin to liquefy.
5. Stir constantly with the spatula so that they do not burn or stick to the bottom of the vessel, and when it is almost cooked...
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mero con Alcaparras (Grouper with Capers)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned grouper
- salt
- oil
- water
- capers
- almonds
- roasted garlic
- parsley

## Directions

1. Place the cleaned grouper in a casserole with salt and oil that is only warm
2. When putting it in, turn it once, then add water
3. When the fish is cooked, make a sauce with crushed capers, almonds, roasted garlic and parsley, pass it through a strainer
4. Shortly after, the grouper can be served with its sauce on top
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mero Fiambre (Cold Grouper)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- grouper
- blue court-bouillon

## Directions

1. Cook the fish in blue court-bouillon and let it cool in the broth
2. Remove and place on a platter, which is placed in the pantry, very tilted, so the fish drains well
3. When about to eat, place on a napkin in a serving dish, and garnish with watercress or lamb's lettuce
4. Serve with cold sauces: mayonnaise, remoulade, tartar, vinaigrette, etc., together with the vegetable that garnishes it
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Migas al Estilo de Teruel (Crumbs in the Style of Teruel)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread two or three days old
- Water
- Fine salt
- Oil
- Two garlic cloves

## Directions

1. Take bread that is two or three days old and divide it in half.
2. Taking either half, make vertical cuts at its angles, and then horizontal cuts, so that small pieces the size of chickpeas result. The crust is better than the crumb.
3. After cutting, place them in a napkin and pour a little water over them, stirring well so they become evenly moistened.
4. Dust them with salt, as fine as possible, in the necessary quantity.
5. This must be done at night, and wrapped in the same napkin so it does not lose moisture, keep them until the following day.
6. Half an hour before the time set for breakfast, fry oil in a pan, burning two garlic cloves.
7. When the oil is at its point, remove the garlic and add the crumbs all at once, stirring constantly with a rasera or iron skimmer for twelve or fifteen minutes, and very hot, serve them to the table.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Migas Canas (Shepherd's Breadcrumbs with Milk)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- a good piece of tallow
- half the kidneys of a ewe
- two bread rolls
- half a quart of water
- salt
- milk from freshly milked goats

## Directions

1. Place an iron cauldron over the fire.
2. Add a good piece of tallow to the cauldron, along with half the kidneys of a ewe.
3. While the tallow melts, take a wooden plate that is quite deep and crumble two bread rolls in it as if for soup.
4. Once the tallow is melted and the solid residues have been removed, add one piece of bread to the cauldron and let it brown there for at least two minutes to remove the raw taste from the tallow, then remove it and discard.
5. Add the bread prepared in the wooden plate, crumbled as for soup, and press it slightly with a wooden spoon.
6. Using the wooden spoon held upside down (with the back facing up), pour about half a quart of water over it so it falls scattered, moistening all the bread.
7. Add a bit of well-ground salt immediately.
8. As soon as the cauldron begins to steam, start stirring and crumbling the bread with the spoon, and from time to time take the cauldron by its handle and give it a special shake (a mixture of force and skill), turning over the entire mass so what is below comes to the top and vice versa.
9. After ten more minutes, remove the cauldron from the fire.
10. Make a hole in the middle of the breadcrumbs with the spoon and pour in freshly milked goat's milk, which transforms the dish into migas canas (breadcrumbs with milk).
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Migas de Ministro (Minister's Breadcrumbs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two French rolls
- salt
- sweet paprika
- two garlic cloves
- watered milk
- one quarter pound of bacon, cut into pieces

## Directions

1. Cut two French rolls into walnut-sized pieces and place them in a bowl or mortar with salt, sweet paprika, two finely chopped garlic cloves, and watered milk so the bread becomes well moistened without becoming too soggy.
2. Invert one bowl over another (the second without an opening) and, holding both with your hands, shake them vigorously by inclining the first bowl's opening downward so any excess water can drain out.
3. When done, the salt and paprika will be well mixed with the bread.
4. In a frying pan over high heat, add one quarter pound of bacon pieces, and when it has released half of its fat, pour it over the bread.
5. Cover again with one bowl over another and shake them anew, while the remaining bacon in the pan melts and becomes golden brown.
6. Then add the bread to the pan, and without setting down the skimmer, crush and pound it while it fries and absorbs all the fat.
7. When the breadcrumbs are dry on the outside and saturated with fat on the inside, and when the pan is clean and shining without a drop of oil, remove them and arrange them in a dome shape on a napkin placed on a serving platter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Migas Vanguardia (Avant-Garde Crumbs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One loaf or large bread
- Water
- Salt to taste
- Oil in quantity equal to the volume of bread
- Half a dozen garlic heads, whole and separated into cloves

## Directions

1. Cut slices from the loaf one finger thick and soak them in water seasoned with salt to taste.
2. Drain the water and dry the bread slices with a napkin.
3. Heat oil in a pan or skillet in quantity equal to the volume of bread.
4. When the oil is very hot, fry and burn half a dozen garlic heads, whole and with their cloves separated.
5. Remove and discard the garlic, and over high heat fry the bread slices in that oil until their surfaces are toasted.
6. Then, holding the pan firmly with one hand, crumble the bread with the other, armed with a spatula or skimmer, crumbling everything equally, without interrupting the frying.
7. Once all the bread pieces are well colored and have acquired a certain hardness and looseness, remove the crumbs and serve them, dusting with a little salt or with sugar, according to whether they are to be eaten with another dish or alone.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mirotón de Carne de Vaca (Beef Miroton)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- good broth
- parsley
- scallions
- tarragon
- chervil
- capers
- pickled gherkins
- salt
- pepper
- beef

## Directions

1. Pour good broth into a pan, add parsley, scallions, tarragon, chervil, capers and pickled gherkins, salt and pepper
2. Chop everything and place the beef, cut into thin slices, on top
3. Make a second layer similar to the one below
4. Cover and simmer very gently for half an hour
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Miscelánea de Perdices (Partridge Miscellany)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- small onions
- carrots
- turnips
- Brussels sprouts
- truffles
- spices
- parsley
- bay leaf
- mint
- thyme
- rosemary

## Directions

1. Bone the partridges after stewing and cut their meat into strips
2. Arrange in a pyramid on a platter with small onions, carrots, turnips, Brussels sprouts, truffles and an abundance of spices, parsley, bay leaf, mint, thyme and rosemary
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Miscelánea de Pichones (Miscellaneous Squabs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- butter
- bacon scraps
- small white sausages
- one carrot
- one head of cabbage, blanched and drained
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Sauté the squabs in butter with bacon scraps
2. Add small white sausages, one carrot, and one head of white cabbage that has been blanched and drained
3. When the squabs are cooked, drain the cabbage again
4. Place the squabs on a serving dish with the cabbage shredded on top and the sausages and bacon scraps arranged around
5. Reduce the sauce and pour over everything
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mock Eggs (Huevos de Burla)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Cream of choice (vanilla, lemon, chocolate, etc.)
- Or: almond paste or any aspic that sets when cooled

## Directions

1. Make a small hole in an egg with the tip of a knife.
2. Loosen the yolk with a thick needle so it can be emptied.
3. Once all the yolk has been removed, fill this shell with the desired cream using a funnel.
4. Place the egg or eggs in egg cups in a pan where they can be bathed in water.
5. Heat them in a double boiler (bain-marie).
6. Wash them and serve on a napkin as soft-boiled eggs.
7. They can also be filled with almond paste or any aspic that sets when cooled.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mollejas de Ternera en Pepitoria (Veal Sweetbreads in Pepitoria Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal sweetbreads
- white roux-based sauce
- fine herbs
- small onions
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- mushrooms
- artichoke hearts
- truffles
- clean broth
- two or three egg yolks
- verjuice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Blanch the veal sweetbreads in boiling water
2. Immediately submerge them in cold water (better if frozen)
3. Prepare a white roux-based sauce and add chopped fine herbs, small onions, salt, pepper and a clove
4. Cook mushrooms, artichoke hearts cut in quarters, and truffles in the sauce
5. Moisten with a ladle of clean broth
6. Twenty minutes before the vegetables finish cooking, add the sweetbreads
7. Thicken the sauce with two or three egg yolks dissolved in cold water and a spoonful of verjuice or vinegar
8. Serve immediately
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mollejas de Ternera Salteadas (Sautéed Veal Sweetbreads)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal sweetbreads
- beef butter
- tomato sauce
- financière garnish

## Directions

1. Blanch and blanch the sweetbreads, then cool in fresh water
2. Drain and cut into small pieces
3. Sauté in beef butter over high heat
4. Allow them to take on good color
5. Place on a serving platter
6. Top with tomato sauce or better yet, a financière garnish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mondongo Cubano (Cuban Tripe Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- mondongo (offal)
- hot water
- lemon juice
- salt
- pork or veal foot
- dried corn
- sweet potato
- fresh corn
- lemon juice
- garlic heads
- ripe plantains
- annatto (bija)
- bread soaked in saffron
- saffron
- cumin
- cilantro

## Directions

1. Clean the mondongo well in hot water and lemon juice, then cut into small pieces.
2. Place in a pot with water and salt, bring to a boil, and add a pork or veal foot with some dried corn.
3. When the meat is tender, add more water, sweet potato, fresh corn, juice of half a lemon, two whole garlic heads, and ripe plantains cut in half.
4. Place a small amount of annatto in a cloth bag and introduce into the pot; remove the bag when it first boils, and continue cooking everything until it looks all white.
5. In a mortar, mix bread soaked in saffron, three garlic cloves, cumin, and cilantro with some of the mondongo broth.
6. Add this mixture to the stew and let it thicken slightly.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Mondongo Veracruzano (Veracruz-Style Tripe Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- mondongo (offal)
- hot water
- lemon juice
- salt
- dried corn
- veal or pork foot
- pumpkin
- yuca
- malanga
- sweet potato
- plantains
- saffron threads
- raisins
- toasted almonds
- capers with vinegar
- herbs

## Directions

1. Clean the mondongo with hot water and lemon juice, cut into small pieces, and place in a casserole with water, salt, and dried corn.
2. Add a veal or pork foot for substance and flavor, and bring to a boil.
3. When the meat is tender, add finely chopped pumpkin, yuca, malanga, sweet potato, and plantains cut in half.
4. When everything is well cooked, add saffron threads, raisins, toasted almonds, two spoonfuls of capers with their vinegar, and herbs.
5. Let cook for about an hour, remove from heat, let rest a bit, and serve hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Morteruelo (Manchego Pâté)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork liver
- Loin
- Poultry
- Oil
- Garlic, fried
- Bread
- Spices

## Directions

1. Take pork liver, loin, and poultry, and sauté them with oil and fried garlic
2. Cook everything thoroughly so that the poultry bones can be removed and the loin and liver fall apart
3. Place all the cooked meat inside a clean mortar and pound it well
4. Pass it through a strainer
5. Place it in the broth where all these meats have cooked and mix well
6. If you like spices, season with spices
7. Grate bread and add it to the broth, incorporating it with the aforementioned meats until everything forms a paste
8. Put it in earthenware jars and store it for some time, keeping it free of flies
9. Eat it as desired
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Muselina de Bacalao (Cod Mousse)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cod soaked and cleaned of skin and bones
- Three tablespoons of flour
- One tablespoon of water
- Two tablespoons of oil
- One egg yolk
- Seasoning
- A few drops of cognac

## Directions

1. Soak and clean the cod, shred it
2. Soak the shredded cod for two hours before serving in a paste made from flour, water, oil, egg yolk, seasoning, and a few drops of cognac
3. With a fork, take the shredded cod pieces and sauté them in plenty of good oil over very high heat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Nabos al Estilo de Westfalia (Turnips Westphalian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces of pork lard
- 1/4 pound of Aviles ham, cut into tiny pieces
- 1 teaspoon of flour
- 1 quart of cold water
- 2 pounds of tender turnips, scraped and washed
- spices to taste

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, blanch the ham pieces in the pork lard
2. Add 1 teaspoon of flour and mix without letting it brown
3. Wet everything with 1 quart of cold water
4. When it comes to a boil, add the 2 pounds of tender turnips, scraped and washed beforehand
5. Season with spices to taste
6. Let cook for an hour and a half over very low heat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Nabos Glaseados (Glazed Turnips)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips of pear shape
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- broth
- white wine
- cinnamon stick

## Directions

1. Scrape and wash the turnips of pear shape
2. Cut them in half, trimming the tip and head
3. Blanch them like carrots and give them a boil in salted water
4. Drain and place them in a single layer in a baking dish without overlapping
5. Cover them with a little butter
6. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and extend with broth, allowing it to reduce over high heat
7. Remove the turnips and place them in the same arrangement on a serving plate
8. Pour over the remaining cooking juices, reinforced with a glass of white wine and a cinnamon stick
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Natillas Amarillas (Yellow Custard)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eight egg yolks
- half pound of powdered sugar
- one quarter liter of milk
- lady fingers
- powdered sugar for dusting
- cinnamon
- confectionery or similar toppings

## Directions

1. Beat eight egg yolks in a saucepan; add half a pound of powdered sugar and beat again; then pour in a quarter liter of milk slowly, without ceasing to beat it so that it binds well.
2. Put everything over low heat, without ceasing to beat it slowly, and when it comes to a boil remove it, pouring it into the platter in which it is to be served, placing some lady fingers on top, and dusted with very fine powdered sugar and a little cinnamon, confectionery or similar items.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Natillas Blancas (White Custard)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve egg whites
- two egg yolks
- half quarter liter of milk
- sugar
- ginger biscuits

## Directions

1. Beat twelve egg whites well to stiff peaks, and add two yolks mixed beforehand with half a quarter liter of very sweetened milk, to achieve a papilla that, placed in a deep platter or terrine in a water bath with heat on top, produces white custard.
2. The surface is completely covered with black ginger biscuits.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Olla Podrida (Mixed Boiled Meats and Vegetables)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickpeas
- Meat
- Hen
- Bacon
- Ham
- Pork feet
- Pork ear
- Stuffings
- Poultry offal

## Directions

1. Once the chickpeas and meat have been prepared as described, skim it
2. Add a hen, bacon, ham, feet, pork ear, stuffings, poultry offal and everything that Nature created to be eaten, cooked together
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Olla Podrida en Pastel (Mixed Boiled Meats and Vegetables in Pastry)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Hen or beef
- Lean bacon
- Poultry as desired
- Pork loin
- Sausages
- Hare
- Blood sausages
- Beef tongue
- Pork feet
- Pork ears
- Cured beef
- Vegetables
- Parsley
- Mint
- Garlic
- Onions
- Rye flour for dark dough
- All spices

## Directions

1. Cook the hen or beef and a piece of lean bacon with all the poultry desired, pork loin, sausages, hare and blood sausages; this must be roasted before being put to cook
2. In another vessel, cook cured beef, beef tongues, pork feet and ears
3. From the broth of both pots, pour into a vessel and cook the vegetables, parsley, mint, garlic and onions there, which should be roasted beforehand
4. Remove all this in pieces, so that it is well divided, and let it cool
5. Make a thick bowl or mold of dark rye flour dough, and fill it with said meats, seasoning it with all spices
6. Once filled, put it in the oven, and when the dough is half cooked, make a hole in it and pour in the broth, letting it cook for one hour
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Otras Salsas Españolas (Other Spanish Sauces)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- broth
- game and poultry substance
- white wine
- parsley
- small onion
- bay leaf
- garlic head
- cloves
- oil
- cilantro
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- testicles
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Boil and skim the foam in a casserole a certain quantity of broth, to which the substance of small game and poultry will be added, and if desired juice, defatting it and straining through a sieve.
2. Also prepare with equal parts of substance and broth, a glass of white wine, a bundle of parsley, a small onion, a bay leaf, a head of garlic, two cloves, two or three spoonfuls of oil, a bundle of cilantro, an onion sliced in rings, all of which should boil for two hours, then defatted and salt and pepper added.
3. With testicles, mushrooms and sufficient quantity of substance or defatted broth, the same sauce as before is achieved.
4. The meatless sauce is made by lining the bottom of a casserole with oil, and placing in it carrots, onions cut in rings, and slices of fish of all kinds; immediately moisten with meatless broth and bring to boil. Add garlic, mushrooms and white wine until everything reduces to a regular consistency.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pajel (Mediterranean Bream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Pajeles
- Short court bouillon

## Directions

1. Cook the pajeles in short court bouillon
2. Place them without overlapping in a baking dish or pan that can go to the fire
3. Sprinkle with oil and a little salt
4. Give them ten minutes in the oven at high heat to make a very delicate dish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pajelete Cantábrico Empapelado (Cantabrian Pajelete in Paper)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Pajelete
- Oil
- Breadcrumbs
- Chopped parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine oil
- One clove of garlic
- White paper
- Oil for coating paper

## Directions

1. Clean the pajelete and grease it with oil, fill the belly with crumbled bread kneaded with chopped parsley, salt and pepper, fine oil and one clove of garlic
2. Wrap it in a sheet of white paper coated with oil and roast it on grills
3. Serve without removing it from the paper
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Paladar a la Casera (Beef Palate, Homestyle)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- palate
- water
- butter
- onion
- broth
- parsley bundle
- salt
- pepper
- mustard

## Directions

1. Clean carefully and cook in water
2. Cut into strips
3. Brown onion in butter
4. When half fried, add the palate with it
5. Moisten with broth, add a bundle of parsley, salt and pepper
6. Let the sauce reduce and serve with a little mustard
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Paladar a la Lionesa (Beef Palate Lyonnaise)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- palate
- onion puree

## Directions

1. Clean and blanch the palate
2. Place it on the grill for some moments to remove the skin
3. Cook it also in blanqueta
4. Cut into pieces and add a puree of onions, well heated
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Paladar de Vaca (Beef or Ox Palate)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef or ox palate
- water

## Directions

1. Clean the palate well
2. Scald it and soak it in hot water to be able to remove the skin
3. When well prepared, cook it for four or five hours in a blanqueta or white stew
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Paladar de Vaca en Salpicón (Beef Palate in Piquant Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- palate
- salt
- vinegar
- pepper
- herbs
- garlic
- parsley
- oil

## Directions

1. Place in a plate salt, a spoonful of vinegar, and pepper
2. Beat it all together
3. Add fine herbs, garlic, parsley, all chopped, and three spoonfuls of oil
4. Cook the pieces of palate cut into thin slices in this mixture
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Paladar de Vaca Esparrillado (Grilled Beef Palate)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate
- refined oil
- salt
- pepper
- garlic cloves or shallots
- parsley
- spicy sauce or tomato sauce or pepitoria sauce

## Directions

1. After cooking, drain the palate and cool it in its own broth.
2. Soak it in a marinade of refined oil with salt, pepper, two cloves of garlic, or better yet, some finely trimmed shallots with a little parsley.
3. Let the palate soak for a couple of hours, turning it frequently so the oil penetrates well on all sides.
4. Place it on the grill like a beefsteak and serve with a spicy sauce, or with tomato, or with pepitoria sauce prepared as explained in the formula for beef tongue in pepitoria.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Paladar de Vaca Frito (Fried Beef Palate)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef palate
- beef broth, half a cup
- vinegar, half a cup
- salt
- pepper
- garlic cloves, about six
- beef butter, sixty grams
- flour, half a tablespoon
- flour for coating
- pork lard for frying
- parsley sprigs

## Directions

1. Cut a cooked and prepared palate into long pieces as thick as fingers.
2. Soak them in a marinade of half a cup of broth and half a cup of vinegar with plenty of salt, pepper, and about six finely chopped cloves of garlic.
3. Warm this marinade slightly so that sixty grams of beef butter kneaded with half a tablespoon of flour can dissolve in it.
4. Let the dish soak for three hours.
5. Remove the pieces, drain them, and coat them with flour for frying very crisp in pork lard.
6. Serve this dish with fried parsley sprigs.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pasta de Buñuelo para Entremeses de Dulce (Fritter Batter for Sweet Courses)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams flour
- warm water as needed
- 60 grams beef butter
- 1 cup white wine or 1 heaping tablespoon of brandy or cognac
- peeled apples, cleaned of seeds and sliced into rounds
- oranges cut into wheels
- torrijas (French toast)
- other sweets as desired

## Directions

1. Use the same formula as French pasta with the sole difference that when mixing add either 1 cup of white wine or 1 heaping tablespoon of brandy or cognac
2. Dip peeled apples cleaned of seeds and cut into slices, oranges cut into wheels, torrijas, and some other sweets, and fry them in this batter
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pasta de Espinacas (Spinach Paste)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinaches
- Water
- Coarse salt
- One quarter pound of beef or pork butter
- Flour, a couple of tablespoons
- Water from cooking the vegetables
- One egg yolk
- Water, two tablespoons
- Croutons
- Hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Wash the spinaches and cut them in any manner.
2. According to the volume they make, place on high heat a pot with water in quantity four or six times greater than that of the vegetable.
3. When it boils in bubbles, add the spinaches and coarse salt by handfuls—that is, a great deal of salt. After ten minutes of violent boiling of the water, and without covering the vessel, remove the spinaches and submerge them in a basin or bucket of cold water.
4. Remove them from there immediately and place in fresh cold water, leaving them for five minutes, after which add them one last time in another cold water, where they can remain ten minutes, or as long as an hour, until beginning to season them.
5. Remove the spinaches from the water, drain and discharge all the water they contain, and set aside on a platter.
6. In a pan or baking dish, melt afterwards one quarter pound of beef or pork butter, and before it is very russet add a couple of tablespoons of flour, and without letting it take color moisten it all to form a somewhat clear pap with the water in which the vegetables cooked; season well, let cook a couple of minutes and remove from the heat.
7. Once the spinaches are dry, chop them very finely on a board, and with their own water, in small quantities, pass the resolved paste through a fine sieve, collecting it in the vessel in which the pap is prepared.
8. Mix and blend well; give it a boil so that all that mass comes together, and away from the heat bind it with an egg yolk, dissolved in two tablespoons of water.
9. To serve this dish place it on a round platter, in a heap or spherical casque, studding its surface with croutons and hard boiled eggs split in half and symmetrically presented.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pasta Dough (for Ravioles)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One egg
- Two tablespoons of water
- Two tablespoons of flour
- A pinch of salt
- A small amount of butter
- A few drops of lemon or cognac

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients in a large bowl
2. Work together with a wooden spatula without handling by hand until a good dough forms
3. The dough will soon separate from the bowl on its own
4. Place on a table or cutting board
5. Dust strongly with flour and continue dusting while working slightly with a rolling pin
6. Spread into a thin sheet the thickness of a coin
7. Carefully and precisely, draw lines on the dough four centimeters apart, first lengthwise and then crosswise at the same distance to create squares
8. Mold and close the squares on the same board
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pasta Española (Spanish Batter for Frying)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- egg
- salt
- pepper
- aguardiente, cognac, or rum (one spoonful)
- milk diluted in water or water

## Directions

1. Place the flour on a plate and make a hollow in the middle. For half a pound of flour, add one egg, salt, and pepper to taste, and one spoonful of aguardiente, cognac, or rum. Mix everything very well together.
2. Add milk diluted in water or water alone gradually until the batter has only the consistency necessary to coat the dishes with a layer the thickness of a small dog's paw.
3. Mix well and beat with a metal fork, then let it rest for one hour.
4. At the moment of using this batter, add half of an egg white beaten until stiff peaks form, but only if you want the fried dish to puff up considerably.
5. Note that this puffing has the disadvantage of impregnating the dish heavily with grease.
6. This batter can also be used to fry fish, but as a general rule and for better taste, fried fish comes out better with simple flouring.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pasta Francesa (French Batter for Frying)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams flour
- one quarter liter of warm water
- 60 grams very fresh butter

## Directions

1. Place 250 grams of flour in a deep plate and gradually add one quarter liter of warm water, enough so that sixty grams of very fresh butter soften and melt.
2. The key to this batter is in how you work it so that it is uniformly bound together and has a consistency like that of well-made chocolate.
3. If not all of the warm water is necessary to reach this state, part of the butter may remain on the surface of the unused water, and in this case it should be collected with a spoon.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pasta Francesa (French Frying Batter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams flour
- 1 cuartillo (approximately 1 liter) of warm water
- 60 grams fresh beef butter
- 1 and a half egg whites, beaten to stiff peaks

## Directions

1. Place 250 grams of flour in a deep plate
2. Gradually add warm water little by little, just enough to soften and melt 60 grams of very fresh beef butter
3. Work the paste so that it is uniformly bound and has a consistency like well-made chocolate
4. If not all the warm water is needed, any remaining beef butter on the surface of the unused water should be collected with a wooden spatula and incorporated into the paste
5. When the oil is ready for frying, beat 1 and a half egg whites to stiff peaks and incorporate them quickly into the paste at the same moment the foods to be fried are dipped into it
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pasta Holandesa (Dutch Frying Batter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams flour
- warm water
- beer
- 60 grams beef butter

## Directions

1. Make this paste the same as the French pasta
2. Substitute half of the necessary water with beer instead of using all water
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pasta Italiana (Italian Frying Batter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams flour
- warm water as needed
- 2 tablespoons refined and clarified oil

## Directions

1. Make this paste exactly the same as the French pasta
2. Use refined and clarified oil instead of beef butter, in the quantity of only 2 tablespoons
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pastel Caliente de Codornices (Hot Quail Pastry)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Pastry dough
- Eight quails
- Clarified butter
- Salt and pepper
- Three hundred grams of calf liver
- Two hundred fifty grams of bacon
- Bread plug
- Truffles, crumbled
- Chicken sweetbreads
- Pastry, for the lid

## Directions

1. Make a pastry mold as described in the previous article
2. Prepare eight quails by cutting them lengthwise in two parts
3. Place them in a sauté pan with clarified butter
4. Season with salt and pepper and sauté over the fire for ten minutes
5. Drain and let cool
6. Make a filling by placing diced bacon in a sauté pan and adding salt and pepper
7. Sauté for four minutes, then add the calf liver also cut into small dice
8. Sauté for another four minutes, let cool, mash everything and strain through a horsehair sieve
9. Put a ring of filling at the bottom of the pastry mold, two centimeters thick, leaving the rest of the mold uncovered
10. Place eight selected half quails all turned the same side in the pastry mold, putting a bed of filling between them to hold them up
11. Form a second layer of filling and quails in the same manner
12. In the hollow left in the center of the rings, place a bread plug wrapped in a slice of bacon
13. Put a layer of filling over the quails and cover with the pastry lid, fluted and glazed, and bake in the oven
14. When the pastry is cooked, remove the lid and the bread plug from the center
15. Place the pastry on the serving dish and pour crumbled truffles inside
16. On top arrange a garnish of chicken sweetbreads and cover with crumbled truffles that project above the sweetbreads
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pastel Caliente à la Financiera (Hot Pastry à la Financière)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Empanada dough
- Flour
- Financière sauce
- Five rooster crests
- Five crayfish
- Five mushrooms
- One beautiful truffle
- Clear glaze

## Directions

1. Make a mold of empanada dough
2. Fill it with flour, cover it and flute the edge
3. When the dough is cooked, remove the lid, leaving a two-centimeter border
4. Remove the flour from the interior, brush the inside and outside with a pastry brush and dry in the oven for four minutes
5. Prepare a financière sauce
6. Make the garnish with five rooster crests, five crayfish, five mushrooms and a beautiful truffle
7. Fill the pastry with the sauce and arrange the garnish in pyramid form
8. Glaze the dough with clear glaze and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pastel de Liebre (Hare Pastry)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole hare, completely deboned
- 250 grams of raw ham
- equal quantity of pork loin
- 500 grams of beef shank
- bacon leaves
- truffles or mushrooms (optional)
- one good glass of cognac
- luten (hermetic sealing paste)

## Directions

1. Completely debone the hare and set aside the loins, cut each into three or four pieces, and the meat from the legs
2. Make a minced mixture with everything else, meat fibers, liver, etc.
3. Prepare an earthenware or porcelain pot that resists fire
4. Line the interior, bottom and walls, with thin bacon leaves
5. Place half of the minced mixture in the pot, and on top place the reserved pieces of deboned hare
6. Cover these pieces with the other half of the minced mixture, to which you can add thin slices of truffles or mushrooms
7. Moisten everything with one good glass of cognac
8. Close with bacon leaves as on the inside, ensuring the pot is completely full
9. Cover the pot with its lid and seal it with luten, a type of paste used to hermetically close vessels
10. Place in the oven at medium-strong heat and leave to cook for four hours
11. Allow to cool and serve in the pot at family tables
12. When wanting to give more solemnity to the dish, submerge the already-cold pot for two minutes in boiling water, and the hare pastry will detach easily, then place it on a platter by inverting the pot over it
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pastel de Perdices (Partridge Pie)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- six good partridges
- two pounds of calf liver
- one pound of fresh bacon
- one pound of onions
- a pair of turnips
- a pair of carrots
- salt
- spices
- aromatic herbs, with rosemary predominating
- one hundred grams of fine fresh butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- one glass of Jerez or good common white wine
- optional truffle slices

## Directions

1. Cook the six partridges in water with the calf liver, fresh bacon, onions, turnips, and carrots with appropriate seasoning, spices, and aromatic herbs, for four hours in continuous, slow cooking.
2. Remove the partridges and debone them entirely.
3. With the remaining cooked ingredients except the aromatic herbs, make a picadillo that is converted into a paste by passing it through a food mill with the aid of the cooking broth, which should be reduced by half and thickened considerably.
4. In a pot, place one hundred grams of fine fresh butter and incorporate two tablespoons of flour to brown it lightly without toasting.
5. Moisten with one glass of Jerez or good common white wine.
6. Incorporate and mix the purée very well, ensuring it is somewhat loose, which is achieved with the auxiliary broth.
7. Fill an earthenware terrine of the kind used for pastels of this class.
8. Cook for one hour in a water bath.
9. When cold, this pastel can include truffle slices if desired, and when kept in a cool place it keeps perfectly for days.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pastel de Salmón (Salmon Pastry)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two kilos of salmon
- Three pounds of fish forcemeat
- Pastry dough
- Salt and spices
- Puff pastry, for the second covering

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and bones from two kilos of salmon
2. Prepare three pounds of fish forcemeat
3. Mold a pastry that can contain the salmon and the forcemeat
4. Spread a layer of forcemeat on the bottom
5. Place slices of salmon four centimeters thick on top
6. Sprinkle with salt and spices
7. Alternate layers of forcemeat and salmon until the pastry is full
8. Cover with a pastry lid and then add a second puff pastry covering, and so on successively
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Patatas Asadas (Roasted Potatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Salt
- Butter or crude oil

## Directions

1. Roast potatoes in ashes or in an oven until the skin toasts and they yield greatly to pressure from the fingers
2. Eat with salt and butter or crude oil in place of bread
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Patatas Cocidas (Steamed Potatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes, unpeeled
- Water
- Salt (implied)

## Directions

1. Place an iron pot on the heat with just enough water to moisten the bottom
2. Place two or three rounded stones the size of eggs in the pot
3. Fill the vessel with unpeeled potatoes so that water does not touch them
4. Fill the space above the potatoes with a strong cloth dampened in water and wrung out, as if it were a plug
5. Cover the pot with its lid and place a weight on top
6. Maintain a medium fire to keep water boiling; potatoes will cook in half an hour from the steam
7. Serve the potatoes in the basket or between the folds of a napkin in an elegant metal basket
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Patatas Cocidas en Aceite (Potatoes Cooked in Oil)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Oil in large quantity
- Potatoes, sliced into wheels

## Directions

1. Put much oil in a pan and at the same time add potatoes cut into wheels.
2. Place the pan over low heat and let the potatoes cook; they cannot take on color or be firm this way, but they taste very good and are impregnated with fat.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Patatas Duquesa (Duchess Potatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Potato puree
- Cow butter
- Beaten eggs
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Lard or cow butter for frying

## Directions

1. Make croquettes or small meatballs of potato puree, manipulated with cow butter, beaten eggs, and plenty of finely chopped parsley.
2. Fry them in lard or sauté them in cow butter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Patatas Fritas (Fried Potatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes, peeled
- Oil or lard for frying

## Directions

1. Use a quantity of fat four to ten times the volume of all the potatoes
2. Maintain a lively and consistent fire throughout the cooking time
3. Use a splendidly wide and deep pan in relation to the quantities of fat
4. Cut peeled potatoes into thin wheels the size of a coin, into small squares the length of a paper cigar and one centimeter thick, or into thin sticks using a cutting apparatus
5. For thin sticks: hold one handle of the cutting device with the left hand and place the other handle into one of the handles of a bowl positioned on the work table; with the same left hand grasp the other handle of the bowl; with the right hand cut the potatoes by passing them over the cuts of the device, pressing firmly and in one direction
6. When cut into sticks, place immediately into very hot oil and remove at once
7. For wheels or small squares, keep them cut in a bowl of water; when ready to fry, remove with hand, place in a strainer, turn them over to drain a little, and while still wet, place in the hot oil where they will sizzle but fry better
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Patatas Guisadas (Stewed Potatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lard
- One onion
- Salt and pepper
- Broth or water
- Flour
- Potatoes cut into angular chunks the size of walnuts

## Directions

1. In lard, sauté a trimmed onion until it takes on color.
2. Season with salt and pepper and moisten with broth or water after sprinkling with a little flour.
3. Add the potatoes cut into angular chunks the size of walnuts and cook over very low heat.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Patatas Sopladas (Puffed Potatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes, sliced thin in wheels
- Oil or lard in large quantity

## Directions

1. Fry a few potatoes cut into thin wheels in a large quantity of oil or lard.
2. Remove the fried potatoes with a large skimming spoon and let them air until the liquid returns to boiling.
3. Return them to the hot oil, and they will come out puffed like fritters.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Patatas á la Camarlengo (Chamberlain's Potatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of potatoes
- One quarter pound of hard cheese, grated (Gruyere, Parma, Chester, or Flemish)
- Flour
- Beaten egg
- Lard for frying
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cook two pounds of potatoes, and when cooked, mash them in a mortar, adding a quarter pound of grated hard cheese.
2. Mix everything very well and mold the paste into croquettes.
3. Coat them in flour, dip them in beaten egg, and pass them through flour again.
4. When completely cold, fry them in lard and dust with salt at the moment of serving.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Patatas á la Mayordoma (Steward's Potatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes, cooked and sliced into wheels
- Fresh cow butter
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Place the hot cooked potatoes sliced into wheels in a tureen or vegetable dish with a lid.
2. Season with salt and pepper and add pieces of fresh cow butter kneaded with finely chopped parsley.
3. Put the lid on the dish and shake and turn the potatoes in the same dish while holding the lid so the butter melts and the mayordoma sauce distributes evenly among the potatoes.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Patatas á lo Pobre (Poor Man's Potatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes, cut into small angular chunks
- Parsley powder
- Garlic cloves, finely minced
- Salt and pepper
- Water

## Directions

1. Fry the potatoes cut into small angular chunks in a pan, and remove them when cooked but before they take on color.
2. Remove all the fat from the pan and return the potatoes to it.
3. Season with salt and pepper and mash them with a fork in the same pan over heat.
4. Add powdered parsley and finely minced garlic cloves; stir and manipulate the dish with the fork.
5. Add a few drops of water so it softens at the moment of serving.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pato Asado (Roasted Duck)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Duck, cleaned and prepared
- Butter or lard

## Directions

1. Spit the duck on a roasting spit after cleaning and preparing it.
2. Remove excess fat beforehand.
3. Roast slowly over the fire so that the remaining fat melts gently.
4. When cooking is nearly complete, increase the heat so the duck takes on color.
5. Serve on a layer of croutons soaked in its fat, with the roasting juice in a gravy boat, completely free of grease.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pato con Nabos ó con Aceitunas (Duck with Turnips or with Olives)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Duck, cleaned and prepared
- Bacon leaves, larger than the duck and one finger thick
- Two slices of very lean veal
- Two carrots
- Two onions with cloves
- Bay leaf
- Bouquet of fresh herbs
- Salt and pepper
- Two ladles of broth
- Turnips cut into small chunks, or parsnips
- Duck fat
- Olives, large and sound, pitted

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the duck, and tie its wings with twine.
2. Place the duck in a pot between two leaves of bacon.
3. Add two slices of very lean veal, two carrots, two onions with their cloves, a bay leaf, a bouquet of fresh herbs, good seasoning of salt and pepper, and two ladles of broth.
4. Cook the duck this way for half an hour and remove it from the pot.
5. Strain the broth and remove the fat.
6. Separately, sauté and brown turnips cut into small chunks or parsnips in the duck's fat.
7. Once the turnips have browned well, place them in the pot with the duck and in the defatted juice, so they cook together.
8. When cooking is finished, place the duck on a platter, the turnips around it, and pour the hot sauce over it and the turnips.
9. For duck with olives: pit the olives, which must be large and sound, and add them to the pot in which the duck cooks in its clean juice ten minutes before serving, so they only give a few boils.
10. Note: Duck with potatoes, with glazed onions, with vegetable purée, with chard and cabbage is also a tasty dish, but turnips stand out the most.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pato Relleno Asado (Stuffed Roasted Duck)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Duck, cleaned and prepared
- Stuffing of choice (same as for fattened pullets or turkey)

## Directions

1. Stuff the duck the same way as a fattened pullet or turkey, using the same stuffing or whichever stuffing is most agreeable to the palate or easiest to prepare.
2. Spit the duck on the roasting spit and place it in a shallow pan to which two supports have been added.
3. Roast in a strong oven without needing to touch it during cooking.
4. Use more time than if roasting a duck naturally.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pavo Asado (Roasted Turkey)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Young, tender, well-fattened turkey
- Bacon leaves
- Greased paper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Place the turkey on the spit, larded with bacon leaves and wrapped in greased paper
2. Roast very slowly for a considerable time so that it cooks through completely
3. At halfway through cooking, remove the bacon and paper so the turkey takes on color
4. Sprinkle with salt only, continuously basting with its own fat
5. Serve very hot, natural, pouring the thoroughly defatted juices over it
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pavo en Galantina (Turkey Galantine)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One cleaned and singed turkey, completely boned raw
- Half pound of veal, minced
- Half pound of raw ham, minced
- One quarter pound of very fresh bacon, minced
- Salt
- Pepper
- All spices
- Cognac
- Truffles, cut like half-pesetas
- Scarlet tongue, in small cubes
- Peeled pistachios

## Directions

1. Bone the turkey completely while raw and separate all its trimmings
2. Shred the meat from the breasts and legs and place in a bowl, seasoning with salt, pepper, and a few drops of oil
3. Let the turkey season for a couple of hours
4. Mince half a pound of veal, half a pound of raw ham, and a quarter pound of very fresh bacon, seasoning well with all spices and moistening with a glass of cognac
5. Knead this mixture with the shredded turkey meat and incorporate truffles cut like half-pesetas, small cubes of scarlet tongue, and some peeled pistachios
6. Shape the mixture into a cylindrical form, wrap it tightly in strong linen cloth with ten or twelve turns, and bind securely with string
7. Place the galantine in a braising pot over coals with a dome-shaped cover on top of the pot
8. Cook for three hours in concentrated broth that covers it
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pavo Relleno (Stuffed Turkey)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Minced poultry or sausage meat
- Cooked chestnuts, cut into four pieces

## Directions

1. Fill the turkey generally with minced poultry or sausage meat mixed with cooked chestnuts cut into four pieces
2. Roast as for regular turkey, but requiring an additional half hour to one hour
3. Follow the same rules as for roasted turkey
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pavo Trufado (Truffled Turkey)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Truffles

## Directions

1. Fill the turkey with truffles, increasing the quantity in proportion to the volume of the turkey
2. Account for the cooking time, as a turkey requires more time than a fattened hen or capon
3. The turkey may rest with the truffles inside for five or six days in a cool place with no humidity, as dampness would spoil the dish and make the flavor repugnant
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pecho de Carnero Esparrillado (Grilled Lamb Breast)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of lamb breast
- Lard
- Bread crumbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Shallots, finely chopped
- Lentil puree, dried pea puree, or bean puree, or sorrel or spinach paste soaked in meat juice

## Directions

1. Cook a piece of lamb breast in the pot of cocido before it is overdone.
2. Remove it, drain it, and coat it with lard to bread with bread crumbs.
3. Grill it on the grill, turning it, and each time dust it with salt, pepper, parsley, and finely chopped shallots.
4. Serve this dish on a puree of lentils, dried peas, or beans, or with sorrel or spinach paste soaked in meat juice.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pepinillos (Pickled Gherkins)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Small cucumbers (no larger than a finger)
- Salt
- Good wine vinegar
- Bay leaves
- Peppercorns
- Oil

## Directions

1. Clean the gherkins well with a brush and cut off the tip
2. Place in a tinned iron pot with two handfuls of ground salt, mixing it with them
3. Leave for twenty-four hours so they release their water
4. Remove and let drain for a few moments
5. Return to the same vessel and pour boiling good vinegar over them in sufficient quantity to cover
6. Cover the vessel and leave for twenty-four hours so they take on a yellowish color
7. Remove the vinegar and cook it alone over high heat
8. When it boils vigorously, add the gherkins and stir so they turn green again; four minutes of boiling is sufficient
9. Remove and place in fresh cold water where they are left to cool
10. Spread on dry linen to air, and when they have released the water, pack in wide-mouthed glass jars or bottles, arranging them one on top of another, placing here and there some bay leaves and some peppercorns
11. Pour white vinegar over them in quantity sufficient for the cucumbers to be completely covered, adding two ounces of salt per azumbre of vinegar
12. Finish the operation by adding oil, which remains on top like that of a small lamp, thus forming a layer that preserves the food from any spoilage
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pepinos Preparados (Prepared Cucumbers)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers
- Salt
- Fresh water

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers and cut into wheels the thickness of a peseta coin
2. Place in a shallow soup plate
3. Add plenty of salt as each wheel is arranged
4. Cover the plate with another plate
5. Every two hours, hold both plates with both hands, keeping them together, and stand them upright so the water the cucumber releases drains
6. After eight hours, remove and wash in very fresh water
7. Let drain and dry
8. Eat with the dressing desired
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pepitoria de gallina á estilo de taberna (Tavern-Style Chicken Pepitoria)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 fat chickens
- butter
- parsley sprigs
- saffron
- small onions
- mushrooms
- flour
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the chickens into pieces
2. Blanch for 3 minutes in boiling water and drain
3. Place in a pan with butter, parsley sprigs, saffron, small onions and mushrooms; stew everything together
4. Add a spoonful of flour and moisten with broth
5. Season with salt and pepper and cook
6. Remove the chicken pieces and reduce the sauce
7. Strain the sauce, dissolve egg yolks in it without letting them cook, and pour over the chicken pieces with lemon juice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pepitoria de Perdices (Partridge Pepitoria)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- pork lard
- flour
- water
- white wine
- small onions
- mushrooms
- garlic
- celery
- bacon

## Directions

1. Cut the partridges into four pieces
2. Brown them over high heat in pork lard, and when very golden, sprinkle with flour
3. Stir well and season
4. Moisten with half a quart of water and a glass of the best white wine available, and add very small onions, mushrooms, one clove of garlic and pieces of celery stalks
5. Cook everything for one hour at low heat
6. Place the dish on a platter and cover with a large sheet of bacon like a veil
7. Before serving, keep it in the oven for thirty minutes at medium heat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdices a la Tártara (Partridges Tartare Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- melted beef butter
- bread crumbs
- pepper
- salt
- aromatic herbs
- mustard sauce

## Directions

1. After plucking, cleaning and singeing, split the partridges in half
2. Soak them in melted beef butter, cover with bread crumbs and sprinkle with pepper, salt and ground aromatic herbs
3. Roast on a grill over low heat and serve with piquant sauce containing plenty of mustard
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdices Cocidas (Stewed Partridges)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- bacon
- thin strips of veal
- bacon slices
- carrots
- onions
- parsley
- meat scraps
- salt and pepper
- broth
- white wine, equal part to the broth
- juice of 1 sour orange
- zest of 1 lemon, grated
- tomato sauce, either fresh or preserved, optional

## Directions

1. Lard the partridges and wrap them with bacon and thin strips of veal
2. Prepare a pot with slices of bacon
3. Place the partridges in it with carrots, onions, parsley and meat scraps
4. Season and moisten with broth and an equal part of white wine
5. Cook everything over low heat
6. Strain the cooking liquid after removing the fat
7. Add the juice of 1 sour orange and a little grated lemon zest
8. Pour the sauce over the partridges
9. Alternatively, in place of broth for moistening, another sauce may be used, and tomato sauce, whether fresh or preserved, is in my opinion what best suits stewed partridge
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdices con Sardinas (Partridges with Sardines)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- sardines
- tomatoes
- lemon
- pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Pluck, clean and brush the partridges
2. Place one well-cleaned, beheaded sardine inside each partridge
3. Roast them in a casserole with tomatoes or lemon juice, a little pepper and parsley
4. When done, remove the sardines
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdices Encebolladas (Partridges with Onions)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- pork lard
- one pound onions
- bay leaves
- thyme
- parsley
- chervil
- broth or water

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the birds
2. Put pork lard in a pan and sauté one pound of finely chopped onions well with good seasoning, two bay leaves, thyme, parsley and chervil if available
3. Once the onion is softened, add broth or water until it forms a paste
4. Scald the partridges over high heat with a little lard in a pan, then add them to the onion mixture
5. Cook at low heat for about one hour if the partridges are not very tough
6. Serve them on a platter, covering them with the onion paste that results from this stew
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdices Escabechadas (Pickled Partridges)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- oil
- vinegar
- bay leaves
- garlic
- spices
- brine

## Directions

1. Pluck, clean and cut the partridges into pieces
2. Fry them in oil; when they are half-fried, transfer them to vinegar with bay leaves, crushed garlic and spices
3. Place them in a vessel, add brine, and cover with oil on top
4. Seal hermetically with parchment and place the vessel in a cool location
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdices Estofadas a la Andaluza (Stewed Partridges Andalusian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- bacon
- onion
- garlic
- spices
- lard
- white wine
- water

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean the partridges, then brown them and place in a suitable pot with diced fried bacon, coarsely chopped onion in abundance, crushed garlic, spices of all kinds, lard and white wine
2. Cook at low heat, placing a small pan with water on a piece of brown paper on top of the pot
3. Some serve without further preparation; others pour beaten egg yolks on top; some add chocolate, either beaten on top or one ounce whole inside the partridge when browning it
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdigones a la Inglesa (Young Partridges English Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Young partridges
- Oil
- Salt and pepper or other sauce

## Directions

1. After plucking, gutting, singeing, and turning the young partridges by the feet, open them from the tail to the crop
2. Flatten them with a mallet
3. Bathe them in an oil marinade
4. Place them on the grill over high heat
5. Serve with a pepper sauce or any other sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdigones a la Parrilla y en Crudo (Young Partridges on the Grill, Raw)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Young partridges
- Butter
- Salsa a la pebre

## Directions

1. Take the young partridges as is customary
2. Split them into two pieces along the kidneys
3. Place them in a pan
4. Sauté them in butter, stirring well
5. Place them on the grill as they fit
6. Serve with salsa a la pebre
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdigones Asados (Roasted Young Partridges)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Young partridges
- Bacon, thin slice

## Directions

1. Truss the young partridges as you would chickens, wrapping them with a thin slice of bacon
2. Stretch the legs and secure them on the spit or roasting rod so they do not bend, following the figure shown for such birds on page 440
3. For red partridges, wrap the head and neck very well in thick wet paper so the feathers do not burn
4. Arrange the bird on a platter with invisible small canes that keep the head upright as if the bird were alive and the body hidden in undergrowth, which is simulated with watercress that completely envelops the roasted meat, well sprinkled with its own juice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdigones en Salsa (Young Partridges in Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Young partridges
- Testicles or sweetbreads
- Mushrooms
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Roast the young partridges
2. Remove the breastbone and chest bone
3. Cut the meat into pieces the size of dice, along with some testicles or sweetbreads and some mushrooms
4. Cook everything in a Spanish sauce, adding the pieces of young partridge only when the testicles and mushrooms are cooked
5. Keep them hot while preparing
6. At the moment of serving, place the mixture in the cavity of the young partridges with sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdigones Salteados (Sautéed Young Partridges)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Young partridges
- Butter or lard
- Flour
- White wine
- Broth
- Salt and pepper
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the young partridges without cutting them
2. Cook them over high heat in butter or lard, turning them frequently so they color evenly
3. Once cooked, remove them and set aside on a platter, keeping it well covered near the fire
4. In the same cooking fat, lightly toast a tablespoon of flour
5. Moisten it with a glass of white wine and a ladle of clean broth
6. Season with salt and pepper and reduce by half
7. Add the juice of one whole lemon
8. Pour this sauce over the young partridges at the moment of serving at the table
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdigones Trufados Asados (Roasted Truffle-Stuffed Young Partridges)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Young partridges
- Truffles
- Truffles, cooked beforehand in white wine, in quantity equal to half the weight of the bird
- White wine

## Directions

1. Stuff the young partridges with a cooked filling mixed with truffles that have been cooked beforehand in white wine, using as many truffles as half the weight of the bird
2. Roast them carefully on the spit so the meat does not dry out
3. Serve with a sauce made with truffles soaked in the juice from the roast
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Perdiz con Coles (Partridge with Cabbage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 partridge, cleaned, emptied, singed and trussed
- thin strips of bacon, salted two hours prior in brine reinforced with pepper and a pinch of nutmeg
- 2 sheets of bacon, one for underneath and one on top
- half a pound of veal loin, sliced
- 2 carrots
- 2 onions with cloves embedded in them
- salt and pepper
- bundle of fine herbs
- 1 glass of fine white wine from San Sadurni de Noya
- 1 bowl of clean broth
- cabbage heads
- half a pound of mixed bacon, cut into slivers
- 1 good piece of Catalan butifarra or a half pound of ham

## Directions

1. Clean, empty, singe and truss the partridge, and lard its entire breast with many very thin strips of bacon that have been placed in brine two hours prior, reinforced with pepper and a pinch of nutmeg
2. Place the partridge in a pot with a perforated removable base, between two broad sheets of bacon, one underneath and one on top, after settling the perforated movable base at the bottom of the vessel
3. Add half a pound of veal loin in slices, 2 carrots, 2 onions with cloves of spice embedded in their flesh, salt and pepper, a bundle of fine herbs, 1 glass of the finest white wine from San Sadurni de Noya and 1 bowl of clean broth
4. Separately, blanch cabbage heads by boiling them 2 or 3 minutes in salted boiling water
5. Drain them well and turn them to air them so they do not retain a drop of water
6. Place them on top of the partridge in the pot, with half a pound of mixed bacon cut into slivers and a good piece of Catalan butifarra or a half pound of ham
7. Moisten everything with sufficient clean broth and cook over very low heat for two hours, with the pot well covered with its lid
8. After this time, take the movable piece of the vessel and remove very slowly and carefully all the contents
9. Place the partridge on a serving platter and set aside the carrots, pieces of veal, butifarra, ham, etc. on another platter, leaving only the cabbages on the movable piece on a plate to squeeze out their juice well, compressing them with a spatula
10. Meanwhile, reduce the sauce from the pot by half, and place the cabbages on top of the partridge, wrapping the bird completely, and on top of the cabbages place the butifarra or ham in slices or small even pieces
11. Make a hash from the veal, bacon, onion if any remains and carrots, on which the partridge will be placed on the platter before garnishing it with the cabbages and butifarra
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pescadilla Frita (Fried Whiting)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- whiting
- flour
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Clean the whiting
2. Coat with flour
3. For whole whiting: form it into a ring by putting the tail in the mouth and pressing with wet fingers to seal
4. Heat oil until it begins to smoke with a blue hue over high heat
5. Fry in plenty of hot oil with enough space for the fish to move freely
6. Season with salt when plating to serve
7. Use three to four pounds of oil for half a dozen medium-sized whiting in a large pan
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pescadilla á la Milanesa (Breaded Whiting)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- whole whiting
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- milk
- toasted bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Flour the whole whiting on the outside, and excessively on the inside
2. Sprinkle with salt and a pinch of pepper, and soak in a beaten mixture of egg and one spoonful of milk
3. Wrap in toasted bread crumbs and soak again in the egg, then wrap again in bread crumbs
4. Let dry a little so the crust takes shape, then fry, allowing more time for cooking than for naturally fried whiting
5. The whiting should result very firm and rolled up, without the shape of the fish being very marked
6. Serve as is, or with any of the infinite known sauces, but thick tomato sauce is what best suits the dish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pescadilla á la Milanesa (Whiting Milanese Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- whiting
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- milk
- toasted breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Clean the whole whiting and coat thoroughly inside and outside with flour
2. Sprinkle with salt and a pinch of pepper
3. Dip in a mixture of beaten egg and one tablespoon of milk
4. Roll in toasted breadcrumbs
5. Dip in the egg mixture again
6. Roll in breadcrumbs again
7. Let dry briefly to form a crust
8. Fry for longer than you would for plain fried whiting
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Picadillo de Pavo en Blanco (Turkey Minced in White Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover turkey
- salt
- pepper
- white roux
- milk
- clean broth from stew
- fried croutons

## Directions

1. Make a very fine mincing with all the meat, seasoning well with salt and pepper.
2. Separately, prepare a white roux that is moistened with half a quartillo of milk and half clean broth from the stew.
3. Cook the meat mince in this sauce until it thickens well.
4. Serve in a dish, rounding the surface of the paste and studding it with fried croutons.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Picadillo de Vaca (Minced Beef)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- poultry substance or other
- broth
- white wine

## Directions

1. Mince the meat very fine
2. Put it on the fire and add a little poultry substance or other, a little broth and white wine to moisten it
3. Leave it on the fire until it is done and serve it without further seasoning
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pichones Dulces (Sweet Squab)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover squab
- squab meat in small pieces
- clean broth
- cold apple compote

## Directions

1. Heat the small pieces of leftover squab in a little clean broth in a bain-marie.
2. Place the pieces on cold apple compote and serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pichones à la Crapodina (Squabs à la Crapaudine)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- breadcrumbs
- one ladle of broth
- three tablespoons of vinegar
- one tablespoon of minced shallots
- one tablespoon of finely chopped parsley
- salt
- pepper
- mustard

## Directions

1. Prepare the squabs with legs and wings extended
2. Bread the squabs with breadcrumbs
3. Grill them over low heat on a grill
4. Prepare the sauce by heating a ladle of broth with three tablespoons of vinegar, one tablespoon of minced shallots, one tablespoon of finely chopped parsley, salt, pepper, and mustard
5. Serve the splayed squabs on the platter with the sauce on the side
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pichones á Escape (Squabs in a Rush)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- four squabs
- 125 grams of lard
- 60 grams of bacon scraps
- two dozen small mushrooms
- one good tablespoon of flour
- salt and pepper
- one or two ladles of clear broth
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Cut four squabs into four pieces each
2. Sauté the pieces in lard with bacon scraps and small mushrooms the size of walnuts
3. Turn the squab pieces frequently; they will cook in only a quarter hour over high heat
4. Once ready, remove the squabs from the pan
5. In the same fat, brown a good tablespoon of flour until it takes on a dark toasted color, and season with salt and pepper
6. Moisten with one or two ladles of clear broth
7. Return the squabs to the sauce to soak well and heat through
8. Arrange the squabs in a mound on a serving dish, pour the sauce over them, and add the juice of one lemon
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pichón Asado en Asador (Roasted Squab on a Spit)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one squab, plucked and cleaned
- one grape leaf
- one thin slice of bacon

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean the bird
2. When the season permits, wrap it in a grape leaf
3. Cover with a thin slice of bacon
4. Roast over gentle heat for half an hour, prepared as shown in the illustration
5. The roasted squab should be well-done but not dry
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pierna de Carnero Adobada y Asada (Marinated and Roasted Leg of Mutton)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One rather lean leg of mutton
- Strips of bacon that have been in brine
- Vinegar
- Broth from the pot without fat
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Shallots, chopped
- A pair of garlic cloves
- Fine clarified oil

## Directions

1. Choose a rather lean piece
2. Skin it and lard it with strips of bacon that have been in brine beforehand
3. Submerge the larded leg of mutton in an escabeche made of vinegar and fat-free pot broth in equal parts, salt, pepper, fine herbs, and chopped shallots with a pair of garlic cloves
4. Allow the leg of mutton to remain in this broth for three days, completely submerged
5. At the moment of roasting, remove it, drain it, and clean it with a cloth
6. Rub the entire surface with fine clarified oil
7. During cooking, continuously moisten the meat with its own juices and serve with them
8. Note: Small legs of mutton from Pamplona, the region of Burgos, and Cuenca are best for this preparation
9. If no spit roaster is available and only coal and coke are used for cooking, roasting can be done in the oven, but it is more difficult as one must exercise exquisite care and constantly turn the leg of mutton while continuously moistening it with its own juices to prevent it from becoming toasted on the outside or burned while remaining raw inside
10. Use oven trays according to the models on page 186, or better, if the kitchen is well equipped, use the roasting pan described in the Appendix
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pierna de Carnero Asada (Roast Leg of Mutton)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One leg of mutton
- Fresh cow butter
- Ground salt
- Optionally: garlic clove

## Directions

1. Allow the leg of mutton to age for two days in summer or three to four days in winter, hanging in a cool pantry or larder
2. Beat the meat vigorously on both sides with a wooden mallet to tenderize it
3. Place the leg on a vertical spit roaster as shown on page 197
4. Begin with a lively fire
5. If the spit does not have mechanical movement, turn the meat frequently
6. When presenting the meat to the fire, lightly grease it with very fresh cow butter
7. Dust the entire surface with ground salt to prepare and draw out the juices
8. Continuously baste the meat with its own juices
9. Optionally, embed a garlic clove in the handle of the leg
10. Once the meat has begun to take color, moderate the fire and continue cooking slowly
11. Cook until no violet-red color remains in the interior of the meat, and when cut it is neither dry from overcooking nor bloody from undercooking
12. For a piece of five to six pounds, approximately one and a half hours will suffice
13. Serve from the spit to the table on a very hot platter with the juices in a separate sauceboat
14. Adapt a screw handle to the leg of mutton before serving
15. Decorate the bone or handle with a frilled paper rosette or wrap it elegantly with a tea napkin secured with a ribbon so it can be held with the left hand and carved without fork assistance
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pierna de Carnero en Agua (Leg of Mutton in Broth)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One leg of mutton, deboned or well tenderized
- A pair of long strips of bacon
- Some carrots, cut up
