- Three or four onions
- A bouquet of fine herbs
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. In a braising pot, which is a large thin metal cooking pot with a lid, place at the bottom a pair of long strips of bacon, some cut-up carrots, three or four onions, a bouquet of fine herbs, and the appropriate seasoning of salt and pepper
2. Place the deboned leg of mutton on top, or if not deboned, well tenderized with a mallet and with the protruding bone part cut but not completely removed from the piece of meat to which it is attached
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pierna de Cordero Rellena (Stuffed Leg of Lamb)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted lamb, cold
- lamb meat trimmed and macerated
- hard egg yolks
- bread crumbled and toasted
- fine herbs finely chopped
- spices
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Debone the cold roasted lamb neck.
2. Break the breast and apply the shoulder blades forming a well-wrapped bundle tied with twine, with the legs and broken bone extremities.
3. Fill the whole package with a filling composed of trimmed and macerated lamb meat, hard egg yolks, toasted breadcrumbs and finely chopped fine herbs seasoned with spices.
4. Tie the lamb meat and place in the oven for half an hour at gentle heat.
5. Serve whole as an appetizer course, with a green sauce, or with a ragout of truffles with ham juice.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pimientos Rellenos (Stuffed Peppers)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- peppers
- picadillo (any ground meat mixture)
- bread, crumbled
- oil
- clean broth

## Directions

1. Hollow out the peppers and clean them inside without breaking them.
2. Fill them with a picadillo kneaded with crumbled bread.
3. Fry them in oil and place them in a baking dish.
4. Moisten them with clean broth and cook them in a camp oven with gentle heat above and below.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Plum-Pudding (Plum-Pudding)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of Malaga raisins
- half a pound of Corinth raisins
- half a pound of beef kidney fat
- a quarter pound of flour
- a quarter pound of very crumbled bread crumb
- two ounces of ground sugar
- three ounces total of citron, orange peel, and angelica in syrup
- a glass of milk
- a glass of rum
- one lemon
- nutmeg, ginger, and cinnamon mixed together to fill half a coffee spoon
- an equal amount of ground salt
- three eggs

## Directions

1. Skin and shred the kidney fat with great care and mince it very finely, as if it were bread crumbs
2. Mix it with the bread crumb and flour, and dissolve with milk
3. Beat the three eggs well with the nutmeg, ginger, and cinnamon
4. Mix with the previous mixture and incorporate the citron, orange peel, angelica, Malaga and Corinth raisins, and the glass of rum with the lemon juice, salt, and sugar
5. Mix everything well with a spatula until the spatula can stand upright planted in the mixture
6. Grease an appropriate English mold with butter
7. Cover it with a thick, coarse cloth soaked in water
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Polla Cebada Trufada (Fattened Hen with Truffles)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fattened hen
- 500 grams truffles
- 250 grams bacon pieces
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and empty the freshly killed bird, carefully removing the main bone of the breastbone without tearing the skin
2. In a separate pan over high heat, sauté 500 grams of truffles cut into quarters in 250 grams of chopped bacon
3. Stir the truffles with a spatula and let them cook slowly for half an hour in the bacon fat
4. Remove from heat and let cool
5. Fill the interior of the bird with the cooled truffle mixture
6. Place the stuffed bird in a cold place in a basting pan for 34 hours in summer or 48 hours in winter, without touching it, so the meat becomes well infused with the truffle aroma
7. Roast in the rotisserie covered with a thin sheet of bacon and wrapped in thick well-greased paper
8. One hour is sufficient to roast the bird over medium heat
9. Remove the paper and bacon so it takes on color
10. Serve very hot, plain or in its own juices, sprinkled with some truffle shavings that were set aside earlier
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pollo Asado (Roast Chicken)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken, four to five months old
- butter or pork fat, melted
- salt
- pepper
- watercress or other edible green vegetable for garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare and arrange the chicken well, threading it onto the spit of the roaster and tying it with string so that it does not swing and the legs remain fixed, making a good figure.
2. Coat the outside with melted butter or pork fat using a kitchen brush. Before tying and fixing on the spit, place inside it about the size of a walnut of the fat being used.
3. Sprinkle with ground salt and a pinch of pepper.
4. Roast over a brisk fire rapidly and with much care, because a well-roasted chicken should not be very browned and should have no part of its meat inside that is not completely white.
5. Serve the roasted chicken alone, without any juice, over a bed of watercress or other green edible vegetable served raw, but not to serve as garnish to strengthen the plate, but only to adorn it.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pollo del Maestro (Master's Chicken)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one plump chicken with full breasts
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- two small glasses of good cognac
- butter from Isigny

## Directions

1. Fill the chicken with oil as if it were a vessel and rinse it, then empty it and let it drain.
2. Incorporate salt, pepper, and a pinch of nutmeg into the interior.
3. Finish this interior preparation with a splash of two small glasses of good cognac, of the kind drunk by Barbieri, from Cognac in the Lower Charente, a French department.
4. Once this is done and the chicken is on the spit, coat it with butter from Isigny and roast it over a brisk fire.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pollo Fiambre á la Éboli (Cold Chicken Éboli Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One good chicken, boned
- Very thin slices of bacon
- Very small chorizos, more or less spicy according to taste
- A few lightly salted anchovies

## Directions

1. Bone a good chicken and fill it with the following: first, very thin slices of bacon; second, very small chorizos, more or less spicy according to your taste; third, a few lightly salted anchovies
2. After filling the bird, roast it on a spit, not in an oven, skillet, or pot
3. Let it cool for at least twelve hours
4. Finally, place it between two large pieces of crumb bread, which by pressing the chicken without destroying it, will become soaked with the juice it releases
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pollo Frito (Fried Chicken)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Pieces of a good-eating chicken
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Chopped shallots
- Onions, divided
- Flour
- Onions, cut in wheels

## Directions

1. Soak the pieces of chicken in oil with salt, pepper, chopped shallots, and divided onions for five hours
2. Remove, drain, and coat heavily with flour, frying them in very good oil
3. Fry onions cut in wheels, also floured in the same oil until well browned
4. Place the browned onions in the center of a platter and arrange the fried chicken pieces around them
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pollo à la Marengo (Chicken Marengo)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken, plucked, cleaned, and cut into pieces
- oil

## Directions

1. Fill the bottom of a casserole with oil and place the chicken, after being plucked, cleaned, and cut into pieces.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pollo á la Marengo (Chicken Marengo)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken, plucked, cleaned and cut into pieces
- fine oil
- one dozen small onions the size of walnuts
- one bay leaf
- half a clove of garlic
- salt and pepper to taste
- one tablespoon of flour
- chicken broth or water
- one small glass of good cognac

## Directions

1. Cut and quarter a good chicken into pieces.
2. In a wide, flat skillet, add enough fine oil to just coat the bottom.
3. Over high heat, sauté the pieces of chicken with a dozen small onions the size of walnuts, one bay leaf, half a clove of garlic, and salt and pepper to taste.
4. The onions and chicken pieces must brown very well and evenly in just under half an hour, stirring the skillet constantly without stopping.
5. After this time, remove the chicken from the skillet with a slotted spoon and set aside on a plate.
6. Pass the fat and onions from the skillet to a saucepan to brown one tablespoon of flour until it takes on a chestnut color, stirring it constantly for five minutes.
7. Moisten with chicken broth or water and add one small glass of good cognac.
8. Once the sauce is thickened, cook over high heat for another five minutes, stirring always with a spatula.
9. Remove the saucepan from the heat, incorporate the quartered chicken, cover the saucepan well and serve after ten minutes.
10. Total time: just under one hour.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pollo á la Veneciana (Chicken Venetian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken, cleaned and singed
- Butter
- White wine
- Broth
- A bunch of parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter mixed with flour
- Grated cheese

## Directions

1. Open the chicken down the back from the neck to the tail and flatten it with a mallet
2. Cook it in butter with white wine and broth, adding a bunch of parsley, salt and pepper over low heat
3. When cooked, strain and reduce the broth, adding butter mixed with flour
4. Place the chicken on a plate that can withstand fire, and pour the sauce over it
5. Cover the chicken and sauce with grated cheese and place over moderate heat in a small oven
6. Serve when it has taken color
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pollos con Tomate (Chicken with Tomato)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- tender chickens, carefully quartered
- one pound of fresh tomatoes per chicken
- pork lard
- salt and pepper to taste
- two tablespoons of flour
- four tablespoons of fresh water

## Directions

1. Scald tender chickens, carefully quartered, in boiling water and give them a strong boil for fifteen minutes.
2. Fry the tomatoes in pork lard until they thicken and the water they contain disappears.
3. Season and pass through a fine food mill to obtain a broth of light consistency.
4. Cook the chicken quarters in this tomato broth over low heat for one hour, allowing them to become saturated with all the flavor of the tomato.
5. A few minutes before serving, make a slurry with two tablespoons of flour and four tablespoons of fresh water and pour it into the saucepan, letting it cook for a very short time.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pollos Guisados (Stewed Chickens)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens, well cleaned
- butter
- onion
- spices
- salt
- white wine
- water
- chicken livers and garlic

## Directions

1. Heat butter in a frying pan and sear the chickens, then place them in the pot where they are to cook.
2. In the remaining fat, fry some onion and add it to the chickens with all the spices.
3. Add a little salt and a glass of white wine, and place over a gentle fire with a small pot of water on top and a cloth or paper to prevent evaporation of the substances, cooking for about one hour.
4. Roast the chicken livers, crush them with a garlic clove, mix them with the broth, and season well.
5. Cover again and allow to boil a couple of times, which produces an excellent sauce.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pollos á la Austríaca (Austrian-Style Chicken)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two chickens
- two pounds of apples, well cleaned and peeled
- one liter of water
- two ounces of sugar
- a little clove
- a touch of vanilla

## Directions

1. Roast two chickens either on a spit, in a saucepan, or in the oven.
2. For the apple compote, cook two pounds of apples that are well cleaned and peeled in one liter of water with two ounces of sugar, a little clove, and a touch of vanilla.
3. Allow three quarters of the water to reduce and pass through a food mill into a purée.
4. Pour into a saucepan and, stirring with a spatula, hasten the complete evaporation of the remaining water.
5. Serve the whole roasted chickens over this compote.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ponche de Huevo (Egg Punch)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 250 grams piloncillo sugar, ground
- 1 quartillo lemon water
- 1 quartillo white sugar syrup, well cooked
- 2 quartillos 92-degree alcohol without anise
- 1 quartillo strong brewed tea

## Directions

1. Mix the four egg yolks with 250 grams of ground piloncillo sugar for a quarter hour
2. Add little by little the lemon water, then the sugar syrup, then the alcohol, then the tea
3. Place everything in a large pot or pan, filling only halfway to prevent inflammation
4. Over gentle heat, constantly stir with a bundle of willow branches, heating well without allowing it to boil
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ponche Inglés (English Punch)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams green tea
- zest of 2 lemons
- 1 quartillo boiling water
- 250 grams ground sugar
- juice of 2 lemons
- 1 bottle rum

## Directions

1. In a large teapot, steep the green tea and lemon zest in boiling water for half an hour
2. In a pan, combine the ground sugar and lemon juice, stirring with a spatula until the sugar melts
3. Add the bottle of rum and the tea from the teapot
4. Heat everything in a bain-marie without allowing it to boil
5. When serving in the punch bowl, ignite the punch
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ponche Natural (Natural Punch)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rum
- sugar
- water
- lemon

## Directions

1. Burn rum and sugar in a punch bowl
2. When the flame goes out, serve in wine glasses filled halfway with boiling water
3. Add a lemon wheel to each glass
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ponche á la Romana (Roman Punch)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds syrup at small thread stage
- zest of 2 lemons
- juice of 8 lemons
- 0.5 quartillo water
- 3 egg whites
- three-quarters of a glass of rum

## Directions

1. Mix the syrup, lemon zest, lemon juice, and water
2. Strain and pour into the freezer to freeze
3. Beat the three egg whites until they form stiff peaks like snow
4. When serving, incorporate the egg whites together with three-quarters of a glass of rum
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Potaje (Chickpea and Cabbage Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- well-burned oil
- onion scraps
- modest piece of ham, cut into small pieces
- one tablespoon of flour
- 350 grams of chickpeas
- one cabbage
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Take a cooking pot and add the well-burned oil, onion scraps, and ham pieces cut small
2. Put all this on the fire to fry, adding immediately one tablespoon of flour
3. Have previously boiled and cooked 350 grams of chickpeas and one cabbage
4. Combine the chickpeas and cabbage with their cooking water to the pot ingredients
5. Season with pepper and salt
6. Cook for half an hour over low heat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Potaje de Calabazas (Squash Potage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Long tender squash
- Butter or oil
- Onion
- Chickpea broth or water
- Spices
- Milk
- Flour
- Saffron
- Celery
- Sugar and cinnamon (optional)
- Bread slices
- Torrijas (fried batter)

## Directions

1. Take long tender squash, scrape the rind, cut into small pieces and blanch in water and salt
2. Put some butter or oil with onion in a casserole and sauté the squash slightly
3. Add chickpea broth or hot water to cover it, or chopped greens
4. Season with all spices and a little milk
5. Take some butter and heat it in a pan; once hot, add a little flour, frying it so it does not turn black or thick in the pan
6. Sprinkle the squash dish with saffron and celery to avoid setting it with eggs
7. If desired, sugar and cinnamon can be added
8. Serve over bread slices and garnish with torrijas
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Potaje de Espinacas (Spinach Potage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Butter or oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fried bread crusts

## Directions

1. Cut, peel and wash the spinach
2. Sauté it with butter or oil, salt and pepper
3. Add a piece of butter for the sauce
4. Serve with fried bread crusts in the soup bowl
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Potaje de Espinacas á la Española (Spanish-Style Spinach Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickpeas, cooked in seasoned water
- Spinach, a handful
- Fried oil
- Flour, one tablespoon
- Egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cook the chickpeas in seasoned water.
2. Cook apart or with them a handful of spinach, cut roughly.
3. Incorporate fried oil with one tablespoon of flour browned in it.
4. At the moment of serving, bind with an egg yolk dissolved in water.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Potaje de Guisantes (Pea Potage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas
- Lettuce hearts
- Sorrel
- Spices and seasonings

## Directions

1. Add the peas to the boiling pot along with lettuce hearts and sorrel
2. Season with the same spices as other potages
3. If the peas are dried, cook them the same way as chickpeas
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Potaje de Habas (Broad Bean Potage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Broad beans
- Oil
- Lettuce
- Spices
- Herbs, especially cilantro
- Salt
- Hot water
- Vinegar
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Shell the broad beans, ensuring they are tender
2. Put oil in a casserole or earthenware pot and place it over coals
3. When hot, add the broad beans, having prepared ahead washed and shredded lettuce as finely as possible without using a knife
4. Squeeze out the water and mix with the beans so both vegetables sauté together
5. The beans will release enough water to serve as broth
6. Apply all spices and herbs, especially green cilantro
7. Season with salt, add hot water and vinegar
8. Cook until everything is softened, finally adding raw eggs so they set curled with the beans
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Potaje de Nabos (Turnip Potage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Turnips
- Salt
- Butter or oil
- White sauce

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the turnips into slices in whatever form seems best
2. Cook them with salt and butter or oil
3. Serve them as soup with an extended white sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Potaje de Zanahorias (Carrot Potage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrots
- Water
- Salt
- Oil
- Onion
- Spices
- Hot water
- Honey or sugar
- Vinegar
- Flour
- Eggs

## Directions

1. On Fridays, cook the carrots with water and salt
2. Cut them into small pieces and sauté with oil and onion
3. Place them in a pot or casserole and season with all spices and salt, adding hot water to cover them
4. Add honey or sugar and vinegar so they are well sweetened with a hint of vinegar acidity
5. Finally, fry some flour until toasted and dissolve it with the same carrot broth
6. If desired, poach eggs and add them one by one to the soup bowl to cook slightly, and serve the soup in this state
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Poupeton de Codornices (Quail Poupeton)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal kidney
- Beef marrow
- Fresh white bacon
- Mushrooms
- Chives
- Bread crumb soaked in meat juice
- Thyme
- Two raw eggs
- Quail, roasted
- Rum

## Directions

1. Make a hash with veal kidney, beef marrow, very fresh white bacon, mushrooms, chives, bread crumb soaked in meat juice, thyme and two raw eggs.
2. Mix well, make a mass and fill a mold whose bottom and sides will be lined with thin bacon leaves.
3. Cover the mold and with moderate heat below and strong heat above the lid, let it cook for one hour.
4. Turn out the poupeton onto a platter as if it were a flan, and around it place as garnish the quail, roasted beforehand and lightly sprinkled at the moment of serving with a few drops of rum.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puchero (Spanish Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters of water
- half kilogram of beef chuck
- 1 veal hand
- 1 marrow bone
- 1 piece of pork knuckle the size of a chocolate ounce
- chickpeas (soaked in cold water and salt 6 hours before)
- salt
- 1 small piece of aged bacon
- 1 bouquet of parsley
- 2 bay leaves
- 3 or 4 leeks, cut and tied in a bundle
- 1 onion with 2 cloves studded in it
- 2 or 3 turnips
- 2 or 3 carrots
- burned sugar or caramel for coloring
- 4 potatoes the size of an egg

## Directions

1. Six hours before starting, soak the chickpeas in cold water and salt.
2. Pour 2 liters of water into a 3-liter pot and place over heat.
3. When the water boils vigorously, add half kilogram of beef chuck, then a veal hand, then a marrow bone, and a piece of pork knuckle the size of a chocolate ounce, adding them gradually and successively so the boiling does not interrupt.
4. While the water continues to boil vigorously, add the chickpeas and salt.
5. Cook everything over high heat for half an hour with great violence in an uncovered pot.
6. Then add a small piece of aged bacon, a bouquet of parsley, 2 bay leaves, 3 or 4 leeks cut and tied in a bundle, an onion with 2 cloves studded in it, 2 or 3 turnips, and 2 or 3 carrots.
7. Without stopping the boil, taste and adjust seasoning, then fill the pot completely with boiling water.
8. Skim off about 6 spoonfuls of fat, then color with a bit of burned sugar or caramel.
9. From this moment, half-cover the pot and adjust so cooking is very slow and continuous for 4 hours.
10. One hour before serving, add 4 potatoes the size of an egg.
11. To serve the broth, strain through a colander lined with fine linen cloth.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puchero a la Española (Spanish Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One piece of meat, larger than needed for usual consumption
- Two cuartillos of water per pound of meat
- Bones from the meat, preferably shin bones
- Soaked chickpeas
- Carrots
- Parsnips
- Turnips
- Leeks
- Celery
- Parsley
- Two bay leaves
- Two large onions
- Half a clove of garlic
- Two cloves of spice
- Two ounces of bacon per pound of meat
- One quarter of a veal hand
- Sugar for coloring (optional)

## Directions

1. Take a piece of meat larger than needed for usual consumption, as the larger the piece, the fresher and more solid it will be, and it will save time and fuel
2. Do not wash the meat so as not to strip it of a large part of its juices
3. Tie or bind the meat after removing the bones so it maintains a presentable shape
4. When the water in the pot boils vigorously, introduce the meat suddenly
5. Use two cuartillos of water per pound of meat
6. After the meat has cooked for a quarter hour, add the bones that the piece had, and some more, preferably shin bones
7. Add pre-soaked chickpeas and other vegetables such as carrots, parsnips, turnips, a bundle made with leeks, celery, parsley and two bay leaves, and two large onions in which are embedded respectively half a clove of garlic and two cloves of spice
8. The proportion of vegetables in the stew, not counting the chickpeas, should be in total weight equal to that of the meat
9. After the vegetables, add the bacon, two ounces per pound of meat, and one quarter of a veal hand
10. After two hours of slow boiling that has begun since the meat was added, taste the broth and correct the seasoning made at the start
11. For the broth to be good, the stew must cook slowly and continuously for six hours
12. To diminish evaporation as much as possible without damaging the cooking, the pot should not be perfectly covered
13. When adding water, do so carefully with boiling water
14. For coloring, burn a little ground sugar in a small pan, and when the caramel has formed, introduce the pan and all into the stew until the sugar detaches
15. To remove the broth from the pot, with a skimmer and carefully, extract all the solid parts that the vessel contains: meats, vegetables, etc., to proceed afterward with their placement and arrangement on the plates to go to the table
16. Strain the broth through fine linen placed in the bottom of the strainer, which should be well soaked in cold water so that all the fat it contains stays in the linen, resulting in clean and transparent liquid with this natural clarification
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puchero a la Habanera (Havana-Style Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds veal loin
- a piece of salt pork
- a handful of chickpeas soaked the night before
- one pound fresh pork
- a quarter chicken
- one plantain
- one chorizo
- a handful of tender beans
- half a cabbage
- a piece of squash
- an eggplant
- a little malanga
- a piece of Castilian squash
- two turnips
- two sweet potatoes
- four tomatoes
- two shallots
- one onion
- a piece of island squash
- two or three garlic cloves
- a few grains of cumin
- a few grains of cilantro
- two grains of pepper
- saffron

## Directions

1. Prepare a medium pot with water filled halfway
2. Add a little salt, two pounds veal from the loin area, a piece of salt pork, a handful of chickpeas soaked from the night before and one pound fresh pork
3. Add a quarter chicken and put to cook, taking care to remove the foam
4. After it has boiled about one hour, add, well peeled and cleaned, a ripe plantain with its skin, a chorizo, a handful of tender beans, half a cabbage, a piece of squash, an eggplant, a little malanga, a piece of Castilian squash, two turnips, two sweet potatoes, four tomatoes, two shallots, one onion and a piece of island squash
5. Let everything cook about another hour and take care to remove any foam that rises again
6. To season it, pound in a mortar with a little salt, two or three garlic cloves, a few grains of cumin and cilantro and two of pepper, and when all is made into a paste, dilute it with broth from the pot and add it to the pot with two threads of crumbled saffron
7. Let it boil a while and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puchero a la Mejicana (Mexican-Style Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds veal
- two pounds beef
- a piece of Veracruz or Chicago salt pork
- half a cabbage
- one lettuce
- two or three turnips
- carrots
- a handful of soaked chickpeas
- turkey scraps
- half a chicken
- some roasted lamb bones
- a little parsley
- three cloves
- one roasted onion

## Directions

1. Take a pot containing about two jars of cold water
2. Add two pounds veal, two pounds beef and a piece of salt pork
3. Put on low fire
4. Add two more jars of water when the first ones have lost their coldness
5. Increase the fire to produce foam
6. Remove the foam as it rises until no more appears
7. After boiling about eight hours on equal fire, and when it has cooked four hours, add half a cabbage, one lettuce, two or three turnips, equal quantity of carrots, a handful of soaked chickpeas, scraps of a turkey, half a chicken and some roasted lamb bones, a little parsley, three cloves and one roasted onion
8. Add hot water if needed until the eight hours are completed
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puchero Alternativo (Alternative Spanish Stew - Without Skimming)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- chickpeas
- water
- beef
- salt

## Directions

1. Place chickpeas alone in boiling water with salt and let them boil for half an hour without removing the white foam that forms, which will gradually disappear on its own.
2. After half an hour, add the meat.
3. This method eliminates the need for skimming and produces a clean-looking stew.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puchero Belga (Belgian Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- from two to six pounds beef
- three medium carrots
- two turnips
- four leeks
- one parsnip or gallego turnip
- a bundle of parsley
- one roasted onion
- two or three cloves
- sufficient salt
- hot water

## Directions

1. Take from two to six pounds of beef and put in a pot, adding half an azumbre of water per pound, and put on moderate fire, which should be increased little by little in order to extract the foam
2. Remove the foam as it rises to the surface until none appears
3. Leave the pot for eight consecutive hours on an equal and moderate fire, and after the first four hours, add three medium carrots, two turnips, four leeks, one parsnip or gallego turnip, all cut in half
4. Add a bundle of parsley, larger or smaller as proportionate, one roasted onion with two or three cloves stuck in it, sufficient salt, and take care to add hot water as the first evaporates
5. If to this mixture is added a whole bird, or at least half a chicken, the scraps of a turkey or ribs of roasted lamb, the result is a tasty stew
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puchero Común (Common Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Chickpeas
- Beef or mutton, well washed and skinned
- Ham
- Bacon
- Small onion
- Half hen
- Turkey offal
- Salt
- Warm water as needed

## Directions

1. In a pot proportioned to the food to be cooked, add water and once hot, add the chickpeas and meat well washed and skinned; for each pound of beef or mutton, add half a pound of chickpeas
2. When it begins to boil, skim it, being careful not to overdo it so as not to deprive the stew of its substance
3. Two hours later, add some ham, bacon and a small onion
4. Let it all boil at low heat, seasoning with salt and adding warm water from time to time
5. If half a hen and turkey offal are added to this mixture, one will achieve with such simple and easy method the best of ordinary stew
6. The broth serves to soak all stews in which a liquid substance is needed without resorting to water
7. The vegetables are cooked separately with aged bacon, chorizo or blood sausage
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puchero de Enfermo (Invalid's Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken or mutton
- chickpeas (optional)
- ham (optional)
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cook the stew with chicken or mutton
2. If it is to be substantial, add some chickpeas and ham
3. The broth should be cooked before serving
4. If greater substance is desired, break up the chicken mixing it with bread crumbs and dilute the broth
5. Then strain it, season it, put it on the fire, but without letting it cook
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puchero de Verdura (Vegetable Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Pure spring water
- Bacon
- Repollo (cabbage)
- Chard or Moorish chard
- Fresh tender beans
- Lettuce
- Cauliflower
- Cardoon
- Escarole
- Salt
- Various vegetables

## Directions

1. First put pure spring water so that the stew does not retain the smell of vegetables cooked the day before
2. When the water is very hot, or better, boiling, add the bacon
3. Three hours before eating, add the cabbage, and two hours before, the Castilian or Moorish chard; both are added while the stew is boiling and stirring from time to time with a wooden spoon
4. If using fresh tender beans, add the chard or lettuce first, and an hour later the beans; if they are already very large, add them before the vegetables, and if they are dry, soak them beforehand and remove the eye, then put them in cold water like all dried legumes; fresh ones of all kinds in boiling water
5. Also cooked vegetables, such as cauliflower, cardoon, lettuce, escarole and others, should be put in boiling water, adding salt with the vegetable when the first boil breaks
6. The most tender ones need an hour and a half of continuous boiling so they do not lose color; the hardest ones two or three hours
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puchero Francés (Pot-au-feu)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds good fresh healthy meat
- eight quartillos cold water
- salt
- carrots
- turnips
- small onions
- parsley
- cloves
- one garlic clove
- one roasted onion or a little burned sugar for color

## Directions

1. For three pounds of good fresh healthy meat put eight quartillos of cold water and salt, letting it cook little by little and skimming it well
2. Then add carrots, turnips, small onions, parsley, cloves, one garlic clove and one roasted onion, or a little burned sugar to give color to the broth
3. After cooking six hours on low fire, the puchero is done, and the broth, after straining, is poured over the soup
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puchero Madrileño a la Moderna (Modern Madrid Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two liters of water
- Half a kilogram of beef from the part called morcillo (chuck)
- One hand of veal
- One bone of canilla (shin bone)
- One piece of codillo (elbow) the size of an ounce of chocolate
- Chickpeas
- Salt

## Directions

1. Six hours before beginning, soak the chickpeas in cold water and salt
2. In a pot of three liters capacity, pour two liters of water
3. Place on the fire, and when it boils vigorously, add half a kilogram of beef from the part called morcillo
4. Add one hand of veal, one shin bone, and one piece of codillo the size of an ounce of chocolate
5. These items should be added in the order indicated, gradually and successively, so that the boiling does not interrupt
6. While the water continues to boil vigorously, incorporate the chickpeas and add salt
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puchero Reconfortante (Fortifying Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- common puchero
- one old chicken
- a piece of lean beef
- a piece of veal
- two carrots
- onions
- leeks
- a bouquet of aromatic herbs
- cloves

## Directions

1. Add to the common puchero one old chicken, a piece of lean beef and another of veal, two carrots, onions, leeks, a bouquet of aromatic herbs, and cloves
2. Cook until reduced to one third
3. Then remove the fat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pure de Guisantes (Pea Puree)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried peas
- Water
- Butter from cows
- Flour
- Broth
- Milk
- Salt
- Pepper
- Ground clove
- Egg yolk

## Directions

1. Soak dried peas overnight in cold water as you would chickpeas
2. Cook over gentle heat in a water boiler
3. Once cooked, pass through a sieve using a mortar pestle, collecting the paste on a deep platter
4. In a quick-cooking pot, reheat 60 grams of butter from cows per pound of pea paste
5. Dust with flour without letting it brown or toast
6. Moisten with broth and thicken
7. Incorporate the pea paste and stir vigorously over high heat with a spatula
8. Add half a quarter-liter of milk and season with salt, pepper, and ground clove
9. Remove from heat at the first boil
10. Away from heat, bind the puree with an egg yolk beaten in two spoonfuls of cold milk
11. Serve in a legume dish as it comes from the pot, or on a round platter shaped like a half orange and surrounded by molded croutons
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puré de Calabaza (Squash Puree)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- squash pulp, in pieces
- goat's milk
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook the squash pulp in pieces in good goat's milk with a bit of salt.
2. Pass it through a sieve.
3. Serve it seasoned outside the heat with butter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puré de Cebollas (Onion Purée)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- three dozen large onions, the size of eggs
- beef butter, a considerable amount
- flour
- salt and pepper
- one ladle of fresh water
- half a quarter-liter of milk
- two egg yolks
- milk for dissolving the yolks

## Directions

1. Peel three dozen large onions and chop them very finely.
2. Sauté them in a pot over high heat in considerable beef butter, without allowing the butter or onions to brown, and dust with flour until everything sets a little.
3. Season moderately with salt and pepper and moisten with one ladle of fresh water.
4. Let cook vigorously for a while, stirring constantly with a spatula, and add half a quarter-liter of milk.
5. From this point forward, cooking must be very slow and will last about fifteen minutes until the broth reduces by half.
6. Pass everything through a fine sieve, and set the paste to curdle in a bain-marie in another pot, with two egg yolks dissolved in milk at the moment of serving.
7. This is a luxury dish that is presented with success at great tables, serving as a complement, though separate, to fried ham or grilled salmon.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Puré de Patatas (Potato Purée)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes of farinaceous variety
- Salted water, one quartillo (approximately 1.15 liters) per two liters of potatoes
- Cow's milk, cooked beforehand, up to half a quartillo
- Two ounces or 60 grams of fresh cow's butter
- One egg yolk
- Two tablespoons of milk
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Steam potatoes of farinaceous variety
2. If large, cut into pieces the size of walnuts
3. Using a purée press with tongs, squeeze the potatoes without great effort, passing the fine paste through the colander attached to the apparatus into a plate below
4. Put a cooking pot on strong heat with one quartillo of salted water for every two liters of potatoes
5. Incorporate the reserved potato paste
6. Without ceasing to stir quickly and vigorously with a boxwood spatula, add cooked cow's milk up to half a quartillo
7. The purée thickens and reaches its point when it boils with bursting bubbles
8. Then remove from strong heat and leave at the side of the stove to cook gently for a quarter of an hour, being careful that the dish does not stick
9. At the moment of serving, remove the pot from the heat and add two ounces or 60 grams of very fresh cow's butter
10. Stir the paste well, holding the pot by a handle, and bind with one egg yolk beaten in two tablespoons of milk in which salt and pepper to taste have been added
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Pértiga al Watter-fisch (Perch in Water-fish Broth)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley roots with stalks and leaves
- parsnips
- onions
- red pepper
- salt
- perch, cleaned inside and out and well-scaled

## Directions

1. Boil in very salted water over high heat three or four fat parsley roots with their stalks and leaves, along with parsnips cut in pieces, sliced onions, and a red pepper for ten minutes.
2. Remove the parsnips, onions, and pepper from the broth; leave the parsley.
3. Without stopping the boiling, add the cleaned and well-scaled perch.
4. When cooked, remove from the pot and let drain and cool.
5. Serve cold without sauce, accompanied by thin, equal slices of rye bread spread with butter on alternating sides, stacked together in pairs.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Rabo de Vaca (Oxtail)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- oxtail
- salted water
- cabbage
- carrots
- turnips
- parsnips
- onions
- bacon pieces
- sausage
- good broth
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the oxtail into three parts
2. Blanch it with salted water
3. Cook in a marmita or casserole with cabbage, carrots, turnips, parsnips, onions, pieces of bacon and sausage
4. Moisten with good broth
5. Simmer gently for four to five hours
6. Drain everything and place in a serving dish
7. Pour the cooking liquid over it after reducing it and adding a bit of Spanish sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Rabo de Vaca en Salsa (Beef Tail in Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tail
- sauce

## Directions

1. Braise the beef tail in the same manner as explained for braised beef cutlet.
2. When it is cooked, whole or in pieces, serve it on a serving dish, pouring any sauce of your choice over it.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Rabo de Vaca á la Hochepot (Oxtail Hochepot)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Oxtail
- Six carrots
- Two onions
- Cloves
- Half a cabbage
- Salt
- Pepper
- Meat stock

## Directions

1. Cut the oxtail into as many pieces as it has vertebrae, the same as if it were meat for a boiled dinner
2. At mid-cooking, add six carrots, two onions with their corresponding cloves, and half a cabbage cut into strips, with good seasoning of salt and pepper
3. When the oxtail is well cooked, after about six hours, remove it from the pot along with the vegetables, and place them on a platter with the pieces of oxtail in the center and the vegetables around it
4. Degrease the broth carefully, add meat stock, reduce over high heat, and pour this broth over the dish to serve it
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Raspadura de Berza (Cabbage Scrape Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Two or three tablespoons of pork fat
- A good piece of ham
- Broth from the stew pot
- Brown bread or rye bread
- Grated cheese of Gruyere or La Mancha (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage finely, wash it and drain it perfectly
2. Put it in a pot with two or three tablespoons of pork fat
3. Sauté over high heat, stirring constantly with a spatula so the cabbage colors evenly without burning
4. Transfer the cabbage to another pot with a good piece of ham
5. Add broth from the stew pot and let it boil until the cabbage is well cooked
6. Drain the contents of the pot well and separate the broth
7. Line the bottom of the pot completely with slices of brown or rye bread
8. Layer with cooked cabbage the thickness of the bread, then bread, then cabbage, and so on until the pot is full, ending with a layer of cabbage
9. Place the pot back over the fire, adding the separated broth in just enough quantity so everything is soaked without oozing
10. Toast in the oven or with fire on the pot lid placed over hot coals
11. Serve in the same pot, saving the ham for another meal or as garnish for another dish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ravioles (Italian Ravioli)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat leftovers
- Flour
- Butter
- Egg
- Four-centimeter square sheets of pasta dough
- Salt
- Lemon or cognac

## Directions

1. Make a minced mixture from the meat leftovers
2. Bind the mixture with a roux made from flour browned in butter and egg, as if making alboidigrilles
3. Take portions the size of hazelnuts and place them in the center of a four-centimeter square sheet of dough
4. Fold the dough over itself to enclose the meat
5. Join the edges of the dough by moistening them slightly and making marks with a knife as if crenellating
6. Fry or bake in a strong oven
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Raw Artichokes (Alcachofas Crudas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender, early artichokes
- Egg yolk
- English mustard
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Salt
- White pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the artichokes well of stem and outer leaves
2. Make a pepper sauce with the yolk of a hard-boiled egg, a little English mustard, two spoonfuls of oil, one of vinegar, salt and white pepper
3. Serve the artichokes with this cold sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Raya con Manteca Negra (Skate with Black Butter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- skate
- water with salt
- half a quartillo of vinegar
- onions
- bouquet of fine herbs
- black butter sauce
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cook the skate as described in the previous recipe: soak in salted water for one hour, then cook in a short broth made with the salted water, half a quartillo of vinegar, onions, and a bouquet of fine herbs
2. Place the cooked skate on a platter
3. Pour the black butter sauce over the skate
4. Serve with boiled potatoes prepared plain and served separately
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Raya en Salsa Blanca (Skate in White Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- skate, cleaned and cut
- water with salt
- half a quartillo of vinegar
- onions
- bouquet of fine herbs
- white sauce
- capers

## Directions

1. After cleaning and cutting the skate regularly across the fibers, soak it for one hour in very salted water
2. Cook it in a short broth made with part of the salted water from the soaking, half a quartillo of vinegar, some onions, and a bouquet of fine herbs
3. When cooked, remove the skin completely and place the skate on a deep platter
4. Pour white sauce over it and add a good quantity of capers
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Reglas de El Practicón para Hacer el Mejor de los Cafés Posibles (Rules of El Practicón for Making the Best Coffee Possible)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Coffee beans
- Felt bag
- Blanket

## Directions

1. Toast it uniformly and remove it from the roaster a little before, when you see that it still lacks a little to be at its point.
2. Put it in a little felt bag and wrap this, covering it, with a blanket so that it sweats and thus completes its true roasting.
3. Grind the precise amount at the moment of making it.
4. Use a vessel of copper or iron tin-plated.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Relieves de Ave Cebada (Leftover Fattened Poultry)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of hens, chickens, and fattened capons
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- spicy sauce or Spanish sauce
- flour
- pork or beef fat
- clean broth from stew
- sherry

## Directions

1. Soak the pieces of poultry in oil for a couple of hours, seasoning heavily with salt and pepper.
2. Remove and let drain.
3. Brown and toast the pieces over live coals on grills.
4. Serve as a fine table dish with a spicy sauce or Spanish sauce.
5. Alternatively, prepare a dark flour roux in pork or beef fat, moisten with clean broth from the stew and a tablespoon of sherry.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Relieves de Capón á la Argelina (Leftover Capon Algerian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover pieces of capon, hen, or chicken, well cut
- clean broth
- roasting juices
- parsley
- verjuice or strong vinegar with tarragon

## Directions

1. Cook the well-cut leftover poultry pieces for a quarter of an hour in clean broth reinforced with roasting juices.
2. When the sauce has reduced by half, incorporate parsley that has been trimmed and blanched in boiling water.
3. Give everything a couple of boils.
4. At the moment of serving, add a tablespoon of verjuice if available, or strong tarragon vinegar if not.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Relleno Cocido (Cooked Filling)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of raw chicken breast
- Butter from cows or pork
- Bread crumbs
- Several spoonfuls of pot broth
- Half a pound of veal breast
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Two or three carrots
- Fine herbs
- Concentrated broth

## Directions

1. Reheat in butter half a pound of raw chicken breast over moderate heat without allowing it to color; when well cooked, remove from pan and let cool and drain to retain as little fat as possible
2. In the same pan, cook bread crumbs slowly over low heat with several spoonfuls of pot broth to reduce to thick paste
3. Separately, cook half a pound of veal breast in much water with salt, pepper and two or three carrots; when cooked, let cool
4. In a marble mortar, successively crush the chicken breast, bread, and veal in that order, then pass each through a fine sieve
5. Combine the three purées and mix exactly in the mortar in equal parts, adding a couple of spoonfuls of finely chopped fine herbs
6. Moisten this filling with concentrated broth and let cook until it acquires good consistency
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Relleno de Chuletas (Chop Stuffing)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen shallots
- equal volume of mushrooms
- a good amount of parsley
- 250 grams of aged bacon, cut into small pieces
- 150 grams of beef or pork butter
- four tablespoons of oil
- a crumb of bread, carefully crumbled
- spices
- salt

## Directions

1. Mince very finely a dozen shallots and an equal volume of mushrooms with a good amount of parsley.
2. In a saucepan, cook 250 grams of aged bacon cut into small pieces in 150 grams of beef or pork butter.
3. Incorporate the shallots and mushrooms, then the parsley.
4. When everything is cooked, add four tablespoons of oil and a carefully crumbled bread crumb.
5. Season generously with spices and salt.
6. Spread this mixture on both sides of each chop.
7. Wrap the chops in white paper cut as shown in the figure and stained with oil.
8. Roast on the grill or in the oven.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Relleno de Pescado (Fish Filling)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish meat from carp, tench, barbel, eel or shad, cleaned of bones and skin
- Fine herbs
- Mushrooms
- One or two truffles for half a pound of fish mixture
- Bread crumbs from half a roll soaked in hot milk
- Salt
- Pepper
- Grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Chop together the clean fish meat from several fish
2. Chop separately fine herbs, mushrooms and one or two truffles for half a pound of fish mixture
3. Combine the fish with the herbs, mushrooms and truffas, add bread crumbs soaked in hot milk, seasoning with salt, pepper and grated nutmeg
4. Use to fill large and small fish of sea and fresh water
5. Alternatively, form into croquettes, coat in frying batter, fry in oil for an excellent dish for fasting days
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Relleno Indigesto (Indigestible Filling)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sausage meat
- Bread crumbs soaked in water
- Salt
- Sweet pepper
- Pine nuts or almonds
- Oil

## Directions

1. Macerate all ingredients together with oil
2. Use to stuff birds, make empanadas, meat cones, and when egg is added, meatballs to be cooked half an hour before serving in the pot
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Rellenos para las Aves Asadas (Stuffing for Roasted Poultry)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- sausage meat
- poultry liver
- two egg yolks
- thirty to forty roasted chestnuts
- crumb of half a roll
- broth from the pot
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare by chopping and mixing the sausage meat with the liver of the bird to be stuffed, in quantity proportioned to the volume of the bird, two egg yolks, thirty or forty roasted chestnuts so they do not fall apart when the whole cooks, the crumb of half a roll soaked in broth from the pot, and the appropriate seasoning of salt and pepper.
2. When the bird is a large turkey or medium-sized duck, the raw liver can be chopped and mixed with the stuffing, but if the bird is small, the liver should be half-cooked beforehand, because the roasting is not taking place long enough for the liver to cook completely as it should.
3. This stuffing can be rectified and improved by the following method: half-cook the liver and chop it very finely, then mix it with scrapings of truffles well seasoned with salt, pepper, and a pinch of nutmeg.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Revoltiño ó Pisto (Vegetable Hash)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Zucchini
- Onions
- Tomatoes

## Directions

1. Prepare as described in the zucchini sauté recipe, combining zucchini, onions, and tomatoes
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Riñonada de Ternera en Caja (Veal Kidney in a Box)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidney
- lean ham cut into very fine strips
- fresh beef lard
- parsley, finely chopped
- six shallots
- mushrooms
- salt and pepper
- one glass of Sherry wine
- white paper
- kitchen twine

## Directions

1. Lard the veal kidney with strips of very finely cut lean ham
2. Separately, cook and sauté in very fresh beef lard, taking care they do not burn, a little finely chopped parsley, six shallots and one portion of mushrooms
3. Season with salt and pepper and moisten with one glass of Sherry wine
4. In this juice, sauté the veal kidney for a few minutes, cut open in the shape of an open book as mentioned before
5. Remove the meat from the pan and also remove the fine herbs and vegetables from the juice, well drained, place them on the meat and close the kidney so it recovers the shape it had before being cut
6. Let it cool, and when it is well cold, wrap it four or six times in as many sheets of paper, like making a package, and around the edge or thickness of the meat tie it with kitchen twine
7. Thus prepared, cook the kidney in the previous juice for two hours in a pan over high heat, covered with a dome-shaped lid heavily weighted with fire
8. At the moment of serving, place the kidney on a platter and with scissors cut in a circle the part of paper that corresponds to the surface, leaving everything else, so the meat is as if inside a box
9. To cover the twine that holds and presses the paper, wrap it around with another white paper, which is ruffled or molded and secured with a pin
10. Pour the well-degreased and reduced sauce from the stew over the meat and serve this dish by cutting the meat within its own paper case
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Riñonada de Ternera Mechada (Larded Veal Kidneys)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidneys
- bacon
- beef fat
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Split the kidneys lengthwise and open the two halves like the pages of a book
2. Lard them with bacon using a fine larding needle
3. Sauté in a pan with beef fat and good seasoning
4. Complete the cooking over low heat, with less heat below and considerably more above the pot lid
5. The points of the bacon lards should be toasted so the dish looks good, and be naturally placed with art and symmetry
6. Serve with the veal juices added at the moment of serving, and arrange the sauce in the central hollow formed by the two open halves of the kidneys without allowing much sauce to appear on the platter
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Riñonada de Ternera á la Española (Veal Kidney Spanish Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidney
- pork lard
- salt and pepper
- two bay leaves
- two ladles of broth or cold water
- parsley sprig

## Directions

1. In pork lard and in a braising pan or earthenware casserole, brown the veal kidney and make it take color
2. The fire must be lively for this operation, which will last ten to twelve minutes
3. Then cover the pan, after seasoning the meat with salt and pepper and adding two bay leaves
4. Cook on very low heat for three hours, taking care to turn the meat every half hour, and at the midpoint of cooking moisten the meat with two ladles of broth or cold water, incorporating at the same time a sprig of parsley
5. Serve as roasted veal Spanish style
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Riñonada de Ternera á la Inglesa (Veal Kidney English Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidney
- beef lard
- one liter of broth
- parsley bouquet
- celery sprig
- salt and pepper
- one hundred twenty-five grams of rice
- one liter of freshly shelled peas

## Directions

1. Brown and color a veal kidney in beef lard and moisten with one liter of broth, adding a bouquet of parsley, a sprig of celery and seasoning generously with salt and pepper
2. After one hour of cooking, set aside half the broth and half-cook one hundred twenty-five grams of rice in it
3. Incorporate the rice with the meat along with one liter of freshly shelled peas, and let everything cook until the rice and peas are done
4. Place the meat on a serving dish that can go to the fire, on top of the rice and peas well mixed together, and put it in the oven with the door open, or leave it near the heat so it does not cool while the decanted and reduced sauce takes on enough consistency by cooking a while
5. Pour the sauce over the dish when serving
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Riñonada de Ternera á la Provenzal (Veal Kidney Provençal Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef tongue, cooled
- veal kidney
- beef lard
- refined oil
- two or three onions
- two cups of broth
- salt and pepper
- two or three glasses of white wine
- mushroom trimmings

## Directions

1. Cool a cooked beef tongue prepared as in the spicy sauce formula and cut the thickest part into thin slices
2. Cut these slices into strips or shreds like bacon for larding
3. Lard the veal kidney prepared as in the previous formula with these strips and brown it well in beef lard
4. Separately, brown and color two or three finely cut onions very well in refined oil
5. Moisten with two cups of broth and strain through a fine sieve to moisten the veal with this sauce
6. Add good seasoning of salt and pepper, two or three glasses of white wine according to the volume of the kidney, and let everything cook very slowly for two hours
7. Pass the onions remaining in the sieve through a fine sieve to convert them into a purée
8. Cook mushroom trimmings in this purée very slowly so the sauce thickens gradually
9. Place this garnish on the meat and in the serving dish to complete the plate
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Riñonada de Ternera á la Provenzal (Veal Kidneys Provençal Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidneys
- beef tongue
- beef fat
- onions
- refined oil
- broth

## Directions

1. Cool a cooked beef tongue that has been prepared as indicated in the spicy beef tongue recipe
2. Cut the thickest part into thin slices and trim these into strips or lards like bacon for larding
3. Lard the veal kidneys, prepared as in the previous recipe, with these strips
4. Sauté until it takes on good color in beef fat
5. In another pan, sauté and brown two or three finely cut onions in refined oil
6. Moisten with two cups of broth and strain through a sieve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Rodaballo á la Holandesa (Turbot Dutch-Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- turbot
- sea water or very salty water
- two lemon halves
- beef butter, raw
- salt

## Directions

1. Rub the turbot on all sides with two lemon halves before placing it in the water.
2. Cook the turbot in a turbotera (a diamond-shaped cooking vessel) with short broth, using sea water or, in its absence, very salty water.
3. The fish is cooked as soon as the water reaches its first boil, and it can be served immediately, or it can remain in the water away from heat for as long as desired without the cooking point being altered.
4. Remove the turbot from the turbotera and place it on a platter over a well-pressed napkin that serves as a bed for the fish.
5. Serve melted beef butter that has been warmed in a bain-marie with a pinch of salt in a sauce boat.
6. When the turbot is served cold, each diner seasons it to taste with oil and vinegar.
7. Garnish the platter with fresh parsley branches, whether served hot or cold.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ropa Vieja de Vaca (Shredded Beef)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- lard
- flour
- broth from the puchero
- salt
- pepper
- thin slices of beef
- vinegar
- tomato

## Directions

1. Cut onions and cook in lard until tender
2. Add a bit of flour and stir until it takes color
3. Add broth from the puchero, salt and pepper
4. When the sauce is almost reduced, add thin slices of leftover beef
5. Sprinkle with a little vinegar and remove from heat when well combined
6. Add tomato if available
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Rosas (Waffle Pastries)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- pasta more liquid than for fritters
- sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. The molds should be well greased internally when the liquid is poured into them with a spoon
2. Close the mold, and with a knife clean the dough that oozes out
3. Place the mold on a small stove or brazier like those for ironing
4. Alternatively, dip the small mold in the liquid batter up to half its height
5. Let it drain a little, and quickly submerge it in hot oil
6. Immediately release the dough from the mold
7. Turn it over and remove it to dust with sugar
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Rosbif (Roast Beef)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef, never as large as the English volume
- Good refined oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley, chopped
- Two bay leaves
- Some chopped shallots
- The juices from the meat

## Directions

1. Twelve hours before putting it on the spit, place the piece of rosbif in good refined oil seasoned with salt, pepper, chopped parsley, two bay leaves, and some chopped shallots
2. Turn the rosbif frequently so that it soaks evenly
3. Roast it for two or three hours, depending on size
4. Do not remove the meat from the spit until its interior is cooked but maintains a rosy color without releasing blood
5. Serve at natural temperature with its own juices in a sauce boat, and around the meat may be placed some potatoes in the Dutch style shaped like zucchini, peeled after being cooked in steam
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ríñones de Carnero a la Mayordoma (Lamb Kidneys in the Steward's Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb kidneys
- Beef butter
- Salt and pepper
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the kidneys the same way as for skewers, threading them on larding needles or small reeds
2. Remove them from the skewers for serving and place them on a very hot platter
3. On the platter, have a mixture of beef butter with salt, pepper and parsley
4. Pass and repass the kidneys over and through this mixture to coat them
5. Present at the table
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ríñones de Carnero al Champagne (Lamb Kidneys with Champagne)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of lamb kidneys
- Half pound of beef butter
- Half ounce salt for seasoning
- Half ounce flour
- One quarter bottle of Champagne
- A good cook

## Directions

1. Combine the kidneys, butter, salt, flour and Champagne according to the formula of Mr. Cirodde
2. Cook with skill and attention of a good cook
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ríñones de Carnero al Jerez (Lamb Kidneys with Sherry)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb kidneys
- Beef butter
- Salt and pepper
- Bread for croutons
- Sherry wine

## Directions

1. Sauté the kidneys as for the previous recipe
2. Fry the bread croutons in the same sherry wine instead of plain oil
3. Moisten the sautéed kidneys with good sherry wine
4. Serve on the wine-soaked croutons
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ríñones de Carnero Salteados (Sautéed Lamb Kidneys)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb kidneys
- Beef butter
- Salt and pepper
- Bread pieces for croutons

## Directions

1. Prepare the kidneys as if for skewers, but this time completely separate the two halves
2. Sauté them in a pan over high heat in beef butter, seasoning with salt and pepper as they cook
3. Separately, fry bread cubes the same size as the kidney pieces and in equal number
4. Place the kidney pieces on a very hot platter, each piece on its crouton
5. Pour meat juice or Spanish sauce over the top
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ríñones Ensartados (Lamb Kidneys on Skewers)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Six lamb kidneys
- Beef butter, melted
- Breadcrumbs
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Remove the membrane from the kidneys
2. Cut each kidney lengthwise along the outer curved line without completely separating the two halves so they form a single piece when opened
3. Thread three kidneys onto each skewer, piercing through both halves of each kidney
4. Soak the prepared kidneys in melted beef butter that has not boiled
5. Coat with breadcrumbs mixed with salt and pepper
6. Place the kidneys on live coals on a grill and turn twice to cook through
7. If using reed skewers or larding needles, remove the kidneys before serving
8. If using presentable skewers, serve the kidneys still threaded on the skewers, each one being one guest's portion, accompanied by a piece of lemon to eat the kidneys plain
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salchichón Casero (Homemade Sausage)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of pork loin
- Half pound of very fat bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg, grated
- A few grains of coriander
- Beef intestines
- Paper for wrapping
- Retama (broom plant) for smoking

## Directions

1. Mince together the pork loin and bacon; the mixture need not be very fine
2. Season with plenty of salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, and a few grains of coriander
3. Fill beef intestines of variable length with this mixture
4. Hang the sausage inside the kitchen chimney to cure in smoke for ten days, being careful it does not burn, and wrap it first in three or four sheets of brown paper
5. During this time, burn a little retama in the chimney three or four times so the sausage comes out exquisite
6. After smoking, cook the sausage in water with salt, pepper, carrots, onions studded with cloves, and a few bay leaves
7. Two hours of slow cooking are sufficient to finish the operation
8. Let the sausage cool in the same water in which it cooked
9. Remove, let drain, and eat sliced into thin slices, the thinner the more flavorful
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmonete Esparrillado (Grilled Red Mullet)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- red mullet
- salt
- pepper
- oil

## Directions

1. Make symmetrical transverse cuts in the skin on both sides so it does not burst with heat
2. Sprinkle the cuts with salt and a pinch of pepper, and sprinkle with oil
3. Leave for an hour apart on a plate
4. Afterwards clean very well with a cloth and roast on the grill over live fire
5. Serve with steward's sauce, but the best sauce for red mullets is the one from the recipe of Miss Sicsú
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmonetes Fritos (Fried Red Mullet)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- red mullets
- flour
- beaten egg

## Directions

1. Flour them first, then coat with beaten egg, and fry them this way so they are more tasty
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmorejo de Becacines del Maestro (Master's Salmorejo of Snipe)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two snipe
- salt

## Directions

1. Roast two snipe on a spit at medium temperature after cleaning them very well on the outside, knowing that inside, like shells and snails, these animals are not gutted
2. When the snipe are half-roasted, divide them according to all the good rules of carving art
3. Then cut the wings, legs, breast, and tail into two pieces each
4. Place the pieces in a serving dish
5. Prepare the sauce in the plate where the carving was done
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmorejo de Chochas (Woodcock in Salmorejo Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcocks
- Tarragon vinegar
- Beef lard, size of a walnut per bird
- Bacon rind
- Beef lard for sautéing
- Clean concentrated broth
- Jamaica rum
- Fried croutons
- Salt
- Strong seasoning

## Directions

1. Remove the giblets from the woodcocks as previously described.
2. Clean them well and return them to the body.
3. Rinse the woodcocks inside with tarragon vinegar.
4. Tie the thighs, neck, and legs with twine, with the beak inserted into the flesh.
5. Insert a piece of beef lard the size of a walnut into each woodcock's body.
6. Season strongly and coat the outside of the woodcocks with fat obtained by rubbing a bacon rind over them.
7. Sauté the prepared birds in beef lard over high heat until evenly browned.
8. Moisten with clean concentrated broth and cook for forty minutes over low heat.
9. Divide each cooked woodcock into four pieces and arrange on a platter so they appear uncut, on top of as many fried croutons soaked in the juices as there are woodcocks, ensuring each bread piece is larger than each woodcock piece.
10. At the table, uncover the croutons and sprinkle them with Jamaica rum.
11. Remove all the contents from inside the bird to a soup plate, mince it, and knead with a little rum.
12. Coat all the croutons with this paste.
13. Serve each diner one crouton and one piece of woodcock.
14. Set fire to the crouton so it slightly chars the woodcock, and eat once the dish has cooled.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmorejo de Chochas a la Francesa (Woodcock in French Salmorejo Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Three woodcocks
- Two shallots
- Two cloves
- Mixed herb bouquet
- Half bottle of red Bordeaux wine
- One liter of Spanish broth
- Fried bread pieces
- Stuffing

## Directions

1. Clean and roast three woodcocks.
2. Once cold, cut them into pieces and remove the skins, placing the pieces in a flat sauté pan.
3. Pound the frames and remaining meat and place in a saucepan with two shallots, two cloves, and a mixed herb bouquet.
4. Add half a bottle of red Bordeaux wine.
5. Reduce to half and add one liter of Spanish broth.
6. Cook very gently for half an hour beside the fire, taking care to skim and decrease.
7. Strain through a sieve and reduce the sauce until it coats the spoon.
8. Sauce the woodcocks in the sauté pan.
9. Arrange in the platter in pyramid form, sauce them, and garnish with pieces of fried bread spread with a filling.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmorejo de Chochas con Limón (Woodcock in Lemon Salmorejo Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Three roasted woodcocks
- Four lemons
- One additional lemon, sliced thin
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Nutmeg, grated
- Two tablespoons of mustard
- One glass of wine
- Woodcock livers and giblets

## Directions

1. Carve three roasted woodcocks on a platter according to proper technique.
2. Pound the livers and remaining giblets.
3. Squeeze the juice from four lemons and add one lemon sliced into thin rounds.
4. Add salt, pepper, spices, and grated nutmeg.
5. Add two tablespoons of mustard and one glass of wine.
6. Combine with the pounded giblets and serve with the carved woodcock.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmorejo de Chochas con Limón (Woodcock Salmorejo with Lemon)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- three roasted woodcocks
- juice of four lemons
- one lemon cut into thin slices
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- grated nutmeg
- two tablespoons of mustard
- one glass of white wine

## Directions

1. Take three roasted woodcocks and carve them in a serving dish according to proper technique
2. Crush the livers and remaining offal
3. Squeeze the juice of four lemons and add one lemon cut into thin slices
4. Add salt, pepper, spices, and grated nutmeg, two tablespoons of mustard, and a glass of white wine
5. Prepare this mixture in a saucepan and place it over hot coals
6. Beat it so it is well mixed and do not let it cook
7. Drizzle with a few drops of oil
8. Place the breasts of the bird on top and serve very hot
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmorejo de Chochas á la Casera (Homestyle Woodcock Salmorejo)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcock offal
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- thyme
- bay leaf

## Directions

1. Grind the offal of the woodcock in a mortar
2. Add white wine, salt, pepper, thyme, and bay leaf
3. Place over fire
4. When reduced to half its volume, pass through a sieve
5. Serve the woodcocks with this sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmón a la Chambord (Chambord Salmon)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon, cooked in blue court-bouillon
- Truffles, cut into wedges
- Mushrooms, cut into wedges
- Jerez wine
- Veal lardoons, sliced thin and glazed
- Squab breast trimmings
- Squab breast meatballs
- Crayfish
- Fresh oysters, shucked
- Fine oil

## Directions

1. Cook the salmon in blue court-bouillon and drain it.
2. Insert wedges of truffles alternating with wedges of mushrooms into the loins.
3. Place the prepared salmon in an oval serving dish and sprinkle with Jerez wine.
4. Cover entirely with thin slices of larded and glazed veal, squab breast trimmings, and squab breast meatballs, which shall adorn the platter with crayfish placed among them, and a garnish of fresh shucked oysters.
5. With the dish thus arranged and carefully sprinkled with fine oil, so that the garnish does not become disarranged or decomposed, place in the oven at high heat for five minutes, and leave it there for half an hour more, but with the oven door open.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmón Empapelado (Papered Salmon)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons Marseille oil
- finely chopped parsley
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- garlic
- nutmeg
- juice of half a lemon
- two pound piece of salmon
- breadcrumbs
- good quality paper

## Directions

1. In a soup plate, put three tablespoons of Marseille oil and beat and mix well with finely chopped parsley, onion, salt, pepper, clove, garlic, nutmeg, and the juice of half a lemon.
2. From a two pound piece of salmon, cut thin slices in the longitudinal direction of the fish, like slices of cured ham.
3. One by one, soak each slice in the mixture and wrap it in breadcrumbs.
4. Place each slice in a good quality paper envelope.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmón Empapelado (Papillote Salmon)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Three tablespoons of Marseille oil
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Onion
- Salt
- Pepper
- Clove
- Garlic
- Nutmeg
- Juice of half a lemon
- Two pounds of salmon
- Breadcrumbs
- White paper

## Directions

1. In a soup plate, put three tablespoons of Marseille oil and beat and mix well with finely chopped parsley, onion, salt, pepper, clove, garlic, nutmeg and the juice of half a lemon.
2. From a piece of two pounds of salmon, cut thin slices lengthwise like ham slices.
3. One by one, soak each slice in the mixture and wrap in breadcrumbs.
4. Place each slice in a sheet of good white paper, closed like a letter, and roast slowly over a grill, to serve on a napkin placed on a platter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmón en Asol (Salmon in Court-Bouillon)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole salmon
- 250 grams fresh butter
- five or six onions
- cloves
- carrots, cut into rounds
- three or four bay leaves
- salt
- pepper
- parsley sprigs

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the salmon through the gills without splitting the belly.
2. Cook the salmon in a blue court-bouillon as indicated at the beginning of the fish section.
3. Add to the court-bouillon 250 grams of very fresh butter, five or six onions studded with cloves, a good quantity of carrots cut into rounds, three or four bay leaves, and season well with salt and pepper.
4. Serve the salmon cooked whole and adorned with sprigs of parsley on a flat, oval wooden platter without paint, or on a well-arranged wooden board, wrapped carefully in a pressed napkin.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salmón Esparrillado (Grilled Salmon)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon, thin slices
- Oil
- Shallots, minced
- Parsley, minced
- Salt and pepper
- Vinegar
- Cow butter

## Directions

1. Very simple to make.
2. Thin slices, well coated with oil, are grilled on grills and are served to eat with a sauce made on the spot, with very fried oil that is poured into a metal sauce boat, and into which a mincing of shallots and parsley is incorporated, everything very finely cut, salt and pepper.
3. If this little sauce is not liked, it is substituted with oil and vinegar, or cow butter, in which the piece of food is dipped in the same plate before bringing it to the mouth.
4. Salmon should always be eaten with potatoes cooked in steam as if they were bread.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salpicón con Trufas (Mixed Meat Ragout with Truffles)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams of beef butter
- same weight of lean ham, cut into small squares
- half of a large onion, finely chopped
- a handful of mushrooms
- four or five truffles in very thin slices
- veal or lamb sweetbreads
- lamb or veal testicles
- salt
- spices
- concentrated broth
- a couple of tablespoons of meat juice or roasting drippings
- three egg yolks dissolved in water
- a teaspoon of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Sauté in 125 grams of beef butter the same weight of lean ham cut into very small squares, and half of a large onion also finely chopped.
2. When the onion has taken a good golden color, add a handful of mushrooms cut up and four or five truffles in very thin slices, veal or lamb sweetbreads, lamb or veal testicles, and good seasoning of salt and spices.
3. Moisten with concentrated broth, to which a couple of tablespoons of meat juice or roasting drippings are added.
4. Cook everything over a slow fire for the space of half an hour.
5. At the moment of serving, away from the fire, add three egg yolks dissolved in water and a teaspoon of lemon juice.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salpicón de Ave ó de Caza (Poultry or Game Ragout)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted chicken, turkey or hen breasts, or good meat from rabbits, hares, partridges, or woodcocks, roasted or braised
- Spanish sauce
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut into very small and equal pieces the breasts of roasted chickens, turkeys or hens, or the good meat from roasted or braised rabbits, hares, partridges, or woodcocks.
2. Cook them in a Spanish sauce, which is thickened when serving with two dissolved egg yolks.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salpicón de Vaca a la Zaragozana (Beef Salad Zaragoza-Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- lean meat
- onion
- oil
- pepper
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook a piece of ham and two parts more of lean meat
2. Cut it fine and mince a little raw onion, which is sprinkled on top
3. Sprinkle with raw oil, pepper, salt and a little vinegar
4. This will be a cold dish to serve preferably in the countryside
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salpicón Simple (Simple Mixed Meat Ragout)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- good lean ham
- a piece of veal tongue or two or three pieces of mutton tongue, cooked beforehand
