- poultry breasts
- leftover rabbit or hare
- poultry livers
- a handful of mushrooms
- artichoke hearts
- a cup of pot broth
- a glass of white wine
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- a bouquet of fines herbes
- potato starch

## Directions

1. Make a brown sauce moistened with a cup of pot broth and a glass of white wine, reduced to achieve some consistency.
2. In this sauce, cook good lean ham, a piece of veal tongue or two or three pieces of mutton tongue (cooked beforehand), poultry breasts, leftover rabbit or hare, poultry livers, a handful of mushrooms, and artichoke hearts.
3. Cut all ingredients into equal size and shape, like dice.
4. Season well with salt, pepper, nutmeg, and a bouquet of fines herbes.
5. Cook slowly for half an hour until all is well soaked in the sauce.
6. Remove the bouquet and decant the sauce into another saucepan.
7. Give the sauce more consistency with a little potato starch.
8. Stir vigorously and add the salpicón when the sauce is well thickened, or better, if the salpicón has been kept very hot, place it on a serving dish and pour the sauce over it at that moment.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Ajo Oli (Garlic and Oil Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- garlic
- olive oil
- fried and refried

## Directions

1. Compose this sauce, very common in Andalusia, with garlic and with fried and refried olive oil.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Amarilla (Yellow Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fried bread
- garlic
- cumin
- one clove
- some strands of saffron
- broth
- breadcrumb for thickening

## Directions

1. Grind in the mortar fried bread, garlic and cumin, one clove, and some strands of saffron
2. Dissolve in broth, thicken with breadcrumb and give it a few boils
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Americana Cocida (Cooked American Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- meat broth
- Madeira wine
- butter
- corn flour, well toasted
- orange juice, sour

## Directions

1. With broth made with meat, etc., but with the addition of Madeira wine, make a sauce with butter and corn flour, well toasted beforehand in a pan in the oven, so that when mixed with the butter it does not have to stay long in it
2. Add half of the broth and let it cook for two hours, and with the other half put in a pan over strong heat make a thick sauce, stirring continuously in all directions so it does not stick
3. When done, mix it with the sauce and pass it through cheesecloth, then add the juice of a sour orange
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Americana Cruda (Raw American Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked yellow squash
- three or four garlic cloves
- three roasted tomatoes, peeled
- sufficient salt
- a few grains of pepper
- half a small cup of vinegar
- double quantity of broth
- one small onion
- a little finely chopped parsley
- sweet pepper, all raw and cold

## Directions

1. Mash all the squash, garlic, roasted tomatoes, salt, and pepper in a mortar
2. Add half a small cup of vinegar with double the quantity of broth
3. Add one small onion, a little finely chopped parsley, and sweet pepper, all raw and cold
4. This sauce is very good for vegetables
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Americana Cruda (Raw American Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow squash, cooked in the pot
- three or four cloves of garlic
- three roasted tomatoes, without skin
- salt
- some peppercorns
- half a small cup of vinegar

## Directions

1. Take yellow squash cooked in the pot, three or four cloves of garlic, three roasted tomatoes without skin, sufficient salt and some peppercorns
2. Pound everything in the mortar
3. Add half a small cup of vinegar
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Bechamel (Béchamel Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- half liter glazed broth
- good meat broth
- half liter cream

## Directions

1. Reduce over high heat half a liter of glaze to which a couple of ladles of good meat broth from the pot has been incorporated
2. Let it cook, stirring the liquid constantly until it decreases by one third of its original volume
3. Separately also reduce by one third half a liter of cream
4. Mix the cream with the reduced sauce, stirring always until it has the consistency of a clear paste
5. Then strain by pressing firmly through a clear strainer
6. If the dish it is destined for is not yet ready, it is necessary to maintain the temperature of the sauce in a water bath so it does not cool
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Bechamel de Vigilia (Lenten Béchamel Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams beef butter or fine Valencia oil
- a handful of mushrooms
- half tablespoon flour
- half liter milk
- salt and pepper
- 2 or 3 cloves of garlic (optional)

## Directions

1. Sauté in 60 grams of beef butter or fine Valencia oil a small handful of carefully trimmed mushrooms
2. Add half a tablespoon of flour and stir very quickly with the spatula so the flour does not brown
3. Moisten little by little with half a liter of milk, seasoned with salt and pepper
4. Let cook until the sauce has taken on consistency
5. 2 or 3 cloves of garlic can be added if desired, which should be removed before serving
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Bechamel Ordinaria (Ordinary Béchamel Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 grams beef butter
- half tablespoon flour
- 1 bowl hot milk
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. To 60 grams of beef butter, melted over gentle heat, add half a tablespoon of flour
2. Stir much so the flour does not brown, and without stopping to stir, gradually add a bowl of very hot milk, and salt and pepper according to taste
3. It is better to thin this sauce rather than make it thick, because as it generally serves to reheat certain dishes, it reduces easily and thickens quickly at the slightest simmer
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Blanca (White Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two large tablespoons of fine flour
- cold water
- two ounces of beef lard
- salt
- a touch of pepper
- one egg yolk without the germ
- two tablespoons of cold water

## Directions

1. In a metal saucepan, add two large tablespoons of fine flour
2. Gradually, with cold water and a boxwood spatula, knead the mixture away from the fire on the kitchen counter
3. Once the dough is formed and without stopping stirring, continue adding water to produce a very clear and uniform liquid
4. Place the saucepan on low heat and continue stirring with the spatula very quickly until the mixture begins to boil
5. Then remove from the heat and leave in a place where it can continue cooking very slowly for ten minutes without needing further attention
6. At the moment of serving, remove the saucepan from the heat and add two ounces of beef lard, salt and a touch of pepper
7. Stir vigorously to mix everything and activate the melting of the lard, and add the liaison
8. The liaison is one egg yolk without the germ, dissolved in two tablespoons of cold water in a glass, all this without ceasing the movement with the spatula
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Blanca a la Cubana (White Cuban Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of cooked veal from the previous day
- poultry broth
- broth from the pot
- four egg yolks
- a little parsley
- butter

## Directions

1. Take pieces of cooked veal from the previous day and cut into smaller pieces
2. Add poultry broth with broth from the pot
3. Put in a pan to boil
4. Add four egg yolks, a little parsley, and butter
5. Cook over low heat until the sauce is bound
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Borracha (Drunken Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter liter glazed broth
- 1 large glass white wine
- salt
- peppercorns
- a handful of parsley root cut into thin strips
- curly leaf parsley

## Directions

1. In a quarter liter of glaze add a large glass of white wine, salt, peppercorns in grain and a small handful of parsley root cut into thin strips
2. Let cook over low heat until the sauce reduces and takes on consistency
3. Separately blanch curly leaf parsley by submerging it briefly in boiling water, cut any which way
4. Remove from water and cool in another very cold water to add it to the drunken sauce at the moment of serving
5. When it is desired that this sauce be for vigil days, use the vigil broth or better the broth that has been used to cook fish with the same procedure, especially since this sauce generally serves for fish cooked plain or in short broth
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Brenca (Brenca Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- poultry of any kind such as chickens, squabs, partridges
- onions
- beef butter
- white wine
- spices
- salt
- lemon juice
- fried bread slices

## Directions

1. Roast poultry of any kind, such as chickens, squabs, partridges, etc., and cut into quarters.
2. Cut onions into strips and fry in beef butter.
3. Generally combine with the roast, reheating everything very well.
4. Add a little white wine, spices, salt and lemon juice.
5. Serve this sauce with slices of fried bread.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Acederas (Sorrel Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- some sorrel leaves
- a small piece of fried bread
- parsley
- garlic
- cumin
- broth from the pot
- bread or toasted flour for thickening
- pepper and clove (optional)

## Directions

1. Take some sorrel leaves, a small piece of fried bread, parsley, garlic and cumin
2. Pound everything in the mortar until well ground and made into a paste
3. Dissolve in broth from the pot and thicken with bread or toasted flour
4. Some also add pepper and clove to this sauce, and give the whole a few boils
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Aceite (Oil Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- 125 grams clarified oil

## Directions

1. In a pan placed over embers or inside a bowl with boiling water, dissolve 4 egg yolks with salt and pepper
2. Once these are warm, pour 125 grams of good clarified oil over them and shake the mixture vigorously
3. Prevent the sauce from cooking and ensure it does not get too hot, as the eggs would separate from the oil, the binding would fail and the sauce would not work
4. Make it at the precise moment of serving as an accompaniment to vegetables cooked in water
5. Alternatively, submerge a bowl for a few minutes in boiling water, remove and dry quickly, and while it retains heat, prepare the mixture of eggs with oil and its corresponding seasoning
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Almendras (Almond Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds
- garlic
- cumin
- cooked egg yolk
- white broth or water

## Directions

1. Make the same as the pine nut sauce, but using almonds
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Cebollas (Onion Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams of pork butter
- 125 grams of ham loin
- 12 large onions the size of apples
- salt
- pepper
- a little flour
- 1 ladle of concentrated broth
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. In the pork butter, sauté and cook well the ham loin cut into pieces, and the onions cut into slices and then crosswise into dice-like pieces
2. Season with salt, pepper, and a little flour
3. Moisten with the ladle of concentrated broth
4. When the ham and onions are well cooked and the sauce has reduced by half, remove from fire and pass through a strainer, pressing well
5. Before serving, bind the sauce with the two egg yolks diluted in cold water
6. The ham can be kept for other uses or cut into small pieces to enhance the appearance and taste of the sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Comadres (Comadres Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one hundred twenty-five grams of beef or pork butter
- twelve large fresh mushrooms, finely chopped
- one carrot
- one turnip
- one onion
- some small onions
- finely chopped parsley
- one cup of broth
- one cup of white wine
- one handful of crumb of tender bread
- two cups of milk

## Directions

1. In one hundred twenty-five grams of beef or pork butter, sauté twelve large fresh mushrooms, finely chopped, a carrot and turnip in slices, one onion, some small onions, and finely chopped parsley
2. When the vegetables are half cooked in the fat, gradually add one cup of broth and then another of white wine
3. Let cook for half an hour over low heat
4. Season according to each person's taste and pass through a hair sieve
5. Separately, cook one handful of crumb of tender bread in two cups of milk until the liquid is consumed
6. Strain through a fine sieve and add this breadcrumb to the preceding sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Estudiantes (Student Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- six shallots or garlic cloves
- parsley
- one cup of water
- one ladle of broth
- one tablespoon of vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Without sautéing in any fat, cook six shallots or garlic cloves with a little parsley in one cup of water.
2. Add one ladle of broth and one tablespoon of vinegar with the corresponding seasoning of salt and pepper.
3. When this has cooked for twenty minutes, the sauce or broth can be served.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Kari (Curry Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one piece of beef butter the size of an egg
- one teaspoon of curry
- nutmeg
- two spoonfuls of flour
- two ladfuls of water or broth
- another piece of beef butter

## Directions

1. Place the butter in a pan and melt it over gentle heat, stirring constantly
2. Add the curry and nutmeg
3. Add the flour and stir
4. Without stopping stirring, add two ladfuls of water or broth
5. Let cook for a quarter hour
6. At the moment of serving, bind with another piece of beef butter, which should melt with the heat of the sauce and away from the fire in the pan
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Manteca Negra (Black Butter Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams of beef butter
- salt
- pepper
- 1 ladle of good vinegar
- 1 small teaspoon of capers

## Directions

1. Melt the beef butter in a pan over fire until it reaches a very dark color
2. Season well and add the ladle of good vinegar
3. Stir vigorously and remove the pan from the fire
4. At the moment of serving, add the small teaspoon of capers to the butter
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Mantequilla (Butter Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon flour
- salt
- white pepper in powder
- a pinch of nutmeg
- 30 grams beef butter
- 2 cloves

## Directions

1. Mix together in a pan 1 tablespoon of flour with ground salt, white pepper in powder and a pinch of nutmeg
2. Add 30 grams of beef butter and 2 cloves of spices
3. Place over low heat with enough water to form a clear paste; stir constantly and gradually add 250 grams of beef butter in parts
4. Keep this sauce on the fire for a quarter hour without stopping to stir, being careful that it does not boil, as it would spoil
5. Before serving, strain through a metal mesh strainer
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Mostaza para Carnes Frías (Mustard Sauce for Cold Meats)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of meat
- mustard
- vinegar
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the pieces of meat
2. Add mustard, vinegar, and oil in proportion to the amount of sauce you want to make
3. Add salt and pepper and lemon juice
4. Stir for fifteen or twenty minutes and serve this way
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Pepinillos (Pickled Cucumber Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- pickled cucumbers
- flour
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- broth

## Directions

1. Chop the pickled cucumbers finely and put in a saucepan with a pinch of flour, butter, salt and pepper.
2. Add the broth, keep over heat until it thickens, and serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Perejil á la Española (Spanish Parsley Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- a small bunch of very green parsley
- vinegar in small quantity
- one clove of garlic, crushed
- some cumin

## Directions

1. Take a small bunch of very green parsley and chop it well with a knife or crescent-shaped blade on a board, or pound it in a mortar until it becomes a paste
2. Dissolve this paste in a little water, and better yet in broth from the pot
3. Add vinegar in small quantity, one crushed clove of garlic and some cumin
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Piñones (Pine Nut Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- a handful of pine nuts, well washed and shelled
- some cloves of garlic
- cumin
- one cooked egg yolk
- white broth or water

## Directions

1. Take a handful of pine nuts well washed and shelled, put them in the mortar with some cloves of garlic, cumin and one cooked egg yolk
2. Grind, and diluted in white broth or water, give it a boil and it is ready to serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Pobres (Poor Man's Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- broth
- fat
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- shallots
- parsley

## Directions

1. In the saucepan add broth and fat, a spoonful of vinegar, salt, pepper, and five or six shallots and parsley.
2. Let boil until the shallots are well cooked.
3. This is a sauce that serves to reheat the meat from stew that remained from the previous meal.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Tomate (Tomato Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams of fat (oil, butter, or lard)
- eight large tomatoes the size of eggs
- two onions of equal size
- broth or water
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the tomatoes and onions in any manner.
2. Fry them well in a pan in the hot fat.
3. Moisten everything with a little broth or water, finishing the cooking over low heat.
4. Season during the frying.
5. Pass the sauce through a strainer, forcing it to pass with a wooden spoon.
6. If the sauce is thin, stir it over the fire so it boils a little and reduces.
7. If it is thick, thin it with broth.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Tomate (Tomato Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- oil or pork lard, one-fifth the volume of the tomatoes
- tomatoes, halved and without seeds
- a good tablespoon of flour
- one tablespoon of oil or lard
- water
- one egg yolk

## Directions

1. In oil or pork lard, one-fifth the volume of the tomatoes used for the sauce, fry halved and unseeded tomatoes
2. When they are fried and refried, pass through a strainer and the residue that remains continues to be recooked in the same pan with a little water
3. Pass this broth again, collecting it separately in a bowl
4. In the same pan, toast a good tablespoon of flour in another tablespoon of oil or lard and lengthen with the water that is in the bowl
5. Pour this over the first passed tomato, give it a boil, and away from the fire bind it with the yolk of an egg
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Tomate a la Andaluza (Andalusian Tomato Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes, cleaned and unwashed
- olive oil
- two cloves of garlic, finely chopped
- salt

## Directions

1. Without peeling, washed and drained tomatoes, fry them well in oil with two cloves of garlic finely chopped.
2. Let them pass and even burn a little.
3. Season with salt.
4. Without straining the sauce, which results very thick, serve the tomato with its skin.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Tomate, Estilo Antiguo (Tomato Sauce, Old Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- cumin, ground
- broth from the pot

## Directions

1. Peel the tomatoes, which is achieved by putting them in water and boiling for a moment so they release the skin easily
2. Make them into a paste, dissolving this paste in broth from the pot and adding some ground cumin
3. If the tomato was preserved in dried form, it will be necessary to put it in water with the vessel over a pot that is on the fire, so that it heats with the steam, taking care to stir it from time to time so that it dissolves perfectly, and in this state it can be served
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Trufas (Truffle Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams of truffles, well cleaned
- 60 grams of fresh beef butter
- one cup of good consumed broth
- two spoonfuls of glaze (if on hand)
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the truffles, brushing them with a suitable brush rather than washing them
2. Sauté them in the fresh beef butter without allowing the butter to brown
3. Moisten with one cup of good consumed broth, to which add, if on hand, two spoonfuls of glaze
4. After twenty minutes of cooking over gentle heat, remove the sauce from the heat
5. Skim off the fat and serve
6. This sauce may also be made very well with oil and should be seasoned somewhat sharply with salt and pepper
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa de Vinagre Criolla (Creole Vinegar Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- broth and vinegar in equal parts
- salt
- three or four finely chopped garlic cloves
- pepper
- sufficient parsley

## Directions

1. Take a pan and add equal parts of broth and vinegar
2. Add salt, three or four finely chopped garlic cloves, and pepper
3. Add sufficient parsley and mash everything
4. Put everything on the fire for about one hour
5. This sauce can be applied to all roasted meats
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Dorada (Golden Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- butter from beef
- white sauce
- brown sauce
- beef broth
- burned sugar or caramel
- egg yolk
- water

## Directions

1. For meatless days, prepare with beef butter, a white sauce reinforced with a pronounced brown sauce, which is bound at the moment of serving with egg yolk diluted in water.
2. For meat meals, to the white sauce add reduced beef broth, which is colored with burned sugar or caramel, as one colors broth.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Española (Spanish Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of bacon
- one pound of ham
- two pounds of veal kidney without bone and without fat
- onions
- carrots
- concentrated broth
- fine herbs in a bundle
- salt
- pepper
- some cloves
- flour for liaison

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, add the bacon and ham, all cut into small pieces or strips.
2. Place on top two pounds of veal kidney without bone and without fat, and make the meat sweat with some spoonfuls of concentrated broth, cooking slowly until the juices run.
3. Incorporate some onions and carrots during the cooking.
4. Prick the meat in different places with the point of a knife so all the juice comes out, then add abundant concentrated broth to cover the contents of the saucepan.
5. Add fine herbs in a bundle, salt, pepper, and some cloves, and let cook very slowly for about two hours.
6. Pass the sauce through a sieve and to give it consistency, bind it with flour, making the liaison separately and dark, done skillfully.
7. Mix the liaison with the sauce, place the saucepan over heat, and reduce by slow cooking to one quarter.
8. When about to use in a stew, for example, add two spoonfuls of this sauce to a small pan with a glass of white wine, Sherry, or Champagne, let reduce considerably over high heat, and incorporate pieces of truffles and mushrooms.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Financiera (Financier Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts of very good concentrated broth
- about three tablespoons of very fine minced mixture made with rooster crests and kidneys, tender parts of chicken offal and well-blanched mushrooms
- truffles or earth truffles if available
- small pieces of artichoke hearts
- a little ham
- pitted olives
- one glass of good Jerez wine
- salt

## Directions

1. In a saucepan add two quarts of very good concentrated broth and about three tablespoons of very fine minced mixture made with rooster crests and kidneys, tender parts of chicken offal and well-blanched mushrooms
2. If there are truffles or earth truffles, add a bit of each if available
3. Add small pieces of artichoke hearts, a little ham, pitted olives, one glass of good Jerez wine and a touch of salt
4. Let it cook together
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa General para Toda Clase de Manjares (General Sauce for All Kinds of Dishes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- broth
- vinegar
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- orange peel
- shallots
- bay leaf
- thyme
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. In a saucepan place one measure of broth, another of vinegar, and two of white wine.
2. Add salt and pepper, one clove, a piece of orange peel, six crushed shallots, bay leaf, thyme, lemon juice.
3. Cover well and place in infusion for twelve hours over coals.
4. Strain through cheesecloth, sieve or fine strainer.
5. Bottle and use as needed.
6. It keeps for a long time and is used for all kinds of meats, poultry, fish and vegetables.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Genovesa (Genoese Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- White sauce base
- one tablespoon of dried parsley in powder or fresh parsley in greater quantity
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. When the mixture is cooking slowly, incorporate one tablespoon of dried parsley in powder or fresh parsley in greater quantity and the juice of half a lemon
2. Continue the operation as previously to obtain classical Genoese sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Glotona (Gluttonous Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams of truffles
- 125 grams of beef butter, divided into two equal parts
- 2 tablespoons of Spanish sauce
- 1 cup of concentrated broth
- 1 glass of Jerez wine

## Directions

1. Cut truffles into thin slices
2. With half of the butter, partially cook the truffles over low heat
3. Add the two tablespoons of Spanish sauce, the cup of concentrated broth, and the glass of Jerez
4. Let the sauce reduce over low heat
5. Remove grease from the sauce
6. At the moment of serving, incorporate the other half of the butter, which should melt away from the fire with the heat of the sauce while stirring gently
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Gran Vía (Gran Vía Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumb
- oil
- four to six garlic cloves
- paprika
- broth from the pot or water
- pine nuts

## Directions

1. In an earthenware pot, sauté bread crumb in oil.
2. Add four to six garlic cloves and plenty of paprika.
3. Before it blackens, moisten everything with broth from the pot or with water.
4. Crush a small handful of pine nuts and add them to the sauce at the same time as the paprika.
5. Serve when ready.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Holandesa (Hollandaise Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- White sauce base
- capers

## Directions

1. Prepare white sauce as directed
2. When adding the liaison, incorporate capers to obtain classical Hollandaise sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Indiana (Indian Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef butter
- saffron
- chili pepper
- broth
- sauce

## Directions

1. Put beef butter in a saucepan with a little saffron and well-crushed chili pepper, and place over fire to fry a little.
2. Add a little broth and the same amount of sauce, and let reduce until it reaches a regular consistency.
3. Strain through cheesecloth and place by the fire.
4. When about to serve, add a little more beef butter.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Inglesa (English Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of bread crumb
- hot milk
- salt
- black pepper
- nutmeg powder
- 60 grams of beef butter

## Directions

1. Soak the bread crumb in hot milk
2. Cook slowly over very low heat until it becomes a thick pap
3. Season with salt, plenty of pepper, and powdered nutmeg
4. At the moment of serving, incorporate 60 grams of melted beef butter, stirring much and quickly with a spatula into the pap
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Italiana (Italian Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two large spoonfuls of oil
- mushrooms, finely chopped
- one onion, sliced into rounds
- garlic
- scallions
- one bay leaf
- one clove
- some parsley
- a pinch of flour
- salt and pepper
- one ladleful of broth
- one ladleful of white wine
- lemon juice (optional)
- some truffles, sliced (optional)

## Directions

1. Place the oil in the pan with the mushrooms, onion, garlic, scallions, bay leaf, clove, and parsley
2. Sauté everything together
3. Add a pinch of flour, salt and pepper
4. Moisten with one ladleful of broth and another of white wine
5. Simmer over gentle heat for thirty to forty minutes
6. Strain and serve, if desired, with lemon juice
7. Some sliced truffles may also be added
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Mayonesa (Mayonnaise Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two raw egg yolks without the germ
- salt
- white pepper
- three to four drops of vinegar
- one and a half tablespoons of refined oil per egg yolk
- lemon juice or good vinegar

## Directions

1. In a porcelain bowl, placed inside a bowl filled with fresh water or in a very cold place (because the sauce breaks with the heat developed by manipulation), add two raw egg yolks without the germ.
2. Season well with salt and white pepper, thinning them with three or four drops of vinegar.
3. Drop by drop, in the proportion of one and a half tablespoons of very refined oil per egg yolk, incorporate the liquid, stirring constantly with a spatula in circular motions, using a stopper with a hole to dispense the oil very slowly.
4. Beat vigorously without stopping and at the same pace for a quarter of an hour.
5. When the oil and eggs have bound together and the sauce has the point of a thin cord and tends to separate from the bowl, flavor it with lemon juice, and if none is at hand, with a little good vinegar.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Mayor (Aspic Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one good hen
- one fine partridge
- one pound of well-cured ham, cut into very thin slices
- one pound of veal shank
- six bird feet, burned beforehand on the grill
- fine herbs bouquet
- two carrots
- two onions
- a few spoonfuls

## Directions

1. Place the hen, partridge, ham, veal shank, burned bird feet, herbs bouquet, carrots, onions, and spoonfuls in the pot
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Mayor (Major Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one good hen
- one beautiful partridge
- one pound of well-cured ham, trimmed into very thin slices
- one pound of veal shoulder
- half a dozen bird feet, charred beforehand on grills
- a bundle of fine herbs
- two carrots
- two onions
- some spoonfuls of concentrated broth
- two eggs with shells
- two spoonfuls of broth
- one glass of vinegar
- one glass of good white wine

## Directions

1. Place the hen, partridge, ham, veal shoulder, charred bird feet, bundle of fine herbs, carrots, and onions in a pot with some spoonfuls of concentrated broth.
2. Cook all this over low heat, and when the juice has taken on good color, add broth in sufficient quantity to cover everything in the pot.
3. Let cook for three hours over gentle heat, after skimming and seasoning appropriately.
4. Pass the boiling juice through a moistened napkin and let cool.
5. Break two eggs in a terrine, and crushing the shells well, beat them together with the yolks and whites, two spoonfuls of broth, one glass of vinegar, and another glass of good white wine.
6. Add all this to the cooled and well-degreased major sauce and place the pot over high heat.
7. Remove the sauce from heat before it begins to boil, cover the pot with a lid upon which good coals are placed, and keep the vessel near the fire.
8. From time to time, uncover the pot to see how the clarification operation is proceeding, and as soon as it is at the right point, strain again through the moistened napkin.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Mayordoma (Maître d'Hôtel Butter Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams of the best and freshest beef butter
- salt
- pepper
- 1 small teaspoon of finely chopped parsley
- several shallots, chopped finely
- a few drops of vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Knead together the beef butter with salt, pepper, parsley, chopped shallots, and vinegar or lemon juice
2. Incorporate this butter mixture away from the fire into cooked fresh or dried vegetables, drained and very hot, so that the butter melts and the mixture combines well
3. Place this butter in a very hot serving dish
4. Serve it on top of grilled fish, chops, steaks, beefsteaks and other dishes, tossing them on all sides until the butter melts
5. Present at the table with the distinctive name 'à la mayordoma'
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Milanesa (Milanese Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley, finely chopped
- spearmint, finely chopped
- tarragon, finely chopped
- one tablespoon of Parmesan cheese
- clean broth in calculated quantity

## Directions

1. Combine finely chopped parsley, spearmint, and tarragon with one tablespoon of Parmesan cheese
2. Add clean broth in calculated quantity
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Napolitana (Neapolitan Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of veal
- half pound of lean ham
- two chicken legs
- half pound of butter
- onion
- carrot
- one or two cloves of spice
- bay leaf
- several cooked and well-crushed egg yolks
- three cups of milk

## Directions

1. Cut one pound of veal, half of lean ham and two chicken legs into small pieces
2. Put them in a saucepan with half a pound of butter over good heat, adding onion, carrot, one or two cloves of spice and bay leaf, which will be reheated until the onion is golden, then remove from heat
3. Add several cooked and well-crushed egg yolks, stirring everything well so they incorporate
4. Then add three cups of milk little by little, stirring constantly without ceasing so the yolks bind with it
5. Bring to heat, stirring everything until it comes to a boil, then move it to the side of the stove so its cooking is slow for about two hours
6. After this time, when its consistency is not too thick, degrease it and pass it through cheesecloth
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa para las Ostras (Sauce for Oysters)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- butter or oil
- flour
- broth or water
- pepper
- salt
- chopped parsley
- one slice or juice of lemon
- oysters
- breadcrumbs, if desired
- mushrooms, optional

## Directions

1. Mix butter or oil with flour
2. Moisten with broth, pepper, and a little salt
3. Add chopped parsley and a slice or juice of lemon
4. Stir everything over the fire
5. Make the sauce a little thick
6. When it begins to boil, add the oysters with the water they have released, which should have been carefully collected in a separate glass
7. Let cook for fifteen or twenty minutes
8. Pour into a plate and serve, if desired with breadcrumbs
9. When adding the parsley, mushrooms can be added if desired
10. If there is no broth, you can moisten with water, but thicken it with egg yolks at the moment of serving
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Pebre (Pebre Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- vinegar
- shallot
- thyme
- bay leaf
- parsley
- small onion
- pepper
- brown sauce
- broth

## Directions

1. Fill a small saucepan about two-thirds with vinegar.
2. Add shallot, thyme, bay leaf, parsley, small onion, and a good amount of pepper.
3. Make a brown sauce which is diluted in broth.
4. Let it boil for a quarter hour.
5. Before serving, strain through a sieve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Perigord (Perigord Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- White sauce base
- truffles

## Directions

1. Prepare white sauce as directed
2. Mix with truffles cut into very small pieces
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Picante (Spicy Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three finely chopped shallots
- one cacillo of good vinegar
- two cacillos of broth
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- thirty to fifty grams of pickled gherkins

## Directions

1. For meat days, cook two or three very finely chopped shallots with one cacillo of good vinegar until the liquid is consumed
2. Then moisten with two cacillos of broth and, according to each person's taste, add salt, pepper, nutmeg, etc.
3. Let cook over low heat for twenty minutes
4. At the moment of using this sauce in a dish, incorporate thirty to fifty grams of finely chopped pickled gherkins
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Picante Habanera (Spicy Havana Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of two lemons
- half a glass of vinegar
- finely chopped dates
- a pinch of pepper
- sufficient parsley
- four or five garlic cloves
- one bay leaf
- a small quantity of cumin
- two small bowls of substantial broth
- four tablespoons of Havana sauce
- flour, if needed

## Directions

1. Squeeze all the juice from two lemons
2. Mix with half a glass of vinegar and finely chopped dates
3. Add a pinch of pepper, sufficient parsley, four or five garlic cloves, one bay leaf, and a small quantity of cumin
4. Put all this in a pan and bring to a boil for as long as necessary until it reduces to half
5. Add about two small bowls of substantial broth and four tablespoons of Havana sauce
6. Let it cook a little to thicken, and if you want it to thicken faster, add a little toasted flour
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Portuguesa (Portuguese Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 grams of beef butter
- 2 raw egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon of lemon juice
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place together in a pan the beef butter, egg yolks, and lemon juice with salt and pepper
2. Heat the pan over low heat enough to melt the butter while continuously stirring
3. Be patient and careful so that the eggs bind well with the butter and lemon without curdling
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Ravigota (Cold Version) (Ravigota Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fine herbs (parsley, chervil, chives, watercress, pimpernel, and celery), trimmed in advance
- one tablespoon of capers
- one pair of anchovies
- one egg yolk
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon of oil
- one tablespoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Pound the fine herbs in a mortar for a considerable time.
2. When all of this forms a uniform paste, add one egg yolk, salt and pepper, one tablespoon of oil and another of vinegar.
3. Beat the mixture for the space of ten minutes.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Ravigota (Hot Version) (Ravigota Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fine herbs (parsley, chervil, chives, watercress, pimpernel, and celery), carefully chopped
- broth
- 30 grams of butter
- half a tablespoon of flour
- salt
- pepper
- a small stream of vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the same fine herbs as for cold ravigota in broth for a quarter of an hour, but only carefully chopped.
2. Incorporate 30 grams of butter, kneaded with half a tablespoon of flour.
3. Season well with salt, pepper, and a small stream of vinegar.
4. As soon as the butter has melted, remove the sauce from the fire to serve it.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Robert (Robert Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 grams of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- two large onions, the size of apples
- half a pint of water
- strong aromatic vinegar
- one full tablespoon of mustard

## Directions

1. Begin by making a blonde sauce with good color using 50 grams of butter and one tablespoon of flour.
2. Incorporate two large onions, the size of apples, finely chopped, and let them brown well, but without burning the little pieces at the edges.
3. Moisten with half a pint of water and let it cook slowly so the sauce reduces.
4. At the moment of serving, remove the sauce from the fire and add a thread of strong aromatic vinegar and one full tablespoon of mustard.
5. Note: the sauce is better when broth from the pot, well cleaned of fat, is used instead of water.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Romana (Roman Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter from cows
- the same amount of lean ham
- one pound of veal
- two feet from a well-fattened hen
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- laurel
- two or three onions
- carrots
- a dozen yolks of hard-boiled eggs, well crushed
- two quarts of milk

## Directions

1. Cut the butter, ham, veal, and hen feet into small pieces.
2. Season with salt, pepper, clove, laurel, onions, and carrots.
3. When all is browned, add a dozen yolks of hard-boiled eggs, well crushed, and mix well for a perfect mixture.
4. Wet with two quarts of milk, stirring constantly with a spatula, and cook for one hour over low heat, never stopping the stirring.
5. Pass through a sieve.
6. This sauce should not be prepared except at the moment of serving, as it keeps the least amount of time.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Rubia (Brown Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of beef lard or a couple of tablespoons of pork lard
- flour in sufficient quantity

## Directions

1. Place the lard in a saucepan
2. Heat the fire considerably, and when the flour has taken on color, stir continuously with a wooden spatula
3. Moisten with broth from the pot, clear water, or the same broth for which the brown sauce is being made
4. Reheat at medium fire in a saucepan, flour in beef or pork lard until everything takes on a truly golden color
5. Without ceasing to stir, once the brown sauce has taken on this color, cover the saucepan hermetically, remove from heat and place some coals on the lid when there is no oven, so that the sauce continues cooking for half an hour without risk of burning from direct fire
6. From time to time uncover the vessel to stir the sauce
7. The flour cooked properly, without tasting burned, will give the brown sauce a good color tone and an exquisite flavor
8. Later moisten with broth, water or as stated previously, without seasoning at all, as this remains for the moment when the brown sauce is incorporated into a dish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Rubia Blanca (White Brown Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- lard or fat

## Directions

1. Proceed as for the previous sauce, but taking care that the flour is not toasted, but rather binds while conserving its white color
2. This is obtained by adding more flour before the fat is smoking hot
3. This sauce serves in white stews and is used for binding in glazes
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Suprema (Supreme Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- glaze
- reduced beef broth
- 50 grams fresh beef butter
- verjuice or juice of one sour lemon

## Directions

1. In a saucepan over high heat, add at once a measure of glaze and another of reduced beef broth.
2. Let reduce by half, and add 50 grams of fresh beef butter.
3. At the moment of serving, the classic way is to flavor the sauce with a spoonful of verjuice, and when this is lacking, replace it with all the juice of one sour lemon.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Veneciana (Venetian Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- six quartillos of white poultry broth
- two ounces of beef butter
- flour
- nutmeg, grated
- three egg yolks
- beef butter the size of a walnut
- lemon juice
- eight or ten tarragon leaves

## Directions

1. Reduce six quartillos of white poultry broth to half over good heat
2. Prepare a roux made with two ounces of beef butter and the same amount of flour, stirring it for five minutes so it does not take color, and mix the said broth with it
3. Move it to the side of the stove so it cooks slowly for the space of one hour, seasoning it with a little grated nutmeg
4. After this time, degrease it and bind with three egg yolks beaten with a piece of beef butter the size of a walnut and lemon juice
5. Strain through cheesecloth, putting it in a bain-marie to heat it
6. At the moment of serving, blanch eight or ten tarragon leaves in boiling water, trim them and add them to the sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Verde (Green Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- oil
- onion
- assorted fine herbs
- equal parts broth and white wine
- spinach greens
- parsley
- chervil

## Directions

1. Sauté onion and all manner of fine herbs with oil
2. Moisten with equal parts broth and white wine
3. Cook as the previous sauce
4. At the moment of serving, strain
5. Add spinach greens, parsley, and chervil
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Verde Criolla (Creole Green Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- a handful of watercress
- equal quantity of purslane, chopped
- water
- spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Take a handful of watercress and equal quantity of chopped purslane
2. Squeeze out the juice from both plants well
3. Once extracted, place in a pan with water and bring to a boil
4. When you believe they are cooked, remove, drain, and mash them
5. Dilute with the desired sauce, adding a little spicy sauce
6. Strain through a fine sieve and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa Vinagreta (Vinaigrette Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked egg yolks
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- oil
- onion
- egg whites

## Directions

1. It is made hot and cold, depending on whether the oil is fried and hot with well-sautéed onion, or raw, with raw onion also.
2. Crush two cooked egg yolks in a bowl and dilute in vinegar, seasoning with salt and pepper.
3. Add trimmed parsley and work everything well until the moment of adding the onion and oil.
4. When serving the sauce, whether cold or hot, cut the two hard egg whites into small, square pieces and mix into the sauce.
5. Some add a hint of garlic, but shallot very finely chopped is preferable, and better still early small onions.
6. This sauce has endless applications.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa á la Embajadora (Ambassador's Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two chicken or poultry breasts
- white poultry broth
- fried bread
- flour
- beef butter
- mushrooms
- nutmeg
- lemon juice
- beef butter the size of a walnut

## Directions

1. Cook two chicken or poultry breasts in white poultry broth, and when cold remove them and pound in the mortar
2. Prepare a roux as before and add the broth in which the breasts have cooked, increasing it with some mushrooms and nutmeg
3. Incorporate the pounded meats, and after degreasing this sauce, strain it through cheesecloth and put it in a bain-marie
4. When serving, add the lemon juice and about the size of a walnut of beef butter
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa á la Grimod (Grimod's Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- cumin, ground
- hot pepper, a little
- saffron
- nutmeg
- broth from the pot

## Directions

1. Prepare as the previous sauce, adding to it a little hot pepper, saffron and nutmeg
2. Follow the same rules and make it a little before serving
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa á la Tártara (Tartar Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- shallots
- chervil
- tarragon
- mustard
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. In a bowl place two or three shallots, chervil and tarragon, all finely chopped.
2. Add mustard, salt, pepper, and a little vinegar and oil.
3. Stir everything perfectly without stopping.
4. If it appears too thick, add vinegar.
5. This sauce is made cold.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Salsa á la Vinagrilla (Vinagrette Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- salt
- vinegar
- oil
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place salt on a plate and dissolve with vinegar.
2. Add oil and then a little pepper.
3. Stir with a fork.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sardinas de Pescador (Fisherman's Sardines)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh sardines
- oil

## Directions

1. Remove sardines from the nets and wash them in sea water
2. Fry them in a pot with oil so they become saturated
3. Thread them in batches on long rods or small canes to hold by one end in the hand
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sardinas de Pescador (Fisherman's Sardines)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh sardines
- Sea water
- Oil

## Directions

1. Remove sardines from the nets and wash them in sea water.
2. Soak the sardines in oil in a pot so they absorb the oil.
3. Thread them by batches onto long sticks or reeds.
4. Hold the sticks by one end in your hand while toasting them over the flame of torches burning on a grill placed outside the boat and fixed at the bow.
5. Alternatively, when buying recently caught sardines, roast them at home in the same manner with sticks in the hearth, but first wash them in sea water and soak them in oil.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sardinas Esparrilladas (Grilled Sardines)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sardines
- Oil
- Fresh butter
- Parsley
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the sardines.
2. Anoint them with oil and open them lengthwise.
3. With them thus extended, roast them over live coals on the grill.
4. Place them on a plate or serving dish where you will have previously put very fresh butter, kneaded with chopped parsley and a pinch of pepper.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sardinas Fritas (Fried Sardines)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Sardines
- Oil
- Flour (optional)
- Egg (optional)
- Frying batter (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the sardines.
2. The sardine can be prepared dry, floured, coated with egg, or wrapped in frying batter.
3. Fry for two minutes over lively fire in plenty of oil.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sesos de Ternera en Manteca Negra (Calf's Brains in Black Butter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's brains
- salt, a good handful
- strong vinegar, one glass
- water, very fresh and acidulated excessively with vinegar or lemon
- 125 grams melted butter
- finely chopped fine herbs
- half a glass vinegar
- water acidulated with vinegar or lemon
- black butter sauce

## Directions

1. Add a good handful of salt and one glass of strong vinegar to boiling water. Blanch the brains in this water.
2. Chill the brains immediately in very fresh water acidulated excessively with vinegar or lemon.
3. Prepare a mixture with 125 grams melted butter, finely chopped fine herbs, and half a glass of vinegar.
4. Cook the brains in this mixture for half an hour, adding some of the acidulated water in which they were chilled.
5. When the brains are cooked, no sauce should remain.
6. Cut the brains into thin slices and place in a metal serving dish.
7. Pour black butter sauce over the brains and serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sesos de Ternera á la Mayordoma (Calf's Brains with Fine Herbs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's brains, blanched and chilled
- salsa rubia-blanca (blonde-white sauce), well seasoned with salt and pepper
- 125 grams beef butter
- 2 heaping tablespoons finely chopped fine herbs, well seasoned with salt and pepper
- vinegar or lemon juice

## Directions

1. Blanch and chill the brains. Cut each brain into three pieces and cook for half an hour in a well-seasoned blonde-white sauce.
2. Place the brains in a serving dish and keep warm near the fire.
3. In a separate hot pan away from the fire, melt 125 grams of beef butter mixed with 2 heaping tablespoons of finely chopped fine herbs, well seasoned with salt and pepper.
4. Add a few drops of vinegar or lemon juice to the butter mixture.
5. Pour this sauce over the brains and serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sesos de Ternera á la Mayordoma (Veal Brains Majordomess Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brains
- white roux-based sauce
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Blanch the brains in boiling water
2. Immediately submerge in very cold water
3. Cut each brain into three pieces
4. Cook for half an hour in a well-seasoned white roux-based sauce
5. Place the brains on a serving platter and keep warm near the fire
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sesos de Vaca (Calf Brains)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- calf brains
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- thyme
- garlic
- parsley

## Directions

1. Place brains in boiling water to clean them of blood and membrane
2. Soak in cold water for two hours
3. Place in clean water and bring to a boil
4. Add vinegar, salt, pepper, bay leaf, thyme, garlic and parsley
5. Simmer for half an hour until cooked
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sesos de Vaca con Manteca Negra (Calf Brains with Black Butter)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf brains
- Black butter

## Directions

1. Cook the brains as described
2. Cut them into small pieces about the size of nuts so they take the sauce better
3. Place them on a platter
4. Pour black butter over them, which is prepared as already stated in the sauces section
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sesos de Vaca Fritos (Fried Calf Brains)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf brains
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine herbs
- Batter for frying
- Pork lard

## Directions

1. Boil the calf brains for half an hour in water with salt, pepper and a bouquet of fine herbs, after scalding and cleaning them
2. Remove the brains from the water, drain and let them cool
3. Dip the small or large pieces in a frying batter
4. Fry them in pork lard, making them take on a good golden color
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sesos de Vaca á la Marinera (Calf Brains Mariner Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf brains
- Beef lard
- Twelve small onions
- Twelve mushrooms
- Salted water
- Beef lard
- One tablespoon of flour
- Two glasses of red wine

## Directions

1. With the brains already cooked, cut and cooled, cook separately in beef lard, sautéing well, twelve small onions until they take color
2. In a separate pan, in salted water mixed with thirty grams of beef lard, cook twelve mushrooms trimmed very neatly
3. Dust the onions with one tablespoon of flour, moistening them first in the water in which the mushrooms have cooked, and also with two glasses of red wine
4. Then combine the mushrooms with the onions and cook everything over low heat for half an hour in this marinera sauce, incorporating the prepared brains broken up beforehand
5. To serve this dish, do not pour all the contents of the pan onto the platter, but first place the brains very well arranged with the help of a skimmer, and pour the sauce over them
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sesos Fritos (Fried Brains)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's brains, blanched, chilled, and cut into small slices
- lard
- dried parsley powder
- salt and pepper
- eggs, beaten and seasoned with salt and pepper
- breadcrumbs
- paste for frying (as explained on page 194)
- egg white, beaten clear

## Directions

1. For the first method: Fry the well-prepared brain slices directly in lard. Dust with dried parsley powder, salt, and pepper when serving.
2. For the second method: Coat the brain pieces in beaten seasoned eggs, then bread them with breadcrumbs, dip again in egg, and fry in hot fat.
3. For the third method (fritters, which is best): Cook blanched and chilled brains in a blonde-white sauce with salt, pepper, parsley, and finely chopped fine herbs.
4. Before the brains are completely cooked, let them cool and cut into walnut-sized pieces. Dust with salt and moisten with vinegar. After a quarter hour, drain carefully and coat in frying paste.
5. Fry the breaded pieces in lard that is not too hot. The greater the amount of beaten egg white in the frying paste, the more the fritters will puff up.
6. Fry in abundant fat, serving immediately from pan to table.
7. Serve alternating the fried brain fritters with fried calf's hands prepared the same way, all garnished with fried parsley.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Setas a la Provenzal (Mushrooms Provençal Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- salt
- fine oil
- garlic
- parsley
- bread

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms well in water with plenty of salt.
2. Dry them.
3. Fry them in fine oil with a good quantity of minced garlic and parsley.
4. Serve very hot with slices of toasted bread on the side.
5. Serve with white wine to drink.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Setas con Pan Frito (Mushrooms with Fried Bread)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- butter
- lemon juice
- salt
- spices
- water
- bread, for making crusts
- garlic, optional

## Directions

1. Sauté in butter with lemon juice, salt, spices, and one spoonful of water.
2. After sautéing and cooking, place over fried bread crusts that have been already arranged on the serving dish.
3. If desired, add one garlic clove while cooking, which should be removed before placing the mushrooms on the bread crusts.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Setas Empapeladas (Papillote Mushrooms)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- butter
- paper
- parsley, chopped
- onion, chopped
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the mushrooms into pieces.
2. Coat with butter and wrap in paper.
3. Mix with chopped parsley and onion, ground salt, and pepper.
4. Roast on the grill over gentle heat and serve in the paper.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Setas Tostadas a la Bordelesa (Roasted Mushrooms Bordeaux Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- large, open mushrooms
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- garlic
- fresh herbs

## Directions

1. Select large and open mushrooms.
2. Clean them and remove the stem.
3. Place them on a grill with the concave part facing up.
4. Fill the hollows with butter, salt, pepper, minced garlic, and finely chopped fresh herbs.
5. Let them roast well and toast on the convex side.
6. Serve on a platter in which butter has been melted.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Setas Tostadas á la Bordelesa (Toasted Boletus Mushrooms Bordeaux Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- large open mushrooms
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- garlic, finely chopped
- fine herbs, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Choose large, open mushrooms.
2. Clean and remove the stem.
3. Place on the grill with the concave part up and fill the hollows with butter, salt, pepper, finely chopped garlic, and fine herbs.
4. Allow to roast well and toast.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Setas «Cepes» á la Bordelesa (Boletus Mushrooms Bordeaux Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- fine Valencia oil
- parsley, finely chopped
- garlic, finely chopped
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- silver spoon

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the mushrooms.
2. Fry in fine Valencia oil.
3. Once fried, sauté with finely chopped parsley and garlic, all very fine.
4. Season with salt and some excess pepper, clove, and nutmeg.
5. During the operation, stir and handle the mushrooms with a silver spoon, real silver.
6. If the spoon darkens or becomes covered with a dark veil that tarnishes it, discard the mushrooms.
7. If the spoon keeps its color, serve the mushrooms immediately from the pan to the table. This is a dish that should be eaten very hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Setas á la Española (Spanish Mushrooms)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- butter or oil
- salt
- pepper
- broth
- pine nut or almond sauce
- fried flour

## Directions

1. Remove the stems, wash thoroughly in plenty of water, and dry well.
2. Sauté in butter or oil.
3. Add salt, pepper, and a little broth.
4. Once well cooked, add pine nut or almond sauce, thickened with fried flour.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Seven Hour Leg of Mutton (Pierna de carnero de las siete horas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton, not deboned
- good ham, lean part

## Directions

1. Place a large piece of good ham lean at the bottom of the pot
2. Place the leg of mutton on top
3. Cook for seven hours
4. Place another equal piece of ham on top of the leg
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sleeping Eggs (Huevos Dormidos)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Vinegar (half the amount of water)
- Salt
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Boil water in a pan with half its amount in vinegar and sufficient salt.
2. Remove from the heat and carefully break the desired eggs on top without breaking them or stirring them.
3. When the whites are set, remove them, starting with the first ones, and place in cold water.
4. Remove from the cold water and place on a platter over a dish of sorrel, spinach, chicory, etc., or over any purée, or with a sauce made from shallots, vinegar, and mustard or another sauce of this type.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Soldados de Pavía (Fried Cod Strips)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Good and long strips of soaked cod
- Frying batter
- A pinch of saffron
- Plenty of oil

## Directions

1. Soak the cod strips
2. Clean them of skin and bones
3. Soak them in frying batter colored with a pinch of saffron
4. Fry in plenty of oil until very toasted and crispy
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Solomillo con Costrones (Sirloin with Croutons)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of roasted sirloin from the previous day
- broth from the pot
- croutons or slices of bread
- pork lard
- beef butter
- parsley, chopped
- roasting juice
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Heat slices of roasted sirloin by soaking them in boiling broth from the pot away from the fire
2. Fry and sauté croutons or bread slices of equal form and size to the sirloin slices in pork lard, having first soaked them in broth
3. Arrange the sirloin pieces and croutons alternately in a crown on a round platter, standing them on edge
4. To the broth that was used to reheat the meat, add a couple of spoonfuls of roasting juice and reduce it over high heat
5. Add 60 grams of beef butter kneaded with considerable chopped parsley, add a thin stream of vinegar, and pour this sauce into the bowl formed by the dish arranged in a crown on the platter
6. Keep the platter warm by the fire or in an open oven
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Solomillo con Macarrones (Sirloin with Macaroni)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of sirloin
- beef or pork butter
- Spanish sauce or broth with meat juice
- Italian macaroni
- lean ham
- truffles
- concentrated broth
- Sherry wine

## Directions

1. Sauté the sirloin pieces in beef or pork butter and season them in Spanish sauce or broth with meat juice
2. Arrange the sirloin on a platter as in the previous recipe
3. Cook good Italian macaroni in broth without fat
4. Separately, cook 60 grams of lean ham and 60 grams of trimmed truffles
5. Cook the truffles and ham slowly for twenty minutes in concentrated broth with a glass of Sherry
6. Incorporate the truffles and ham into the cooked macaroni
7. Fill the hollow formed by the meat slices on the platter with this macaroni garnish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Solomillo Salteado a la Financiera (Sirloin Sautéed Financier Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- thin, delicate slices of sirloin
- beef butter
- Spanish sauce or good concentrated broth
- roasting juice
- Sherry wine
- financier garnish
- mushrooms or truffles

## Directions

1. Cut thin and delicate slices of sirloin
2. Sauté them in beef butter over high heat, but without cooking them completely
3. Remove from the sauté pan and cook them slowly for a quarter hour in some spoonfuls of Spanish sauce, or if not available, in good concentrated broth lengthened with roasting juice and a glass of Sherry
4. Prepare a dark financier garnish separately as previously explained
5. Place the sirloin slices in a crown on a platter, laying them down and overlapping one upon another
6. Pour the garnish into the hollow, reinforced with the reduced sauce in which the meat finished cooking
7. For variation, mushrooms or truffles cooked in the sauce used for the meat can be used instead of the financier garnish
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa al Estilo de Flandes (Flemish Style Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- crusts of hard bread
- turnips
- potatoes
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook in water with salt and a piece of butter, crusts of hard bread, turnips and potatoes cut into slices and pieces of equal size.
2. When everything has cooked, mash it and pass through a fine sieve.
3. Return the pot to the fire and clarify the broth if it is thick.
4. Add a handful of finely chopped parsley and another piece of butter.
5. For meat days, meat broth makes this soup more excellent.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa al Estilo de Monaco (Monaco Style Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- bread cut into equal small squares
- sugar
- milk
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Toast equal squares of bread sprinkled with sugar on both sides.
2. Place them in the tureen and pour over them very hot, sweetened milk.
3. Dissolve egg yolks in a little milk in the proportion of two yolks per liter of milk used.
4. Add this binding at the moment of serving, but the soup should not be very hot then, as it would curdle.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Bisque (Bisque Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- many crayfish
- 1 ounce beef butter or lard (for 1 liter of soup)
- 1 spoonful flour

## Directions

1. Cook many crayfish excessively, seasoning well.
2. Remove the feet and tails, which are set aside on a plate and crush and flatten the bodies or corselets, aiding the operation with a bit of the water in which they cooked.
3. Give everything a boil and strain through a sieve the pasty broth or purée that results, which will be more concentrated according to whether the quantity of crayfish and water respectively is greater or lesser.
4. In a pan, place to melt 1 ounce of beef butter—for a liter of soup—or lard, and sauté a spoonful of flour, allowing it to take color.
5. Moisten with the water of the crayfish until the paste curdles, and then increase in the necessary quantity, adding the crayfish purée.
6. Allow to cook five minutes on medium heat; bind as has been said and pour it into the tureen, where the cleaned tails and feet will be beforehand.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Bureto (Meat Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- bread, grated
- cheese, grated - 4 ounces for forty cups
- chard
- lettuce
- liver
- parsley
- sorrel
- pepper
- saffron
- clove
- cinnamon
- salt
- meat
- broth
- fifty eggs for one hundred cups

## Directions

1. Cook the chard, lettuce, and liver
2. Chop all together with a handful of parsley and sorrel
3. Place the chopped mixture in the pot where the soup is to be made, with the cheese
4. Add pepper, saffron, clove, and cinnamon
5. When the meat is cooked, season it with salt, saffron, and pepper so it takes on color
6. Pour the broth into the pot with the herbs and bring to a boil
7. Gradually add bread, stirring with a ladle until it thickens
8. Remove from heat and place in a straw basket to rest
9. Prepare a small pot with a bit of warm broth
10. Beat fifty eggs in another pot for one hundred cups and mix with the warm broth
11. Gradually pour the egg mixture into the bureto pot, stirring constantly so it does not become lumpy
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Calada (Strained Broth Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Very fine bread cuts
- Boiling broth
- Mint leaf (optional)

## Directions

1. Place very fine cuts of bread in the soup tureen.
2. Pour boiling broth over it in quantity according to whether you want the soup clear or thick.
3. Let it soak for a few minutes, being careful to cover the tureen or pot.
4. Serve. Some add a mint leaf. French bread is better than Spanish bread for this and for all soups, and bread that is two or three days old is preferable to fresh bread.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Colbert (Colbert Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh vegetables
- thin slices instead of julienne cuts
- eggs, as many as there are diners

## Directions

1. Prepare a julienne soup but with fresh vegetables cut into thin slices instead of thin strips
2. Heat the soup until it is thick
3. When serving, add poached eggs to the soup tureen, one egg per diner
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Colón (Columbus Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of lamb
- pork butter
- onion, finely chopped
- 8 or 9 potatoes
- half a pound of rice
- spearmint, chopped
- oregano
- salt
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Cut 1 pound of lamb into small pieces
2. Sauté in pork butter with a little finely chopped onion
3. In a large pot bring water to boil and incorporate the fried meat
4. Add 8 or 9 potatoes cut in pieces, half a pound of rice, a little chopped spearmint, a pinch of oregano, and salt
5. Skim off foam
6. When about to serve, beat 2 eggs well with their whites and introduce them into the pot
7. The result should be brothy and served as soup in soup bowls
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa con Caldo de Gallina (Soup with Chicken Broth)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 liters water
- 1 chicken

## Directions

1. In a liter and a half of water, cook a chicken after cleaning it well and scalding and singeing its feet.
2. Allow to cook on low heat until the liquid is reduced by half, which serves for the sick, as well as the soup made with it is for convalescents.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa con Caldo de Substancias (Soup with Meat Broth)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal hands
- pieces of beef
- game
- old fowl
- vegetables
- garlic
- clove

## Directions

1. Place veal hands, pieces of beef, game and old fowl in the broth from the pot; stir and add everything to the same broth from the pot as the gelatin forms; skim.
2. Incorporate vegetables, garlic and clove; allow to reduce on low heat for five hours, and finally strain to make bread soup or prepare rice, noodles, etc.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Crecy, de Puré de Zanahorias (Crecy Soup, Puréed Carrot)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 to 15 carrots
- sufficient quantity of butter
- good broth

## Directions

1. Place in a saucepan with sufficient quantity of butter 10 to 15 carrots cut into slices
2. Moisten with good broth when they are well browned
3. Cook them
4. Strain, return to cook for four hours
5. Place over any kind of soup, bound as a paste
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Crema (Cream Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of broth
- twelve egg yolks
- four egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks and whites very well separately
2. Then mix them and beat more in a pan
3. Away from the fire, pour the hot strained broth from the pot little by little over the mixture, with its natural seasoning
4. Cook in a bain-marie so it curdles like custard
5. Cook the eight remaining egg whites in a bain-marie in any vessel, and when they are hard, cut them so they result in very thin strips, imitating noodles
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Acederas (Sorrel Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- sorrel leaves
- one lettuce heart
- a good handful of parsley, finely chopped
- butter
- two egg yolks
- water

## Directions

1. Cut a good amount of sorrel leaves and add one lettuce heart and a good handful of finely chopped parsley.
2. Place everything in a pot and cook slowly for a quarter hour with a little butter, stirring constantly.
3. When the herbs are completely broken down and well cooked, add the necessary amount of water.
4. As soon as it has boiled, strain the soup.
5. Only when the soup is in the tureen, bind it with a pair of egg yolks dissolved in water using the known procedure.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Ajo Crudo (Raw Garlic Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Salt
- Oil
- Garlic

## Directions

1. Put a pot with water, salt, oil and garlic
2. Cook everything together, season, strain the soup and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Ajo Frito (Fried Garlic Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Oil
- Garlic
- Paprika
- Salt
- Water
- Eggs (optional)

## Directions

1. Heat oil to fry with garlic
2. When the garlic is fried, remove from heat, add paprika and salt all together
3. To prevent the paprika from burning, turn it once, add water and cook
4. Season and the soup can be strained
5. Eggs can also be poached in the broth
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Albondiguillas (Meatball Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1/4 cuartillo milk
- 2 ounces fresh beef butter
- salt and pepper
- flour
- lard
- broth from the pot

## Directions

1. In a bowl, place four eggs, a quarter part of a cuartillo of milk, two ounces of fresh beef butter, a bit of salt and pepper; beat everything mixing it with a bit of flour until a consistent paste is made.
2. Form the meatballs the size of an olive, dusting them with flour, and fry them in lard.
3. Place them in the tureen and pour the broth from the pot over them.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Alcalde Mayor (Lord Mayor's Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Four pig ears
- Four pig hands
- Four liters of water
- Parsley
- Celery
- Thyme
- Onions
- Cloves
- 250 grams of butter
- Flour of the finest quality
- Fine herbs including parsley, chervil, bay leaf and mint

## Directions

1. Cook four pig ears and four pig hands slowly over low heat in four liters of water for four hours
2. Add a bouquet made with parsley, celery and thyme and some onions with cloves stuck in them
3. After four hours, remove the ears from the broth, which will be very cooked, and leave the hands to cook for two more hours
4. Strain the broth through a fine sieve, let cool, and when cold remove all the solidified fat from the surface
5. Debone the ears and hands and cut the meat into very small pieces, place in a covered dish with a napkin until ready to prepare the soup
6. In a pan over gentle heat, melt 250 grams of butter, to which add, stirring constantly with a spatula, all the flour it can absorb
7. Keep this mixture over the fire for ten minutes while stirring, taking care that the flour does not brown
8. Gradually incorporate the prepared broth, letting it cook for a while after each portion is added
9. When all the broth is in the pan, season and dress the meat from the ears and hands with two tablespoons of fine herbs - parsley, chervil, bay leaf, mint, all chopped like tobacco
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Arroz (Rice Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- broth from previous day's puchero
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Wash the rice thoroughly and place it in a saucepan with cold water.
2. As the rice absorbs the water, add more until it is cooked about halfway, then gradually incorporate broth little by little and complete the cooking.
3. Add a pinch of salt to the broth when it is put to cook with the rice.
4. For a more delicate preparation, cook slowly until the rice is slightly overcooked and almost dissolved in the broth, which will naturally change from caramelized to whitish color.
5. Alternatively, for the traditional Spanish method, boil the rice in broth at a rolling boil for twenty to thirty minutes, stirring occasionally.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Arroz con Leche (Rice Soup with Milk)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- water
- salt
- lemon zest
- milk
- orange flower water, one tablespoon

## Directions

1. Give the rice a couple of boils in water with a pinch of salt
2. Drain the rice well until no water remains
3. Finish cooking the rice in milk, which should have had the lemon zest infused in it
4. When serving, add one tablespoon of orange flower water
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Arroz con Leche de Almendras (Rice Soup with Almond Milk)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- water
- salt
- lemon zest
- bay leaves, two
- almonds, 250 grams, shelled and crushed
- water for crushing, one spoonful
- water, one quartillo, slightly warm
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the rice slowly in water with little salt, the lemon zest, and two bay leaves
2. Prepare almond milk by crushing 250 grams of shelled almonds in a marble mortar, adding one spoonful of water during the crushing to prevent the oil from separating
3. Place the paste in a white napkin to drain well
