4. Dissolve the paste in one quartillo of slightly warm water, passing it through the napkin by compression five or six times, continuously wetting the napkin with the milk
5. Sugar the rice and give it the right cooking point by adding the almond milk very slowly while it cooks gently
6. Remove the lemon zest, bay leaves, and cinnamon if used
7. Serve the rice flavored with orange flower water
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Caldo Consumado (Consommé Broth Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two liters of water
- One kilogram of beef, carefully cleaned of all fat
- Vegetables and seasoning as used in a good puchero
- One hen

## Directions

1. In two liters of water with the same vegetables and seasoning as used in a good puchero, cook one kilogram of beef, with the piece carefully cleaned of all fat.
2. At the same time, over very high heat, half-roast a hen, that is, color and brown it well, without it being cooked inside.
3. Place the hen in the pot and let it cook until the beef is perfectly cooked.
4. This operation cannot last less than eight hours. The boiling shall be very slow, but without any interruption, and the broth should reduce by half.
5. This consommé broth, which is taken plain or which serves as the best broth for all broth-based soups, is easy to make and is not relatively expensive for a household that habitually prepares it. The hen meat, minced, has many applications: for fillings, croquettes, etc.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Caldo Consumado de Almejas (Clam Consommé Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- five pounds of clams
- water
- two ounces of cow butter
- salt
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- parsley
- bay leaf
- two more ounces of cow butter
- three tablespoons of corn starch
- tamized pasta
- two cups of Jerez or other white wine
- three hard-boiled egg whites
- one egg yolk
- three tablespoons of cold water

## Directions

1. Cook the clams in water without seasoning for half an hour
2. Remove clams from shells and sauté them in a saucepan with two ounces of cow butter
3. Before they take color, add half a pint of the water in which they began to cook
4. Season with salt, pepper, clove, nutmeg, parsley, and bay leaf
5. Cook slowly for one hour
6. Remove the saucepan from heat and pass the stew through a strainer, using the reserved water to help
7. Empty the saucepan and without cleaning it, melt two more ounces of cow butter over high heat
8. Incorporate three tablespoons of corn starch to create a thick paste
9. Add the first water, then add the tamized pasta and two cups of Jerez or white wine
10. Stir with a spatula for ten minutes over high heat
11. In the serving tureen, have ready three hard-boiled egg whites cut into small squares
12. Before pouring the saucepan contents into the tureen, bind the soup off the heat with an egg yolk dissolved in three tablespoons of cold water
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Caldo de Cangrejos (Crayfish Broth Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- parsley
- oil
- cornstarch
- 24 crayfish per liter of water

## Directions

1. Sauté a little onion and minced parsley, all in oil; add a small spoonful of cornstarch and lengthen while seasoning with twice the water that will make the broth.
2. When it boils vigorously, add twenty-four crayfish per liter of water, cook until the water is reduced by half, and with the broth soak toasted bread and serve.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Cebolla (Onion Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound of onions, finely chopped
- two ounces of beef butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- one liter of cold water
- egg yolk
- bread slices, fried or toasted
- quarter pound of grated Gruyere cheese

## Directions

1. Fry the finely chopped onions in a pan or pot with beef butter
2. Season and spice to taste
3. When the onion has taken on much color and before it begins to toast at the edges, add one tablespoon of flour
4. When it is golden, add one liter of cold water, pouring it little by little and letting it boil each time
5. Let it cook slowly for a few minutes
6. Remove from heat and bind with egg yolk as explained for other soups
7. Place fried or toasted bread slices in the soup tureen, sprinkled with grated Gruyere cheese
8. Stir everything with a spatula as you pour the broth into the tureen to prevent the cheese from clumping
9. Serve hot
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Cerveza (Beer Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of very strong beer
- 30 grams of sugar
- five grams of cilantro
- rye bread or very dark bread
- one egg yolk
- water

## Directions

1. Heat one liter of very strong beer with 30 grams of sugar and five grams of cilantro.
2. Strain this liquid over rye or very dark bread cut into thin slices.
3. At the moment of serving, bind with one egg yolk dissolved in water.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Coles (Cabbage Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage or kale (curly kale and white head varieties are best)
- 250 to 500 grams fresh pork knuckle (codillo)
- broth from previous day's puchero
- water for blanching

## Directions

1. Trim and wash the cabbages thoroughly in several changes of fresh water.
2. Blanch them by scalding with boiling water for ten minutes.
3. After blanching, drain the cabbages thoroughly.
4. Place them in the bottom of a wide, flat-bottomed pot with the fresh pork knuckle.
5. Pour broth from the previous day's puchero over them.
6. Cook very slowly until the pork knuckle and cabbages are well cooked.
7. Strain the broth to clarify the soup, placing a portion of the kale on top and reserving the remainder.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Crema de Cebada Perlada (Pearl Barley Cream Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- butter from cows
- flour
- water
- pearl barley, cooked to saturation
- egg yolk
- fried bread cubes

## Directions

1. Make a roux with butter and flour in sufficient quantity
2. Add the water from cooked pearl barley to create a purée
3. Remove from heat and bind with an egg yolk beaten in half a cup of water
4. Serve with small cubes of fried bread in the soup
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Farro (Barley Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- barley - four ounces for four bowls
- almonds, blanched - four ounces
- sugar - four ounces
- meat broth or water

## Directions

1. Shake the barley, which comes half-milled, to remove the chaff
2. Wash the barley three times with warm water
3. Cook it with meat broth until somewhat thick
4. Pound blanched almonds in a mortar (use few, only to whiten the barley)
5. Pound everything together
6. Pass through a cloth, pressing well, so only the sugar remains
7. Ensure it is very sweet
8. Do not cook more than a couple of boils, as it would turn brown
9. Alternatively, make it with almond milk, which is better because with broth it turns brown, and with milk it is very white though less substantial
10. To make it with almond milk, cook first with water or broth
11. Serve the soup
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Fideos (Noodle Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- noodles (fideo), broken by hand
- broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Add broken noodles by the handful to boiling broth.
2. Let them cook without becoming too soft, stirring occasionally with a wooden spoon.
3. Remove the saucepan from the heat when the noodles begin to dissolve, as this would cloud the soup and diminish its pleasant flavor.
4. For clear soup, use thirty grams of noodles per liter of broth. For thicker soup, double the amount of noodles.
5. Serve immediately as this soup is only good when made at the moment of serving.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Fideos con Leche (Noodle Soup with Milk)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- milk, one liter
- noodles, thirty grams
- salt
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Add thirty grams of noodles per liter of milk to boiling milk
2. Be careful that the soup does not become too thick
3. Season only with a pinch of salt and sugar to the consumer's taste
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Guisantes (Pea Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of fine, fresh peas
- a bouquet of fine herbs
- two or three small onions
- salt
- pepper
- optional: a handful of finely chopped mint leaves

## Directions

1. Cook half a liter of fine, fresh peas with a bouquet of fine herbs and two or three small onions.
2. When the soup is cooking, incorporate the whole peas, which should be very cooked.
3. Strain the prepared and filtered broth, carefully removing all the fat that rises to the surface.
4. Return the broth to the fire and season to taste with salt and pepper.
5. Pour the strained soup into the tureen where the peas have already been prepared.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Leche (Milk Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- bread, finely sliced
- salt
- sugar to taste
- milk, two liters
- sugar, 300 grams
- lemon zest, whole
- bay leaves, three to four
- cinnamon stick, one small piece
- egg yolks, six

## Directions

1. In one pot, cook two liters of milk with 300 grams of sugar, the whole zest of a lemon, three or four bay leaves, and a small stick of cinnamon
2. In another pot, away from heat, dissolve six egg yolks in the third part of the cooked milk
3. Place this mixture over low heat, stirring constantly until it thickens slightly
4. Pour the rest of the milk into a soup tureen with the finely sliced bread
5. At the moment of serving, incorporate the thickened milk and egg mixture
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Menudillos (Chicken Giblet Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken giblets
- lard
- rice or toasted bread soup

## Directions

1. Mince very finely and clean perfectly the giblets of chicken.
2. Sauté them in a pan with lard and incorporate them into rice soup or toasted bread soup when it is already in the tureen.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Nullas (Noodle Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 grams flour de flor (fine flour)
- salt
- a pinch of pepper
- six egg yolks
- water, as needed
- broth from puchero

## Directions

1. Combine the fine flour with salt and a pinch of pepper. Incorporate the necessary water to give the dough good consistency, adjusting so that the flour can be absorbed by the yolks without adding water.
2. Work and knead the dough, then extend it with a rolling pin into thin sheets.
3. Cut the sheets into long strips about half a centimeter wide or even thinner, or shape the scraps as desired.
4. Let the nullas rest for a couple of hours exposed to air before using.
5. Add them to the broth as described for noodles, and they must cook for at least half an hour.
6. This is a very nutritious soup.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Ostras (Oyster Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three dozen small oysters
- fish broth
- butter
- croutons

## Directions

1. In a mortar, crush and pound two or three dozen small oysters.
2. Place them in fish broth and let cook for half an hour.
3. Fry some croutons in butter and place them in the tureen.
4. At the moment of serving, pour over them the oyster broth, strained and made into a thick purée.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Papilla Real (Royal Cream Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of beef butter
- three tablespoons of flour
- one and a half quartillos of good concentrated broth

## Directions

1. Melt the butter in a pan
2. Incorporate three tablespoons of flour and season without allowing it to take color
3. Add one and a half quartillos of good concentrated broth
4. Cook everything for a quarter hour
5. Sieve the soup as indicated for others, thicken it, and pour it into the tureen, where the corresponding garnish will already be placed
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Papilla Real (Royal Pap Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of cow butter
- three tablespoons of flour
- one and a half pints of good concentrated broth

## Directions

1. For eight persons, melt a quarter pound of cow butter in a saucepan
2. Incorporate three tablespoons of flour and season without letting it take color
3. Add one and a half pints of good concentrated broth
4. Cook everything for a quarter of an hour
5. Strain the soup
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Pescado (Fish Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fish broth
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make the necessary quantity of fish broth as previously indicated, and with it prepare a soup that is rather clear than thick.
2. With part of the broth and over low heat, stirring always, thicken it by allowing some egg yolks to set.
3. Incorporate it into the soup at the moment of serving.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Pescadores (Fishermen's Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- large fish
- small fish or trash fish
- water
- oil
- fennel
- bay leaves
- lemon zest
- garlic head
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- bread

## Directions

1. Separate the large and beautiful fish from the small fish or trash fish
2. Boil the latter in water and when cooked, drain by pressing in a strainer
3. Put in a pot as many ladles of the drippings and broth in which the fish has cooked as there are diners
4. Sprinkle with very little good quality oil
5. Add a bundle of herbs composed of fennel, one or two bay leaves, lemon zest, a head of garlic and an onion
6. Add salt and pepper and boil very vigorously over ardent heat until the oil is well mixed, does not float on top and forms with the juice a whitish pearly sauce
7. Cut the large fish, which should be very fresh and alive if possible, principally red or golden fish
8. Boil with much heat and when cooked, stop the heat and pour the broth over bread cut into slices
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Pote Gallego (Galician Cabbage and Bean Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Four pounds of cabbage
- One pound of dried white beans
- Four pounds of potatoes
- One quarter pound of lard (pork fat)
- Two or three pounds of beef
- Two pounds of ham
- One pound total of blood sausage and longaniza or chorizo
- Bread crusts for toasting

## Directions

1. Cook everything except the cabbage and beans together in a pot over low heat for six hours
2. Cook the cabbage separately by blanching it
3. Cook the beans separately so they do not fall apart before time
4. Add the blanched cabbage to the pot, then add the beans
5. Make the soup with the broth and toasted bread crusts, soaking them well
6. Serve the vegetables and meat separately in different serving dishes
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puchero (Puchero Bread Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Pieces of hard bread or well-toasted bread slices
- Broth rich in fat
- Additional broth as desired

## Directions

1. Over low heat and in a pot with some broth rich in fat, cook pieces of hard bread or slices of well-toasted bread.
2. When the bread has soaked well and a toasted crust (called gratin in culinary terms) has formed at the bottom of the pot, incorporate the desired broth to give the soup more or less consistency.
3. Before serving this soup, carefully remove the fat. When transferring the broth from the puchero to another dish for serving or preparation, strain it through fine linen cloth that has been previously moistened in cold water.
4. This soup, which is the same as what in French cuisine is called croute au pot, should be served very hot. Since the gratin is the most delicate part of the dish, it should be served in the pot or pan in which it was made, because when transferred to a tureen it loses all its merit.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puerros a la Crema (Leek Cream Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of cow butter or pork lard
- one pound of leeks, cut into hazelnut-sized pieces
- two heaping tablespoons of flour
- pot broth or water
- salt to taste
- small fried croutons

## Directions

1. For six persons, put two ounces of cow butter or pork lard in a saucepan
2. When the fat is rusty, sauté in it one pound of leeks, cut into pieces the size of hazelnuts, using heads, stalks and leaves
3. Before they take color, add two heaping tablespoons of flour
4. When the flour begins to toast, moisten everything with pot broth or water and season to taste
5. Let cook for half an hour
6. At the moment of serving, bind the soup as indicated for others
7. In the tureen, if desired, have small fried croutons beforehand
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Apio (Celery Puree Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 celery stalks
- butter
- sugar
- broth
- half a quartillo of Spanish sauce
- cow butter
- poultry broth
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take 12 celery stalks, peel and wash them well to remove dirt
2. Blanch for 10 minutes
3. Refresh and cut into four parts
4. Sauté with butter and a little sugar
5. Cover with broth and cook well
6. Add half a quartillo of Spanish sauce
7. Pass through a sieve like all such soups, and when hot add a little cow butter and poultry broth with nutmeg
8. Serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Apio (Puréed Celery Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 celery stalks
- butter
- sugar
- broth
- half a quart of Spanish sauce
- cow butter
- poultry broth
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take 12 celery stalks; peel and wash well to remove dirt
2. Then blanch for ten minutes; cool and cut into four parts
3. Brown with butter and a little sugar
4. Cover with broth and let cook well
5. Add half a quart of Spanish sauce
6. Pass as all the others
7. While hot, add a little cow butter and poultry broth with nutmeg, and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Castaña (Chestnut Puree Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of dried but fresh chestnuts
- one and a half ounces of beef butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- broth or pap to form the soup
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cook the chestnuts well in water
2. Pass them through a food mill
3. In a pan, melt one and a half ounces of beef butter
4. When it begins to be shiny, add one tablespoon of flour
5. Before it takes color, moisten everything with the broth or pap that will form the soup
6. At the first boil, add the chestnut paste
7. Season everything and pass through a finer strainer after a quarter hour of cooking
8. Keep this soup near the fire until the moment of serving, when it is thickened away from the fire with egg yolk as already explained
9. Pour it into the tureen, where there will be toasted or fried bread in small pieces of the same shape
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Caza (Hunter's Style Game Puree Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one hare or partridge
- game essence
- butter
- onion slices
- carrots
- half a quartillo of Jerez wine
- thyme
- bay leaf
- two quartillos of broth
- bread croutons
- small game meat balls

## Directions

1. Debone a hare or partridge, and with the bones prepare a substance or essence called game essence
2. Coat the bottom of a pan with this essence and butter, placing some onion slices and some carrots
3. On top of all this place the bones, add half a quartillo of Jerez wine, thyme and bay leaf
4. Move to low heat to cook
5. Add two quartillos of broth, letting it simmer at the side of the stove for two hours
6. Sauté the meats, pound in a mortar and pass with broth through a strainer
7. Heat in a bain-marie, adding the said essence, and serve with bread croutons, or small meat balls or pellets of game meat
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Cebollas (Onion Puree Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of onions
- quarter pound of cow butter
- salt
- pepper
- thyme
- laurel
- 1 liter of cold water
- broth from the cooking pot
- pearl crackers

## Directions

1. Cut 2 pounds of onions well and sauté over high heat in a pan with a quarter pound of cow butter
2. Season during the sautéing with salt, a hint of pepper, thyme, and laurel
3. Before they take color, moisten with 1 liter of cold water
4. Let cook over low heat with the pan covered for five quarters of an hour
5. Pass through a vegetable mill and obtain a puree white as snow
6. Bind off the heat and serve by thinning with broth from the cooking pot and adding some pearl crackers
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Codornices (Puréed Quail Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 quails
- 8 nuts of cow butter
- good white wine, about half a quart
- 4 quail breasts
- bread crumb
- egg
- good broth

## Directions

1. Remove the breasts from a dozen quails
2. Fry them in cow butter for eight nuts worth of time
3. Then add good white wine, about half a quart, and let it reduce
4. With the other four breasts make a very fine forcemeat to form with bread crumb and egg some very small balls
5. Add good broth, and crush the breasts in a mortar
6. Mix everything together and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Coliflores (Cauliflower Puree Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 cauliflowers
- salt
- 2 ladles of sauce
- sugar
- butter
- egg yolks
- milk
- poultry broth

## Directions

1. Divide 2 or 3 cauliflowers into florets, trimming the stalks well
2. Cook in salted water for about 12 minutes to blanch
3. Place in cold water, drain well
4. Put with about 2 ladles of boiling sauce
5. Cook for about 20 minutes over very low heat so it reduces without burning
6. Pass through a sieve with poultry broth, then through a strainer
7. Add a little sugar, butter, and egg yolks beaten with milk
8. Serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Garbanzos (Puréed Chickpea Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of chickpeas
- cold water
- salt
- hot meat broth
- onion
- bacon
- carrot
- herbs

## Directions

1. Place the chickpeas the day before in cold water with a little salt to soak
2. Cook in a saucepan with hot meat broth, with one onion, bacon, carrot, and a small bundle of herbs
3. Ensure the boiling is slow
4. When cooked, remove the onion, carrot, and bacon
5. Pass through a fine sieve
6. Well degrease the broth and season
7. Heat and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Guisantes a la Parisiense (Pea Puree Soup Parisian Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of dried peas
- four liters of pot broth
- beef butter
- three carrots
- three turnips
- three onions
- white part of one celery stalk

## Directions

1. Soak one liter of dried peas in water for twenty-four hours
2. After that time, drain them well and cook them in four liters of pot broth
3. In a separate pot, sauté three carrots, three turnips, and three onions, all cut into slices, in beef butter
4. When the vegetables have taken on color and the peas have cooked for half an hour, add the sautéed vegetables to the broth
5. Cook at medium heat for three hours
6. Remove the peas and reduce them to a puree
7. Mix the puree with the broth, adjusting the consistency with more or less liquid, or letting it reduce if too thin
8. Twenty minutes before finishing, cut the white part of a celery stalk into strips, sauté it in beef butter, and cook it in the pea soup for that time
9. Season according to taste after the soup is finished
10. Serve hot
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Guisantes Secos (Puréed Dried Pea Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of dried peas
- cow butter
- onions
- carrots
- broth
- herbs

## Directions

1. Brown in a saucepan with cow butter, onions and carrots cut
2. When they are tender, add 2 pounds of dried peas, browning them with the vegetables for some time
3. Moisten with broth
4. Well covered, add a small bundle of herbs and cook beside the fire for about two hours
5. Pass through a fine sieve
6. Then season, heat, and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Guisantes Verdes (Puréed Green Pea Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of green peas
- boiling water
- salt
- sugar
- broth
- bread croutons

## Directions

1. Cook 2 pounds of green peas in boiling salted water over strong fire so it does not lose its color
2. Pass to cold water and then through a fine sieve
3. Place in a clean saucepan with a little sugar
4. Add sufficient broth so it is neither too clear nor too thick
5. Serve well hot with bread croutons
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Guisantes, Lentejas, Judías, Habas, etc. (Pea, Lentil, Bean, and Broad Bean Puree Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- green legumes of choice
- small slices of carrots
- onions
- butter
- fried bread pieces

## Directions

1. Cook any of these green legumes in broth, adding some small slices of carrots and onions and some butter
2. Once cooked, pound in a mortar and pass through a sieve
3. Pour this juice over pieces of fried bread, and once they are well soaked it can be served
4. If you wish the puree to keep its green color, you can add the juice of some mashed spinach
5. If these purees are to be used on fasting days, use fish or amphibian broth and oil
6. These purees can also be made from dried legumes by the same method, as well as from potatoes, sorrel, celery, or any other herbs or vegetables
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Judías Blancas (Puréed White Bean Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- white beans
- cold water
- bacon
- onion
- carrot
- bay leaf
- thyme
- parsley
- cloves
- broth

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as the red bean soup
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Langosta (Puréed Lobster Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lobsters
- boiling water
- salt
- carrots
- onion
- thyme
- bay leaf
- herbs
- reduced sauce, half a quart
- glass of Sherry or Madeira wine

## Directions

1. Place 2 lobsters to cook in boiling salted water with carrots, onion, thyme, bay leaf, and a small bundle of herbs for twenty minutes
2. Remove from fire and when cold, remove the meat from the tails, bodies, and legs, as well as the roe under the tail
3. Pound all these meats and roe in a mortar, reserving the meat from one of the tails
4. When all is paste, incorporate with half a quart of reduced sauce and a glass of Sherry or Madeira
5. Operate in all ways as is known, and when served place in the soup tureen the reserved tail, cut into small cubes
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Lentejas (Puréed Lentil Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- water
- broth
- bread croutons
- rice

## Directions

1. After selecting and washing, cook as the previous soups
2. Pass through a fine sieve
3. Serve with bread croutons or with rice
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Patatas (Puréed Potato Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds of white potatoes
- water
- salt
- cow butter
- poultry broth
- 4 egg yolks
- water

## Directions

1. Cook 4 pounds of white peeled potatoes, cut in quarters, in water and salt
2. Before total cooking, remove all the water well, and in the same covered saucepan place in the oven so they finish cooking and become floury
3. Then stir with a spoon, adding a little cow butter
4. Pass through a fine sieve
5. Dissolve with poultry broth and pass through a tamis
6. At the time of serving, add 4 egg yolks beaten in water
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Perdices (Partridge Puree Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 partridges
- Spanish sauce
- Sherry wine
- juice or essence from bones

## Directions

1. Roast 4 partridges
2. Remove all the meat and pound it in a mortar
3. Add Spanish sauce reduced with Sherry wine
4. Pass through a sieve and strainer
5. When serving add the juice, essence, or broth obtained from the bones
6. Serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Ranas Especial (Special Frog Leg Puree Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 dozen frog legs
- cow butter
- onion
- carrots
- white wine
- pepper
- sauce
- poultry broth
- white broth
- egg yolks
- milk

## Directions

1. Take 4 dozen frog legs, wash and trim the nails
2. Blanch in salted water for 4 minutes
3. Refresh and dry them
4. Sauté with cow butter, finely cut onion and carrots
5. Moisten with half a quartillo of white wine and a little pepper
6. After 10 minutes remove and pound in a mortar
7. Add a quartillo of sauce, pass this through a sieve and strainer
8. Thin with poultry broth, a little white broth, and beaten egg yolks with milk and cow butter
9. Serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré de Raíces Variadas (Puréed Mixed Root Vegetable Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 onions
- 8 carrots
- 2 turnips
- 2 parsnips
- boiling water
- cow butter
- broth

## Directions

1. Peel, wash, and cut 2 onions, 8 carrots, 2 turnips, and 2 parsnips
2. Place in boiling water for a quarter hour over strong fire
3. Cool and dry, placing them in a sieve
4. Brown with cow butter, stirring from time to time so they do not stick to the bottom of the saucepan
5. When ready, moisten with broth, letting them boil slowly beside the fire
6. After cooking, pass as the previous ones
7. Heat, season, and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Puré á la Reina (Puréed Soup à la Queen)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 chickens
- 18 blanched almonds
- 6 cooked egg yolks
- 1/2 pound of rice, washed and cooked in broth
- 2 quarts of white broth
- bread croutons
- small meatballs or forcemeat balls

## Directions

1. Roast four chickens on a spit
2. Once cold, remove the meat and pound in a mortar
3. Add 18 blanched almonds and six cooked egg yolks
4. When all is well reduced to paste, add 1/2 pound of rice well washed and cooked in broth
5. Put all this in a saucepan, adding about two quarts of white broth
6. Heat without boiling and pass through a sieve then through a clean piece of fine cloth
7. Return to a clean saucepan
8. At the time of serving, heat in a bain-marie
9. Serve with croutons, small meatballs, or small forcemeat balls
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Rabo de Vaca (Oxtail Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oxtail
- fine beef butter
- flour
- cayenne pepper
- broth from the pot

## Directions

1. In the pot that is customarily used in a house, incorporate a whole oxtail.
2. When cooked, remove it and cut it into small pieces, preserving the bone in those that allow it.
3. Sauté these pieces in fine beef butter and toast them well.
4. Dust them with flour and a pinch of Cayenne pepper, and moisten with the broth from the pot.
5. Serve this soup very clean and clarified broth, with two or three small pieces of oxtail in each diner's plate.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Ranas (Frog Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty frogs
- carrots
- turnips
- celery
- leeks
- water

## Directions

1. Clean thirty frogs, keeping only the legs
2. Cook the legs with carrots, turnips, celery and leeks
3. Add water in the proportion of one liter for 150 grams of frogs
4. Cook and treat and serve as an ordinary stew
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Ranas Frita (Fried Frog Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- frog legs
- butter
- frying batter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Before placing the frog legs in the pot, they may be passed through hot butter; forty legs are then sufficient for a soup for six people
2. Alternatively, soak the frog legs in a frying batter, fry them and serve with fried parsley
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Ranas Refinada (Refined Frog Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- frog legs
- bread crumb
- cold milk

## Directions

1. When mashing the frog legs, add a piece of bread crumb soaked in cold milk
2. Serve as a refined soup for the discerning palate
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Reinas (Soup of Queens)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three chickens
- Good consummate broth
- Skins and broken bones from the roasted birds
- Half a dozen sweet almonds, well blanched
- A crumb of bread the size of an egg
- Broth from the stew pot

## Directions

1. Roast two or three chickens on a spit according to the number of diners
2. Prepare good consummate broth separately and incorporate into it the skins and broken bones of the roasted birds for good flavor
3. Clean the chicken meat very well, removing any sinews and all hard parts or those that have browned
4. Pound the meat in a marble mortar with half a dozen well-blanched sweet almonds
5. Soak a crumb of bread the size of an egg in broth from the stew pot
6. Incorporate the bread crumb into the bird puree, moistening it with broth so it is more thin than thick and can pass easily through a fine strainer
7. Heat this puree at the moment of serving
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Tortuga (Turtle Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- at least 500 grams of turtle meat
- 1 kilogram of beef
- 1 kilogram of veal
- 1 kilogram of mutton
- carrots
- onions
- seasoning and spices as appropriate
- 1 bottle of Madeira or Port wine
- quenelles cut into slices

## Directions

1. Cut the meat of a turtle into pieces as large as walnuts, which can be compared to that of a calf's kidney.
2. Make the meat release its slime in successive changes of water.
3. At least 500 grams of turtle are needed for a good complete soup.
4. Prepare a rich broth with 1 kilogram of beef, equal weight of veal and the same of mutton, letting it cook for six hours with carrots, onions and the appropriate seasoning and spices.
5. In this broth, strained, cooled and defatted, cook the pieces of turtle over low heat for four hours.
6. At the moment of serving, add a bottle of Madeira or Port wine with quenelles cut into slices; but these are not added to the soup until everything is ready.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Tortuga (Turtle Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 500 grams turtle
- 1 kilogram beef
- 1 kilogram veal
- 1 kilogram mutton
- carrots
- onions
- seasoning and spices
- 1 bottle Madeira or Port wine
- quenelles

## Directions

1. Prepare a finished broth with 1 kilogram beef, equal weight of veal and equal weight of mutton, allowing to cook for six hours with carrots, onions and corresponding seasoning and spices.
2. Strain the broth, cool it and remove the fat.
3. In this prepared broth, cook the pieces of turtle on low heat for the space of four hours.
4. At the moment of serving, add a bottle of Madeira or Port wine with quenelles cut into slices, but these are only placed in the soup when everything is in the tureen.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa de Ángel (Angel Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- rice - eight pounds
- milk - twenty pounds
- eggs - six dozen
- sugar - six pounds
- cinnamon - two ounces (one and a half ounces for infusion, half ounce for powder)
- meat broth - one quarter of the total liquid, without spices

## Directions

1. Infuse one and a half ounces of cinnamon in water in a small covered pot, covered with doubled paper
2. Mix half ounce of cinnamon powder with half pound of sugar
3. Clean the rice well and wash it with warm water, twice if necessary
4. Dry the rice and grind it, then pass through a sieve used for sifting flour
5. Spread the flour on cloths if the weather is hot
6. Ensure the milk is not sour by testing it in a clean pan over heat
7. Place the milk in a pot over heat and bring to a boil
8. Dissolve the rice flour in a pot with milk over heat with the sugar
9. Place the pot containing the soup and paste over heat
10. Add half the eggs with egg whites and the other half without them
11. Stir constantly with a ladle, adding little by little, until it cooks and becomes thick
12. Stop when the mixture no longer tastes of flour
13. Serve with sugar and cinnamon on top
14. Add one quarter broth of meat without spices; it should not have much fat, and the pot should not be rancid - only meat and bacon
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Dorada (Golden Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread slices
- Sugar
- Vigil broth
- Egg yolks
- Lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Take toasted bread slices with which to assemble the soup
2. Place the dish over a little heat, adding ground sugar
3. When the dish is very hot, add enough vigil broth to cover the soup, putting more sugar on top
4. Cover with another plate and let it braise well
5. When the broth is well absorbed into the bread, beat eight egg yolks, adding a little broth, the juice of half a lemon or a few drops of vinegar
6. Pour it all on top, adding more sugar
7. Finish cooking with heat from above inside a bell-shaped cover
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Española (Spanish Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken breasts
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Bread crumb
- Broth from the pot
- Flour
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut chicken breasts into small pieces and cook slowly in butter with salt, pepper and nutmeg
2. Pound the cooked meat in a marble mortar until it becomes a paste
3. In the same pan where the breasts cooked, place a bread crumb well soaked in broth and cook slowly over very low heat, stirring often with a wooden spoon to form a thick paste
4. Let cool and incorporate into the mortar with the chicken breasts and some egg yolks according to the quantity of meatballs being prepared
5. Do this work in a very cool place, and in high-end kitchens add small pieces of ice during the pounding
6. Spread the mixture on a marble table dusted with flour and form quenelles of equal size
7. Poach them for ten minutes in pot broth in a large pan so they float freely and cook without losing shape
8. As the meatballs are removed from the broth, coat them with butter using a blunt knife
9. For Spanish soup, mold the quenelles paste into meatballs the size of hazelnuts
10. Place them in the soup tureen with some very thin slices of French bread, then pour in sufficient broth so the soup is clear
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Flamenca (Flemish Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half liters of water
- one piece of bread crust
- four turnips
- four potatoes of regular size
- parsley
- one ounce of beef butter

## Directions

1. Cook the bread crust, turnips, and potatoes in one and a half liters of water
2. Season, crumble everything and pass through a food mill
3. Add enough water to form a thick pap and let it cook very slowly for twenty minutes, stirring constantly with a spatula
4. Add a couple of tablespoons of very finely chopped parsley and one ounce of beef butter
5. As soon as this melts, and without allowing the soup to cook, remove it from the fire and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Fría (Cold Soup - Akroschka)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beer
- ham loin
- slices of cold meats
- raw onion
- shallots
- wheat grains macerated in brine
- slices of pickled gherkins
- ice chips
- crackers or toasted bread

## Directions

1. Soak in beer: ham loins, slices of cold meats, cut raw onion, a strong dose of finely chopped shallots, grains of wheat macerated in brine, slices of pickled gherkins, and very fine ice chips like pine nuts.
2. This forms the foundation of the broth with which crackers or toasted bread are strained.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Inglesa de Guisantes Verdes (English Green Pea Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- water, three liters
- fresh green peas, one liter, freshly shelled
- butter
- cucumber pulp, three cucumbers, peeled, seeded, and sliced
- lettuce hearts, three tender ones
- parsley, chopped
- fine fresh peas, half liter
- bouquet of fine herbs
- small onions, two or three
- salt and pepper to taste
- mint leaves, finely chopped, one handful

## Directions

1. Cook one liter of fresh, freshly shelled peas well in three liters of water
2. Drain and crush the peas in a mortar
3. Dissolve them in their own broth and pass through a fine hair sieve
4. In butter, sauté the sliced pulp of three peeled and seeded cucumbers with three tender lettuce hearts and chopped parsley for about one hour over low heat, taking care not to let the butter darken
5. Remove the vegetables with a skimmer and add to the prepared and strained broth
6. Carefully remove all the butter that will rise to the surface
7. Put the broth back on the fire and season to taste with salt and pepper
8. Separately, cook half a liter of fine, fresh peas with a bouquet of fine herbs and two or three small onions
9. When the soup is boiling, incorporate the whole peas, which should be very well cooked
10. Pour the soup into the tureen, already cut and prepared
11. English cooks add a handful of finely chopped mint leaves to this soup
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Italiana (Italian Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridge, pheasant, woodcock or other game bird breasts
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Bread crumb
- Broth from the pot
- Flour
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make meatballs with game bird breasts, without any mixture of poultry
2. Proceed in the same manner as for Spanish soup but the result will be more coarse
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Japonesa (Japanese Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled eggs
- broth from the cooking pot
- parsley, dried and pulverized

## Directions

1. Use 6 hard-boiled eggs for 6 diners
2. In 4 spoonfuls of broth from the cooking pot, mash and thin the 6 cooked yolks from the hard-boiled eggs and form a paste
3. Once well worked, thin over the heat in a pan by adding all the broth little by little without ceasing to stir while it cooks
4. As soon as the mixture has homogeneous consistency, pour the contents of the pan into the soup tureen where the whites from the hard-boiled eggs are prepared beforehand, cut into equal small pieces like small dice, and mixed with dried parsley pulverized
5. Serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Juliana (Vegetable Julienne Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrots
- Turnips
- Leeks
- Potatoes
- Onions
- Celery
- Butter from cows
- Lettuce leaves
- Parsley
- Chervil
- Peas, fresh (if season permits)
- Tender beans (if season permits)
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut carrots, turnips, leeks, potatoes, onions, and celery into thin threads
2. Sauté them in cow butter so they take on color
3. Add finely chopped lettuce leaves, parsley, and chervil
4. If the season permits, augment with a handful of fresh peas and another of tender beans
5. When the vegetables are slightly cooked, add the necessary water and season
6. Off the heat, incorporate a piece of fresh cow butter
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Juliana Purée (Vegetable Julienne Soup Purée)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Carrots
- Turnips
- Leeks
- Potatoes
- Onions
- Celery
- Butter from cows
- Lettuce leaves
- Parsley
- Chervil
- Peas, fresh (if season permits)
- Tender beans (if season permits)
- Water
- Salt
- Bread, fried in small pieces

## Directions

1. Cut carrots, turnips, leeks, potatoes, onions, and celery into thin threads
2. Sauté them in cow butter so they take on color
3. Add finely chopped lettuce leaves, parsley, and chervil
4. If the season permits, augment with a handful of fresh peas and another of tender beans
5. Cook the vegetables much longer so they can be passed through a food mill
6. Pass through a food mill to make a purée
7. Serve with small pieces of fried bread
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Normanda (Norman Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- fish broth
- butter
- sorrel
- chervil
- onions
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Make a broth with sea fish
2. In a pot, fry butter with cut sorrel, chervil and onions
3. Moisten with the fish broth, salt and add pepper
4. Bring to a boil for one hour
5. Blanch the broth and thicken with egg yolks beaten in water
6. Serve over slices of bread
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Parmentier (Parmentier Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half liters of good pot broth
- one pound of potatoes of the best class among the most floury
- half a quartillo of milk
- one tablespoon of flour
- 50 grams of beef butter
- a couple of tablespoons of chervil
- parsley
- spearmint

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of potatoes in one and a half liters of good pot broth
2. Break them up and mash with a fork
3. Pass through a food mill to form a light pap, which is then thickened over low heat
4. Add half a quartillo of milk in which one tablespoon of flour is well dissolved
5. At the moment of boiling, and away from the fire, melt 50 grams of beef butter in the soup
6. Add a couple of tablespoons of very finely chopped chervil, or in its place, one of parsley with a hint of spearmint
7. In the tureen when the soup is poured, there will be small squares of fried bread; but for the soup to have all its character, instead of bread, put some cooked potatoes cut raw into small balls with a vegetable-shaped knife
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Provenzal (Provençal Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- oil
- vegetables for concentrated broth
- fish

## Directions

1. Put water and oil in a pot and add the indicated vegetables for concentrated broth
2. Cut the fish into pieces and place in the pot
3. Salt and moisten with vigil broth if available, or otherwise with water
4. Sprinkle again with very good oil
5. Cook for a quarter hour
6. Remove the fish and serve each type separately with one of the most appropriate condiments
7. Strain the broth and soak and soak the fried croutons or slices of bread
8. Serve
9. A little white wine or brandy may be added if desired
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Puré à la Condé (Condé Style Puree Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of red beans
- cold water
- pieces of bacon
- one onion
- carrots
- a bunch of bay leaf, thyme, parsley and two cloves

## Directions

1. Clean and wash two pounds of red beans, putting them to cook in cold water with some pieces of bacon, an onion, carrots and a bunch of bay leaf, thyme, parsley and two cloves
2. Once cooked, remove the bacon, legumes and water
3. Pass with broth through a linen strainer and season
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Puré á la Coüdé (Puréed Bean Soup Coüdé Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of red beans
- cold water
- small pieces of bacon
- 1 onion
- carrot
- bay leaf
- thyme
- parsley
- 2 cloves

## Directions

1. Clean and wash two pounds of red beans
2. Place them to cook in cold water with small pieces of bacon, one onion, carrot, and a bundle of bay leaf, thyme, parsley, and two cloves
3. Once cooked, remove the bacon, vegetables, and water
4. Pass through a fine sieve with broth
5. Season, heat, and serve with fried bread croutons
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopa Turca (Turkish Soup)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of the best and freshest cow butter
- two tablespoons of corn starch
- half a pint of milk
- one liter of pot broth
- three hard-boiled eggs
- equal volume of beets
- double the volume of truffles in shavings or slivers
- one egg yolk

## Directions

1. For eight persons, melt a quarter pound of cow butter in a saucepan
2. Partially fry two tablespoons of corn starch in it
3. Moisten with half a pint of milk
4. Season and add spices, then extend with one liter of pot broth
5. Add three hard-boiled eggs cut into dice shape
6. Add an equal volume of beets, cut the same way
7. Add double the volume of truffles in shavings or slivers
8. At the moment of serving and off the heat, bind the soup with the egg yolk dissolved according to the ritual
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Sopas de Ajo Inmortales (Immortal Garlic Soups)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- bread
- salt
- paprika
- pure oil
- Spanish garlic

## Directions

1. Place a wide and deep earthenware bowl on the fire
2. Fill it with water
3. Break bread into small fragments and add to the water
4. Sprinkle with salt and paprika
5. Drizzle with pure oil
6. Peel two cloves of Spanish garlic and hide them in the spongy bread mixture
7. Heat everything together over the fire until it condenses
8. When a brown crust forms around the edges, place the bowl on a serving dish
9. Serve hot
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Stewed Leg of Mutton (Pierna de carnero estofada)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton, deboned and prepared
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- carrots
- broth

## Directions

1. Debone and prepare the leg of mutton as described for the previous preparation
2. Cook the leg of mutton always over low heat and with the pot covered, with salt, pepper, onions, carrots, etc., and only as much broth as necessary so the meat does not stick to the vessel
3. Turn the leg from time to time so it cooks evenly
4. Keep the pot covered at all times with parchment paper and its lid on top
5. Serve with the little juice that remains after the operation
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Stuffed Artichokes (Alcachofas Rellenas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes
- Oil
- Ham
- Poultry breast
- Breadcrumb paste
- Dried parsley
- Garlic
- Broth

## Directions

1. Soak the artichokes in oil before cooking
2. Cook the artichokes
3. Soak them again in the same oil in which they were first soaked
4. After a while, remove them and with a wooden spoon and fingers, hollow out each artichoke by opening the leaves
5. Fill with a chopped mixture, generally of ham and poultry breast
6. Close the artichoke and tie it with twine to keep the filling inside during cooking, as the artichoke tends to open naturally
7. Pour a little oil over the filling and sprinkle breadcrumb paste mixed with dried parsley and small pieces of garlic on top
8. Cook in the oven if you prefer them dried out, but it is better to simmer them for a quarter hour over low heat with a few spoonfuls of broth in a braising pan with strong heat on top of the lid
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Stuffed Eggs (Huevos Rellenos)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Hard-boiled eggs
- Breadcrumbs soaked in milk
- Fresh butter
- Onions
- Parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fine spices

## Directions

1. Cut hard-boiled eggs in half lengthwise.
2. Mash the yolks with an equal amount of breadcrumbs soaked in milk and squeezed dry.
3. Add the same quantity of fresh butter.
4. Add finely chopped onions and parsley, salt, pepper, and fine spices.
5. Mash this mixture and soften it with raw egg yolks.
6. Fill the hard whites with this mixture.
7. Place the remaining mixture on a fireproof plate forming a layer about the thickness of a finger.
8. Arrange the eggs on top.
9. Place the plate in a camp oven with light heat above and below so they take on color.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Suspiros de Monja (Nun's Sighs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of butter
- four ounces of sugar
- one glass of water
- grated lemon zest
- flour
- three eggs
- powdered sugar for serving

## Directions

1. Put two ounces of butter, four ounces of sugar, one glass of water, and grated lemon zest in a saucepan
2. Add flour and bring to a boil until a paste forms that detaches easily from the pan
3. Mix in three eggs and stir everything well
4. Spread the mixture on a plate using the handle of a kitchen spoon and cut into small pieces
5. Drop the pieces into hot oil (not too hot) and fry them
6. Drain on a napkin and serve sprinkled with finely ground sugar
7. This is a traditional dish for All Saints' Day
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ternera Asada (Roasted Veal)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- beef fat or pork fat for wrapping
- salt

## Directions

1. Wrap the piece of veal meat destined for roasting in paper greased with beef fat or pork fat
2. Roast over fire, being careful to cook thoroughly
3. A quarter hour before removing the meat from the roaster, remove the paper wrapping so it takes on the golden color it needs
4. Season with salt only at the moment the paper is removed
5. Serve the roasted veal on a platter with all its juices, well degreased
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ternera Asada á las Finas Hierbas (Roasted Veal with Fine Herbs)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- parsley
- two or three shallots
- refined oil
- salt
- pepper
- beef or pork fat
- flour
- broth or veal cooking juices
- vinegar
- watercress
- salt water

## Directions

1. Prepare a marinade with lots of parsley and two or three shallots, chopped fine and dissolved in a couple spoonfuls of refined oil, to which salt and pepper are added
2. Rub the piece of veal successively on all sides with this marinade so that by the time it goes to the roaster it has absorbed all the oil from the marinade
3. Spread the chopped fine herbs well soaked in oil over the entire surface of the veal
4. Wrap the meat tightly in greased paper, fastened securely with string to prevent the herbs from shifting or falling
5. When the roast is nearly cooked and only needs to take on color, carefully remove the paper
6. Using a metal spatula, collect the fine herbs adhered to the paper and meat
7. Place these herbs in a pan with 125 grams of beef or pork fat and a spoonful of flour to make a blonde sauce, moistened and extended with a little broth or the degreased juices from the roast and a thread of vinegar
8. Serve this sauce separately in a sauce boat, and the veal on a platter, garnished with watercress dipped in salted water
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ternera Riñones Fritos (Fried Veal Kidneys)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidneys
- pork lard
- tomato sauce
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and trim the kidneys
2. Heat pork lard in a pan over very high heat
3. Fry the kidneys, being careful to distinguish between sautéing and frying, and remove them from the pan as soon as they begin to burst
4. Mix the fried kidneys with a very thick tomato sauce
5. Serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ternera Riñones Salteados (Sautéed Veal Kidneys)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidneys
- flour
- lard or butter
- broth or water
- parsley
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Heat fat in a pan over high heat and cook the kidneys until they take on color
2. Remove the kidneys from the pan and set aside on a plate
3. In the same fat, brown a good spoonful of flour, and before it takes on much color, deglaze with broth or water
4. Incorporate finely chopped parsley and bring the sauce to a boil
5. Add the kidneys back to the pan and cook for three minutes
6. Serve hot and the kidneys will be tender
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ternera Riñones Salteados con Vino (Sautéed Veal Kidneys with Wine)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidneys
- flour
- lard or butter
- broth
- wine, such as white wine, red wine, Sherry, Bordeaux, or Champagne
- parsley
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare as for the previous recipe but use only half the broth
2. Add two glasses of white wine, red wine, Sherry, Bordeaux, Champagne, or similar wine
3. Cook and serve as directed in the previous recipe
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ternera Riñones à la Dinamarquesa (Veal Kidneys Danish Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal kidneys
- refined oil
- fresh butter from cows
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- clove spice
- nutmeg
- good rum

## Directions

1. Cut the kidneys and soak them in refined oil for two hours
2. Remove the kidneys from the oil and sauté them over high heat in a pan with very fresh butter for five minutes at most
3. Set the kidneys aside on a plate
4. Prepare finely chopped onion in an amount equal to the volume of the kidneys
5. In the butter remaining in the pan, adding more if necessary, sauté the onion, stirring well with a spatula so that it glazes and gelatinizes without taking on color
6. Season with salt, pepper, a pinch of clove spice, and a hint of nutmeg
7. Over high heat, incorporate the kidneys and pour over them a glass of good rum
8. Shake the pan vigorously for two minutes, allowing the contents to remain in constant movement
9. Serve on a platter, preferably within a basket of fried bread croutons made from a loaf
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ternillas de Ternera con Guisantes (Veal Sweetbreads with Peas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal sweetbreads
- butter or pork fat
- broth

## Directions

1. Cut the sweetbreads into small pieces and brown them in butter or pork fat.
2. Moisten with degreased broth, only enough so the sweetbreads can cook without sticking to the pan.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ternillas de Ternera con Guisantes (Veal Sweetbreads with Peas)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal sweetbreads
- butter from cows or pork
- defatted broth
- bundle of fine herbs
- one liter of freshly shelled peas
- flour
- water or broth

## Directions

1. Cut the sweetbreads into small pieces and brown them in cow or pork butter.
2. Moisten with defatted broth, only as much as necessary so the sweetbreads can cook without sticking to the pan.
3. When almost cooked, add a bundle of fine herbs and one liter of freshly shelled peas.
4. Once the peas are cooked and consequently the meat as well, remove everything and let it drain in the strainer.
5. Reduce the sauce, which generally becomes quite thin with the juice of the peas, skim off the fat and add one tablespoon of flour well dissolved in water or broth from the pot.
6. When the sauce has taken on a certain consistency, pour it into a covered serving dish or vegetable bowl, in which the sweetbreads and peas should already be placed.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Ternillas de Ternera en Pepitoria (Veal Sweetbreads in Pepitoria Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- veal sweetbreads
- butter from cows or pork
- flour
- clean broth
- bundle of fine herbs
- onions the size of walnuts
- mushrooms
- salt and pepper
- parsley
- egg yolks
- water

## Directions

1. Brown the sweetbreads in cow or pork butter, being careful that it does not take on color.
2. Add a heaping tablespoon of flour and brown it without letting it toast.
3. Moisten with clean broth and add a bundle of fine herbs, a good handful of onions the size of walnuts, and an equal quantity of trimmed mushrooms.
4. Season with restraint and cook over low heat until the sweetbreads are very tender.
5. Sprinkle generously with finely chopped parsley.
6. Serve by binding the sauce away from the heat with two or three egg yolks dissolved in half a glass of water.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tinola (Philippine Chicken Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- lard
- garlic
- onion
- parsley
- salt
- water
- potatoes
- or zucchini

## Directions

1. Heat lard in a pan or pot until very hot
2. Add a little garlic, half an onion, and parsley, taking care to fry all of this without burning it
3. Once well fried, add the chicken, turning it so it takes on color
4. Immediately after, add water until the chicken is covered
5. When the chicken is half-cooked, add potatoes cut into pieces
6. When they are well cooked, the tinola is finished
7. The dish should be brothy
8. Note: the chicken should be cut at the joints, as this prevents small bones from remaining in the broth
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tocinillo de Leche Asado (Roasted Suckling Pig Leftovers)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- leftovers from roasted suckling pig
- spicy sauce

## Directions

1. Carve carefully what remains of the roasted suckling pig into presentable pieces
2. Toast them on grills over lively heat
3. Serve with a spicy sauce
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tocino de Cielo (Heavenly Bacon)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarter liters of water
- two pounds of piloncillo sugar
- three dozen egg yolks

## Directions

1. Clarify two pounds of piloncillo sugar in two quarter liters of water. Strain through a felt strainer; then beat three dozen yolks until they bind in a small cord and mix well. Continue beating, and then...
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tocino de Cielo (Spanish Custard Cake)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- two quartillos of water
- two pounds of piloncillo sugar
- three dozen egg yolks

## Directions

1. Clarify two pounds of piloncillo sugar in two quartillos of water.
2. Strain through a felt cloth.
3. Beat three dozen egg yolks until they bind together in cordoncillo.
4. Mix well.
5. Continue beating.
6. Place in a flan mold or small molds.
7. Cook the dish in a water bath.
8. Do not remove from the mold until completely cold.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tomates Crudos (Raw Tomatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes with tender skin
- salt
- pepper
- oil

## Directions

1. Choose tomatoes of tender skin, without wrinkles, the size of eggs. Wash and dry them, and remove the stem.
2. Cut them in half crosswise and place them on a plate or dish with the cut side up.
3. Sprinkle generously with ground salt and a pinch of pepper, and drop by drop, pour oil over each half so that they soak and are well moistened.
4. To eat them, place them on radish dishes or in a soup plate, but inverted, with the rounded surface on top, sprinkled again with salt.
5. Eat them alone as a side dish between courses, or as a complement to the salad served to each diner.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tomates Rellenos (Stuffed Tomatoes)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- minced poultry, ham or meat
- tomato pulp
- toasted breadcrumbs
- oil
- dried parsley
- seasoning

## Directions

1. Cut the tomatoes as in the previous recipe and hollow out a little.
2. Complete a filling with the pulp removed from them, made of minced poultry, ham or meat.
3. Fill the tomatoes making a small pile on top, and sprinkle generously with toasted breadcrumbs kneaded with oil, dried parsley powder, and good seasoning.
4. Do this already placing the tomatoes on the metal dish in which they are to be served, and place it in the oven or cover it with a dome cover with fire on top.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Torrijas de Anchoas (Anchovy Toasts)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Long, thin slices of bread
- Oil
- Virgin oil
- Lemon juice
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Cebolleta (small onion)
- Shallots
- Anchovies
- White wine

## Directions

1. Fry the long, thin slices of bread in oil.
2. Separate them on a plate.
3. Pour over them a sauce made with virgin oil, lemon juice, pepper, parsley, cebolleta, and shallots, all finely chopped.
4. Place two or three small fillets of anchovies rinsed in white wine on each toast.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tortilla a la Española (Spanish Omelet)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve eggs
- half a pound of potatoes
- lard or oil in large quantity
- salt
- pepper
- three tablespoons of fat

## Directions

1. Fry the potatoes cut like coins in lard or oil in large quantity.
2. Before they toast or become crispy, remove them from the pan and set aside on a plate, sprinkling them with a little salt.
3. Remove excess fat from the pan, leaving three tablespoons, and put the pan back over high heat.
4. At this moment, the twelve egg whites should be beaten beforehand, separately, until foamy, and the twelve yolks, also well beaten, with two tablespoons of fresh water and the seasoning of salt and pepper that the omelet is to have.
5. Mix the whites and yolks and beat them together while the pan is on the fire.
6. When the fat smokes blue, without stopping beating, pour the mixture into the pan and beat it still inside for a few seconds, during which the set-aside potatoes are incorporated.
7. With the left hand shake the pan in a circle, imprinting it with a circular movement so that the omelet, as soon as it sets, begins to turn on its base, but in the opposite direction.
8. That is the moment to turn it over to the other side, using a tinplate kitchen utensil if needed.
9. With this apparatus turn the omelet at will, and proceed in the same way so that it sets on the other side.
10. As the setting becomes firmer, increase the shaking so that the dish colors equally, according to the operator's preference.
11. The Spanish omelet should be solid, well-combined, round and of great thickness.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tortilla al Ron (Rum Omelet)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eight very fresh eggs
- 125 grams of ground sugar
- zest of one large lemon, grated
- three or four tablespoons of thick cream
- a pinch of salt
- six tablespoons of good rum

## Directions

1. Prepare the omelet exactly as the Sweet Omelet.
2. Once made, place it on a silver or metal serving dish.
3. Sprinkle with sugar.
4. Pour six tablespoons of good rum over it.
5. Set the rum on fire to serve.
6. Alternatively, you can make the omelet with Kirsch or any other high-proof spirit.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tortilla Celestina (Celestina Omelet)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Egg yolks
- Sweet filling

## Directions

1. Make a very thin omelet with egg yolks alone
2. Make it slightly thick
3. When folding it, add a little sweet filling between its two layers
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tortilla con Leche (Omelet with Milk)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- one cup of milk per pair of eggs
- a small amount of flour

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs, and when they are well beaten, pour into them one cup of milk for each pair of eggs.
2. With a little flour beat again, and immediately pour everything into the pan, making a French omelet, which results in the smoothest and most exquisite of omelets.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tortilla Dulce (Sweet Omelet)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eight very fresh eggs
- 125 grams of ground sugar
- zest of one large lemon, grated
- three or four tablespoons of thick cream
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Separate the egg yolks from the whites of eight very fresh eggs.
2. Incorporate 125 grams of ground sugar into the yolks, beating them vigorously.
3. Add the grated zest of one large lemon, three or four tablespoons of thick cream, and a pinch of salt.
4. Combine the prepared yolks with the whites.
5. Beat everything together.
6. Make the omelet in the Spanish or French style as explained in pages 664-668.
7. Once the omelet is made and placed on the serving dish, sprinkle with sugar.
8. Pass a heated stoker from the stove-cooker over it, drawing lines well to make the caramelization pleasing to the sight.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tortilla Francesa al Natural (French Omelet, Plain)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- ten to twelve eggs
- two tablespoons of very fresh water
- salt
- a pinch of pepper
- two ounces of fresh unsalted beef butter
- one ounce of fresh beef butter

## Directions

1. Beat ten to twelve eggs in a bowl with a steel fork with long tines.
2. Add two tablespoons of very fresh water, which serves to make the mixture of yolks and whites more intimate.
3. Season with salt and a small pinch of pepper.
4. Melt two ounces of very fresh unsalted beef butter over high heat in the pan, and when it is shiny and about to take color, pour in the beaten eggs and stir with a spatula until it begins to set.
5. Once it sets well and always holding the pan by the handle, lift the omelet on one side with the tip of a kitchen knife, and through the opening pass more beef butter in the amount of one ounce.
6. Once it has melted, fold the omelet in half over itself, and with the pan tilted mold and straighten the edges or sides, and turn it out onto a serving platter to serve it very hot.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tortilla Literaria (Literary Omelet)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon steaks
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- broth
- white wine
- mushrooms (champignons)
- truffles
- chicken livers
- rooster crests
- ham
- flour
- butter
- nutmeg
- eggs

## Directions

1. Take a good salmon steak, coat it with oil, salt and pepper, and place two or three bay leaves on each side, then roast it on a grill.
2. In a mixture of broth and white wine, cook a medium jar of mushrooms and a smaller jar of truffles if you do not have fresh ones.
3. Gather chicken livers, rooster crests, and ham pieces, and sauté all together.
4. In a very light flour sauce, shred the aforementioned salmon, adding a good piece of butter.
5. Finally, mix everything that has been mentioned, sprinkle it with nutmeg and beat the eggs, folding the omelet in the French manner so that it does not become compacted and hard, but rather juicy and loose.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tortilla Literaria (Literary Omelet)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- a good slice of salmon
- oil
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take a good slice of salmon, coat it with oil, salt and pepper, and on each side...
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tortilla Rellena de Dulce (Sweet-Filled Omelet)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- peach jam
- apricot jam
- stewed peach slices
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. Make the omelet as you would with potatoes, mushrooms, or pea purée à la française, and with butter, but being careful not to add salt.
2. At the moment of folding it in the pan, add peach jam, apricot jam, stewed peach slices, currant jelly, etc.
3. Use a large, wide, and flat pan so that the surface is exaggerated in relation to its thickness, which should be slight.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tortilla Soplada o Vanidosa (Soufflé Omelet)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- 125 grams of ground sugar
- zest of one lemon or orange, grated
- a hint of vanilla or other aroma of choice
- two ounces of butter

## Directions

1. Separate the egg yolks from the whites of six eggs.
2. Put 125 grams of ground sugar into the yolks along with the grated zest of a lemon or orange, or a hint of vanilla—any aroma you wish—and beat it all very quickly.
3. Beat the whites separately until they form stiff peaks, with nothing else added.
4. In a silver or metal oval serving dish, melt two ounces of butter.
5. Place the dish over high heat.
6. Mix the whites and yolks quickly and pour into the dish.
7. Cover with a dome cover loaded with considerable heat.
8. Five minutes, by the clock, are enough for the omelet to puff up.
9. At that moment sprinkle with sugar, an operation that must be done skillfully between the kitchen and the table so that it is presented puffed up and burning.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tostadas Alcoyanas (Alicoy Toasts)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- ground almonds in equal quantity to eggs by weight
- sugar
- a pinch of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix well all ingredients
2. Work the mixture for a long time
3. Place in strong paper boxes
4. Bake in the oven with a white coating of sugar on top
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tostón al Golpe de Estado (Roasted Suckling Pig)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One suckling pig, fifteen to twenty days old
- Water
- Salt
- Bacon rind
- Small wooden sticks or reeds for supports

## Directions

1. Select a young suckling pig between fifteen and twenty days of age.
2. Kill the animal and submerge it completely in a caldron of boiling water.
3. Once clean and white, split it down the belly, opening it lengthwise from the snout to the tail inclusive.
4. Empty it entirely and wash it well inside and out, drying it thoroughly with burlap.
5. Extend it as if it were tanned hide and pierce it lengthwise with the spit of the roaster, using small canes or reeds as crosspieces so that it does not close.
6. Prepare a simple brine of water and salt.
7. With a kitchen brush dipped in this brine, coat and moisten it well on all sides.
8. Place it before a fire of burning wood coals, turning it constantly.
9. At each turn, rub it with a piece of good bacon rind, then moisten it again with the brine, until the skin forms blisters and acquires a hazelnut color.
10. One and a half hours should suffice for the operation.
11. The bacon rind is preferable to butter because butter softens what is toasting, while bacon makes it crisp and friable.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Truchas a la Provenzal (Trout Provençal)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine oil
- Four or five trout of half a pound each
- Salt
- Pepper
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. In a metal baking dish that is to go to the table, pour fine oil to coat the bottom and place four or five trout of half a pound each.
2. Season with salt and pepper, and over high heat brown them, moving the dish so they do not stick.
3. When they are browned on one side, turn them to the other, and cover them then with breadcrumbs mixed together.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Truchas de las Tres Efes (Trout of the Three F's - Fine, Fresh, Fried and Cold)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine fresh trout
- Oil
- Salt

## Directions

1. Fry the fine and fresh trout whole in oil, with no other seasoning than sprinkling them with ground salt after washing and draining well, and eat cold.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Truchas Escabechadas (Marinated Trout)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Trout, fried
- Strong vinegar
- Lemon slices
- Bay leaves
- Pepper
- Clove
- Onion, sliced in rings
- Bundle of fine herbs
- Garlic clove
- Oil

## Directions

1. After frying the trout, place them undrained in a baking dish and, while still very hot, pour over them strong vinegar, some lemon slices, two bay leaves, a pinch of pepper, clove, onion rings, a bundle of fine herbs and a clove of garlic.
2. Put the dish on the heat, and as soon as it begins to boil, cover and set aside until the dish cools.
3. Then remove the trout and broth and place in a deep platter so that they are well bathed, and add enough oil so that a thin layer remains on top, which is what preserves the escabeche in all its best flavor.
4. When you want to eat these trout, serve them without the broth.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Truchas à la Provençale (Trout Provençal Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Fine oil
- Four or five trout of half a pound each
- Salt
- Pepper
- Breadcrumbs
- Parsley
- Chives
- Garlic

## Directions

1. In a metal baking dish that will go to the table, pour fine oil to coat the bottom and arrange four or five trout of half a pound each.
2. Season with salt and pepper, and over high heat brown them, moving the baking dish so they do not stick.
3. When browned on one side, turn them over and cover with breadcrumbs mixed with salt, pepper, parsley, chives, and garlic, all chopped very finely.
4. With very low heat below and heat above on the pan lid, cook for about a quarter hour, taking care to frequently moisten the surface with the little oil at the bottom, and serve when it takes on good color.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Turtle Soup (English Style) (Sopa Inglesa)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 liters of water
- 2 kilos of beef sirloin (solomillo)
- 2 or 3 carrots
- 1 onion
- 1 celery stalk
- 1 bunch of fine herbs
- 20 grams of pepper
- 30 grams of salt
- 1 kilo of lean veal with little bone
- half a calf's head, well deboned, without brains and without tongue
- 250 grams of very lean ham
- 4 shallots
- 120 grams of beef butter
- 6 cloves
- a little nutmeg
- 1 sprig of thyme
- 3 coffee spoons of finely chopped parsley
- half a lemon in slices
- 60 grams of fine flour
- Cayenne pepper
- juice of 1 lemon
- 2 glasses of Sherry
- 2 dozen quenelles of meat or poultry breast

## Directions

1. In a pot or marmite with 4 liters of water, add 2 kilos of beef sirloin, 2 or 3 carrots, 1 onion, 1 celery stalk, 1 bunch of fine herbs, 20 grams of pepper and 30 grams of salt.
2. When the meat is well cooked, strain the broth, let it cool and put it back on the fire with 1 kilo of lean veal with very little bone. Let it cook slowly until the meat separates from the bone, being careful that the boiling does not accelerate so that the broth does not reduce too much.
3. Separately, have ready half a calf's head, well deboned, without brains and without tongue. These should have been cooked beforehand to reinforce the substance of the soup with their broth.
4. Pass the first broth through a hair sieve, letting the meat drain well. Let it cool to remove the solidified fat.
