Then wrap the half calf's head like a cloth, securing it with twine, and place this package and the tongue loose in a marmite, pouring the first cold broth over it and letting it cook over low heat for three quarters of an hour, or at most one hour.
5. Place the half calf's head and the tongue in a deep baking dish, pour the broth over it and let it cool. This precaution is so that the calf's head does not take on color.
6. On the other hand, cut 250 grams of very lean ham into very thin slices, which are then subdivided into small square pieces, and mince 4 shallots.
7. In a 4-liter pot, melt 120 grams of beef butter; add the ham with the shallots, half a dozen cloves, a little nutmeg, 1 sprig of thyme, 3 coffee spoons of finely chopped parsley, and half a lemon in slices.
8. Keep this mixture on the fire for one hour, being careful that the butter does not blacken and constantly sautéing the contents of the pot.
9. Then add 60 grams of fine flour gradually, and after cooking this for a few minutes, pour the defatted broth free of the sediment that forms at the bottom of the baking dish in small portions.
10. Keep everything on the fire, but without cooking, for four hours, and after that time strain the broth through a sieve, passing it to another vessel. Cut the calf's head and tongue into fine and equal pieces, and as if they were slices of bread, add them to the broth.
11. For this soup to be more delicate, cut the skin and fatty parts of the calf's head into thin leaves; when the soup is for a family meal, without guests, cut the meat of the head and glutinous parts at the same time, but it is preferable to set aside the lean meat, even if the soup remains clearer.
12. With everything prepared this way, put the soup in a baking dish and let it cook for one hour, so that the calf's head is tender. Add a touch of Cayenne pepper and a little salt if the seasoning does not seem very spiced.
13. Three minutes before serving, add the juice of 1 lemon, 2 glasses of Sherry and 2 dozen quenelles of meat or poultry breast to the turtle soup.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Té (Tea)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Tea leaves
- Water

## Directions

1. Heat the teapot by pouring boiling water into it, then empty it
2. Put in as many heaping teaspoons of tea as there are cups desired
3. Pour a small quantity of boiling water over the leaves
4. Let it rest for three or four minutes, covering the teapot well
5. Pour boiling water in the necessary amount
6. Serve the tea
7. More tea can be obtained by adding boiling water again, but never reuse leaves that have already released their aroma this way
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Tínola (Chicken Filipino Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One cleaned chicken
- Salt
- Butter
- Garlic
- Half an onion
- Parsley
- Water
- Potatoes in pieces
- Calabash or zucchini as a substitute according to taste

## Directions

1. After cleaning the chicken, divide it into pieces by cutting at the joints and season lightly with salt
2. In a skillet or pot, heat butter until very hot, then add a little garlic, half an onion and parsley, being careful to fry all this without burning
3. Once this is well fried, add the chicken, turning it so it takes color
4. Immediately add water until it covers the chicken, and when the chicken is half cooked, add potatoes in pieces
5. When the potatoes are well cooked the tínola is ready
6. This dish should be broth-like
7. The potatoes can be replaced with calabash or zucchini according to taste
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Uspot (Veal Stew)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal shanks
- Water
- Salt
- Bacon
- Onion
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Ginger
- Broth
- Six egg yolks
- White bread crumb

## Directions

1. Take veal shanks cut into pieces and boil them with water and salt
2. When they are half cooked, remove them and place them in a pan
3. Take bacon cut into cubes and fry it, and have a quantity of onion sliced lengthwise and fry it in the bacon, and add it to the pan over the meat, and season with pepper, nutmeg and ginger
4. Then add broth until it covers and cook slowly
5. For each plate, have six hard-boiled egg yolks and a crumb of white bread soaked in vinegar
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vaca Cocida (Boiled Beef)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef from the hindquarters
- Bones
- Frameworks, feet and necks of birds and game
- Salt
- Legumes
- Garlic
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Choose preferably beef from the hindquarters or part of it, remove the bones, roll it and tie it with twine
2. Place it in a pot with the frameworks, feet and necks of birds and game used for appetizers
3. Skim well over high heat; once it has cooked a little, add salt, all varieties of legumes and garlic, to taste
4. Remove the beef when it is cooked through, being careful to withdraw it when properly cooked, although the broth may have less substance, because if you attend to the latter, the meat will come out shredded and overcooked
5. Serve it either surrounded by fresh parsley, chopped in sauce garnished with onions and legumes, or with pastries
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vaca Cocida a la Marinero (Beef Cooked Sailor-Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- small onions
- flour
- red wine
- broth
- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- thyme
- beef slices

## Directions

1. Fry small onions in butter, add a spoonful of flour and brown it
2. Add a small glass of red wine and half a glass of broth, some mushrooms, salt, pepper, bay leaf and thyme
3. When the sauce is made, pour it over the slices of meat arranged on a fire-resistant plate
4. Leave this over coals for half an hour and serve
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vaca Cocida en Pepitoria (Boiled Beef in Pepitoria Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsley, finely chopped
- Onions, finely chopped
- Sixty grams of beef or pork lard, or oil
- A good tablespoon of flour
- Salt and spices
- A couple of ladles of broth
- Two egg yolks
- Water
- Cooked beef

## Directions

1. Finely chop parsley and onions.
2. Sauté them in sixty grams of beef or pork lard, or oil if preferred, being careful that the fat does not brown.
3. Add a good tablespoon of flour, season and spice.
4. Moisten with a couple of ladles of broth and let the mixture cook for ten minutes.
5. Cut the cooked beef into thin slices, removing cartilage and fatty parts.
6. Give it a couple of boils in the sauce, which will be thickened when serving with two egg yolks dissolved in water.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vaca del Puchero (Boiled Beef)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Leg of beef
- Chickpeas
- Bacon

## Directions

1. Cook the beef in water with chickpeas, bacon and other ingredients as desired.
2. Serve with the broth.
3. Cold leftovers from good quality pieces make an excellent cold dish for breakfast, prepared in various ways.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vaca Estofada (Braised Beef)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- A good piece of leg of beef, three or four pounds
- Two ounces of lard
- Three pounds of onions
- A quarter pound of bacon in loose pieces
- A hand of veal
- Salt and seasoning
- Parsley and other herbs in a bouquet
- Carrots, turnips, and parsnips if available
- One quarter of a unit of red wine
- Half a unit of vinegar

## Directions

1. In a pot of adequate capacity, place the piece of beef shaped like a brick.
2. Pour two ounces of lard and brown the meat well.
3. Add three pounds of trimmed onions, a quarter pound of bacon in loose pieces, a hand of veal, salt and seasoning, parsley and other herbs in a bouquet, carrots, turnips, and parsnips if available.
4. The water released by the onion is all the liquid the stew should have.
5. When the onion softens and begins to paste, add one quarter of a unit of red wine and half of vinegar.
6. Cook everything over high heat for one hour until the broth reduces by half, then three more hours consecutively over very low heat with the pot hermetically sealed.
7. Serve this piece of meat as a first course with the vegetables around it and the hand of veal in small pieces alternating with them, all moistened with the little broth or liquid that remains.
8. Leftovers, kept whole for the next morning breakfast, can be served cold with fried eggs, breadcrumbs, salad, or as desired, and make an excellent second course.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vaca Guisada (Braised Beef)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Butter or oil
- Parsley
- Onion
- Garlic

## Directions

1. Sauté the meat in a pan or casserole with butter or oil, chopped parsley, onion and garlic
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vaca Guisada (Stewed Beef)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- butter or oil
- parsley
- onion
- garlic
- water
- spices

## Directions

1. Brown the meat in a pan or casserole with butter or oil, parsley, chopped onion and garlic
2. When the meat is half cooked, transfer it to a pot
3. Add a little water and let it cook
4. Sprinkle with spices dissolved in water
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vaca Mechada (Larded Beef)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- bacon
- butter
- sorrel

## Directions

1. Soak the meat, and after removing the skins well, place inside many small pieces of bacon at intervals
2. Put butter in a casserole, and when hot, add the meat, which should not be stirred
3. Cover with an iron lid and with fire on top and coals below, cook it slowly
4. Afterwards prepare a sauce of cooked sorrel, which should be poured not on top of the meat but at its sides
5. Serve, being careful not to let the juice reduce too much
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vaca Mechada (Larded Beef)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- beef
- bacon pieces
- lard
- sorrel
- salt

## Directions

1. Soak the meat and remove the skin
2. Insert small pieces of bacon at intervals throughout the meat
3. Heat lard in a cazuela pot
4. Place the meat in the pot without turning it
5. Cover with an iron lid and place fire on top with coals underneath
6. Cook slowly
7. Prepare a sauce from cooked sorrel
8. Pour the sorrel sauce around the sides of the meat, not on top
9. Serve, being careful not to let the juices reduce completely
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vieiras al Estilo de Vigo (Scallops Vigo Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- large scallops
- parsley, chopped
- garlic
- scallions
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- refined oil
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Select large scallops about the size of the palm of a hand
2. Remove the meat from the shell and cut it
3. Mix with a hash of parsley, garlic, scallions, pepper, clove and nutmeg
4. Fill the shell with this mixture
5. Pour a spoonful of refined oil over the top
6. Cover everything with bread crumbs
7. Roast the scallops in the oven at high heat for a quarter of an hour
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vieiras al Estilo de Vigo (Scallops Vigo Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Large scallops, size of palm of hand
- parsley, minced
- garlic
- scallions
- pepper
- clove
- nutmeg
- refined oil
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Remove the meat from the scallop shell
2. Cut the meat and mix it with minced parsley, garlic, scallions, pepper, clove, and nutmeg
3. Fill the shell with this mixture
4. Pour one tablespoon of refined oil over the top
5. Cover everything with breadcrumbs
6. Roast the scallops in the oven at high heat for a quarter of an hour
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vinagre al Estragón (Tarragon Vinegar)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- One quart of strong vinegar
- Fresh tarragon branches

## Directions

1. Heat a quart of strong vinegar without allowing it to boil.
2. Submerge two or three freshly cut sprigs of tarragon in the hot vinegar.
3. When the vinegar has cooled, pour it into a bottle and add new sprigs of fresh tarragon to infuse.
4. Leave the tarragon branches in the bottle, replenishing the vinegar as it is used so that it remains saturated with the aroma of the plant.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Vino Caliente (Hot Wine)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 liters red wine
- 5 grams cinnamon stick
- 2 cloves
- zest of 2 sour oranges
- 250 grams piloncillo sugar

## Directions

1. For twenty-four hours, steep the cinnamon stick, cloves, and orange zest in the red wine
2. Strain the wine through a coffee filter
3. Put over gentle heat in a pan with the piloncillo sugar
4. When the wine is about to boil, pour it into a punch bowl
5. Drink it boiling hot, as it becomes unpleasant and cloying when lukewarm or cold
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Zanahorias Salteadas (Sautéed Carrots)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Young carrots
- Pork lard
- Salt

## Directions

1. Prepare and cut carrots as in the previous formula
2. Sauté and fry the young carrots in a spacious pan with pork lard, sprinkling with salt, but there should be only enough fat in the pan to season the dish, accounting for what it absorbs
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Zanahorias Tempranas a la Crema (Early Carrots in Cream)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Early carrots
- Water, a few spoonfuls
- A touch of cow's butter
- Salt and pepper
- Finely chopped fine herbs
- Half a cup of cream, or double the amount of milk if cream is not available
- Two egg yolks
- One tablespoon of milk
- A pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Prepare carrots as in the previous recipe and cut into thin slices the width of a peseta coin
2. Cook in a few spoonfuls of water with a touch of cow's butter, salt, pepper, and trimmed fine herbs
3. When they lack little to be entirely cooked, pour over them half a cup of cream, or if not available, double that amount of milk
4. Finish cooking over slow heat
5. At the moment of serving these carrots in a covered legume dish, bind the sauce with two egg yolks dissolved in one tablespoon of milk with a pinch of salt
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Zanahorias Tiernas a la Mayordoma (Young Carrots in the Steward's Style)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Young carrots
- Fresh butter
- One tablespoon of flour
- A good pinch of finely chopped fine herbs
- Water or broth, a few spoonfuls
- Salt and pepper
- Two or three egg yolks
- Two tablespoons of fresh water

## Directions

1. Scrape the carrots and cut them into four pieces lengthwise
2. Sauté over high heat in fresh butter with one tablespoon of flour and a good pinch of finely chopped fine herbs
3. Temper the heat so the butter does not color
4. Moisten with a few spoonfuls of water or broth, seasoning strongly with salt and pepper
5. Once the carrots are cooked, remove the pan from heat and place them on a serving dish
6. Pour over them a binding of two or three egg yolks, dissolved in two tablespoons of fresh water
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Zorzal Asado (Roasted Thrush)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Thrush
- Pitted olive
- Salt

## Directions

1. Clean and empty the thrush
2. Place a pitted olive and a pinch of salt inside
3. Skewer with other thrushes on an iron rod
4. Roast, being careful not to expose them to direct flame
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Zorzal en Salsa (Thrush in Sauce)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- Thrush, well fattened
- Piece of bacon
- Peppercorn
- Butter for frying
- Thrush livers and kidneys
- Broth
- Flour

## Directions

1. Clean the thrush well and place a piece of bacon and a peppercorn inside
2. Fry in good butter
3. Remove the thrush's livers and kidneys, smoke and salt them, then grind in a mortar
4. Dilute the resulting paste in a small amount of broth
5. Add a little flour
6. Pour the sauce over the cooked thrush
7. Cook over high heat for half an hour until the dish is delicate, flavorful, and nourishing
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina

title: Zorzales Asados (Roasted Thrush)
author: Ángel Muro
body:
## Ingredients

- thrush
- thin sheets of bacon, very thin

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean the thrush very well.
2. Open them not to remove the innards, but only the crop or gizzard.
3. Make a hole below one leg, press the bird, and the crop will come out on its own.
4. Wrap with thin sheets of bacon, but very thin.
5. Thread them by half-dozens, inserting a thin spit rod between the wing and the leg, in such a way that the bacon sheets do not come loose.
6. Fix the rod to the spit so it does not move, and roast at high heat for twenty minutes.
source_title: El Practicón: tratado completo de cocina
